sections.title_en: Opening

entries.farsi: بیان بعضی از حکمت تأخیر این مجلّد دویم که / اگر جملۀ حکمت الهی بنده را معلوم شود در فواید
entries.english: Touching this Second Book, the reason of its postponement is set forth, namely, that if all the Divine Wisdom be made known to His slave, the slave in the benefits of that act will be left without the power to perform it and the infinite Wisdom of God will destroy his understanding, he will not engage in that act.

entries.farsi: آن کار بنده از آن کار فرو ماند و حکمت بی پایان / حقتعالی ادراک او را ویران سازد و بدان کار
entries.english: Therefore the High God makes a small portion of that infinite Wisdom a toggle in his nose and leads him towards that act; for if He give him no information about those benefits, he will not move at all, inasmuch as the motive arises from advantages to ourselves or others, for the sake of which we act accordingly; while if He should pour down upon him the wisdom of that, he will also be unable to move; just as a camel will not go unless there be a toggle in his nose, and if the toggle be big he will likewise lie down.

entries.farsi: .نپردازد / پس حقتعالی شمّۀ از آن حکمت بی پایان مهار
entries.english: And there is nothing but We have the storehouses thereof, and We do not send it down but in a certain measure. Without water, earth does not become a brick, nor does it become a brick when there is much water.

entries.farsi: بینی او کند و اورا بدان کار کشد که اگر او را از / آن فایده هیچ خبرنکند هیچ نجنبد، زیرا که ویرا
entries.english: And the heaven, He raised it and set up the scales. He gives every thing by the scale, not without calculation and balance,save to those who have been transmuted from the creaturely state of existence and have become.

entries.farsi: جنبانیدن از بهر آنست که از بهر آنمصلحت آفریده / شده است، و اگر حکمت آن بر وی فرو ریزد هم
entries.english: And He bestows on whom He pleases without calculation, whoever has not tasted does not know.

entries.farsi: نتواند جنبیدن چنانکه در بینی شتر اگر مهار نبود / نرود و اگر مهار سخت بزرگ بود هم نرود و فرو
entries.english: Some one asked, “What is love?” I answered, “You will know when you become we”

entries.farsi: ٍو اِن مِن شَیيً الاّ عِندَنا خَزَائنُهُ و ما نُنزُّله الاّ بِقَدَر / معلوم.خاک بی آب کلوخ نشود و چون آب بسیار
entries.english: Love is uncalculated affection. For that reason it has been said to be in reality the attribute of God and unreal in relation to His slave. He loves them is the entire sum. What is they love Him?

sections.title_en: In The Name Of God The Merciful The Compassionate

entries.farsi: مهلتى بايست تا خون شير شد / تا نزايد بخت تو فرزند نو
entries.english: This Masnavi has been delayed for a while: an interval was needed in order that the blood might turn to milk.

entries.farsi: خون، نگردد شير شيرين خوش شنو / چون ضياء الحق حسام الدين عنان
entries.english: Blood does not become sweet milk until your fortune gives birth to a new babe. Listen well.

entries.farsi: ‏باز گردانيد ز اوج آسمان / چون به معراج حقايق رفته بود
entries.english: When the Light of God, Husamu’ddin, drew the reins back from the zenith of Heaven

entries.farsi: بى‏بهارش غنچه‏ها نشكفته بود / چون ز دريا سوى ساحل باز گشت
entries.english: After he had gone in the ascension to realities, without his -- springtide the buds did not blossom.

entries.farsi: باز گشتش روز استفتاح بود / مطلع تاريخ اين سودا و سود
entries.english: When he returned from the Sea towards the shore, the lyre of the poetry of the Masnavi became attuned

entries.farsi: ‏چنگ شعر مثنوى با ساز گشت / مثنوى كه صيقل ارواح بود
entries.english: The Masnavi, which was the polisher of spirits — his return was the day of seeking commencement

entries.farsi: سال اندر ششصد و شصت و دو بود / بلبلى ز ينجا برفت و باز گشت
entries.english: The date of beginning this gainful traffic was in the year six hundred and sixty-two.

entries.farsi: بهر صيد اين معانى باز گشت / ساعد شه مسكن اين باز باد
entries.english: A nightingale flew away and returned: it became a falcon for hunting these spiritual truths.

entries.farsi: تا ابد بر خلق اين در باز باد / آفت اين در هوا و شهوت است
entries.english: May this falcon’s resting-place be the fore-arm of the King! May this gate be open to the people unto everlasting!

entries.farsi: ور نه اينجا شربت اندر شربت است / اين دهان بر بند تا بينى عيان
entries.english: The bane of this gate is sensuality and lust; else, draught on draught is here.

entries.farsi: چشم بند آن جهان حلق و دهان / اى دهان تو خود دهانه‏ى دوزخى
entries.english: Throat and mouth are the eye-bandage to yonder world: close this mouth that you may see plain.

entries.farsi: ‏وى جهان تو بر مثال برزخى / نور باقى پهلوى دنياى دون
entries.english: O mouth, truly you are the mouth of Hell; and, O world; you are like the intermediate state.

entries.farsi: ‏شير صافى پهلوى جوهاى خون / چون در او گامى زنى بى‏احتياط
entries.english: The everlasting light is beside this low world, the pure milk is beside rivers of blood.

entries.farsi: شير تو خون مى‏شود از اختالط / يك قدم زد آدم اندر ذوق نفس
entries.english: When you take one step in it without precaution, your milk will be turned to blood through commixture.

entries.farsi: ‏شد فراق صدر جنت طوق نفس / همچو ديو از وى فرشته مى‏گريخت
entries.english: Adam took one step in sensual pleasure: separation from his high place in Paradise became a collar on the neck of his soul.

entries.farsi: ‏بهر نانى چند آب چشم ريخت / گر چه يك مو بد گنه كاو جسته بود
entries.english: The angels were fleeing from him as from a devil: how many tears did he shed for the sake of a single loaf!

entries.farsi: ليك آن مو در دو ديده رسته بود / بود آدم ديده‏ى نور قديم
entries.english: Although the sin which he had compassed was a hair, yet that hair had grown in his eyes.

entries.farsi: موى در ديده بود كوه عظيم / گر در آن آدم بكردى مشورت
entries.english: Adam was the eye of the Eternal Light: a hair in the eye is a great mountain.

entries.farsi: ‏در پشيمانى نگفتى معذرت / ز آن كه با عقلى چو عقلى جفت شد
entries.english: If Adam had taken counsel in that, he would not have uttered excuses in penitence,

entries.farsi: مانع بد فعلى و بد گفت شد / نفس با نفس دگر چون يار شد
entries.english: Because when one intellect is joined with another intellect, it prevents evil action and evil speech;

entries.farsi: عقل جزوى عاطل و بى‏كار شد / چون ز تنهايى تو نوميدى شوى
entries.english: When the nafs is associated with another nafs, the partial intellect becomes idle and useless.

entries.farsi: ‏زير سايه‏ى يار خورشيدى شوى / رو بجو يار خدايى را تو زود
entries.english: When because of loneliness you fall into despair, you become a sun under the shadow of the friend.

entries.farsi: چون چنان كردى خدا يار تو بود / آن كه در خلوت نظر بر دوخته ست
entries.english: Go; seek at once the friend of God: when you have done so, God is your friend.

entries.farsi: ‏آخر آن را هم ز يار آموخته ست / خلوت از اغيار بايد نه ز يار
entries.english: He who has fixed his gaze upon seclusion, after all ‘tis from the friend that he has learned that

entries.farsi: پوستين بهر دى آمد نه بهار / عقل با عقل دگر دو تا شود
entries.english: One must seclude one’s self from strangers, not from the friend: the fur-coat is for winter, not for spring.

entries.farsi: نور افزون گشت و ره پيدا شود / نفس با نفس دگر خندان شود
entries.english: The intellect is paired with another intellect, light increases and the way becomes plain;

entries.farsi: ظلمت افزون گشت و ره پنهان شود / يار چشم تست اى مرد شكار
entries.english: The nafs makes merry with another nafs, darkness increases, the way becomes hidden.

entries.farsi: از خس و خاشاك او را پاك دار / هين به جاروب زبان گردى مكن
entries.english: The friend is your eye, O huntsman: keep him pure from sticks and straws.

entries.farsi: ‏چشم را از خس ره آوردى مكن / چون كه مومن آينه‏ى مومن بود
entries.english: Beware! Do not make a dust with thy tongue’s broom; do not make a present of rubbish to your eye.

entries.farsi: روى او ز آلودگى ايمن بود / يار آيينه ست جان را در حزن
entries.english: Since the true believer is a mirror for the true believer, his face is safe from defilement.

entries.farsi: ‏در رخ آيينه‏اى جان دم مزن / تا نپوشد روى خود را در دمت
entries.english: The friend is a mirror for the soul in sorrow: breathe not on the face of the mirror, O my soul

entries.farsi: ‏دم فرو خوردن ببايد هر دمت / كم ز خاكى چون كه خاكى يار يافت
entries.english: Lest it cover its face on account of thy breath, thou must swallow your breath at every moment.

entries.farsi: از بهارى صد هزار انوار يافت / آن درختى كاو شود با يار جفت
entries.english: Are you less than earth? When a plot of earth finds a friend, that is, a springtide, it finds a hundred thousand flowers.

entries.farsi: ‏از هواى خوش ز سر تا پا شكفت / در خزان چون ديد او يار خالف
entries.english: The tree that is united with a friend, that is, the sweet air, blossoms from head to foot;

entries.farsi: ‏در كشيد او رو و سر زير لحاف / گفت يار بد بال آشفتن است
entries.english: In autumn, when it sees a repugnant companion, it withdraws its face and head under the coverlet

entries.farsi: چون كه او آمد طريقم خفتن است / پس بخسبم باشم از اصحاب كهف
entries.english: And says, “A bad comrade is stirring up trouble: since he has come, my course is to sleep.

entries.farsi: به ز دقيانوس باشد خواب كهف / يقظه شان مصروف دقيانوس بود
entries.english: Therefore I will sleep; I will be one of the Men of the Cave: that prisoner of woe is better than Decianus.”

entries.farsi: خوابشان سرمايه‏ى ناموس بود / خواب بيدارى ست چون با دانش است
entries.english: Their time of waking was expended by Decianus; their sleep was the capital of their renown.

entries.farsi: ‏واى بيدارى كه با نادان نشست / چون كه زاغان خيمه بر بهمن زدند
entries.english: Sleep, when it is accompanied by wisdom, is wakefulness; alas for the man awake who consorts with the ignorant!

entries.farsi: بلبالن پنهان شدند و تن زدند / ز آنكه بى‏گل‏زار بلبل خامش است
entries.english: When the crows pitch their tents on Bahman, the nightingales hide themselves and are mute,

entries.farsi: غيبت خورشيد بيدارى كش است / آفتابا ترك اين گلشن كنى
entries.english: Because the nightingale is silent without the rose-garden: the absence of the sun kills wakefulness.

entries.farsi: تا كه تحت الارض را روشن كنى / آفتاب معرفت را نقل نيست
entries.english: O sun, you take leave of this rose-garden in order to illumine below the earth;

entries.farsi: ‏مشرق او غير جان و عقل نيست / خاصه خورشيد كمالى كان سرى ست
entries.english: The Sun of Divine knowledge has no motion: its place of rising is none other but the spirit and intellect;

entries.farsi: روز و شب كردار او روشنگرى ست / مطلع شمس آى گر اسكندرى
entries.english: Especially the perfect Sun which is of yonder: day and night its action is illumination.

entries.farsi: ‏بعد از آن هر جا روى نيكوفرى / بعد از آن هر جا روى مشرق شود
entries.english: If you are an Alexander, come to the Sun’s rising-place: after that, wherever you go, you possess goodly splendour,

entries.farsi: شرقها بر مغربت عاشق شود / حس خفاشت سوى مغرب دوان
entries.english: After that, wherever you go, it will become the place of sunrise: the places of sunrise will be in love with thy place of sunset.

entries.farsi: ‏حس در پاشت سوى مشرق روان / راه حس راه خران است اى سوار
entries.english: Your bat-like senses are running towards the sunset; your pearl-scattering senses are faring towards the sunrise.

entries.farsi: اى خران را تو مزاحم شرم دار / پنج حسى هست جز اين پنج حس
entries.english: The way of sense-perception is the way of asses, O rider: have shame, O you that art jostling with asses!

entries.farsi: ‏آن چو زر سرخ و اين حسها چو مس / اندر آن بازار كايشان ماهرند
entries.english: Besides these five senses there are five senses: those are like red gold, while these senses are like copper.

entries.farsi: حس مس را چون حس زر كى خرند / حس ابدان قوت ظلمت مى‏خورد
entries.english: In the bazaar where they are expert, how should they buy the copper sense like the sense of gold?

entries.farsi: حس جان از آفتابى مى‏چرد / اى ببرده رخت حسها سوى غيب
entries.english: The bodily sense is eating the food of darkness; the spiritual sense is feeding from a Sun.

entries.farsi: ‏دست چون موسى برون آور ز جيب / اى صفاتت آفتاب معرفت
entries.english: O you that have borne the baggage of your senses to the Unseen, put forth thy hand, like Moses, from your bosom.

entries.farsi: ‏و آفتاب چرخ بند يك صفت / گاه خورشيد و گهى دريا شوى
entries.english: O you whose attributes are the Sun of Divine knowledge, while the sun in heaven is confined to a single attribute,

entries.farsi: ‏گاه كوه قاف و گه عنقا شوى / تو نه اين باشى نه آن در ذات خويش
entries.english: Now you become the Sun, and now the Sea; now the mountain of Qaf, and now the Anqa.

entries.farsi: ‏اى فزون از وهمها و ز بيش بيش / روح با علم است و با عقل است يار
entries.english: In your essence you are neither this nor that, O you that are greater than imaginations and more than more!

entries.farsi: روح را با تازى و تركى چه كار / از تو اى بى‏نقش با چندين صور
entries.english: The Spirit is associated with knowledge and reason: What has the Spirit to do with Arabic and Turkish?

entries.farsi: هم مشبه هم موحد خيره‏سر / گه مشبه را موحد مى‏كند
entries.english: Both the muwahhid and the mushabbih immanence are bewildered by you; O you who, being without image are in so many forms.

entries.farsi: گه موحد را صور ره مى‏زند / گه ترا گويد ز مستى بو الحسن
entries.english: Sometimes the muwahhad is destroying the mushabbih; sometimes forms are waylaying the muwahhid.

entries.farsi: ‏يا صغير السن يا رطب البدن / گاه نقش خويش ويران مى‏كند
entries.english: Sometimes Abu ‘l-Hasan in drunkenness says to thee, “O you whose teeth are small, O you whose body is tender!”

entries.farsi: از پى تنزيه جانان مى‏كند / چشم حس را هست مذهب اعتزال
entries.english: Sometimes he is laying waste to his own image: he is destroying in order to assert the transcendence of the beloved.

entries.farsi: ‏خويش را سنى نمايند از ضالل / هر كه در حس ماند او معتزلى ست
entries.english: The doctrine held by the eye of sense is Mu`tazilism, whereas the eye of Reason is Sunni in respect of union.

entries.farsi: ‏ديده‏ى عقل است سنى در وصال / سخره‏ى حس‏اند اهل اعتزال
entries.english: Those in thrall to sense-perception are Mu`tazilites, from misguidedness they represent themselves as Sunnis.

entries.farsi: ‏گر چه گويد سنيم از جاهلى ست / هر كه بيرون شد ز حس سنى وى است
entries.english: Any one who remains in sense-perception is a Mu`tazilite; though he may say he is a Sunni, it is from ignorance.

entries.farsi: ‏اهل بينش چشم عقل خوش پى است / گر بديدى حس حيوان شاه را
entries.english: Any one who has escaped from sense-perception is a Sunni: the man endowed with vision is the eye of sweet-paced Reason.

entries.farsi: پس بديدى گاو و خر اهلل را / گر نبودى حس ديگر مر ترا
entries.english: If the animal sense could see the King, then the ox and the ass would behold Allah.

entries.farsi: جز حس حيوان ز بيرون هوا / پس بنى آدم مكرم كى بدى
entries.english: If, besides the animal sense, you had not another sense outside of the desire of the flesh,

entries.farsi: ‏كى به حس مشترك محرم شدى / نامصور يا مصور گفتنت
entries.english: Then how should the sons of Adam have been honoured? How by means of the common sense should they have become privileged?

entries.farsi: ‏باطل آمد بى‏ز صورت رستنت / نامصور يا مصور پيش اوست
entries.english: Your calling “formless” or “formed” is vain, without your liberation from form.

entries.farsi: ‏كاو همه مغز است و بيرون شد ز پوست / گر تو كورى نيست بر اعمى حرج
entries.english: “Formless” or “formed,” He is with him that is all kernel and has gone forth from the husk.

entries.farsi: ‏ور نه رو كالصبر مفتاح الفرج / پرده‏هاى ديده را داروى صبر
entries.english: If you are blind, it is no crime in the blind; if not, go, for patience is the key to success.

entries.farsi: هم بسوزد هم بسازد شرح صدر / آينه‏ى دل چون شود صافى و پاك
entries.english: The medicine of patience will burn the veils over your eye and will also affect the opening of your breast.

entries.farsi: ‏نقشها بينى برون از آب و خاك / هم ببينى نقش و هم نقاش را
entries.english: When the mirror of your heart becomes clear and pure, you will behold images outside of water and earth.

entries.farsi: فرش دولت را و هم فراش را / چون خليل آمد خيال يار من
entries.english: You will behold both the image and the image-Maker, both the carpet of empire and the carpet-Spreader.

entries.farsi: ‏صورتش بت معنى او بت شكن / شكر يزدان را كه چون شد او پديد
entries.english: The phantom of my Friend seemed like Khalil— its form an idol, its reality a breaker of idols.

entries.farsi: در خيالش جان خيال خود بديد / خاك درگاهت دلم را مى‏فريفت
entries.english: Thanks be to God that when he appeared, my spirit beheld in his phantom its own phantom.

entries.farsi: خاك بر وى كاو ز خاكت مى‏شكيفت / گفتم ار خوبم پذيرم اين از او
entries.english: The dust of your threshold was bewitching my heart: dust on him that was patient without your dust!

entries.farsi: ور نه خود خنديد بر من زشت رو / چاره آن باشد كه خود را بنگرم
entries.english: I said, “If I am beautiful, I shall receive this from him; and if not, he has indeed laughed at ugly me.

entries.farsi: ‏ور نه او خندد مرا من كى خرم / او جميل است و محب للجمال
entries.english: The plan is this, that I look at myself otherwise he will laugh at me: how shall I buy? “

entries.farsi: ‏كى جوان نو گزيند پير زال / خوب خوبى را كند جذب اين بدان
entries.english: He is beautiful and a lover of beauty: how should a fresh young man choose a decrepit old woman?

entries.farsi: ‏طيبات و طيبين بر وى بخوان / در جهان هر چيز چيزى جذب كرد
entries.english: The Beautiful attracts the beautiful: know this. Recite thereon the good women for the good men.

entries.farsi: گرم گرمى را كشيد و سرد سرد / قسم باطل باطالن را مى‏كشند
entries.english: In this world everything attracts something: the Hot draws the hot and the cold the cold.

entries.farsi: باقيان از باقيان هم سر خوشند / ناريان مر ناريان را جاذب‏اند
entries.english: The worthless sort attract the worthless; the lasting rejoiced by the lasting.

entries.farsi: نوريان مر نوريان را طالب‏اند / چشم چون بستى ترا تاسه گرفت
entries.english: Those of the Fire attract those of the Fire; those of the Light seek those of the Light.

entries.farsi: نور چشم از نور روزن كى شكفت / تاسه‏ى تو جذب نور چشم بود
entries.english: When you shut your eye, you felt uncomfortable: how could the light of the eye do without the light of the window?

entries.farsi: تا بپيوندد به نور روز زود / چشم باز ار تاسه گيرد مر ترا
entries.english: Your discomfort was the light in your eye straining to be joined speedily with the daylight.

entries.farsi: دان كه چشم دل ببستى بر گشا / آن تقاضاى دو چشم دل شناس
entries.english: If you feel distress while your eyes are unclosed, know that you have shut the eye of your heart, open it.

entries.farsi: ‏كاو همى‏جويد ضياى بى‏قياس / چون فراق آن دو نور بى‏ثبات
entries.english: Recognize that that is the craving of the eyes of your heart which is seeking the immeasurable Light.

entries.farsi: تاسه آوردت گشادى چشمهات / پس فراق آن دو نور پايدار
entries.english: Inasmuch as separation from those two impermanent lights brought you discomfort, you opened your eyes,

entries.farsi: تاسه مى‏آرد مر آن را پاس دار / او چو مى‏خواند مرا من بنگرم
entries.english: Separation, then, from those two steadfast lights will bring you discomfort: guard them!

entries.farsi: تسخرى باشد كه او بر وى كند / كى ببينم روى خود را اى عجب
entries.english: Since He is calling me, I will look to see whether I am worthy to be drawn or whether I am ill-favoured.

entries.farsi: ‏اليق جذب‏ام و يا بد پيكرم / گر لطيفى زشت را در پى كند
entries.english: If a charming person makes an ugly one at his heels, it is a mockery that he makes of him.

entries.farsi: ‏تا چه رنگم همچو روزم يا چو شب / نقش جان خويش مى‏جستم بسى
entries.english: How, I wonder, shall I behold my own face, so as to see what complexion I have and whether I am like day or like night?

entries.farsi: ‏هيچ مى‏ننمود نقشم از كسى / گفتم آخر آينه از بهر چيست
entries.english: For a long while I was seeking the image of my soul, my image was not displayed by any one.

entries.farsi: تا بداند هر كسى كاو چيست و كيست / آينه‏ى آهن براى پوستهاست
entries.english: “After all,” I said, “what is a mirror for? That every one may know what and who he i

entries.farsi: ‏آينه‏ى سيماى جان سنگين بهاست / آينه‏ى جان نيست الا روى يار
entries.english: The mirror of iron is for husks; the mirror that shows the aspect of the heart is of great price.

entries.farsi: رو به دريا كار برنايد به جو / زين طلب بنده به كوى تو رسيد
entries.english: The soul’s mirror is naught but the face of the friend, the face of that friend who is of yonder country.

entries.farsi: روى آن يارى كه باشد ز آن ديار / گفتم اى دل آينه‏ى كلى بجو
entries.english: I said, “O heart, seek the Universal Mirror, go to the Sea: the business will not succeed by means of the river.”

entries.farsi: درد مريم را به خرما بن كشيد / ديده‏ى تو چون دلم را ديده شد
entries.english: In this quest your slave arrived at your dwelling-place, the pains drew Mary to the palm-tree.

entries.farsi: اين دل ناديده غرق ديده شد / آينه‏ى كلى ترا ديدم ابد
entries.english: When your eye became an eye for my heart, my blind heart went and became drowned in vision.

entries.farsi: ديدم اندر چشم تو من نقش خود / گفتم آخر خويش را من يافتم
entries.english: I saw that you are the Universal Mirror unto everlasting: I saw my own image in your eye.

entries.farsi: ‏در دو چشمش راه روشن يافتم / گفت وهمم كان خيال تست هان
entries.english: I said, “At last I have found myself: in his eyes I have found the shining Way.”

entries.farsi: ‏ذات خود را از خيال خود بدان / نقش من از چشم تو آواز داد
entries.english: My false instinct said, “Beware! That is your phantom: distinguish your essence from your phantom”;

entries.farsi: كه منم تو تو منى در اتحاد / كاندر اين چشم منير بى‏زوال
entries.english: My image gave voice from your eye, “I am you and you are I in oneness;

entries.farsi: از حقايق راه كى يابد خيال / در دو چشم غير من تو نقش خود
entries.english: For how should a phantom find the way into this illumined eye which never ceases from the realities?”

entries.farsi: گر ببينى آن خيالى دان و رد / ز آن كه سرمه‏ى نيستى در مى‏كشد
entries.english: “If you behold your image in the eyes of any other than me, know that it is a phantom and reprobate,

entries.farsi: باده از تصوير شيطان مى‏چشد / چشمشان خانه‏ى خيال است و عدم
entries.english: Because he is applying the eye salve of non-existence and is imbibing the wine of Satan’s illusion-making.

entries.farsi: ‏نيستها را هست بيند الجرم / چشم من چون سرمه ديد از ذو الجلال
entries.english: Their eye is the home of imagination and non-existence: necessarily it sees as existent the things which are non-existent;

entries.farsi: ‏خانه‏ى هستى است نه خانه‏ى خيال / تا يكى مو باشد از تو پيش چشم
entries.english: Since my eye saw eye salve from the Glorious, it is the home of existence, not the home of imagination.”

entries.farsi: ‏در خيالت گوهرى باشد چو يشم / يشم را آن گه شناسى از گهر
entries.english: So long as a single hair of you is before your eye, in your imagination a pearl will be as jasper.

entries.farsi: كز خيال خود كنى كلى عبر / يك حكايت بشنو اى گوهر شناس
entries.english: You will know jasper from pearls at the time when you pass away from your imagination entirely.

entries.farsi: ‏تا بدانى تو عيان را از قياس
entries.english: O connoisseur of pearls, listen to a story, that you may distinguish actual seeing from inference.

sections.title_fa: هالل پنداشتن آن شخص خيال را در عهد عمر
sections.title_en: How in the time of Umar, may God be well-pleased with him, a certain person imagined that what he saw was the new moon.

entries.farsi: ماه روزه گشت در عهد عمر / بر سر كوهى دويدند آن نفر
entries.english: The Fasting-month came round in `Umar’s time. Some people ran to the top of a hill,

entries.farsi: تا هالل روزه را گيرند فال / آن يكى گفت اى عمر اينك هالل
entries.english: In order to take the new moon as a good omen, and one of them said, “Look, O `Umar, here is the new moon!”

entries.farsi: چون عمر بر آسمان مه را نديد / گفت كاين مه از خيال تو دميد
entries.english: As ‘Umar did not see the moon in the sky, he said, “This moon has risen from your imagination

entries.farsi: ور نه من بيناترم افلاک را / چون نمى‏بينم هالل پاك را
entries.english: Otherwise, I am a better seer of the heavens, how is it that I do not see the pure crescent?

entries.farsi: گفت تر كن دست و بر ابرو بمال / ‏آن گهان تو بر نگر سوى هالل
entries.english: Wet your hand,” said he, “and rub it on your eyebrow, and then look up towards the new moon.”

entries.farsi: چون كه او تر كرد ابرو مه نديد / گفت اى شه نيست مه شد ناپديد
entries.english: When he wet his eyebrow, he did not see the moon. “O King,” he said, “there is no moon; it has disappeared.”

entries.farsi: گفت آرى موى ابرو شد كمان / ‏سوى تو افكند تيرى از گمان
entries.english: “Yes,” said `Umar, “the hair of your eyebrow had become like a bow and shot at thee an arrow of opinion.”

entries.farsi: چون يكى مو كج شد او را راه زد / تا به دعوى الف ديد ماه زد
entries.english: When one hair became crooked, it waylaid him, so that, making a false claim, he boasted to have seen the moon.

entries.farsi: موى كج چون پرده‏ى گردون بود / چون همه اجزات كج شد چون بود
entries.english: Inasmuch as a crooked hair veils the sky, how will it be when all your members are crooked?

entries.farsi: راست كن اجزات را از راستان / سر مكش اى راست رو ز آن آستا
entries.english: Straighten your members by the straight. O you, who go straight, turn not your head aside from that threshold.

entries.farsi: هم ترازو را ترازو راست كرد / هم ترازو را ترازو كاست كرد
entries.english: Balance makes balance correct; balance also makes balance defective’.

entries.farsi: هر كه با ناراستان هم سنگ شد / در كمى افتاد و عقلش دنگ شد
entries.english: Whoever weighs the same as the unrighteous falls into deficiency, and his understanding becomes dazed.

entries.farsi: رو أَشِدَّاءُ عَلَى الْكُفَّارِ باش / ‏خاك بر دل دارى اغيار پاش
entries.english: Go, be hard on the infidels, sprinkle dust on fondness for the strangers.

entries.farsi: بر سر اغيار چون شمشير باش / ‏هين مكن روباه بازى شير باش
entries.english: Be as a sword upon the heads of the strangers: come, do not play foxy tricks, be a lion,

entries.farsi: تا ز غيرت از تو ياران نگسلند / ز آنكه آن خاران عدوى اين گلند
entries.english: In order that the friends, moved by jealousy, may not break with you, because those thorns are the enemies of this rose.

entries.farsi: آتش اندر زن به گرگان چون سپند / ز آن كه آن گرگان عدوى يوسفند
entries.english: Set fire to the wolves as rue-seed, because those wolves are the enemies of Joseph.

entries.farsi: جان بابا گويدت ابليس هين / تا به دم بفريبدت ديو لعين
entries.english: Iblis calls you “father’s soul”—beware! The accursed Devil in order that he may beguile you with words.

entries.farsi: اين چنين تلبيس با بابات كرد / آدمى را اين سيه رخ مات كرد
entries.english: He practiced the like imposture on your father: this black-faced one checkmated an Adam.

entries.farsi: بر سر شطرنج چست است اين غراب / ‏تو مبين بازى به چشم نيم خواب
entries.english: This crow is busy on the chessboard; look not you upon his game with an eye that is half-asleep,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه فرزين بندها داند بسى / ‏كه بگيرد در گلويت چون خسى
entries.english: Because he knows many formidable moves which will stick in your throat like a straw

entries.farsi: در گلو ماند خس او سالها / چيست آن خس مهر جاه و مالها
entries.english: His straw will stay in your throat for years. What is that straw? Love of rank and wealth.

entries.farsi: مال خس باشد چو هست اى بى‏ثبات / ‏در گلويت مانع آب حيات
entries.english: Wealth is the straw, since in your throat, O infirm one, it is an obstacle to the Water of Life.

entries.farsi: گر برد مالت عدوى پر فنى / ‏ره زنى را برده باشد ره زنى
entries.english: If an artful enemy carries off your wealth, a robber will have carried off a robber.

sections.title_fa: دزديدن مارگير مارى را از مارگيرى ديگر
sections.title_en: How a snake-catcher stole a snake from another snake-catcher.

entries.farsi: دزدكى از مارگيرى مار برد / ز ابلهى آن را غنيمت مى‏شمرد
entries.english: A petty thief carried off a snake from a snake-catcher and in his folly was accounting it a prize.

entries.farsi: وارهيد آن مارگير از زخم مار / مار كشت آن دزد او را زار زار
entries.english: The snake-catcher escaped from the snake’s bite; the man who had robbed him was miserably killed by the snake.

entries.farsi: مارگيرش ديد پس بشناختش / ‏گفت از جان مار من پرداختش
entries.english: The snake-catcher saw him; then he recognized him and said, “My snake has emptied him of life.

entries.farsi: در دعا مى‏خواستى جانم از او / كش بيابم مار بستانم از او
entries.english: My soul desired of Him in prayer that I might find him and take the snake from him.

entries.farsi: شكر حق را كان دعا مردود شد / من زيان پنداشتم آن سود شد
entries.english: Thanks to God that that prayer was rejected: I thought that it was a loss, but it has turned out to be a gain.”

entries.farsi: بس دعاها كان زيان است و هالك / ‏وز كرم مى‏نشنود يزدان پاك
entries.english: Many are the prayers which are loss and destruction, and from kindness the Holy God is not hearing them.

sections.title_fa: التماس كردن همراه عيسى عليه السلام زنده كردن استخوانها را از او
sections.title_en: How the companion of Jesus, on whom be peace, entreated Jesus, on whom be peace, to give life to the bones.

entries.farsi: گشت با عيسى يكى ابله رفيق / ‏استخوانها ديد در حفره‏ى عميق
entries.english: A certain foolish person accompanied Jesus He spied some bones in a deep hole.

entries.farsi: گفت اى همراه آن نام سنى / ‏كه بد آن تو مرده را زنده كنى
entries.english: He said, “O companion, that exalted Name by which you make the dead to live;

entries.farsi: مر مرا آموز تا احسان كنم / استخوانها را بد آن با جان كنم
entries.english: Teach me, that I may do good and by means of it endow the bones with life.”

entries.farsi: گفت خامش كن كه آن كار تو نيست / ‏اليق انفاس و گفتار تو نيست
entries.english: Jesus said, “Be silent, for that is not your work: it is not proper for your breath and speech,

entries.farsi: كان نفس خواهد ز باران پاك‏تر / وز فرشته در روش دراك‏تر
entries.english: For it wants breath purer than rain and more piercing in action than the angels.

entries.farsi: عمرها بايست تا دم پاك شد / تا امين مخزن افلاک شد
entries.english: Lifetimes were needed that the breath might be purified, so that he was entrusted with the treasury of the Heavens.

entries.farsi: خود گرفتى اين عصا در دست راست / دست را دستان موسى از كجاست
entries.english: You have grasped this rod firmly in your hand: how will Moses cunning come to you?”

entries.farsi: گفت اگر من نيستم اسرار خوان / هم تو بر خوان نام را بر استخوان
entries.english: He said, “If I am not one to pronounce mysteries, you pronounce the Name over the bones.”

entries.farsi: گفت عيسى يا رب اين اسرار چيست / ‏ميل اين ابله در اين بيگار چيست
entries.english: Jesus cried, “O Lord, what are these hidden purposes? What is this fool’s inclination in this fruitless work?

entries.farsi: چون غم خود نيست اين بيمار را / چون غم جان نيست اين مردار را
entries.english: How has this sick man no care for himself? How has this corpse no care for life?

entries.farsi: مرده‏ى خود را رها كرده ست او / مرده‏ى بيگانه را جويد رفو
entries.english: He has left his own dead and seeks to mend the dead of a stranger.”

entries.farsi: گفت حق ادبارگر ادبار جوست / خار روييده جزاى كشت اوست
entries.english: God said, “The backslider seeks backsliding: the thistle that has grown is the retribution for his sowing.”

entries.farsi: آن كه تخم خار كارد در جهان / هان و هان او را مجو در گلستان
entries.english: He that sows the seed of thistles in the world be warned not to look for him in the rose-garden.

entries.farsi: گر گلى گيرد به كف خارى شود / ور سوى يارى رود مارى شود
entries.english: If he takes a rose in his hand, it becomes a thistle; and if he goes to a friend, he becomes a snake.

entries.farsi: كيمياى زهر و مار است آن شقى / ‏بر خالف كيمياى متقى
entries.english: The damned wretch is an elixir which transmutes into poison and snakes; contrary to the elixir of the God-fearing man.

sections.title_fa: ‏اندرز كردن صوفى خادم را در تيمار داشت بهيمه و الحول گفتن خادم
sections.title_en: How the Sufi enjoined the servant to take care of his beast and how the servant said, “La haul”

entries.farsi: صوفيى مى‏گشت در دور افق / ‏تا شبى در خانقاهى شد قنق
entries.english: A Sufi was wandering round the world till one night he became a guest at a khanaghah

entries.farsi: يك بهيمه داشت در آخر ببست / ‏او به صدر صفه با ياران نشست
entries.english: He had a beast: he tied it in the stable; he sat at the top of the dais with his friends.

entries.farsi: پس مراقب گشت با ياران خويش / ‏دفترى باشد حضور يار بيش
entries.english: Then he engaged with his friends in meditation: the presence of the friend a book, more.

entries.farsi: دفتر صوفى سواد حرف نيست / جز دل اسپيد همچون برف نيست
entries.english: The Sufi’s book is not ink and letters: it is nothing but a heart white as snow.

entries.farsi: زاد دانشمند آثار قلم / ‏زاد صوفى چيست آثار قدم
entries.english: The scholar’s provision is pen-marks. What is the Sufi’s provision? Footprints

entries.farsi: همچو صيادى سوى اشكار شد / گام آهو ديد بر آثار شد
entries.english: He stalks the game, like a hunter: he sees the musk-deer’s track and follows the footprints.

entries.farsi: چند گاهش گام آهو در خور است / ‏بعد از آن خود ناف آهو رهبر است
entries.english: For some while the track of the deer is proper for him, afterwards it is the navel of the deer that is his guide.

entries.farsi: چون كه شكر گام كرد و ره بريد / الجرم ز آن گام در كامى رسيد
entries.english: When he has given thanks for the track and has traversed the way, of necessity by means of that track he arrives at a goal.

entries.farsi: رفتن يك منزلى بر بوى ناف / ‏بهتر از صد منزل گام و طواف
entries.english: To go one stage by the scent of the musk-gland is better than a hundred stages of the track and roaming about.

entries.farsi: آن دلى كاو مطلع مهتابهاست / بهر عارف فتحت ابوابهاست
entries.english: The heart that is the rising-place of the moonbeams is the opening of the doors for the Gnostic.

entries.farsi: با تو ديوار است و با ايشان در است / با تو سنگ و با عزيزان گوهر است
entries.english: To you it is a wall, to them it is a door; to you a stone, to venerated ones a pearl.

entries.farsi: آن چه تو در آينه بينى عيان / ‏پير اندر خشت بيند بيش از آن
entries.english: What you see plainly in the mirror— the Pir sees more than that in the brick.

entries.farsi: پير ايشان‏اند كاين عالم نبود / جان ايشان بود در درياى جود
entries.english: The Pirs are they whose spirits, before this world existed, were in the Sea of Bounty.

entries.farsi: پيش از اين تن عمرها بگذاشتند / پيشتر از كشت بر برداشتند
entries.english: Before this body they passed lifetimes; before the sowing they took up the wheat.

entries.farsi: مشورت مى‏رفت در ايجاد خلق / ‏جانشان در بحر قدرت تا به حلق
entries.english: They have received the spirit before the form; they have bored the pearls before the sea.

entries.farsi: پيشتر از نقش جان پذرفته‏اند / پيشتر از بحر درها سفته‏اند
entries.english: Consultation was going on as to bringing mankind into existence; their spirits were in the Sea of Omnipotence up to the throat.

entries.farsi: مطلع بر نقش هر كه هست شد / پيش از آن كاين نفس كل پا بست شد
entries.english: When the angels were opposing that, they were secretly clapping their hands at the angels.

entries.farsi: چون ماليك مانع آن مى‏شدند / بر ماليك خفيه خنبك مى‏زدند
entries.english: He was made acquainted with the form of every existent being, before this Universal Soul became fettered.

entries.farsi: آن عيان نسبت به ايشان فكرت است / ور نه خود نسبت به دوران رويت است
entries.english: Before the heavens they have seen Saturn, before the seeds they have seen the bread.

entries.farsi: بى‏دماغ و دل پر از فكرت بدند / بى‏سپاه و جنگ بر نصرت زدند
entries.english: Without brain and mind they were full of thought, without army and battle they gained victory.

entries.farsi: پيشتر ز افلاک كيوان ديده‏اند / پيشتر از دانه‏ها نان ديده‏اند
entries.english: That immediate intuition in relation to them is thought; else, indeed, in relation to those who are far it is vision.

entries.farsi: فكرت از ماضى و مستقبل بود / چون از اين دو رست مشكل حل شود
entries.english: Thought is of the past and future; when it is emancipated from these two, the difficulty is solved.

entries.farsi: روح از انگور مى را ديده است / ‏روح از معدوم شى را ديده است
entries.english: The spirit has beheld the wine in the grape, the spirit has beheld thing in nothing;

entries.farsi: ديده چون بى‏كيف هر با كيف را / ديده پيش از كان صحيح و زيف را
entries.english: It has beheld every conditioned thing as unconditioned; it has beheld the genuine coin and the alloyed before the mine;

entries.farsi: پيشتر از خلقت انگورها / خورده مى‏ها و نموده شورها
entries.english: Before the creation of grapes it has quaffed wines and shown the excitements.

entries.farsi: در تموز گرم مى‏بينند دى / ‏در شعاع شمس مى‏بينند فى
entries.english: In hot July they see December; in the sunbeams they see the shade.

entries.farsi: در دل انگور مى را ديده‏اند / در فناى محض شى را ديده‏اند
entries.english: In the heart of the grape they have seen the wine; in absolute fana they have seen the object.

entries.farsi: آسمان در دور ايشان جرعه نوش / ‏آفتاب از جودشان پوش
entries.english: The sky is draining draughts from their circling cup; the sun is clad in cloth of gold by their bounty.

entries.farsi: چون از ايشان مجتمع بينى دو يار / هم يكى باشند و هم ششصد هزار
entries.english: When you see two of them met together as friends, they are one, and at the same time six hundred thousand.

entries.farsi: بر مثال موجها اعدادشان / ‏در عدد آورده باشد بادشان
entries.english: Their numbers are in the likeness of waves: the wind will have brought them into number

entries.farsi: مفترق شد آفتاب جانها / در درون روزن ابدان ما
entries.english: The Sun, which is the spirits, became separated in the windows, which are bodies.

entries.farsi: چون نظر در قرص دارى خود يكى است / و آن كه شد محجوب ابدان در شكى است
entries.english: When you gaze on the Sun’s disk, it is itself one, but he that is screened by the bodies is in some doubt.

entries.farsi: تفرقه در روح حيوانى بود / نفس واحد روح انسانى بود
entries.english: Separation is in the animal spirit; the human spirit is one essence.

entries.farsi: يك زمان بگذار اى همره مالل / ‏تا بگويم وصف خالى ز آن جمال
entries.english: Inasmuch as God sprinkled His light upon them, His light never becomes separated.

entries.farsi: چون كه حق رش عليهم نوره / مفترق هرگز نگردد نور او
entries.english: O my comrade on the way; dismiss your weariness for a moment, that I may describe a single mole of that Beauty.

entries.farsi: در بيان نايد جمال حال او / هر دو عالم چيست عكس خال او
entries.english: The beauty of His state cannot be set forth: what are both the worlds? The reflection of His mole

entries.farsi: چون كه من از خال خوبش دم زنم / ‏نطق مى‏خواهد كه بشكافد تنم
entries.english: When I breathe a word concerning His beauteous mole, my speech would fain burst my body.

entries.farsi: همچو مورى اندر اين خرمن خوشم / ‏تا فزون از خويش بارى مى‏كشم
entries.english: Like an ant, I am so happy in this granary that I am dragging a burden too great for me.

sections.title_fa: ‏بسته شدن تقرير معنى حكايت به سبب ميل مستمع به استماع ظاهر صورت حكايت
sections.title_en: How the explanation of the meaning of the tale was stopped because of the hearer’s desire to hear the superficial form of it.

entries.farsi: كى گذارد آن كه رشك روشنى است / تا بگويم آن چه فرض و گفتنى است
entries.english: When will He who is envied by Light allow me to tell that which is obligatory and ought to be told?

entries.farsi: بحر كف پيش آرد و سدى كند / جر كند و ز بعد جر مدى كند
entries.english: The sea casts foam in front and makes a barrier: it draws back and after drawing back flows in.

entries.farsi: اين زمان بشنو چه مانع شد مگر / مستمع را رفت دل جاى دگر
entries.english: Hear what has interfered at the present time: I think the hearer’s mind has wandered elsewhere.

entries.farsi: خاطرش شد سوى صوفى قنق / اندر آن سودا فرو شد تا عنق
entries.english: His thoughts have turned to the Sufi guest: he is sunk up to the neck in that business’

entries.farsi: الزم آمد باز رفتن زين مقال / ‏سوى آن افسانه بهر وصف حال
entries.english: It behooves me to go back from this discourse to that story in order to describe what happened.

entries.farsi: صوفى آن صورت مپندار اى عزيز / همچو طفالن تا كى از جوز و مويز
entries.english: O dear friend, do not imagine the Sufi to be the form: how long, like children, with walnuts and raisins?

entries.farsi: جسم ما جوز و مويز است اى پسر / گر تو مردى زين دو چيز اندر گذر
entries.english: Our body is walnuts and raisins, O son; if you are a man, relinquish these two things;

entries.farsi: بشنو اكنون صورت افسانه را / ليك هين از كه جدا كن دانه را
entries.english: And if you do not relinquish them, the grace of God will enable you to pass beyond the nine tiers.

entries.farsi: ور تو اندر نگذرى اكرام حق / ‏بگذراند مر ترا از نه طبق
entries.english: Now listen to the outward form of the tale, but take heed to separate the grain from the chaff.

entries.farsi: حلقه‏ى آن صوفيان مستفيد / چون كه در وجد و طرب آخر رسيد
entries.english: When at last that circle of Sufis who were seeking profit came to an end in ecstasy and enthusiasm,

entries.farsi: خوان بياوردند بهر ميهمان / ‏از بهيمه ياد آورد آن زمان
entries.english: They brought dishes of food for the guest, and he then thought about his beast.

entries.farsi: گفت خادم را كه در آخر برو / راست كن بهر بهيمه كاه و جو
entries.english: He said to the attendant,” Go into the stable and make the straw and barley all right for the animal.”

entries.farsi: گفت ال حول اين چه افزون گفتن است / ‏از قديم اين كارها كار من است
entries.english: “Good gracious “he replied, “why so many instructions? These things have long been in my care.”

entries.farsi: گفت تر كن آن جوش را از نخست / كان خر پير است و دندانهاش سست
entries.english: The Sufi said, “First wet his barley, for it is an old ass, and his teeth are shaky.”

entries.farsi: گفت الحول اين چه مى‏گويى مها / از من آموزند اين ترتيبها
entries.english: “Good gracious!” said he, “why are you telling this, Sir? They are taught by me these arrangements.”

entries.farsi: گفت پالانش فرو نه پيش پيش / ‏داروى منبل بنه بر پشت ريش
entries.english: The Sufi said, “First of all take off ’ his saddle and put the salve of manbal on his sore back.”

entries.farsi: گفت الحول آخر اى حكمت گزار / جنس تو مهمانم آمد صد هزار
entries.english: “Good gracious!” exclaimed the servant. “Why, O purveyor of wisdom, I have had a thousand guests of your sort,

entries.farsi: جمله راضى رفته‏اند از پيش ما / هست مهمان جان ما و خويش ما
entries.english: And all have departed from us well-pleased: the guest is our life and our kinsman.”

entries.farsi: گفت آبش ده و ليكن شير گرم / ‏گفت الحول از توام بگرفت شرم
entries.english: The Sufi said, “Give him water, but lukewarm.” “Good gracious!” cried the other, “I am ashamed of you.”

entries.farsi: گفت اندر جو تو كمتر كاه كن / گفت الحول اين سخن كوتاه كن
entries.english: The Sufi said, “Put a little straw in his barley.” “Good gracious! Cut short this speech,” he replied.

entries.farsi: گفت جايش را بروب از سنگ و پشك / ‏ور بود تر ريز بر وى خاك خشك
entries.english: The Sufi said, “ Sweep his place of stones and dung, and if it is wet, sprinkle dry earth on it.”

entries.farsi: گفت الحول اى پدر الحول كن / ‏با رسول اهل كمتر گو سخن
entries.english: “Good gracious!” cried he, “implore God’s grace, O father, and say little to a messenger who knows his business.”

entries.farsi: گفت بستان شانه پشت خر بخار / گفت الحول اى پدر شرمى بدار
entries.english: The Sufi said, “Take the comb and curry the ass’s back.” “Good gracious! Do have some shame, O father,” said he.

entries.farsi: خادم اين گفت و ميان را بست چست / ‏گفت رفتم كاه و جو آرم نخست
entries.english: The servant said this and briskly girded up his loins. “I go,” said he; “first I will fetch the straw and barley.”

entries.farsi: رفت و از آخر نكرد او هيچ ياد / خواب خرگوشى بدان صوفى بداد
entries.english: Off he went and never thought of the stable at all: he gave that Sufi the sleep of the hare.

entries.farsi: رفت خادم جانب اوباش چند / كرد بر اندرز صوفى ريش‏خند
entries.english: The servant went off to some rascals and made a mockery of the sufi’s admonition.

entries.farsi: صوفى از ره مانده بود و شد دراز / خوابها مى‏ديد با چشم فراز
entries.english: The Sufi was fatigued by his journey and stretched his limbs: with his eyes closed he was dreaming

entries.farsi: كان خرش در چنگ گرگى مانده بود / پاره‏ها از پشت و رانش مى‏ربود
entries.english: That his ass was left in the clutch of a wolf, was tearing pieces from its back and thighs.

entries.farsi: گفت الحول اين چه ماليخولياست / ‏اى عجب آن خادم مشفق كجاست
entries.english: “Good gracious!” he exclaimed, “What sort of melancholy is this? Oh, where is that kindly servant?”

entries.farsi: باز مى‏ديد آن خرش در راه رو / گه به چاهى مى‏فتاد و گه به گو
entries.english: Again he would see his ass going along the road and falling now into a well and now into a ditch.

entries.farsi: گونه‏گون مى‏ديد ناخوش واقعه / فاتحه مى‏خواند او و القارعه
entries.english: He was dreaming various unpleasant dreams; he was reciting the Fatiha and the Qari’a.

entries.farsi: گفت چاره چيست ياران جسته‏اند / رفته‏اند و جمله درها بسته‏اند
entries.english: He said “What can be done to help? My friends have hurried out: they have departed and made all the doors fast.”

entries.farsi: باز مى‏گفت اى عجب آن خادمك / ‏نه كه با ما گشت هم نان و نمك
entries.english: Again he would say, “Oh, I wonder—that wretched servant! Did not he partake of bread and salt with us?

entries.farsi: من نكردم با وى الا لطف و لين / ‏او چرا با من كند بر عكس كين
entries.english: I showed him nothing but courtesy and mildness: why should he on the contrary show hatred towards me?

entries.farsi: هر عداوت را سبب بايد سند / ور نه جنسيت وفا تلقين كند
entries.english: Every enmity must rest on some cause; otherwise, our common humanity would dictate faithfulness.

entries.farsi: باز مى‏گفت آدم با لطف وجود / كى بر آن ابليس جورى كرده بود
entries.english: Then he would say again, “When had Adam, the kind and generous, done an injury to Iblis?

entries.farsi: آدمى مر مار و كژدم را چه كرد / كاو همى‏خواهد مر او را مرگ و درد
entries.english: What was done by man to snake and scorpion that they wish death and pain upon him?

entries.farsi: گرگ را خود خاصيت بدريدن است / ‏اين حسد در خلق آخر روشن است
entries.english: To rend is the instinct of the wolf: after all, this envy is conspicuous in mankind.”

entries.farsi: باز مى‏گفت اين گمان بد خطاست / ‏بر برادر اين چنين ظنم چراست
entries.english: Again he would say, “It is wrong thus to think evil: why have I such thoughts against my brother?”

entries.farsi: باز گفتى حزم سوء الظن تست / ‏هر كه بد ظن نيست كى ماند درست
entries.english: Then he would say, “Prudence consists in your thinking evil: how shall he that thinks no evil remain unhurt?”

entries.farsi: صوفى اندر وسوسه و آن خر چنان / ‏كه چنين بادا جز اى دشمنان
entries.english: The Sufi was in anxiety, and the ass was in such a plight that—may it befall our enemies!

entries.farsi: آن خر مسكين ميان خاك و سنگ / كژ شده پالان دريده پالهنگ
entries.english: That poor ass was amidst earth and stones, with his saddle crooked and his halter torn,

entries.farsi: خر همه شب ذكر مى‏كرد اى اله / ‏جو رها كردم كم از يك مشت كاه
entries.english: Killed by the journey, without fodder all the night long, now at the last gasp and now perishing

entries.farsi: خسته از ره جمله‏ى شب بى‏علف / ‏گاه در جان كندن و گه در تلف
entries.english: All night the ass was repeating, “O God, I give up the barley; less than one handful of straw?”

entries.farsi: با زبان حال مى‏گفت اى شيوخ / ‏رحمتى كه سوختم زين خام شوخ
entries.english: With mute eloquence he was saying, “O Shaykhs, some pity, for I am consumed because of this raw impudent rogue

entries.farsi: آن چه آن خر ديد از رنج و عذاب / ‏مرغ خاكى بيند اندر سيل آب
entries.english: What that ass suffered of pain and torment, the land-bird suffers in a flood of water.

entries.farsi: بس به پهلو گشت آن شب تا سحر / آن خر بى‏چاره از جوع البقر
entries.english: Then that night till dawn the wretched ass, from exceeding hunger, rolled on his side.

entries.farsi: روز شد خادم بيامد بامداد / زود پالان جست بر پشتش نهاد
entries.english: Day rose. The servant came at morn and quickly looked for the saddle and laid it on the ass’s back.

entries.farsi: خر فروشانه دو سه زخمش بزد / كرد با خر آن چه ز آن سگ مى‏سزد
entries.english: After the fashion of ass-dealers he gave him two or three blows: he did to the ass what is befitting from such a cur.

entries.farsi: خر جهنده گشت از تيزى نيش / ‏كو زبان تا خر بگويد حال خويش
entries.english: The sharpness of the sting set the ass jumping; where is the tongue that he may describe his own state?

sections.title_fa: ‏گمان بردن كاروانيان كه بهيمه ى صوفى رنجور است
sections.title_en: How the people of the caravan supposed the Sufi’s beast was ill

entries.farsi: چون كه صوفى بر نشست و شد روان / ‏رو در افتادن گرفت او هر زمان
entries.english: When the Sufi mounted and got going, he began to fall on his face every time,

entries.farsi: آن يكى گوشش همى‏پيچيد سخت / ‏و آن دگر در زير گامش جست لخت
entries.english: Every time the people lifted him up: they all thought he was ill.

entries.farsi: هر زمانش خلق بر مى‏داشتند / جمله رنجورش همى‏پنداشتند
entries.english: One would twist his ears hard, while another sought for the part under his palate,

entries.farsi: و آن دگر در نعل او مى‏جست سنگ / ‏و آن دگر در چشم او مى‏ديد زنگ
entries.english: And another searched for the stone in his shoe, and another looked at the dirt in his eye

entries.farsi: باز مى‏گفتند اى شيخ اين ز چيست / دى نمى‏گفتى كه شكر اين خر قوى است
entries.english: They were also saying, “O Shaykh, what is the cause of this? Were not you saying yesterday? ‘Thanks, this ass is strong’?”

entries.farsi: گفت آن خر كاو به شب الحول خورد / جز بدين شيوه نداند راه كرد
entries.english: He replied, “The ass that ate La hawl during the night cannot get along except in this manner.

entries.farsi: چون كه قوت خر به شب الحول بود / شب مسبح بود و روز اندر سجود
entries.english: Inasmuch as the ass’s food by night was La hawl, he was glorifying God by night and in prostrating himself by day.”

entries.farsi: آدمى خوارند اغلب مردمان / ‏از سالم عليك‏شان كم جو امان
entries.english: Most people are man-eaters: put no trust in their saying, “Peace to you.”

entries.farsi: از دم ديو آن كه او الحول خورد / هم چو آن خر در سر آيد در نبرد
entries.english: The hearts of all are the Devil’s house: do not accept the idle chatter of devilish men.

entries.farsi: خانه‏ى ديو است دلهاى همه / ‏كم پذير از ديو مردم دمدمه
entries.english: He that swallows La hawl from the breath of the Devil, like that ass falls headlong in the fight

entries.farsi: هر كه در دنيا خورد تلبيس ديو / و ز عدوى دوست رو تعظيم و ريو
entries.english: Whoever swallows the Devil’s imposture in this world and veneration and deceit from the foe that has the face of a friend?

entries.farsi: در ره اسالم و بر پول صراط / در سر آيد همچو آن خر از خباط
entries.english: In the Way of Islam and on the bridge Sirat he will fall upon his head from giddiness, like that ass.

entries.farsi: عشوه‏هاى يار بد منيوش هين / ‏دام بين ايمن مرو تو بر زمين
entries.english: Beware! Do not hearken to the blandishments of the bad friend: see the snare, do not walk securely on the earth.

entries.farsi: صد هزار ابليس الحول آر بين / ‏آدما ابليس را در مار بين
entries.english: See the hundred thousand devils who utter La Hawl, O Adam, in the serpent behold Iblis!

entries.farsi: دم دهد گويد ترا اى جان و دوست / ‏تا چو قصابى كشد از دوست پوست
entries.english: He gives vain words, he says to you, “O my soul and beloved,” that he may strip the skin off his beloved, like a butcher.

entries.farsi: دم دهد تا پوستت بيرون كشد / و اى او كز دشمنان افيون چشد
entries.english: He gives vain words that he may strip off your skin: woe to him that tastes opium from enemies.

entries.farsi: سر نهد بر پاى تو قصاب‏وار / دم دهد تا خونت ريزد زار زار
entries.english: He lays his head at your feet and butcher-like gives vain words, that he may shed your blood miserably.

entries.farsi: همچو شيرى صيد خود را خويش كن / ‏ترك عشوه‏ى اجنبى و خويش كن
entries.english: Like a lion, hunt your prey yourself: leave the flattery of stranger or kinsman.

entries.farsi: همچو خادم دان مراعات خسان / بى‏كسى بهتر ز عشوه‏ى ناكسان
entries.english: Know that the regard of the base is like that servant; it is better to have nobody than the flattery of nobod

entries.farsi: در زمين مردمان خانه مكن / ‏كار خود كن كار بيگانه مكن
entries.english: Do not make your home in men’s land: do your own work, don’t do the work of a stranger

entries.farsi: كيست بيگانه تن خاكى تو / كز بر اى اوست غمناكى تو
entries.english: Who is the stranger? Your earthen body, for the sake of which is your sorrow.

entries.farsi: تا تو تن را چرب و شيرين مى‏دهى / ‏جوهر خود را نبينى فربهى
entries.english: So long as you are giving your body greasy and sweet, you will not see fatness in your essence.

entries.farsi: گر ميان مشك تن را جا شود / روز مردن گند او پيدا شود
entries.english: If the body be set in the midst of musk, on the day of death its stench will become manifest.

entries.farsi: مشك را بر تن مزن بر دل بمال / ‏مشك چه بود نام پاك ذو الجلال
entries.english: Do not put musk on your body; rub it on your heart. What is musk? The holy name of the Glorious

entries.farsi: آن منافق مشك بر تن مى‏نهد / روح را در قعر گلخن مى‏نهد
entries.english: The hypocrite puts musk on his body and puts his spirit at the bottom of the ash-pit.

entries.farsi: بر زبان نام حق و در جان او / گندها از فكر بى‏ايمان او
entries.english: On his tongue the name of God and in his soul stenches from his infidel thought.

entries.farsi: ذكر با او همچو سبزه‏ى گلخن است / ‏بر سر مبر ز گل است و سوسن است
entries.english: In relation to him praise of God is the herbage of the ash-pit: it is roses and lilies upon a dunghill.

entries.farsi: آن نبات آن جا يقين عاريت است / ‏جاى آن گل مجلس است و عشرت است
entries.english: Those plants are certainly there on loan; the proper place for those flowers is the symposium and festivity.

entries.farsi: طيبات آيد به سوى طيبين / ‏للخبيثين الخبيثات است هين
entries.english: The good women come to the good men; there is to the wicked men the wicked women. Mark!

entries.farsi: اصل كينه دوزخ است و كين تو / جزو آن كل است و خصم دين تو
entries.english: Do not bear malice: they that are led astray by malice, their graves are placed beside the malicious.

entries.farsi: كين مدار آنها كه از كين گمرهند / گورشان پهلوى كين داران نهند
entries.english: The origin of malice is Hell, and your malice is a part of that whole and is the enemy of your religion.

entries.farsi: چون تو جزو دوزخى پس هوش دار / جزو سوى كل خود گيرد قرار
entries.english: Since you are a part of Hell, take care! The part gravitates towards its whole.

entries.farsi: تلخ با تلخان يقين ملحق شود / كى دم باطل قرين حق شود
entries.english: He that is bitter will assuredly be attached to those who are bitter: how should vain breath be joined with the truth?

entries.farsi: اى برادر تو همان انديشه‏اى / ‏ما بقى تو استخوان و ريشه‏اى
entries.english: 0 brother, you are that same thought; as for the rest, you are flesh and bone.

entries.farsi: گر گل است انديشه‏ى تو گلشنى / ‏ور بود خارى تو هيمه‏ى گلخنى
entries.english: If your thought is a rose, you are a rose-garden; and if it is a thorn, you are fuel for the bath-stove.

entries.farsi: گر گالبى، بر سر و جيبت زنند / ور تو چون بولى برونت افكنند
entries.english: If you are rose-water, you are sprinkled on head and bosom; and if you are like urine, you are cast out.

entries.farsi: طبله‏ها در پيش عطاران ببين / ‏جنس را با جنس خود كرده قرين
entries.english: Look at the trays in front of druggists— each kind put beside its own kind,

entries.farsi: جنسها با جنسها آميخته / ‏زين تجانس زينتى انگيخته
entries.english: Things of each sort mixed with things of the same sort, and a certain elegance produced by this homogeneity;

entries.farsi: گر در آميزند عود و شكرش / ‏بر گزيند يك يك از يكديگرش
entries.english: If his aloes-wood and sugar get mixed, he picks them out from each other, piece by piece

entries.farsi: طبله‏ها بشكست و جانها ريختند / نيك و بد در همدگر آميختند
entries.english: The trays were broken and the souls were spilled: good and evil ones were mingled with each oth

entries.farsi: حق فرستاد انبيا را با ورق / ‏تا گزيد اين دانه‏ها را بر طبق
entries.english: God sent the prophets with scrolls, that He might pick out these grains on the dish.

entries.farsi: پيش از ايشان ما همه يكسان بديم / ‏كس ندانستى كه ما نيك و بديم
entries.english: Before this we were one community, none knew whether we were good or bad.

entries.farsi: قلب و نيكو در جهان بودى روان / ‏چون همه شب بود و ما چون شب روان
entries.english: False coin and fine were current in the world, since all was night, and we were as night-travellers,

entries.farsi: تا بر آمد آفتاب انبيا / گفت اى غش دور شو صافى بيا
entries.english: Until the sun of the prophets rose and said, “Be gone, 0 alloy! Come, 0 you who are pure!”

entries.farsi: چشم داند فرق كردن رنگ را / چشم داند لعل را و سنگ را
entries.english: The eye can distinguish colours; the eye knows ruby and stone.

entries.farsi: چشم داند گوهر و خاشاك را / چشم را ز آن مى‏خلد خاشاكها
entries.english: The eye knows the jewel and the rubbish; hence bits of rubbish sting the eye.

entries.farsi: دشمن روزند اين قالبكان / ‏عاشق روزند آن زرهاى كان
entries.english: These vile counterfeiters are enemies of day; those pieces of gold from the mine are lovers of day,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه روز است آينه‏ى تعريف او / تا ببيند اشرفى تشريف او
entries.english: Because day is the mirror that makes it known, so that the ashrafi may see its gift of honour

entries.farsi: حق قيامت را لقب ز آن روز كرد / روز بنمايد جمال سرخ و زرد
entries.english: Hence God bestowed the title of “Day” on the Resurrection, day displays the beauty of red and yellow.

entries.farsi: پس حقيقت روز سر اولياست / ‏روز پيش ماهشان چون سايه‏هاست
entries.english: In reality, then, day is the inmost consciousness of the saints, beside their moon day is as shadows.

entries.farsi: عكس راز مرد حق دانيد روز / عكس ستاريش شام چشم دوز
entries.english: The eye knows the jewel and the rubbish; hence bits of rubbish sting the eye.

entries.farsi: ز آن سبب فرمود يزدان وَ الضحى / ‏وَ الضُّ حى‏ نور ضمير مصطفى
entries.english: For that reason God said, by the morn: by the morn is the light of the hidden mind of Mustafa.

entries.farsi: قول ديگر كاين ضحى را خواست دوست / ‏هم بر اى آن كه اين هم عكس اوست
entries.english: The other view, that the Beloved meant this morn, is just for the reason that this too is the reflection of him;

entries.farsi: ور نه بر فانى قسم گفتن خطاست / خود فنا چه اليق گفت خداست
entries.english: Else it is wrong to swear by a transient thing: how indeed is transience proper to the speech of God?

entries.farsi: ال أُحِبُّ الآْ فِلِينَ گفت آن خليل / ‏كى فنا خواهد از اين رب جليل
entries.english: That Friend said, “I love not them that set”: how, then, did the Lord of all created beings mean . . .?

entries.farsi: آفتابش چون بر آمد ز آن فلك / با شب تن گفت هين ما ودعك
entries.english: Again, and by the night is his occultation and his earthen rust-dark body.

entries.farsi: باز وَ اللَّيْلِ است ستارى او / و آن تن خاكى زنگارى او
entries.english: When his sun rose from that sky, it said to the night of the body, “Lo, He has not forsaken you.”

entries.farsi: وصل پيدا گشت از عين بال / ‏ز آن حالوت شد عبارت ما قلى
entries.english: Union was made manifest out of the essence of affliction: that sweetness was expressed by He has not hated.

entries.farsi: هر عبارت خود نشان حالتى است / ‏حال چون دست و عبارت آلتى است
entries.english: In fact, every expression is the symbol of a state: the state is as a hand, while the expression is a tool.

entries.farsi: آلت زرگر به دست كفشگر / همچو دانه‏ى كشت كرده ريگ در
entries.english: The goldsmith’s tool in the hand of a shoemaker is like a seed sown in sand;

entries.farsi: و آلت اسكاف پيش برزگر / پيش سگ كاه استخوان در پيش خر
entries.english: And the cobbler’s tool before the husbandman is straw before a dog bones before an ass.

entries.farsi: بود انا الحق در لب منصور نور / بود انا اهلل در لب فرعون زور
entries.english: “I am God” on the lips of Mansur was the light; “I am Allah” on the lips of Pharaoh was a lie.

entries.farsi: شد عصا اندر كف موسى گوا / شد عصا اندر كف ساحر هبا
entries.english: In the hand of Moses the rod became a witness in the hand of the magician the rod became motes in the air.

entries.farsi: زين سبب عيسى بدان همراه خود / در نياموزيد آن اسم صمد
entries.english: On this account Jesus did not teach his fellow-traveller that Name of the Lord,

entries.farsi: كاو نداند نقص بر آلت نهد / سنگ بر گل زن تو آتش كى جهد
entries.english: For he would not know and would attribute imperfection to the tool. Strike stone on clay, and how should fire leap forth?

entries.farsi: آن كه بى‏جفت است و بى‏آلت يكى است / در عدد شك است و آن يك بى‏شكى است
entries.english: Hand and tool are as stone and iron; there must be a pair: a pair is the condition for bringing to birth.

entries.farsi: دست و آلت همچو سنگ و آهن است / ‏جفت بايد جفت شرط زادن است
entries.english: The One is He who has no consort and no tool; in number there is doubt, and that One is beyond doubt.

entries.farsi: آن كه دو گفت و سه گفت و بيش ازين / ‏متفق باشند در واحد يقين
entries.english: Those who say “two” or “three” or more than these are certainly agreed in One.

entries.farsi: احولى چون دفع شد يكسان شوند / دو سه گويان هم يكى گويان شوند
entries.english: When squinting has been put aside, they become alike: the assertors of two or three become assertors of Unity.

entries.farsi: گر يكى گويى تو در ميدان او / گرد بر مى‏گرد از چوگان او
entries.english: If you are a ball in His polo-field, keep spinning round from His polo-stick.

entries.farsi: گوى آن گه راست و بى‏نقصان شود / كه ز زخم دست شه رقصان شود
entries.english: The ball becomes right and flawless at the time when it is made to dance by the stroke of the King’s hand.

entries.farsi: گوش دار اى احول اينها را به هوش / داروى ديده بكش از راه گوش
entries.english: Pay attention to these, O squint eyed one: apply the eye-salve by way of the ear.

entries.farsi: پس كالم پاك در دلهاى كور / مى‏نپايد مى‏رود تا اصل نور
entries.english: Holy words, then, do not abide in blind hearts, go to the Light whence they came,

entries.farsi: و آن فسون ديو در دلهاى كژ / مى‏رود چون كفش كژ در پاى كژ
entries.english: While the spell of the Devil goes into crooked hearts as a crooked shoe on to a crooked foot

entries.farsi: گر چه حكمت را به تكرار آورى / ‏چون تو نااهلى شود از تو برى
entries.english: Though you may learn Wisdom by rote, it leaves you when you are unworthy;

entries.farsi: ور چه بنويسى نشانش مى‏كنى / ‏ور چه مى‏الفى بيانش مى‏كنى
entries.english: And though you write it and note it, and though you brag and expound it,

entries.farsi: او ز تو رو در كشد اى پر ستيز / بندها را بگسلد وز تو گريز
entries.english: It withdraws its face from you, O disputatious one: it breaks its bonds and flees from you.

entries.farsi: ور نخوانى و ببيند سوز تو / علم باشد مرغ دست‏آموز تو
entries.english: If you read not and it sees your ardour, Knowledge will be a bird docile to your hand.

entries.farsi: او نپايد پيش هر نااوستا / همچو طاوسى به خانه‏ى روستا
entries.english: It does not abide with every unskilled apprentice: like a peacock in the house of a peasant.

sections.title_fa: ‏يافتن پادشاه باز را به خانه‏ى كمپير زن
sections.title_en: How the King found his falcon in the house of a decrepit old woman.

entries.farsi: دين نه آن باز است كاو از شه گريخت / ‏سوى آن كمپير كاو مى‏آرد بيخت
entries.english: It is not like the falcon that fled from the King to the old crone who was sifting flour

entries.farsi: تا كه تتماجى پزد اوالد را / ديد آن باز خوش خوش زاد را
entries.english: That she might cook porridge for her children. She saw the beautiful well-born falcon,

entries.farsi: پايكش بست و پرش كوتاه كرد / ناخنش ببريد و قوتش كاه كرد
entries.english: She tied its little foot and clipped its wings; she cut its talons and fed it with straw.

entries.farsi: گفت نااهالن نكردندت به ساز / پر فزود از حد و ناخن شد دراز
entries.english: “Unworthy folk,” said she, “have not kept thee in trim: your wings are overgrown and thy talons have become long.

entries.farsi: دست هر نااهل بيمارت كند / سوى مادر آ كه تيمارت كند
entries.english: Every unworthy one’s hand makes thee ill: come to your mother that she may take care of you.”

entries.farsi: مهر جاهل را چنين دان اى رفيق / ‏كژ رود جاهل هميشه در طريق
entries.english: Know, O friend, that such is the affection of the fool: the fool ever walks crookedly on the way.

entries.farsi: روز شه در جستجو بى‏گاه شد / سوى آن كمپير و آن خرگاه شد
entries.english: The King’s day became late in searching: he went to the old woman and the tent.

entries.farsi: ديد ناگه باز را در دود و گرد / شه بر او بگريست زار و نوحه كرد
entries.english: Suddenly he spotted the falcon amidst smoke and dust: the King wept and lamented over it.

entries.farsi: گفت هر چند اين جز اى كار تست / ‏كه نباشى در وفاى ما درست
entries.english: He said, “Although this is the consequence of your action, in that you are not firm in keeping faith with me,

entries.farsi: چون كنى از خلد زى دوزخ فرار / غافل از ال يستوى اصحاب نار
entries.english: How should you make Hell your abode after Paradise, heedless of the people of the Fire are not equal?

entries.farsi: اين سزاى آن كه از شاه خبير / خيره بگريزد به خانه‏ى گنده پير
entries.english: This is the fitting reward for one that unconscionably flees from the King who knows well to the house of an old hag.”

entries.farsi: باز مى‏ماليد پر بر دست شاه / ‏بى‏زبان مى‏گفت من كردم گناه
entries.english: The falcon was rubbing its wings against the King’s hand: without tongue it was saying, “I have sinned.”

entries.farsi: پس كجا زارد كجا نالد لئيم / ‏گر تو نپذيرى بجز نيك اى كريم
entries.english: Where then should the vile plead piteously, where should he moan, if you will accept nothing but good, O Bountiful?

entries.farsi: لطف شه جان را جنايت جو كند / ز آنكه شه هر زشت را نيكو كند
entries.english: The King’s grace makes the soul sin-seeking, because the King makes every foul thing fair.

entries.farsi: خدمت خود را سزا پنداشتى / ‏تو لواى جرم از آن افراشتى
entries.english: Go; do not commit foulness, for our fair deeds appear foul in the sight of our Beauteous.

entries.farsi: رو مكن زشتى كه نيكيهاى ما / زشت آمد پيش آن زيباى ما
entries.english: You deemed your service worthy: thereby you raised the banner of sin.

entries.farsi: چون ترا ذكر و دعا دستور شد / ز آن دعاكردن دلت مغرور شد
entries.english: Forasmuch as praise and prayer were passed on to you, through making that prayer your heart became vain.

entries.farsi: هم سخن ديدى تو خود را با خدا / اى بسا كاو زين گمان افتد جدا
entries.english: You regarded yourself as speaking with God. Oh, many a one that becomes separated by this opinion.

entries.farsi: گر چه با تو شه نشيند بر زمين / ‏خويشتن بشناس و نيكوتر نشين
entries.english: Although the King sit with you on the ground, know yourself and sit better.

entries.farsi: آن كه تو مستش كنى و شير گير / گر ز مستى كج رود عذرش پذير
entries.english: The falcon said, “O King, I am penitent, I am converted, I am embracing Islam anew.

entries.farsi: باز گفت اى شه پشيمان مى‏شوم / ‏توبه كردم نو مسلمان مى‏شوم
entries.english: He whom you make drunk and pot-valiant’— if from drunkenness he walks crookedly, accept his excuse.

entries.farsi: گر چه ناخن رفت چون باشى مرا / بر كنم من پرچم خورشيد را
entries.english: Though my talons are gone, when you are mine I tear off the forelock of the sun;

entries.farsi: ور چه پرم رفت چون بنوازيم / ‏چرخ بازى گم كند در بازيم
entries.english: And though my wings are gone, when you are kind to me the heavenly sphere lessens its play at my play.

entries.farsi: گر كمر بخشيم كه را بر كنم / ‏گر دهى كلكى علمها بشكنم
entries.english: If you bestow a belt on me, I will uproot the mountain; if you give me a pen, I will break the banners.

entries.farsi: آخر از پشه نه كم باشد تنم / ‏ملك نمرودى به پر بر هم زنم
entries.english: After all, my body is not inferior to a gnat: with my wings I confound the kingdom of Nimrod.

entries.farsi: در ضعيفى تو مرا بابيل گير / هر يكى خصم مرا چون پيل گير
entries.english: Suppose me to be the flocks of birds in weakness, suppose every one of my enemies to be as the elephant,

entries.farsi: قدر فندق افكنم بندق حريق / ‏بندقم در فعل صد چون منجنيق
entries.english: I cast a baked pellet the size of a hazel-nut, my pellet in its effect is like a hundred catapults.”

entries.farsi: موسى آمد در وغا با يك عصاش / زد بر آن فرعون و بر شمشيرهاش
entries.english: Moses came to battle with his one rod and made an onset against Pharaoh and his swords.

entries.farsi: نوح چون شمشير در خواهيد ازو / موج طوفان گشت از او شمشير خو
entries.english: Every Prophet who by himself has knocked at that door has alone fought against the whole world.

entries.farsi: هر رسولى يك تنه كان در زده ست / ‏بر همه آفاق تنها بر زده ست
entries.english: When Noah begged of Him a sword, through Him the waves of the Flood became of sword-like temper.

entries.farsi: احمدا خود كيست اسپاه زمين / ‏ماه بين بر چرخ و بشكافش جبين
entries.english: O Ahmad, who indeed are the armies of the earth? Behold the moon in heaven split her brow,

entries.farsi: تا بداند سعد و نحس بى‏خبر / دور تست اين دور نه دور قمر
entries.english: In order that the ignorant astrologer may know that this cycle is your cycle, not the cycle of the moon.

entries.farsi: دور تست ايرا كه موساى كليم / ‏آرزو مى‏برد زين دورت مقيم
entries.english: It is your cycle, because Moses, he who spoke, was constantly yearning after this cycle of yours.

entries.farsi: چون كه موسى رونق دور تو ديد / كاندر او صبح تجلى مى‏دميد
entries.english: When Moses beheld the splendour of your cycle, in which the dawn of Revelation was arising,

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب آن چه دور رحمت است / ‏بر گذشت از رحمت آن جا رويت است
entries.english: He said, “O Lord, what cycle of mercy is that? It is beyond mercy: there vision.

entries.farsi: غوطه ده موساى خود را در بحار / از ميان دوره‏ى احمد بر آر
entries.english: Plunge Thy Moses in the seas and bring him up from the midst of the cycle of Ahmad.”

entries.farsi: گفت يا موسى بدان بنمودمت / راه آن خلوت بدان بگشودمت
entries.english: God said, “O Moses, on that account I have shown you; on that account I have opened to you the way to that communion,

entries.farsi: كه تو ز آن دورى درين دور اى كليم / ‏پا بكش زيرا دراز است اين گليم
entries.english: Because in this cycle, O Kalim, you are far from that: draw back your foot, for this blanket is long.

entries.farsi: من كريمم نان نمايم بنده را / تا بگرياند طمع آن زنده را
entries.english: I am kind; I show my servant bread in order that desire may cause that living one to weep.

entries.farsi: بينى طفلى بمالد مادرى / ‏تا شود بيدار واجويد خورى
entries.english: A mother rubs the nose of her babe that it may wake and seek some food

entries.farsi: كاو گرسنه خفته باشد بى‏خبر / و آن دو پستان مى‏خلد زو مهر در
entries.english: For it may have fallen asleep hungry unawares; and it prods her two breasts for the sake of milk.

entries.farsi: كنت كنزا رحمة مخفية / فابتعثت أمة مهدية
entries.english: I was a treasure, a hidden mercy, so I sent forth a rightly guided Imam.

entries.farsi: هر كراماتى كه مى‏جويى به جان / ‏او نمودت تا طمع كردى در آن
entries.english: Every grace that you are seeking with your soul, He showed it to you that you might desire it.

entries.farsi: چند بت بشكست احمد در جهان / ‏تا كه يا رب گوى گشتند امتان
entries.english: How many idols did Ahmad break in the world, that the communities might cry “O Lord”!

entries.farsi: گر نبودى كوشش احمد تو هم / ‏مى‏پرستيدى چو اجدادت صنم
entries.english: Had it not been for the efforts of Ahmad, you also, like your ancestors, would be worshipping idols.

entries.farsi: اين سرت وارست از سجده‏ى صنم / ‏تا بدانى حق او را بر امم
entries.english: This head of yours has been delivered from bowing to idols, in order that you may acknowledge his rightful claim upon the communities.

entries.farsi: گر بگويى شكر اين رستن بگو / كز بت باطن همت برهاند او
entries.english: If you speak, speak thanks for this deliverance that he may also deliver you from the idol within.

entries.farsi: مر سرت را چون رهانيد از بتان / ‏هم بدان قوت تو دل را وارهان
entries.english: Since he has delivered your head from idols, deliver your heart also by means of that strength.

entries.farsi: سر ز شكر دين از آن بر تافتى / ‏كز پدر ميراث مفت‏اش يافتى
entries.english: You have neglected to give thanks for the Religion because you got it for nothing as an inheritance from your father.

entries.farsi: مرد ميراثى چه داند قدر مال / ‏رستمى جان كند و مجان يافت زال
entries.english: How should a man who inherits know the value of wealth? A Rustam tore his soul; Zal got it cost-free.

entries.farsi: رحمتم موقوف آن خوش گريه‏هاست / ‏چون گريست از بحر رحمت موج خاست
entries.english: When I cause to weep, My mercy is aroused: that crying one drinks of My bounty.

entries.farsi: چون بگريانم بجوشد رحمتم / ‏آن خروشنده بنوشد نعمتم
entries.english: If I do not wish to give, indeed I do not show him, when I have closed his heart, I open it.

entries.farsi: گر نخواهم داد خود ننمايمش / ‏چونش كردم بسته دل بگشايمش
entries.english: My mercy is dependent on that goodly weeping: when he weeps, waves rise from the sea of mercy.”

sections.title_fa: حلوا خريدن شيخ احمد خضرويه قدس اللهَّ سره العزيز جهت غريمان به الهام حق
sections.title_en: How by Divine inspiration Shaykh Ahmad son of Khizruya, may God sanctify his revered spirit, bought halwa for his creditors.

entries.farsi: بود شيخى دايما او وامدار / از جوانمردى كه بود آن نامدار
entries.english: There was a Shaykh who was continually in debt because of the generosity which that illustrious one had.

entries.farsi: ده هزاران وام كردى از مهان / ‏خرج كردى بر فقيران جهان
entries.english: He used to make myriads of debts from the great, and spend upon the poor of the world.

entries.farsi: هم به وام او خانقاهى ساخته / ‏جان و مال و خانقه درباخته
entries.english: He had also built a khanaghah by debt; he had devoted life and wealth and khanaghah.

entries.farsi: وام او را حق ز هر جا مى‏گزارد / كرد حق بهر خليل از ريگ آرد
entries.english: God was paving his debts from every quarter: God made flour out of sand for the Friend’s sake.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه در بازارها / دو فرشته مى‏كنند ايدر دعا
entries.english: The prophet said that two angels are praying here in the markets,

entries.farsi: كاى خدا تو منفقان را ده خلف / ‏اى خدا تو ممسكان را ده تلف
entries.english: “O God, Give the prodigal a boon in return, and O God give the miserly a bane.”

entries.farsi: خاصه آن منفق كه جان انفاق كرد / حلق خود قربانى خالق كرد
entries.english: Especially the prodigal who has freely spent his soul and made his throat a sacrifice to the Creator

entries.farsi: حلق پيش آورد اسماعيل‏وار / كارد بر حلقش نيارد كرد كار
entries.english: He offers his throat, like Ismail: the knife cannot do anything to his throat.

entries.farsi: پس شهيدان زنده زين رويند و خوش / ‏تو بدان قالب بمنگر گبروش
entries.english: On this account, then, the martyrs are living in joy: do not, infidel-like, look at the body,

entries.farsi: چون خلف دادستشان جان بقا / جان ايمن از غم و رنج و شقا
entries.english: Since God has given them in return the spirit of everlastingness— a spirit safe from grief and pain and misery.

entries.farsi: شيخ وامى سالها اين كار كرد / مى‏ستد مى‏داد همچون پاى مرد
entries.english: The debtor Shaykh acted in this fashion for years, taking and giving like a steward.

entries.farsi: تخمها مى‏كاشت تا روز اجل / ‏تا بود روز اجل مير اجل
entries.english: He was sowing seeds till the day of death, that on the day of death he might be a most glorious prince.

entries.farsi: چون كه عمر شيخ در آخر رسيد / در وجود خود نشان مرگ ديد
entries.english: When the Shaykh’s life reached its end and he saw in his existence the signs of death,

entries.farsi: وامداران گرد او بنشسته جمع / ‏شيخ بر خود خوش گدازان همچو شمع
entries.english: The creditors were seated together around him; the Shaykh was gently melting on himself, like a candle.

entries.farsi: شيخ گفت اين بد گمانان را نگر / نيست حق را چار صد دينار زر
entries.english: The creditors had become despairing and sour-faced: the pain in hearts was accompanied by pain in lungs’.

entries.farsi: وامداران گشته نوميد و ترش / ‏درد دلها يار شد با درد شش
entries.english: “Look at these evil-thinking men,” said the Shaykh. “Has not God four hundred gold dinars?”

entries.farsi: كودكى حلوا ز بيرون بانگ زد / الف حلوا بر اميد دانگ زد
entries.english: A boy outside shouted “halwa!” and bragged of the halwa in hope of some money.

entries.farsi: شيخ اشارت كرد خادم را به سر / كه برو آن جمله حلوا را بخر
entries.english: The Shaykh with his head directed the attendant to go and buy the whole of the halwa,

entries.farsi: تا غريمان چون كه آن حلوا خورند / يك زمانى تلخ در من ننگرند
entries.english: “So that the creditors, when they eat the halwa, for a while may not look bitterly on me”

entries.farsi: در زمان خادم برون آمد به در / تا خرد او جمله حلوا ز ان پسر
entries.english: The attendant at once went out through the doorway to buy with gold all the halwa

entries.farsi: گفت او را جمله‏ى حلوا به چند / گفت كودك نيم دينارى و اند
entries.english: He said to the boy, “How much is the halwa in the lump?” The boy said, “Half a dinar and some small change.”

entries.farsi: گفت نه از صوفيان افزون مجو / نيم دينارت دهم ديگر مگو
entries.english: “Nay,” he replied;” don’t ask too much from Sufis: I will give you half a dinar. Say no more.”

entries.farsi: او طبق بنهاد اندر پيش شيخ / ‏تو ببين اسرار سر انديش شيخ
entries.english: The boy put the tray before the Shaykh. Behold the mysterious secret thoughts of the Shaykh!

entries.farsi: كرد اشارت با غريمان كين نوال / ‏نك تبرك خوش خوريد اين را حالل
entries.english: He made a sign to the creditors “ Look, this gift is a present: eat this gladly, it is lawful.”

entries.farsi: چون طبق خالى شد آن كودك ستد / گفت دينارم بده اى با خرد
entries.english: When the tray was emptied, the boy took it and said, “Give me the gold, O Sage.”

entries.farsi: شيخ گفتا از كجا آرم درم / ‏وام دارم مى‏روم سوى عدم
entries.english: The Shaykh said, “From where shall I get the money? I am in debt and going towards non-existence.”

entries.farsi: كودك از غم زد طبق را بر زمين / ‏ناله و گريه بر آورد و حنين
entries.english: The grieving boy dashed the tray upon the ground; he raised lamentation and weeping and moaning.

entries.farsi: مى‏گريست از غبن كودك هاى هاى / ‏كاى مرا بشكسته بودى هر دو پاى
entries.english: The boy was weeping with loud sobs because of the swindle,, “Would that both my legs had been broken!

entries.farsi: كاشكى من گرد گلخن گشتمى / بر در اين خانقه نگذشتمى
entries.english: Would that I had loitered round the bath-stove and had not passed by the door of this khanaghah!

entries.farsi: صوفيان طبل خوار لقمه جو / سگ دالن و همچو گربه روى شو
entries.english: Greedy, gluttonous Sufis, dogs at heart and washing their faces like cats!”

entries.farsi: از غريو كودك آن جا خير و شر / گرد آمد گشت بر كودك حشر
entries.english: At the boy’s clamour good and bad gathered there and crowded round the boy.

entries.farsi: پيش شيخ آمد كه اى شيخ درشت / ‏تو يقين دان كه مرا استاد كشت
entries.english: He came to the Shaykh and said, “ O cruel Shaykh, know for sure that my master will kill me.

entries.farsi: و آن غريمان هم به انكار و جحود / رو به شيخ آورده كاين بارى چه بود
entries.english: If I go to him empty-handed, he will kill me: will you give leave?”

entries.farsi: گر روم من پيش او دست تهى / ‏او مرا بكشد اجازت مى‏دهى
