entries.english: And those creditors too turned to the Shaykh with disbelief and denial, saying, “What game was this?

entries.farsi: مال ما خوردى مظالم مى‏برى / ‏از چه بود اين ظلم ديگر بر سرى
entries.english: You have devoured our property and are carrying off your iniquities: for what reason was this other injustice on the top?”

entries.farsi: تا نماز ديگر آن كودك گريست / شيخ ديده بست و در وى ننگريست
entries.english: Till afternoon prayers the boy wept; the Shaykh closed his eyes and did not look at him.

entries.farsi: با ازل خوش با اجل خوش شاد كام / ‏فارغ از تشنيع و گفت خاص و عام
entries.english: The Shaykh, unconcerned with abuse and opposition, had withdrawn his moon-like face under the coverlet,

entries.farsi: شيخ فارغ از جفا و از خالف / ‏در كشيده روى چون مه در لحاف
entries.english: Pleased with eternity, pleased with death, joyous, unconcerned with revilement and the talk of high or low.

entries.farsi: آن كه جان در روى او خندد چو قند / از ترش رويى خلقش چه گزند
entries.english: He in whose face the Beloved smiles sweetly, what harm can befall him from the sour looks of people?

entries.farsi: آن كه جان بوسه دهد بر چشم او / كى خورد غم از فلك وز خشم او
entries.english: He on whose eye the Beloved bestows a kiss, how should he grieve at Heaven and its anger?

entries.farsi: در شب مهتاب مه را بر سماك / ‏از سگان و عوعو ايشان چه باك
entries.english: On a moonlight night what cares the moon in Simak for dogs and their barking?

entries.farsi: سگ وظيفه‏ى خود به جا مى‏آورد / مه وظيفه‏ى خود به رخ مى‏گسترد
entries.english: The dog is performing his task; the moon is smoothly fulfilling her task by means of her countenance.

entries.farsi: كارك خود مى‏گزارد هر كسى / ‏آب نگذارد صفا بهر خسى
entries.english: Every one is executing his little business: water does not lose purity because of a bit of weed.

entries.farsi: خس خسانه مى‏رود بر روى آب / ‏آب صافى مى‏رود بى‏اضطراب
entries.english: The weeds float weedily on the surface of the water: the pure water flows on undisturbed.

entries.farsi: آن مسيحا مرده زنده مى‏كند / و آن جهود از خشم سبلت مى‏كند
entries.english: Mustafa splits the moon at midnight; Abu Lahab from hatred talks drivel.

entries.farsi: مصطفى مه مى‏شكافد نيم شب / ‏ژاژ مى‏خايد ز كينه بو لهب
entries.english: The Messiah brings the dead to life, and the Jew tears his moustache in wrath.

entries.farsi: بانگ سگ هرگز رسد در گوش ماه / ‏خاصه ماهى كاو بود خاص اله
entries.english: Does the dog’s bark ever reach the moon’s ear, especially that Moon who is the chosen of God?

entries.farsi: مى‏خورد شه بر لب جو تا سحر / در سماع از بانگ چغزان بى‏خبر
entries.english: The king drinks wine on the bank of the stream till dawn, and in listening to the music is unaware of the croaking of the frogs.

entries.farsi: تا كسى ندهد به كودك هيچ چيز / قوت پيران از اين بيش است نيز
entries.english: The division of the boy would have been a few coins; the Shaykh’s influence stopped that generosity,

entries.farsi: هم شدى توزيع كودك دانگ چند / همت شيخ آن سخا را كرد بند
entries.english: So that no one should give the boy anything: the power of the Pirs is even greater than this.

entries.farsi: شد نماز ديگر آمد خادمى / ‏يك طبق بر كف ز پيش حاتمى
entries.english: It was afternoon prayers, a servant came with a tray in his hand from one like Hatim,

entries.farsi: صاحب مالى و حالى پيش پير / هديه بفرستاد كز وى بد خبير
entries.english: A man of property and condition: he sent it as a present to the Pir, for he knew about him.

entries.farsi: چار صد دينار بر گوشه‏ى طبق / ‏نيم دينار دگر اندر ورق
entries.english: Four hundred dinars, and in a corner of the tray another half-dinar in a piece of paper

entries.farsi: خادم آمد شيخ را اكرام كرد / و آن طبق بنهاد پيش شيخ فرد
entries.english: The servant advanced and did honour to the Shaykh, and laid the tray before the peerless Shaykh.

entries.farsi: آه و افغان از همه برخاست زود / كاى سر شيخان و شاهان اين چه بود
entries.english: When he uncovered the face of the tray, the people beheld the miracle from him.

entries.farsi: چون طبق را از غطا واكرد رو / خلق ديدند آن كرامت را از او
entries.english: Immediately cries of sorrow and lamentation arose from all “ O head of the Shaykhs and kings, what was this?

entries.farsi: اين چه سر است اين چه سلطانى است باز / اى خداوند خداوندان راز
entries.english: What secret is this? Once more, what sovereignty is this, O lord of the lords of mystery?

entries.farsi: ما ندانستيم ما را عفو كن / ‏بس پراكنده كه رفت از ما سخن
entries.english: We knew not. Pardon us. Very deranged were the words that went out from us.

entries.farsi: ما كه كورانه عصاها مى‏زنيم / ‏الجرم قنديلها را بشكنيم
entries.english: We who blindly brandish staffs cannot help breaking lamps.

entries.farsi: ما چو كران ناشنيده يك خطاب / ‏هرزه گويان از قياس خود جواب
entries.english: We, like deaf men, without having heard a single thing spoken, are answering idly from our own surmise.

entries.farsi: ما ز موسى پند نگرفتيم كاو / گشت از انكار خضرى زرد رو
entries.english: We have not taken warning from Moses, who was made shamefaced by disbelief in a Khizr,

entries.farsi: با چنان چشمى كه بالا مى‏شتافت / ‏نور چشمش آسمان را مى‏شكافت
entries.english: Notwithstanding an eye that sped aloft, and the light of his eye was piercing heaven.

entries.farsi: كرده با چشمت تعصب موسيا / از حماقت چشم موش آسيا
entries.english: O Moses, through foolishness the eye of a mill-mouse has fanatically set itself against your eye.”

entries.farsi: شيخ فرمود آن همه گفتار و قال / ‏من بحل كردم شما را آن حالل
entries.english: The Shaykh said, “I forgive all that talk and chatter: it is lawful to you.

entries.farsi: سر اين آن بود كز حق خواستم / ‏الجرم بنمود راه راستم
entries.english: The secret of this was that I besought God: consequently He showed me the right way,

entries.farsi: گفت آن دينار اگر چه اندك است / ‏ليك موقوف غريو كودك است
entries.english: And said, ‘ though that dinar is little, yet it is dependent on the boy’s outcry

entries.farsi: تا نگريد كودك حلوا فروش / ‏بحر رحمت در نمى‏آيد به جوش
entries.english: Until the halwa-selling boy weeps, the sea of mercy is not aroused ‘.”

entries.farsi: اى برادر طفل طفل چشم تست / ‏كام خود موقوف زارى دان درست
entries.english: O brother, the child is the child of your eye: know surely that your desire is dependent on distress.

entries.farsi: گر همى‏خواهى كه آن خلعت رسد / پس بگريان طفل ديده بر جسد
entries.english: If you wish that that robe of honour should come; then make the child of your eye weep over your body.

sections.title_fa: ترسانيدن شخصى زاهد را كه كم گرى تا كور نشوى
sections.title_en: How a certain person frightened an ascetic, saying, “Weep little, lest thou become blind.”

entries.farsi: زاهدى را گفت يارى در عمل / ‏كم گرى تا چشم را نايد خلل
entries.english: A comrade in the work said to an ascetic, “Weep little, lest your eye come to harm.”

entries.farsi: گفت زاهد از دو بيرون نيست حال / چشم بيند يا نبيند آن جمال
entries.english: The ascetic said, “The case is not outside of two: the eye will see, or it will not see, that Beauty.

entries.farsi: گر ببيند نور حق خود چه غم است / ‏در وصال حق دو ديده چه كم است
entries.english: If it sees the Light of God, what is there to grieve about? How little are two eyes in union with God!

entries.farsi: ور نخواهد ديد حق را گو برو / اين چنين چشم شقى گو كور شو
entries.english: And if it shall not see God, let it go! Let such a miserable eye become blind!”

entries.farsi: غم مخور از ديده كان عيسى تراست / ‏چپ مرو تا بخشدت دو چشم راست
entries.english: Do not grieve for your eye when that Jesus is yours; do not go to the left that he may give you two right eyes.

entries.farsi: عيسى روح تو با تو حاضر است / ‏نصرت از وى خواه كاو خوش ناصر است
entries.english: The Jesus of your spirit is present with you: beg aid from him, for he is a goodly helper ;

entries.farsi: ليك بيگار تن پر استخوان / ‏بر دل عيسى منه تو هر زمان
entries.english: But do not every moment lay on the heart of Jesus the unprofitable work of a body full of bones,

entries.farsi: زندگى تن مجو از عيسى‏ات / ‏كام فرعونى مخواه از موسى‏ات
entries.english: Like the fool whom we mentioned in the story for the sake of the righteous.

entries.farsi: همچو آن ابله كه اندر داستان / ذكر او كرديم بهر راستان
entries.english: Seek not from your Jesus the life of the body; ask not from your Moses the wish of a Pharaoh.

entries.farsi: بر دل خود كم نه انديشه‏ى معاش / ‏عيش كم نايد تو بر درگاه باش
entries.english: Burden not your heart with thoughts of livelihood; livelihood will not fail: be at the Court.

entries.farsi: اين بدن خرگاه آمد روح را / يا مثال كشتيى مر نوح را
entries.english: This body is a tent for the spirit, or like an ark for Noah.

entries.farsi: ترك چون باشد بيابد خرگهى / ‏خاصه چون باشد عزيز درگهى
entries.english: When the Turcoman is there, he will find a tent, especially when he is one held in honour at the Court.

sections.title_fa: تمامى قصه‏ى زنده شدن استخوانها به دعاى عيسى عليه السلام
sections.title_en: Conclusion of the story of the coming to life of the bones at the prayer of Jesus,

entries.farsi: حكم يزدان از پى آن خام مرد / صورت آن استخوان را زنده كرد
entries.english: on whom be peace!

entries.farsi: خواند عيسى نام حق بر استخوان / ‏از براى التماس آن جوان
entries.english: Jesus pronounced the Name of God over the bones on account of the young man’s entreaty.

entries.farsi: از ميان بر جست يك شير سياه / ‏پنجه‏اى زد كرد نقشش را تباه
entries.english: For the sake of that foolish man the decree of God gave life to the form which those bones had possessed.

entries.farsi: كله‏اش بر كند مغزش ريخت زود / مغز جوزى كاندر او مغزى نبود
entries.english: A black lion sprang forth, hit once with its paw, and destroyed his image.

entries.farsi: گر و را مغزى بدى اشكستنش / ‏خود نبودى نقص الا بر تنش
entries.english: It tore up his skull: his brain was scattered on the spot— the brain of a nut, for in him was no brain.

entries.farsi: گفت عيسى چون شتابش كوفتى / ‏گفت ز آن رو كه تو زو آشوفتى
entries.english: If he had had a brain, his being broken to pieces would have been no injury at all except to his body.

entries.farsi: گفت عيسى چون نخوردى خون مرد / گفت در قسمت نبودم رزق خورد
entries.english: Jesus said, “How did you maul him so quickly?” The lion said, “Because you were troubled by him.”

entries.farsi: اى بسا كس همچو آن شير ژيان / ‏صيد خود ناخورده رفته از جهان
entries.english: Jesus asked, “How did not you drink the man’s blood?” “In the dispensation it was not granted to me to drink,” replied the lion.
Oh, many a one that like that raging lion has departed from the world without having eaten his prey!

entries.farsi: قسمتش كاهى نه و حرصش چو كوه / ‏وجه نه و كرده تحصيل وجوه
entries.english: His portion is not a straw, while his greed is as a mountain; he has no means, though he has gotten the means.

entries.farsi: اى ميسر كرده بر ما در جهان / سخره و بيگار ما را وارهان
entries.english: O You, who has made it easy for us to do unrewarded and fruitless labour in the world, deliver us!

entries.farsi: طعمه بنموده به ما و آن بوده شست / ‏آن چنان بنما به ما آن را كه هست
entries.english: To us it seems bait and it is a hook: show it to us even as it is.

entries.farsi: گر مرا روزى بدى اندر جهان / ‏خود چه كاراستى مرا با مردگان
entries.english: The lion said, “O Messiah, this prey was merely for the purpose that warning might be taken.

entries.farsi: گفت آن شير اى مسيحا اين شكار / بود خالص از براى اعتبار
entries.english: Had there been for me in the world an allotted portion, what business indeed should I have had with the dead?”

entries.farsi: اين سزاى آن كه يابد آب صاف / ‏همچو خر در جو بميزد از گزاف
entries.english: This is the punishment deserved by him that finds pure water, and like an ass urinates impertinently in the stream.

entries.farsi: گر بداند قيمت آن جوى خر / او بجاى پا نهد در جوى سر
entries.english: If the ass knows the value of that stream, instead of his foot he will put his head in it.

entries.farsi: او بيابد آن چنان پيغمبرى / ‏مير آبى زندگانى پرورى
entries.english: He finds a prophet like that, a lord of the Water, a cherisher of life:

entries.farsi: هين سگ نفس ترا زنده مخواه / ‏كاو عدوى جان تست از ديرگاه
entries.english: How does not he die before him, saying “O lord of the Water, make me living by the command ‘Be’?”

entries.farsi: چون نميرد پيش او كز امر كن / ‏اى امير آب ما را زنده كن
entries.english: Take heed! Do not wish your currish soul alive, for it is the enemy of your spirit since long ago.

entries.farsi: خاك بر سر استخوانى را كه آن / ‏مانع اين سگ بود از صيد جان
entries.english: Dust be on the head of the bones that hinder this cur from hunting the spirit!

entries.farsi: سگ نه‏اى بر استخوان چون عاشقى / ‏ديوچه‏وار از چه بر خون عاشقى
entries.english: You are not a cur, how are you in love with bones? Why are you in love with blood, like a leech?

entries.farsi: آن چه چشم است آن كه بيناييش نيست / ‏ز امتحانها جز كه رسواييش نيست
entries.english: What eye is that that has no sight, and gets nothing but disgrace from the tests?

entries.farsi: سهو باشد ظنها را گاه گاه / ‏اين چه ظن است اين كه كور آمد ز راه
entries.english: Opinions are sometimes erroneous, what opinion is this that is blind to the road?

entries.farsi: ديده آ بر ديگران نوحه‏گرى / ‏مدتى بنشين و بر خود مى‏گرى
entries.english: O eye, you lament for others: sit down awhile and weep for yourself!

entries.farsi: ز ابر گريان شاخ سبز و تر شود / ز آنكه شمع از گريه روشن‏تر شود
entries.english: The bough is made green and fresh by the weeping cloud, for the reason that the candle is made brighter by weeping.

entries.farsi: هر كجا نوحه كنند آن جا نشين / ز آنكه تو اوليترى اندر حنين
entries.english: Wherever people are lamenting, sit there, because you have a better right to moan,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه ايشان در فراق فانى‏اند / غافل از لعل بقاى كانى‏اند
entries.english: Inasmuch as they are with parting from that which passes away, and are forgetful of the ruby of everlastingness that belongs to the mine;

entries.farsi: ز آن كه بر دل نقش تقليد است بند / رو به آب چشم بندش را برند
entries.english: Inasmuch as the stamp of blind imitation is a lock upon the heart; —go, scrape off its lock with tears—;

entries.farsi: ز آن كه تقليد آفت هر نيكويى است / ‏كه بود تقليد اگر كوه قوى است
entries.english: Inasmuch as imitation is the bane of every good quality: imitation is a straw, if it is a mighty mountain.

entries.farsi: گر ضريرى لمترست و تيز خشم / ‏گوشت پاره‏ش دان چو او را نيست چشم
entries.english: If a blind man is big and choleric, deem him a piece of flesh, since he has no eye.

entries.farsi: گر سخن گويد ز مو باريكتر / آن سرش را ز آن سخن نبود خبر
entries.english: Though he speaks words finer than a hair, his heart has no knowledge of those words.

entries.farsi: مستيى دارد ز گفت خود و ليك / ‏از بر وى تا به مى راهى است نيك
entries.english: He has certain intoxication from his own words, but there is a good way between him and the Wine.

entries.farsi: همچو جوى است او نه او آبى خورد / آب از او بر آب خواران بگذرد
entries.english: He is like a river-bed: it does not drink any water; the water passes through it to the water-drinkers.

entries.farsi: آب در جو ز آن نمى‏گيرد قرار / ز آن كه آن جو نيست تشنه و آب خوار
entries.english: The water does not settle in the river-bed because the riverbed is not thirsty and water-drinking.

entries.farsi: نوحه‏گر باشد مقلد در حديث / ‏جز طمع نبود مراد آن خبيث
entries.english: Like a reed-flute, he makes a piteous lament, but he seeks a buyer.

entries.farsi: نوحه‏گر گويد حديث سوزناك / ‏ليك كو سوز دل و دامان چاك
entries.english: The imitator in his discourse is a professional mourner: that wicked man has no motive except cupidity.

entries.farsi: همچو نايى ناله‏ى زارى كند / ليك بيگار خريدارى كند
entries.english: The professional mourner utters burning words, but where is the glow of heart and the torn skirt?

entries.farsi: از محقق تا مقلد فرق‏هاست / كاين چو داود است و آن ديگر صداست
entries.english: Between the true knower and the blind imitator there are differences, for the former is like David, while the other is an echo.

entries.farsi: منبع گفتار اين سوزى بود / و آن مقلد كهنه آموزى بود
entries.english: The source of the former’s words is a glow, whereas the imitator is one who learns old things.

entries.farsi: هين مشو غره بدان گفت حزين / بار بر گاو است و بر گردون حنين
entries.english: Beware! Do not be duped by those sorrowful words: the ox bears the load, but it is the cart that moans.

entries.farsi: هم مقلد نيست محروم از ثواب / ‏نوحه‏گر را مزد باشد در حساب
entries.english: Even the imitator is not disappointed of the recompense: the professional mourner gets his wages at the reckoning.

entries.farsi: كافر و مومن خدا گويند ليك / ‏در ميان هر دو فرقى هست نيك
entries.english: Infidel and true believer say “God,” but there is a good difference between the two.

entries.farsi: آن گدا گويد خدا از بهر نان / ‏متقى گويد خدا از عين جان
entries.english: The beggar says “God” for the sake of bread; the devout man says “God” from his very soul.

entries.farsi: گر بدانستى گدا از گفت خويش / ‏پيش چشم او نه كم ماندى نه پيش
entries.english: If the beggar distinguished from his own saying of, neither less nor more would remain before his eye.

entries.farsi: سالها گويد خدا آن نان خواه / ‏همچو خر مصحف كشد از بهر كاه
entries.english: For years that bread-seeker says “God”; like the ass, he carries the Qur’an for the sake of straw.

entries.farsi: گر بدل در تافتى گفت لبش / ذره ذره گشته بودى قالبش
entries.english: Had the word on his lips shone forth in his heart, his body would have been shivered to atoms.

entries.farsi: نام ديوى ره برد در ساحرى / ‏تو به نام حق پشيزى مى‏برى
entries.english: In sorcery the name of a demon finds the way; you are earning a petty coin by means of the Name of God

sections.title_fa: خاريدن روستايى در تاريكى شير را به گمان آن كه گاو اوست
sections.title_en: How a peasant stroked a lion in the dark, because he thought it was his ox.

entries.farsi: روستايى گاو در آخر ببست / ‏شير گاوش خورد و بر جايش نشست
entries.english: A peasant tied an ox in the stable: a lion ate his ox and sat in its place.

entries.farsi: روستايى شد در آخر سوى گاو / گاو را مى‏جست شب آن كنج كاو
entries.english: The peasant went into the stable to the ox: the man, groping into corners, was seeking the ox at night.

entries.farsi: دست مى‏ماليد بر اعضاى شير / پشت و پهلو گاه بالا گاه زير
entries.english: He was rubbing his hand on the limbs of the lion, back and side, now above, now below.

entries.farsi: گفت شير ار روشنى افزون شدى / ‏زهره‏اش بدريدى و دل خون شدى
entries.english: The lion said, “If the light were to become greater, his gall bladder would burst and his heart would turn to blood.

entries.farsi: اين چنين گستاخ ز آن مى‏خاردم / ‏كاو درين شب گاو مى‏پنداردم
entries.english: He is stroking me like this so boldly because in this night he thinks I am the ox.”

entries.farsi: حق همى‏گويد كه اى مغرور كور / نه ز نامم پاره پاره گشت طور
entries.english: Haqq is saying, “O blind dupe, did not Tur fall in pieces at My Name?

entries.farsi: كه لو انزلنا كتابا للجبل / ‏النصدع ثم انقطع ثم ارتحل
entries.english: For if We had sent down a Book to the mountain, it would have been split apart, then cut to pieces, and then it would have departed.

entries.farsi: از من ار كوه احد واقف بدى / ‏پاره گشتى و دلش پر خون شدى
entries.english: If Mount Uhud had been acquainted with Me, blood would have gushed in jets from the mountain.”

entries.farsi: از پدر وز مادر اين بشنيده‏اى / ‏الجرم غافل در اين پيچيده‏اى
entries.english: You have heard this from your father and mother; in consequence you have embraced it thoughtlessly.

entries.farsi: گر تو بى‏تقليد از اين واقف شوى / ‏بى‏نشان از لطف چون هاتف شوى
entries.english: If you become acquainted with Him without blind imitation, by grace you will become immaterial, like a voice from Heaven

entries.farsi: بشنو اين قصه پى تهديد را / تا بدانى آفت تقليد را
entries.english: Hear this story as a deterrent, in order that you may know the destructive power of blind imitation.

sections.title_fa: فروختن صوفيان بهيمه‏ى مسافر را جهت سماع
sections.title_en: How the Sufis sold the traveller’s beast for the mystic dance.

entries.farsi: صوفيى در خانقاه از ره رسيد / مركب خود برد و در آخر كشيد
entries.english: A Sufi, after journeying, arrived at a khanaghah; he took his mount and led it to the stable.

entries.farsi: آب كش داد و علف از دست خويش / ‏نه چنان صوفى كه ما گفتيم پيش
entries.english: With his own hand he gave it a little water and some fodder: not such a Sufi as the one we told of before.

entries.farsi: احتياطش كرد از سهو و خباط / چون قضا آيد چه سود است احتياط
entries.english: He took precaution for it against neglect and craziness, when the destiny comes to pass, of what avail is precaution?

entries.farsi: صوفيان در جوع بودند و فقير / كاد فقر أن يعي كفرا يبير
entries.english: The Sufis were destitute and poor: poverty almost comprises an infidelity that brings to perdition.

entries.farsi: از سر تقصير آن صوفى رمه / ‏خر فروشى در گرفتند آن همه
entries.english: O rich man, who is well fed, beware of laughing at the unrighteousness of the suffering poor.

entries.farsi: اى توانگر كه تو سيرى هين مخند / بر كجى آن فقير دردمند
entries.english: On account of their destitution that Sufi flock, all of them, adopted selling the donkey,

entries.farsi: كز ضرورت هست مردارى مباح / ‏بس فسادى كز ضرورت شد صالح
entries.english: Saying, “of necessity a carcass is lawful; many a vicious act that necessity made a virtuous one.”

entries.farsi: هم در آن دم آن خرك بفروختند / لوت آوردند و شمع افروختند
entries.english: They instantly sold the little donkey; they bought dainty delicacies and lit candles.

entries.farsi: ولوله افتاد اندر خانقه / ‏كامشبان لوت و سماع است و شره
entries.english: Jubilation arose in the khanaghah,” “To-night there are delicacies, music, dancing and Sema.

entries.farsi: چند از اين صبر و از اين سه روزه چند / چند از اين زنبيل و اين دريوزه چند
entries.english: How much of this wallet and this beggary? How much of this patience and of this three-day fasting?

entries.farsi: دولت امشب ميهمان داريم ما / كان كه آن جان نيست جان پنداشتند
entries.english: We also are of creatures, we have soul. Good luck to-night: we have the guest.”

entries.farsi: ما هم از خلقيم و جان داريم ما / دولت امشب ميهمان داريم ما
entries.english: Thereby they were sowing the seed of falsehood, for they deemed soul what is not soul.

entries.farsi: و آن مسافر نيز از راه دراز / خسته بود و ديد آن اقبال و ناز
entries.english: And the traveller, too, was tired by the long journey and saw that favour and fondness

entries.farsi: صوفيانش يك به يك بنواختند / نرد خدمتهاى خوش مى‏باختند
entries.english: The Sufis, one by one, caressed him: they were playing the game of pleasant attentions.

entries.farsi: گفت چون مى‏ديد ميالنشان به وى / ‏گر طرب امشب نخواهم كرد كى
entries.english: When he saw their affection towards him, he said, “If I don’t make merry to-night, when?”

entries.farsi: لوت خوردند و سماع آغاز كرد / خانقه تا سقف شد پر دود و گرد
entries.english: They ate the food and began the Sama; the khanaghah was filled with smoke and dust up to the roof

entries.farsi: دود مطبخ گرد آن پا كوفتن / ‏ز اشتياق و وجد جان آشوفتن
entries.english: The smoke of the kitchen, the dust of beating thefeet, the tumult of soul by longing and ecstasy.

entries.farsi: گاه دست افشان قدم مى‏كوفتند / گه به سجده صفه را مى‏روفتند
entries.english: Now, waving their hands, they would beat their feet; now, in prostration, they would sweep the dais.

entries.farsi: دير يابد صوفى آز از روزگار / ز آن سبب صوفى بود بسيار خوار
entries.english: After long does the Sufi gain his desire from Fortune: for that reason the Sufi is a great eater;

entries.farsi: جز مگر آن صوفيى كز نور حق / ‏سير خورد او فارغ است از ننگ دق
entries.english: Except, to be sure, the Sufi who has eaten his fill of the Light of God: he is free from the shame of beggary;

entries.farsi: از هزاران اندكى زين صوفيند / باقيان در دولت او مى‏زيند
entries.english: Those Sufis are a few among thousands; the rest are living in his empire.

entries.farsi: چون سماع آمد از اول تا كران / ‏مطرب آغازيد يك ضرب گران
entries.english: When the Sema had come from beginning to end, the minstrel struck up a heavy strain.

entries.farsi: خر برفت و خر برفت آغاز كرد / زين حراره جمله را انباز كرد
entries.english: He commenced, “The donkey is gone, and the donkey is gone”; he made the whole sharers in this enthusiasm.

entries.farsi: زين حراره پاى كوبان تا سحر / كف‏زنان خر رفت و خر رفت اى پسر
entries.english: From this enthusiasm beating their feet till dawn, clapping their hands, “The donkey is gone; the donkey is gone, O son!”

entries.farsi: از ره تقليد آن صوفى همين / ‏خر برفت آغاز كرد اندر حنين
entries.english: By way of imitation that Sufi began in impassioned feeling this same, “The donkey is gone.”

entries.farsi: چون گذشت آن نوش و جوش و آن سماع / ‏روز گشت و جمله گفتند الوداع
entries.english: When the pleasure and excitement and music and dancing were over, day dawned and they all said, “Farewell!”

entries.farsi: خانقه خالى شد و صوفى بماند / گرد از رخت آن مسافر مى‏فشاند
entries.english: The monastery was deserted, and the Sufi remained: that traveller set about shaking the dust from his baggage.

entries.farsi: رخت از حجره برون آورد او / تا به خر بر بندد آن همراه جو
entries.english: He brought out the baggage from his cell, in order that he might tie it on the donkey, seeking people to travel with.

entries.farsi: تا رسد در همرهان او مى‏شتافت / ‏رفت در آخر خر خود را نيافت
entries.english: He was hurrying that he might overtake his fellow-travellers; he went into the stable but did not find the donkey.

entries.farsi: گفت آن خادم به آبش برده است / ‏ز انكه خر دوش آب كمتر خورده است
entries.english: He said, “The servant has taken it to water, because it drank little water last night.”

entries.farsi: خادم آمد گفت صوفى خر كجاست / ‏گفت خادم ريش بين جنگى بخاست
entries.english: The servant came, and the Sufi said to him, “Where is the donkey?” “Look at your beard,” replied the servant, and a quarrel arose.

entries.farsi: گفت من خر را به تو بسپرده‏ام / ‏من ترا بر خر موكل كرده‏ام
entries.english: He said, “I have entrusted the donkey to you, I have put you in charge of the donkey.

entries.farsi: بحث با توجيه كن حجت ميار / آن چه بسپردم ترا واپس سپار
entries.english: Discuss with propriety, don’t argue: deliver back to me what I delivered to you

entries.farsi: از تو خواهم آن چه من دادم به تو / باز ده آن چه فرستادم به تو
entries.english: I demand from you what I gave to you: return what I entrusted to you.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه دستت هر چه برد / بايدش در عاقبت واپس سپرد
entries.english: The Prophet said that whatever your hand has taken must in the end be restored.

entries.farsi: ور نه‏اى از سركشى راضى بدين / ‏نك من و تو خانه‏ى قاضى دين
entries.english: And if you, from insolence, are not content with this, look here, let us to the house of the Cadi of religion.”

entries.farsi: گفت من مغلوب بودم صوفيان / ‏حمله آوردند و بودم بيم جان
entries.english: The servant said, “I was overpowered: the Sufis rushed, and I was in fear for my life.

entries.farsi: تو جگر بندى ميان گربگان / ‏اندر اندازى و جويى ز آن نشان
entries.english: Do you include liver with the scraps that you feed to the cats, and seek a trace of it?

entries.farsi: در ميان صد گرسنه گرده‏اى / ‏پيش صد سگ گربه‏ى پژمرده‏اى
entries.english: One cake of bread amongst a hundred hungry people, one half dead cat before a hundred dogs? “

entries.farsi: گفت گيرم كز تو ظلما بستدند / قاصد خون من مسكين شدند
entries.english: “I suppose,” Said the Sufi, “that they took it from you by violence, aimed at the life of wretched me;

entries.farsi: تو نيايى و نگويى مر مرا / كه خرت را مى‏برند اى بى‏نوا
entries.english: You did not come and say to me, they are taking away your donkey, O poor man!’

entries.farsi: تا خر از هر كه بود من واخرم / ‏ور نه توزيعى كنند ايشان زرم
entries.english: So that I might buy back the donkey from whomever he is, or else they might divide my money.

entries.farsi: صد تدارك بود چون حاضر بدند / اين زمان هر يك به اقليمى شدند
entries.english: There were a hundred ways of mending when they were present; now each one is gone to a separate place.

entries.farsi: من كه را گيرم كه را قاضى برم / ‏اين قضا خود از تو آمد بر سرم
entries.english: Whom should I seize? Whom should I take to the Cadi? It is from you in truth that this judgment has come upon me.

entries.farsi: چون نيايى و نگويى اى غريب / ‏پيش آمد اين چنين ظلمى مهيب
entries.english: Why didn’t you come and say, ‘O stranger, such a terrible outrage has occurred’?”

entries.farsi: گفت و اهلل آمدم من بارها / تا ترا واقف كنم زين كارها
entries.english: “By God,” said he, “I came several times to inform you of these doings,

entries.farsi: باز مى‏گشتم كه او خود واقف است / ‏زين قضا راضى است مردى عارف است
entries.english: You were always saying, ‘the donkey is gone, O son,’ with more gusto than all who said it.

entries.farsi: تو همى‏گفتى كه خر رفت اى پسر / از همه گويندگان با ذوق‏تر
entries.english: I was going back, ‘He himself is aware; he is satisfied with this consequence: he is an Arif.”

entries.farsi: گفت آن را جمله مى‏گفتند خوش / ‏مر مرا هم ذوق آمد گفتنش
entries.english: The Sufi said, “They all were saying merrily, I also took delight in saying it.

entries.farsi: مر مرا تقليدشان بر باد داد / كه دو صد لعنت بر آن تقليد باد
entries.english: Blind imitation of them has brought me to ruin: two hundred curses on that imitation

entries.farsi: خاصه تقليد چنين بى‏حاصالن / ‏خشم ابراهيم با بر آفالن
entries.english: Especially imitation of such good-for-nothing rascals— the wrath of Abraham on them that sink!

entries.farsi: عكس ذوق آن جماعت مى‏زدى / ‏وين دلم ز آن عكس ذوقى مى‏شدى
entries.english: The delight of that company was casting a reflection, and this heart of mine was becoming seduced by that reflection.”

entries.farsi: عكس چندان بايد از ياران خوش / ‏كه شوى از بحر بى‏عكس آب كش
entries.english: The reflection from good friends is necessary until you become, without reflection, a drawer of water from the Sea.

entries.farsi: ‏ عكس كاول زد تو آن تقليد دان / ‏چون پياپى شد شود تحقيق آن
entries.english: Know that the reflection first cast is imitation, when it has become successive it turns into realisation.

entries.farsi: تا نشد تحقيق از ياران مبر / از صدف مگسل نگشت آن قطره در
entries.english: Until it has become realisation, do not part from the friends; do not break away from the shell: the rain-drop has not become a pearl. If you wish eye, understanding and hearing to be pure, tear the curtains of selfish desire to pieces.

entries.farsi: صاف خواهى چشم و عقل و سمع را / بر دران تو پرده‏هاى طمع را
entries.english: Because the Sufi’s imitation, from tama, blocked his understanding from the light and radiance

entries.farsi: ز انكه آن تقليد صوفى از طمع / ‏عقل او بر بست از نور و لمع
entries.english: Greed for the food, desire for that delight, and for the Sema hindered his understanding from knowledge.

entries.farsi: طمع لوت و طمع آن ذوق و سماع / ‏مانع آمد عقل او را ز اطالع
entries.english: If desire were to arise in the mirror, that mirror would be like us in hypocrisy.

entries.farsi: گر طمع در آينه برخاستى / ‏در نفاق آن آينه چون ماستى
entries.english: If the scale had desire for riches, how would the scale give a true description of the case?

entries.farsi: ‏ گر ترازو را طمع بودى به مال / ‏راست كى گفتى ترازو وصف حال
entries.english: Every prophet has said in sincerity to his people, “I ask not from you the wages for my message.

entries.farsi: هر نبيى گفت با قوم از صفا / من نخواهم مزد پيغام از شما
entries.english: I am a guide; God is your purchaser: God has appointed me to act as broker on both sides.

entries.farsi: من دليلم حق شما را مشترى / ‏داد حق دالليم هر دو سرى
entries.english: What are the wages for my work? The sight of the Friend, even though Abu Bakr gives me forty thousand

entries.farsi: چيست مزد كار من ديدار يار / گر چه خود بو بكر بخشد چل هزار
entries.english: My wages are not his forty thousand: how should glass beads be like the pearls of Aden?”

entries.farsi: چل هزار او نباشد مزد من / ‏كى بود شبه شبه در عدن
entries.english: I will tell you a story: listen to it attentively, that you may know that a sense of entitlement is a plug in the ear.

entries.farsi: يك حكايت گويمت بشنو به هوش / ‏تا بدانى كه طمع شد بند گوش
entries.english: Whoever is greedy becomes a stammered; with desire how should the eye and the heart become bright?

entries.farsi: ‏ هر كه را باشد طمع الكن شود / با طمع كى چشم و دل روشن شود
entries.english: The image of power and wealth before his eye is just as a hair in the eye,

entries.farsi: پيش چشم او خيال جاه و زر / همچنان باشد كه موى اندر بصر
entries.english: Except, to be sure, the intoxicated that is filled with God: though you give treasures, he is free;

entries.farsi: جز مگر مستى كه از حق پر بود / گر چه بدهى گنجها او حر بود
entries.english: When any one enjoys vision, this world becomes carrion in his eyes.

entries.farsi: ليك آن صوفى ز مستى دور بود / الجرم در حرص او شب كور بود
entries.english: But that Sufi was far removed from intoxication; consequently he was night-blind in greed.

entries.farsi: صد حكايت بشنود مدهوش حرص / ‏در نيايد نكته‏اى در گوش حرص
entries.english: The man dazed by entitlement may hear a hundred stories, not a single point comes into the ear of greed.

sections.title_fa: تعريف كردن مناديان قاضى مفلسى را گرد شهر
sections.title_en: How the criers of the Cadi advertised an insolvent round the town.

entries.farsi: بود شخصى مفلسى بى‏خان و مان / ‏مانده در زندان وبند بى‏امان
entries.english: There was an insolvent person without house or home, who remained in prison and pitiless bondage.

entries.farsi: لقمه‏ى زندانيان خوردى گزاف / ‏بر دل خلق از طمع چون كوه قاف
entries.english: He would unconscionably eat the rations of the prisoners; on account of appetite he was like Mount Qaf on the hearts of the people.

entries.farsi: زهره نه كس را كه لقمه‏ى نان خورد / ز انكه آن لقمه‏ربا كاوش برد
entries.english: No one had the pluck to eat a mouthful of bread, because that snatcher of portions would carry off his entire meal.

entries.farsi: هر كه دور از دعوت رحمان بود / او گدا چشم است اگر سلطان بود
entries.english: Any one who is far from the feast of the Merciful has the eye of a beggar, though he is a sultan.

entries.farsi: مر مروت را نهاده زير پا / گشته زندان دوزخى ز آن نان ربا
entries.english: He had trodden virtue underfoot; the prison had become a hell on account of that robber of bread.

entries.farsi: هيچ كنجى بى‏دد و بى‏دام نيست / ‏جز به خلوت‏گاه حق آرام نيست
entries.english: If you flee in hope of some relief, on that side also a calamity comes to meet you.

entries.farsi: گر گريزى بر اميد راحتى / ‏ز آن طرف هم پيشت آيد آفتى
entries.english: No corner is without wild beasts; there is no rest but in the place where you are alone with God.

entries.farsi: كنج زندان جهان ناگزير / نيست بى‏پا مزد و بى‏دق الحصير
entries.english: The corner of this world’s inevitable prison is not exempt from the charges for visitors and housewarming.

entries.farsi: آدمى را فربهى هست از خيال / ‏گر خيالاتش بود صاحب جمال
entries.english: By God, if you go into a mouse-hole, you will be afflicted by some one who has the claws of a cat.

entries.farsi: و اهلل ار سوراخ موشى در روى / ‏مبتالى گربه چنگالى شوى
entries.english: Man has fatness from whim, if his fantasies are beautiful;

entries.farsi: ور خيالاتش نمايد ناخوشى / ‏مى‏گدازد همچو موم از آتشى
entries.english: And if his fancies show anything unlovely he melts away as wax by a fire.

entries.farsi: در ميان مار و كژدم گر ترا / با خيالات خوشان دارد خدا
entries.english: If amidst snakes and scorpions God keep you with the visions of them that are fair,

entries.farsi: مار و كژدم مر ترا مونس بود / كان خيالت كيمياى مس بود
entries.english: The snakes and scorpions will be friendly to you, because that idea is the elixir which transmutes your copper.

entries.farsi: صبر شيرين از خيال خوش شده ست / ‏كان خيالات فرج پيش آمده ست
entries.english: Patience is sweetened by fair vision since the fantasiess of relief have come before.

entries.farsi: آن فرج آيد ز ايمان در ضمير / ضعف ايمان نااميدى و زحير
entries.english: That relief comes into the heart from faith: weakness of faith is despair and torment.

entries.farsi: صبر از ايمان بيابد سر كله / ‏حيث ال صبر فال إيمان له
entries.english: Patience gains a crown from faith: where one has no patience, he has no faith.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر خداش ايمان نداد / هر كه را صبرى نباشد در نهاد
entries.english: The Prophet said, “God has not given faith to any one in whose nature there is no patience.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى در چشم تو باشد چو مار / هم وى اندر چشم آن ديگر نگار
entries.english: That same one in your eyes is like a snake is a picture in the eyes of another,

entries.farsi: ز انكه در چشمت خيال كفر اوست / ‏و آن خيال مومنى در چشم دوست
entries.english: Because in your eyes is the idea of his being an infidel, while in the eyes of his friend is the idea of his being a believer ;

entries.farsi: كاندر اين يك شخص هر دو فعل هست / ‏گاه ماهى باشد او و گاه شست
entries.english: For both the effects exist in this one person: now he is a fish and now a hook.

entries.farsi: نيم او مومن بود نيميش گبر / نيم او حرص آورى نيميش صبر
entries.english: Half of him is believer, half of him infidel; half of him cupidity, half of him patience.

entries.farsi: گفت يزدانت فمنكم مومن / ‏باز منكم كافر گبر كهن
entries.english: Your God has said, “of you believing”; again, of you unbelieving” an old fire-worshipper.

entries.farsi: همچو گاوى نيمه‏ى چپش سياه / ‏نيمه‏ى ديگر سپيد همچو ماه
entries.english: Like an ox, his left half black, the other half white as the moon.

entries.farsi: هر كه اين نيمه ببيند رد كند / هر كه آن نيمه ببيند كد كند
entries.english: Whoever sees the former half spurns; whoever sees the latter half seeks.

entries.farsi: يوسف اندر چشم اخوان چون ستور / هم وى اندر چشم يعقوبى چو حور
entries.english: Joseph was like a beast of burden in the eyes of his brothers; at the same time in the eves of a Jacob he was like a houri.

entries.farsi: از خيال بد مر او را زشت ديد / چشم فرع و چشم اصلى ناپديد
entries.english: Through evil idea the derivative eye and the original unseen eye regarded him as ugly.

entries.farsi: چشم ظاهر سايه‏ى آن چشم دان / ‏هر چه آن بيند بگردد اين بد آن
entries.english: Know that the outward eye is the shadow of that eye: whatever that eye may see, this eye turns to that.

entries.farsi: تو مكانى اصل تو در المكان / ‏اين دكان بر بند و بگشا آن دكان
entries.english: You are of where, your origin is in Nowhere: shut up this shop and open that shop.

entries.farsi: شش جهت مگريز زيرا در جهات / ‏ششدره است و ششدره مات است مات
entries.english: Do not flee to the six directions, because in directions there is the shashdara, and the shashdara is mate, mate.

sections.title_fa: ‏شكايت كردن اهل زندان پيش وكيل قاضى از دست آن مفلس
sections.title_en: How the prisoners laid a complaint of the insolvent’s high handedness
before the agent of the Cadi.

entries.farsi: با وكيل قاضى ادراك‏مند / اهل زندان در شكايت آمدند
entries.english: The prisoners came to complain to the Cadi’s agent, possessed of discernment,

entries.farsi: كه سالم ما به قاضى بر كنون / ‏باز گو آزار ما زين مرد دون
entries.english: Saving, “Take now our salutations to the Cadi and relate the sufferings inflicted on us by this vile man;”

entries.farsi: كاندر اين زندان بماند او مستمر / ياوه تاز و طبل‏خوار است و مضر
entries.english: For he has remained in this prison continuously, and he is an idle gad-about, a sycophant, and a nuisance

entries.farsi: چون مگس حاضر شود در هر طعام / ‏از وقاحت بى‏صال و بى‏سالم
entries.english: Like a fly, he impudently appears at every meal without invitation and without salaam.

entries.farsi: پيش او هيچ است لوت شصت كس / ‏كر كند خود را اگر گوييش بس
entries.english: To him the food of sixty persons is nothing; he feigns himself deaf if you say to him, ‘Enough!’

entries.farsi: مرد زندان را نيايد لقمه‏اى / ‏ور به صد حيلت گشايد طعمه‏اى
entries.english: No morsel reaches the man in prison, or if by means of a hundred contrivances he discover some food,

entries.farsi: در زمان پيش آيد آن دوزخ گلو / حجتش اين كه خدا گفتا كلوا
entries.english: That hell-throat at once comes forward this his argument, that God has said, Eat.

entries.farsi: زين چنين قحط سه ساله داد داد / ظل موالنا ابد پاينده باد
entries.english: Justice, justice against such a three years’ famine! May the shadow of our lord endure for ever!

entries.farsi: يا ز زندان تا رود اين گاوميش / ‏يا وظيفه كن ز وقفى لقمه‏ايش
entries.english: Either let this buffalo go from prison, or make him a regular allowance of food from a trust-fund.

entries.farsi: اى ز تو خوش هم ذكور و هم اناث / ‏داد كن المستغاث المستغاث
entries.english: O you, by whom both males and females are happy, do justice! Your help is invoked and sought.”

entries.farsi: سوى قاضى شد وكيل با نمك / ‏گفت با قاضى شكايت يك به يك
entries.english: The courteous agent went to the Cadi and related the complaint to him point by point.

entries.farsi: خواند او را قاضى از زندان به پيش / ‏پس تفحص كرد از اعيان خويش
entries.english: The Cadi called him from the prison into his presence, and inquired from his own officers.

entries.farsi: گشت ثابت پيش قاضى آن همه / ‏كه نمودند از شكايت آن رمه
entries.english: All the complaints which that flock had set forth were proved to the Cadi.

entries.farsi: گفت قاضى خيز از اين زندان برو / سوى خانه‏ى مرده‏ريگ خويش شو
entries.english: The Cadi said, “ Get up and depart from this prison: go to the house which is your inherited property.”

entries.farsi: گفت خان و مان من احسان تست / ‏همچو كافر جنتم زندان تست
entries.english: He replied, “My house and home consist in thy beneficence; as an infidel, thy prison is my Paradise.

entries.farsi: گر ز زندانم برانى تو به رد / خود بميرم من ز تقصيرى و كد
entries.english: If you will drive me from the prison and turn me out, truly I shall die of destitution and beggary.”

entries.farsi: همچو ابليسى كه مى‏گفت اى سالم / ‏رب أنظرني إلى يوم القيام
entries.english: Like the Devil, who was saying, “O Preserver, O my Lord, grant me a respite till the day of Resurrection;

entries.farsi: كاندر اين زندان دنيا من خوشم / ‏تا كه دشمن زادگان را مى‏كشم
entries.english: For I am happy in the prison of this world, in order that I may be slaying the children of mine enemy,

entries.farsi: هر كه او را قوت ايمانى بود / و ز براى زاد ره نانى بود
entries.english: If any one have some food of faith and a single loaf as provision for the journey,

entries.farsi: مى‏ستانم گه به مكر و گه به ريو / تا بر آرند از پشيمانى غريو
entries.english: I may seize it, now by plot and now by guile, so that in repentance they may raise an outcry;

entries.farsi: گه به درويشى كنم تهديدشان / ‏گه به زلف و خال بندم ديدشان
entries.english: Sometimes I may threaten them with poverty, sometimes bind their eyes with tress and mole.”

entries.farsi: قوت ايمانى در اين زندان كم است / ‏وان كه هست از قصد اين سگ در خم است
entries.english: In this prison the food of faith is scarce, and that which exists is in the noose through the attack of this cur.

entries.farsi: از نماز و صوم و صد بى‏چارگى / ‏قوت ذوق آيد برد يك بارگى
entries.english: From prayer and fasting and a hundred helplessnesses the food of spiritual feeling comes, he at once carries it off.

entries.farsi: أستعيذ اللهَّ من شيطانه / ‏قد هلكنا آه من طغيانه
entries.english: I seek refuge with God from His Satan: we have perished, alas, through his overweening disobedience.

entries.farsi: يك سگ است و در هزاران مى‏رود / هر كه در وى رفت او او مى‏شود
entries.english: He is one cur, and he goes into thousands: into whomsoever he goes, he becomes he.

entries.farsi: هر كه سردت كرد مى‏دان كاو در اوست / ‏ديو پنهان گشته اندر زير پوست
entries.english: Whoever makes you cold know that he is in him: the Devil has become hidden beneath his skin.

entries.farsi: چون نيابد صورت آيد در خيال / ‏تا كشاند آن خيالت در وبال
entries.english: When he finds no form, he comes into imagination, in order that that whim may lead you into woe:

entries.farsi: گه خيال فرجه و گاهى دكان / ‏گه خيال علم و گاهى خان و مان
entries.english: Now the imagination of recreation, now of the shop; now the imagination of knowledge, and now of house and home

entries.farsi: هان بگو الحولها اندر زمان / ‏از زبان تنها نه بلك از عين جان
entries.english: Beware! Say at once “God help me!” again and again’, not with tongue alone but from your very soul.

entries.farsi: گفت قاضى مفلسى را وانما / گفت اينك اهل زندانت گوا
entries.english: The Cadi said, “Show plainly that you are insolvent.” “Here are the prisoners,” he replied, “as your witnesses.”

entries.farsi: گفت ايشان متهم باشند چون / ‏مى‏گريزند از تو مى‏گريند خون
entries.english: “They,” said the Cadi, “are suspect, because they are fleeing from you and weeping blood;

entries.farsi: از تو مى‏خواهند هم تا وارهند / زين غرض باطل گواهى مى‏دهند
entries.english: Also, they are suing to be delivered from you: by reason of this self-interest the testimony they give is worthless.”

entries.farsi: جمله اهل محكمه گفتند ما / هم بر ادبار و بر افالسش گوا
entries.english: All the people belonging to the court of justice said, “We bear witness both to his insolvency and to his degeneracy.”

entries.farsi: هر كه را پرسيد قاضى حال او / گفت موال دست ازين مفلس بشو
entries.english: Every one whom the Cadi questioned about his condition said, “My lord, wash your hands of this insolvent.”

entries.farsi: گفت قاضى كش بگردانيد فاش / ‏گرد شهر اين مفلس است و بس قالش
entries.english: The Cadi said, “March him round the city for all to see, ‘this man is an insolvent and a great rogue.’

entries.farsi: كو به كو او را مناداها زنيد / طبل افالسش عيان هر جا زنيد
entries.english: Make proclamations concerning him, street by street; beat the drum of his insolvency everywhere in open view.

entries.farsi: هيچ كس نسيه بنفروشد بدو / قرض ندهد هيچ كس او را تسو
entries.english: Let no one sell to him on credit; let no one lend him a farthing.

entries.farsi: هر كه دعوى آردش اينجا به فن / ‏بيش زندانش نخواهم كرد من
entries.english: Whosoever may bring here a claim against him for fraud, I will not put him in prison any more.

entries.farsi: پيش من افالس او ثابت شده است / نقد و كالا نيستش چيزى به دست
entries.english: His insolvency has been proven to me: he has nothing in his possession, neither money nor goods.”

entries.farsi: آدمى در حبس دنيا ز آن بود / تا بود كافالس او ثابت شود
entries.english: Man is in the prison of this world in order that peradventure his insolvency may be proven.

entries.farsi: مفلسى ديو را يزدان ما / هم منادى كرد در قرآن ما
entries.english: Our God has also proclaimed in our Qur’an the insolvency of Iblis,

entries.farsi: كاو دغا و مفلس است و بد سخن / ‏هيچ با او شركت و سودا مكن
entries.english: Saying, “He is a swindler and insolvent and liar: do not make any partnership or game with him.”

entries.farsi: ور كنى او را بهانه آورى / ‏مفلس است او صرفه از وى كى برى
entries.english: And if you do so bring pretexts to him, he is insolvent: how will you get profit from him?

entries.farsi: حاضر آوردند چون فتنه فروخت / ‏اشتر كردى كه هيزم مى‏فروخت
entries.english: When the trouble started, they brought on the scene the camel of a Kurd who sold firewood.

entries.farsi: كرد بى‏چاره بسى فرياد كرد / هم موكل را به دانگى شاد كرد
entries.english: The helpless Kurd made a great outcry; he also gladdened the officer with a penny;

entries.farsi: اشترش بردند از هنگام چاشت / ‏تا شب و افغان او سودى نداشت
entries.english: They took away his camel from the time of forenoon until nightfall, and his lamentation was of no use.

entries.farsi: بر شتر بنشست آن قحط گران / ‏صاحب اشتر پى اشتر دوان
entries.english: Upon the camel sat that sore famine, while the owner of the camel was running at its heels.

entries.farsi: سو به سو و كو به كو مى‏تاختند / تا همه شهرش عيان بشناختند
entries.english: They sped from quarter to quarter and from street to street, till the whole town knew him by sight.

entries.farsi: پيش هر حمام و هر بازارگاه / ‏كرده مردم جمله در شكلش نگاه
entries.english: Before every bath and market-place all the people gazed on his figure.

entries.farsi: ده منادى گر بلند آوازيان / ‏كرد و ترك و روميان و تازيان
entries.english: Ten loud-voiced criers, Turks and Kurds and Anatolians and Arabs,

entries.farsi: مفلس است اين و ندارد هيچ چيز / قرض تا ندهد كس او را يك پشيز
entries.english: “This man is insolvent and has nothing: let no one lend him a single dollar;

entries.farsi: ظاهر و باطن ندارد حبه‏اى / ‏مفلسى قلبى دغايى دبه‏اى
entries.english: He does not possess a single mite, patent or latent: he is bankrupt, a piece of falsehood, a cunning knave, an oil-bag

entries.farsi: هان و هان با او حريفى كم كنيد / چون كه كاو آرد گره محكم كنيد
entries.english: Beware and beware! Have no dealings with him; when he brings the ox, make fast the knot.

entries.farsi: ور به حكم آريد اين پژمرده را / من نخواهم كرد زندان مرده را
entries.english: And if you bring this decayed fellow to judgment, I will not put a corpse in prison.

entries.farsi: خوش دم است او و گلويش بس فراخ / ‏با شعار نو دثار شاخ شاخ
entries.english: He is fair-spoken and his throat is very wide; with a new inner garment and a tattered outer garment.

entries.farsi: گر بپوشد بهر مكر آن جامه را / عاريه است او و فريبد عامه را
entries.english: If he puts on that garment for the purpose of deceiving, it is borrowed in order that he may beguile the common folk.”

entries.farsi: حرف حكمت بر زبان ناحكيم / ‏حله‏هاى عاريت دان اى سليم
entries.english: Know, O simple man, that words of wisdom on the tongue of the unwise are borrowed robes.

entries.farsi: گر چه دزدى حله‏اى پوشيده است / ‏دست تو چون گيرد آن ببريده دست
entries.english: Although a thief has put on a robe, how should he whose hand is cut off take your hand?

entries.farsi: چون شبانه از شتر آمد به زير / كرد گفتش منزلم دور است و دير
entries.english: When at nightfall he came down from the camel, the Kurd said to him, “My abode is far and a long way off.

entries.farsi: بر نشستى اشترم را از پگاه / ‏جو رها كردم كم از اخراج كاه
entries.english: You have ridden on my camel since early morning: I let the barley go, less than the cost of straw.”

entries.farsi: گفت تا اكنون چه مى‏كرديم پس / ‏هوش تو كو، نيست اندر خانه كس
entries.english: “What, then,” he rejoined, “were we doing “? Where are your wits? Is nobody at home?

entries.farsi: طبل افالسم به چرخ سابعه / ‏رفت و تو نشنيده‏اى بد واقعه
entries.english: The drum of my insolvency reached the Seventh Heaven, and you have not heard the bad news!

entries.farsi: گوش تو پر بوده است از طمع خام / پس طمع كر مى‏كند كور اى غالم
entries.english: Your ear has been filled with foolish hope; hope, then, makes deaf blind, my lad.”

entries.farsi: تا كلوخ و سنگ بشنيد اين بيان / ‏مفلس است و مفلس است اين قلتبان
entries.english: Even clods and stones heard this advertisement— “he is insolvent, he is insolvent, this scoundrel.”

entries.farsi: تا به شب گفتند و در صاحب شتر / بر نزد كاو از طمع پر بود پر
entries.english: They said it till nightfall, and it made no impression on the owner of the camel, because he was full of hope, full.

entries.farsi: هست بر سمع و بصر مهر خدا / در حجب بس صورت است و بس صدا
entries.english: God’s seal lies upon the hearing and sight: within the veils are many forms and sounds.

entries.farsi: آن چه او خواهد رساند آن به چشم / ‏از جمال و از كمال و از كرشم
entries.english: He communicates to the eye what He wills of beauty and of perfection and of amorous looks;

entries.farsi: و انچه او خواهد رساند آن به گوش / ‏از سماع و از بشارت وز خروش
entries.english: And He communicates to the ear what He wills of music and glad tidings and cries.

entries.farsi: كون پر چاره ست و هيچت چاره نى / ‏تا كه نگشايد خدايت روزنى
entries.english: The world is full of remedies, but you have no remedy till God opens a window for you

entries.farsi: گر چه تو هستى كنون غافل از آن / ‏وقت حاجت حق كند آن را عيان
entries.english: Though you are unaware of that just now, God will make it plain in the hour of need.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه يزدان مجيد / از پى هر درد درمان آفريد
entries.english: The Prophet said that the glorious God has created a remedy for every pain;

entries.farsi: ليك ز آن درمان نبينى رنگ و بو / بهر درد خويش بى‏فرمان او
entries.english: But of that remedy for your pain you will not see the colour or scent without His command.

entries.farsi: چشم را اى چاره جو در المكان / ‏هين بنه چون چشم كشته سوى جان
entries.english: Come, O you that seek the remedy, set your eye on non-spatiality, as the eye of one killed towards the spirit.

entries.farsi: اين جهان از بى‏جهت پيدا شده ست / ‏كه ز بى‏جايى جهان را جا شده ست
entries.english: This world has been produced from that which is without spatial relations, for the world has received place from placelessness.

entries.farsi: باز گرد از هست سوى نيستى / ‏طالب ربى و ربانيستى
entries.english: Turn back from existence towards non-existence, you seek the Lord and belong to the Lord.

entries.farsi: جاى دخل است اين عدم از وى مرم / ‏جاى خرج است اين وجود بيش و كم
entries.english: This non-existence is the place of income: do not flee from it; this existence of more and less is the place of expenditure.

entries.farsi: كارگاه صنع حق چون نيستى است / ‏پس برون كارگه بى‏قيمتى است
entries.english: Since God’s workshop is non-existence, outside of the workshop there is worthlessness.

entries.farsi: ياد ده ما را سخنهاى دقيق / ‏كه ترا رحم آورد آن اى رفيق
entries.english: Put into our heart subtle words which may move You to mercy, O Gracious One!

entries.farsi: هم دعا از تو اجابت هم ز تو / ايمنى از تو مهابت هم ز تو
entries.english: From You both the prayer and the answer; from You safety, from You also dread.

entries.farsi: گر خطا گفتيم اصالحش تو كن / ‏مصلحى تو اى تو سلطان سخن
entries.english: If we have spoken faultily, correct it: You are the Corrector, O You Sultan of speech.

entries.farsi: كيميا دارى كه تبديلش كنى / ‏گر چه جوى خون بود نيلش كنى
entries.english: You the alchemy whereby You may transmute it, and though it be a river of blood, may make it a Nile.

entries.farsi: اين چنين ميناگريها كار تست / ‏اين چنين اكسيرها اسرار تست
entries.english: Such alchemical operations are Thy work, such elixirs are Your secrets.

entries.farsi: آب را و خاك را بر هم زدى / ‏ز آب و گل نقش تن آدم زدى
entries.english: You did beat water and earth together: from water and clay You did mould the body of Adam.

entries.farsi: نسبتش دادى و جفت و خال و عم / ‏با هزار انديشه و شادى و غم
entries.english: You gave him lineage and wife and uncles, maternal and paternal, with a thousand thoughts and joys and griefs.

entries.farsi: باز بعضى را رهايى داده‏اى / ‏زين غم و شادى جدايى داده‏اى
entries.english: Again, to some You have given deliverance: You have parted them from this grief and joy;

entries.farsi: برده‏اى از خويش و پيوند و سرشت / ‏كرده‏اى در چشم او هر خوب زشت
entries.english: You have borne them away from kindred and relatives and nature, You have made every fair thing foul in his eyes.

entries.farsi: هر چه محسوس است او رد مى‏كند / و انچه ناپيداست مسند مى‏كند
entries.english: He spurns all that is perceived by the senses, and leans for support on that which is invisible.

entries.farsi: عشق او پيدا و معشوقش نهان / يار بيرون فتنه‏ى او در جهان
entries.english: His love is manifest and his Beloved is hidden: the Friend is outside; His fascination is in the world.

entries.farsi: اين رها كن عشقهاى صورتى / ‏نيست بر صورت نه بر روى ستى
entries.english: Give up this. Loves for what is endued with form have not as their object the form or the lady’s face.

entries.farsi: آن چه بر صورت تو عاشق گشته‏اى / ‏چون برون شد جان چرايش هشته‏اى
entries.english: That which is the object of love is not the form, whether it be love for this world or yonder world.

entries.farsi: آن چه معشوق است صورت نيست آن / ‏خواه عشق اين جهان خواه آن جهان
entries.english: That which you have come to love for its form— why have you abandoned it after the spirit has fled?

entries.farsi: صورتش بر جاست اين سيرى ز چيست / ‏عاشقا واجو كه معشوق تو كيست
entries.english: Its form is still there: whence this satiety? O lover, inquire who your beloved is.

entries.farsi: آن چه محسوس است اگر معشوقه است / ‏عاشق استى هر كه او را حس هست
entries.english: If the beloved is that which the senses perceive, every one that has senses would be in love.

entries.farsi: چون وفا آن عشق افزون مى‏كند / كى وفا صورت دگرگون مى‏كند
entries.english: Inasmuch as constancy is increased by that love, how is constancy altered by the form?

entries.farsi: پرتو خورشيد بر ديوار تافت / ‏تابش عاريتى ديوار يافت
entries.english: The sunbeam shone upon the wall: the wall received a borrowed splendour.

entries.farsi: اى كه تو هم عاشقى بر عقل خويش / ‏خويش بر صورت پرستان ديده بيش
entries.english: Why set your heart on a piece of turf, O simple man? Seek out the source which shines perpetually.

entries.farsi: بر كلوخى دل چه بندى اى سليم / ‏واطلب اصلى كه تابد او مقيم
entries.english: You who are in love with your intellect, deeming yourself superior to worshippers of form,

entries.farsi: پرتو عقل است آن بر حس تو / عاريت ميدان ذهب بر مس تو
entries.english: That is a beam of Intellect on your sense-perception; regard it as borrowed gold on your copper.

entries.farsi: چون زر اندود است خوبى در بشر / ور نه چون شد شاهد تو پير خر
entries.english: Beauty in humankind is like gilding; else, how did your sweetheart become an old donkey?

entries.farsi: اندك اندك مى‏ستانند آن جمال / ‏اندك اندك خشك مى‏گردد نهال
entries.english: She was like an angel, she became like a demon; for that loveliness in her was a borrowed thing.

entries.farsi: چون فرشته بود همچون ديو شد / كان مالحت اندر او عاريه بد
entries.english: Little by little He takes away that beauty: little by little the sapling withers.

entries.farsi: رو نُعَمِّرْهُ نُنَكِّسْ هُ بخوان / ‏دل طلب كن دل منه بر استخوان
entries.english: Go; recite to whom so We grant length of days, him We cause to decline. Seek the heart, set not thy heart on bones;

entries.farsi: كان جمال دل جمال باقى است / ‏دولتش از آب حيوان ساقى است
entries.english: For that beauty of the heart is the lasting beauty: its lips give to drink of the Water of Life.

entries.farsi: خود هم او آب است و هم ساقى و مست / ‏هر سه يك شد چون طلسم تو شكست
entries.english: Truly it is both the water and the giver of drink and the drunken: all three become one when your talisman is shattered.

entries.farsi: آن يكى را تو ندانى از قياس / ‏بندگى كن ژاژ كم خا ناشناس
entries.english: That oneness you cannot know by reasoning. Do service and refrain from foolish gabble, O undiscerning man!

entries.farsi: معنى تو صورت است و عاريت / ‏بر مناسب شادى و بر قافيت
entries.english: Your reality is the form and that which is borrowed: you rejoice in what is relative and rhyme.

entries.farsi: معنى آن باشد كه بستاند ترا / بى‏نياز از نقش گرداند ترا
entries.english: Reality is that which seizes you and makes you independent of form.

entries.farsi: معنى آن نبود كه كور و كر كند / مرد را بر نقش عاشق‏تر كند
entries.english: Reality is not that which makes blind and deaf and causes a man to be more in love with form.

entries.farsi: كور را قسمت خيال غم فزاست / ‏بهره‏ى چشم اين خيالات فناست
entries.english: The portion of the blind is the fancy that increases pain; the share of the eye is these fancies of dying to self.

entries.farsi: حرف قرآن را ضريران معدن‏اند / خر نبينند و به پالان بر زنند
entries.english: The blind are a mine of the letter of the Qur’an: they do not see the donkey, and cling to the pack-saddle.

entries.farsi: چون تو بينايى پى خر رو كه جست / ‏چند پالان دوزى اى پالان پرست
entries.english: Since you have sight, go after the donkey which has jumped: how long stitching the saddle, O saddle-worshipper?

entries.farsi: خر چو هست آيد يقين پالان ترا / كم نگردد نان چو باشد جان ترا
entries.english: When the donkey is there, the saddle will certainly be yours: bread does not fail when you have the spirit.

entries.farsi: پشت خر دكان و مال و مكسب است / ‏در قلبت مايه‏ى صد قالب است
entries.english: The back of the donkey is shop and wealth and gain ; the pearl of your heart is the stock for a hundred bodies.

entries.farsi: خر برهنه بر نشين اى بو الفضول / ‏خر برهنه نه كه راكب شد رسول
entries.english: Mount the donkey bare-backed, O busybody: did not the Prophet ride the donkey bare-backed?

entries.farsi: النَّبىّ قد ركب معروريا / و النَّبىّ قيل سافر ماشيا
entries.english: The Prophet rode bare-backed; and the Prophet, it is said, journeyed on foot.

entries.farsi: بار صبر و شكر او را بردنى است / ‏خواه در صد سال و خواهى سى و بيست
entries.english: The donkey, your fleshly soul, has gone off; tie it to a peg. How long will it run away from work and burden, how long?

entries.farsi: شد خر نفس تو بر ميخيش بند / چند بگريزد ز كار و بار چند
entries.english: It must bear the burden of patience and thanksgiving, whether for a hundred years or for thirty or twenty.

entries.farsi: هيچ وازر وزر غيرى بر نداشت / هيچ كس ندرود تا چيزى نكاشت
entries.english: None that is laden supported another’s load; none reaped until he sowed something.

entries.farsi: طمع خام است آن مخور خام اى پسر / خام خوردن علت آرد در بشر
entries.english: It is a raw hope; eat not what is raw, O son: eating brings illness to men.

entries.farsi: كان فالنى يافت گنجى ناگهان / ‏من همان خواهم نه كار و نه دكان
entries.english: “So-and-so suddenly found a treasure; I would like the same: neither work nor shop!”

entries.farsi: كار بخت است آن و آن هم نادر است / ‏كسب بايد كرد تا تن قادر است
entries.english: That is Fortune’s doing, and moreover it is rare: one must earn a living so long as the body is able.

entries.farsi: كسب كردن گنج را مانع كى است / ‏پا مكش از كار آن خود در پى است
entries.english: How does earning a livelihood prevent the treasure? Do not retire from work: that, indeed, is behind

entries.farsi: تا نگردى تو گرفتار اگر / كه اگر اين كردمى يا آن دگر
entries.english: See that you are not made captive by “if,” saying, “If I had done this or the other,”

entries.farsi: كز اگر گفتن رسول با وفاق / ‏منع كرد و گفت آن هست از نفاق
entries.english: For the sincere Prophet forbade saying “if,” and said, “That is from hypocrisy”;

entries.farsi: كان منافق در اگر گفتن بمرد / وز اگر گفتن بجز حسرت نبرد
entries.english: For the hypocrite died in saying “if,” and from saying “if ” he won nothing but remorse.

sections.title_fa: ‏مثل
sections.title_en: Parable

entries.farsi: آن غريبى خانه مى‏جست از شتاب / ‏دوستى بردش سوى خانه‏ى خراب
entries.english: A certain stranger was hastily seeking a house: a friend took him to a house in ruins.

entries.farsi: گفت او اين را اگر سقفى بدى / ‏پهلوى من مر ترا مسكن شدى
entries.english: He said, “If this had a roof, it would be a home for you beside me.

entries.farsi: هم عيال تو بياسودى اگر / در ميانه داشتى حجره‏ى دگر
entries.english: Your family too would be comfortable, if it had another room in it.”

entries.farsi: گفت آرى پهلوى ياران خوش است / ‏ليك اى جان در اگر نتوان نشست
entries.english: “Yes,” said he, “it is nice beside friends, but my dear soul, one cannot lodge in ‘if ’.”

entries.farsi: اين همه عالم طلب‏كار خوشند / وز خوش تزوير اندر آتشند
entries.english: The entire world is seekers of happiness, and on account of a false happiness they are in the fire.

entries.farsi: طالب زر گشته جمله پير و خام / ‏ليك قلب از زر نداند چشم عام
entries.english: Old and young have become gold-seekers, but the common eye does not distinguish alloy from gold.

entries.farsi: پرتوى بر قلب زد خالص ببين / ‏بى‏محك زر را مكن از ظن گزين
entries.english: The pure shot a beam on the alloy: see that you choose not the gold on the ground of opinion, without a touchstone.

entries.farsi: گر محك دارى گزين كن ور نه رو / نزد دانا خويشتن را كن گرو
entries.english: If you have a touchstone, choose; otherwise, go, devote yourself to him that knows.

entries.farsi: يا محك بايد ميان جان خويش / ‏ور ندانى ره مرو تنها تو پيش
entries.english: Either you must have a touchstone within your own soul, or if you know not the Way, do not go forward alone.

entries.farsi: بانگ غوالن هست بانگ آشنا / آشنايى كه كشد سوى فنا
entries.english: The cry of the ghouls is the cry of an acquaintance— an acquaintance who would lure you to perdition.

entries.farsi: بانگ مى‏دارد كه هان اى كاروان / ‏سوى من آييد نك راه و نشان
entries.english: She keeps on crying, “Listen, O caravan people! Come towards me, here is the track and the landmarks.”

entries.farsi: نام هر يك مى‏برد غول اى فالن / ‏تا كند آن خواجه را از آفالن
entries.english: The ghoul mentions the name of each, saying “O so-and-so,” in order that she may make that personage one of those who sinks.

entries.farsi: چون رسد آن جا ببيند گرگ و شير / عمر ضايع راه دور و روز دير
entries.english: When he reaches the spot, he sees wolves and lions, his life lost, the road far off, and the day late.

entries.farsi: چون بود آن بانگ غول آخر بگو / مال خواهم جاه خواهم و آبرو
entries.english: Say, what is the ghoul’s cry like? “I desire riches, I desire position and renown.’

entries.farsi: از درون خويش اين آوازها / منع كن تا كشف گردد رازها
entries.english: Prevent these voices from your heart, so that mysteries may be revealed.

entries.farsi: ذكر حق كن بانگ غوالن را بسوز / چشم نرگس را از اين كركس بدوز
entries.english: Repeat the name of God, drown the cry of the ghouls, and close your narcissus-eye to this vulture.

entries.farsi: صبح كاذب را ز صادق واشناس / رنگ مى را باز دان از رنگ كاس
entries.english: Know the difference between the false dawn and the true; distinguish the colour of the wine from the colour of the cup,

entries.farsi: تا بود كز ديده‏گان هفت رنگ / ‏ديده‏اى پيدا كند صبر و درنگ
entries.english: That, perchance, from the eyes which see the seven colours patience and waiting may produce a eye,

entries.farsi: رنگها بينى بجز اين رنگها / گوهران بينى به جاى سنگها
entries.english: You may behold colours other than these, and may behold pearls instead of stones.

entries.farsi: گوهر چه بلكه دريايى شوى / ‏آفتاب چرخ پيمايى شوى
entries.english: What pearl? No, you will become an ocean; you will become a sun traversing the sky.

entries.farsi: كار كن در كارگه باشد نهان / تو برو در كارگه بينش عيان
entries.english: The Worker is hidden in the workshop: go you and in the workshop see Him plain.

entries.farsi: كار چون بر كار كن پرده تنيد / خارج آن كار نتوانيش ديد
entries.english: Inasmuch as the work has woven a veil over the Worker, you cannot see Him outside of that work.

entries.farsi: كارگه چون جاى باش عامل است / آن كه بيرون است از وى غافل است
entries.english: Since the workshop is the dwelling-place of the Worker, he that is outside is unaware of Him.

entries.farsi: پس در آ در كارگه يعنى عدم / ‏تا ببينى صنع و صانع را بهم
entries.english: Come, then, into the workshop, which means non-existence, that you may see the work and the Worker together.

entries.farsi: كارگه چون جاى روشن ديده‏گى است / ‏پس برون كارگه پوشيدگى است
entries.english: As the workshop is the place of clairvoyance, then outside of the workshop there is blindness.

entries.farsi: رو به هستى داشت فرعون عنود / الجرم از كارگاهش كور بود
entries.english: The rebellious Pharaoh kept his face towards existence; consequently he was blind to His workshop.

entries.farsi: الجرم مى‏خواست تبديل قدر / تا قضا را باز گرداند ز در
entries.english: Consequently he was wishing to alter the pre- destination, that he might turn back the destiny from his door.

entries.farsi: خود قضا بر سبلت آن حيله‏مند / زير لب مى‏كرد هر دم ريش‏خند
entries.english: Truly the destiny every moment was laughing derisively under its lip at the moustache of that cunning plotter.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران طفل كشت او بى‏گناه / ‏تا بگردد حكم و تقدير اله
entries.english: He killed hundreds of thousands of innocent babes, in order that the ordainment and predestination of God might be averted.

entries.farsi: تا كه موساى نبى نايد برون / ‏كرد در گردن هزاران ظلم و خون
entries.english: In order that the prophet Moses might not come forth, he laid on his neck thousands of iniquities and murders.

entries.farsi: آن همه خون كرد و موسى زاده شد / و ز براى قهر او آماده شد
entries.english: He caused all that bloodshed, and Moses was born and was made ready for his chastisement

entries.farsi: گر بديدى كارگاه ال يزال / ‏دست و پايش خشك گشتى ز احتيال
entries.english: Had he seen the workshop of the Everlasting, he would have ceased to move hand or foot in plotting.

entries.farsi: اندرون خانه‏اش موسى معاف / ‏و ز برون مى‏كشت طفالن را گزاف
entries.english: Moses safe within his house, while outside he was killing the infants in vain,

entries.farsi: همچو صاحب نفس كاو تن پرورد / بر دگر كس ظن حقدى مى‏برد
entries.english: Even as the sensual man who pampers his body and suspects some one else of a bitter hatred,

entries.farsi: كاين عدو و آن حسود و دشمن است / ‏خود حسود و دشمن او آن تن است
entries.english: Saying, “ This one is a foe, and that one is envious and an enemy,” in truth his envier and enemy is that body.

entries.farsi: او چو موسى و تنش فرعون او / او به بيرون مى‏دود كه كو عدو
entries.english: He is like Pharaoh, and his body is his Moses: he keeps running outside, asking, “Where is my enemy?”

entries.farsi: نفسش اندر خانه‏ى تن نازنين / ‏بر دگر كس دست مى‏خايد به كين
entries.english: His fleshly soul luxuriating in the house, which is his body, he gnaws his hand in anger against some one else.

sections.title_fa: ‏مالمت كردن مردم شخصى را كه مادرش را كشت به تهمت
sections.title_en: How men blamed a person who killed his mother because he suspected her.

entries.farsi: آن يكى از خشم مادر را بكشت / ‏هم به زخم خنجر و هم زخم مشت
entries.english: A certain man killed his mother in wrath, with blows of a dagger and also with blows of his fist.

entries.farsi: آن يكى گفتش كه از بد گوهرى / ‏ياد ناوردى تو حق مادرى
entries.english: Some one said to him, “From evil nature you have not borne in mind what is due to motherhood.

entries.farsi: هى تو مادر را چرا كشتى بگو / او چه كرد آخر بگو اى زشت خو
entries.english: Hey, tell why you killed your mother. What did she do? Pray, tell, O foul villain!”

entries.farsi: گفت كارى كرد كان عار وى است / ‏كشتمش كان خاك ستار وى است
entries.english: He said, “She disgraced herself; I killed her because that earth covers her.”

entries.farsi: گفت آن كس را بكش اى محتشم / گفت پس هر روز مردى را كشم
entries.english: The other said, “O honoured sir, kill that one.” “Then,” he replied, “I should kill a man every day

entries.farsi: كشتم او را رستم از خونهاى خلق / ‏ناى او برم به است از ناى خلق
entries.english: I killed her, I was saved from shedding the blood of a multitude: it is better that I cut her throat than the throats of people.”

entries.farsi: نفس تست آن مادر بد خاصيت / ‏كه فساد اوست در هر ناحيت
entries.english: That mother of bad character, whose wickedness is in every quarter, is your nafs.

entries.farsi: هين بكش او را كه بهر آن دنى / ‏هر دمى قصد عزيزى مى‏كنى
entries.english: Come; kill it, for on account of that vile you are every moment assailing one who is venerable.

entries.farsi: از وى اين دنياى خوش بر تست تنگ / ‏از پى او با حق و با خلق جنگ
entries.english: Through it this fair world is narrow to you, for its sake war with God and man.

entries.farsi: نفس كشتى باز رستى ز اعتذار / كس ترا دشمن نماند در ديار
entries.english: You have killed the nafs; you are delivered from excusing yourself: nobody in the world remains your enemy.

entries.farsi: گر شكال آرد كسى بر گفت ما / از براى انبيا و اوليا
entries.english: If any one should raise a difficulty about my words in regard to the prophets and saints,

entries.farsi: كانبيا را نه كه نفس كشته بود / پس چراشان دشمنان بود و حسود
entries.english: “Had not the prophets killed the nafs? Why, then, had they enemies and enviers? “

entries.farsi: گوش كن تو اى طلب‏كار صواب / ‏بشنو اين اشكال و شبهت را جواب
entries.english: Give ear, O seeker of truth, and hear the answer to this difficulty of doubt.

entries.farsi: دشمن خود بوده‏اند آن منكران / ‏زخم بر خود مى‏زدند ايشان چنان
entries.english: Those unbelievers were enemies to themselves: they were striking at themselves such blows

entries.farsi: دشمن آن باشد كه قصد جان كند / دشمن آن نبود كه خود جان مى‏كند
entries.english: An enemy is one who attempts life; he that is himself destroying his own life is not an enemy.

entries.farsi: نيست خفاشك عدوى آفتاب / ‏او عدوى خويش آمد در حجاب
entries.english: The little bat is not an enemy to the sun: it is an enemy to itself in the veil.

entries.farsi: تابش خورشيد او را مى‏كشد / رنج او خورشيد هرگز كى كشد
entries.english: The glow of the sun kills it; how should the sun ever suffer annoyance from it?

entries.farsi: دشمن آن باشد كز او آيد عذاب / ‏مانع آيد لعل را از آفتاب
entries.english: An enemy is one from whom torment proceeds, hinders the ruby from the sun.

entries.farsi: كى حجاب چشم آن فردند خلق / ‏چشم خود را كور و كژ كردند خلق
entries.english: All the infidels hinder themselves from the rays of the prophets’ jewel.

entries.farsi: مانع خويشند جمله‏ى كافران / ‏از شعاع جوهر پيغمبران
entries.english: How should people veil the eyes of that peerless one? The people have blinded and distorted their own eyes.

entries.farsi: چون غالم هندويى كاو كين كشد / از ستيزه‏ى خواجه خود را مى‏كشد
entries.english: Like the Indian slave who bears a grudge and kills himself to spite his master:

entries.farsi: سر نگون مى‏افتد از بام سرا / تا زيانى كرده باشد خواجه را
entries.english: He falls headlong from the roof of the house that he may have done some harm to his master.

entries.farsi: گر شود بيمار دشمن با طبيب / ‏ور كند كودك عداوت با اديب
entries.english: If the sick man become an enemy to the physician, or if the boy show hostility to the teacher,

entries.farsi: در حقيقت ره زن جان خودند / راه عقل و جان خود را خود زدند
entries.english: In truth they act as brigands against themselves: they themselves waylay their own mind and spirit.

entries.farsi: گازرى گر خشم گيرد ز آفتاب / ‏ماهيى گر خشم مى‏گيرد ز آب
entries.english: If a fuller take offence at the sun, if a fish is taking offence at the water,

entries.farsi: تو يكى بنگر كه را دارد زيان / ‏عاقبت كه بود سياه اختر از آن
entries.english: Just once consider whom that injures, and whose star is eclipsed by it in the end.

entries.farsi: گر ترا حق آفريند زشت رو / هان مشو هم زشت رو هم زشت خو
entries.english: If God create you with ugly features, take care lest you become both ugly-featured and ugly-natured;

entries.farsi: ور برد كفشت مرو در سنگالخ / ‏ور دو شاخ استت مشو تو چار شاخ
entries.english: And if your shoes are ripped, do not go into stony ground; and if you have two spikes, don’t become four-spiked.

entries.farsi: تو حسودى كز فالن من كمترم / ‏مى‏فزايد كمترى در اخترم
entries.english: You are envious, saying, “ I am inferior to so-and-so: he is increasing my inferiority in fortune.”

entries.farsi: خود حسد نقصان و عيبى ديگر است / ‏بلكه از جمله كميها بدتر است
entries.english: Indeed envy is another defect and fault; it is worse than all inferiorities.

entries.farsi: آن ابو جهل از محمد ننگ داشت / ‏وز حسد خود را به بالا مى‏فراشت
entries.english: That Devil, through the Shame and disgrace of inferiority, cast himself into a hundred damnations.

entries.farsi: آن بليس از ننگ و عار كمترى / ‏خويش را افكند در صد ابترى
entries.english: Because of envy, he wished to be at the top. At the top, forsooth! No, do not shed blood.

entries.farsi: از حسد مى‏خواست تا بالا بود / خود چه بالا بلكه خون‏پالا بود
entries.english: Abu Jahl was put to shame by Mohammed, and because of envy was raising himself to the top.

entries.farsi: بو الحكم نامش بد و بو جهل شد / اى بسا اهل از حسد نااهل شد
entries.english: His name was Abu ‘l-Hakam, and he became Abu Jahl: oh, many a worthy has become unworthy because of envy.

entries.farsi: من نديدم در جهان جست و جو / هيچ اهليت به از خوى نكو
entries.english: I have not seen in the world of search and seeking any worthiness better than a good disposition.

entries.farsi: انبيا را واسطه ز آن كرد حق / ‏تا پديد آيد حسدها در قلق
entries.english: God made the prophets the medium in order that feelings of envy should be displayed in the agitation.

entries.farsi: ز انكه كس را از خدا عارى نبود / حاسد حق هيچ ديارى نبود
entries.english: Inasmuch as no one was disgraced by God, no one was envious of God

entries.farsi: آن كسى كش مثل خود پنداشتى / ‏ز آن سبب با او حسد برداشتى
entries.english: The person whom he deemed like himself— he would bear envy against him for that reason

entries.farsi: چون مقرر شد بزرگى رسول / ‏پس حسد نايد كسى را از قبول
entries.english: As the grandeur of the Prophet has become established, none feels envy, since he is accepted;

entries.farsi: پس به هر دورى وليى قايم است / ‏تا قيامت آزمايش دايم است
entries.english: Therefore in every epoch a saint arises; the probation lasts until the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: هر كه را خوى نكو باشد برست / ‏هر كسى كاو شيشه دل باشد شكست
entries.english: Whoever has a good disposition is saved; whosoever is of frail heart is broken.

entries.farsi: پس امام حى قايم آن ولى است / ‏خواه از نسل عمر خواه از على است
entries.english: That saint, then, is the living Imam who arises, whether he be a descendant of ‘Umar or of ‘Ali.

entries.farsi: مهدى و هادى وى است اى راه جو / هم نهان و هم نشسته پيش رو
entries.english: He is the Mahdi and the Hadi, O seeker of the way: he is both hidden and seated before your face.

entries.farsi: او چو نور است و خرد جبريل اوست / ‏و آن ولى كم از او قنديل اوست
entries.english: He is as the Light, and Reason is his Gabriel; the saint that is lesser than he is his lamp.

entries.farsi: و انكه زين قنديل كم مشكات ماست / ‏نور را در مرتبه ترتيبهاست
entries.english: That who is lesser than this lamp is our lamp-niche: the Light has gradations in degree,

entries.farsi: ز انكه هفصد پرده دارد نور حق / ‏پرده‏هاى نور دان چندين طبق
entries.english: Because the Light of God has seven hundred veils: regard the veils of the Light as so many tiers.

entries.farsi: از پس هر پرده قومى را مقام / ‏صف صف‏اند اين پرده‏هاشان تا امام
entries.english: Behind each veil a certain class has its place of abode: these veils of theirs are, rank by rank, up to the Imam.

entries.farsi: اهل صف آخرين از ضعف خويش / ‏چشمشان طاقت ندارد نور بيش
entries.english: Those in the last rank, through their weakness, their eyes cannot endure the light in front;

entries.farsi: روشنيى كاو حيات اول است / ‏رنج جان و فتنه‏ى اين احول است
entries.english: And that front rank, from weakness of sight, cannot support the light that is more advanced.

entries.farsi: و آن صف پيش از ضعيفى بصر / تاب نارد روشنايى بيشتر
entries.english: The light that is the life of the first is heartache and tribulation to this squinter;

entries.farsi: احوليها اندك اندك كم شود / چون ز هفصد بگذرد او يم شود
entries.english: Being crossed eyed decreases, and when he passes beyond the seven hundred, he becomes the Sea.

entries.farsi: آتشى كاصالح آهن يا زر است / ‏كى صالح آبى و سيب تر است
entries.english: The fire that treats iron or gold well— how is it good for fresh quinces and apples?

entries.farsi: سيب و آبى خاميى دارد خفيف / ‏نه چو آهن تابشى خواهد لطيف
entries.english: The apple and quince have a slight crudity: unlike iron, they want a gentle heat;

entries.farsi: ليك آهن را لطيف آن شعله‏هاست / ‏كاو جذوب تابش آن اژدهاست
entries.english: But those flames are gentle for the iron, for it is drawing to the heat of that dragon.

entries.farsi: هست آن آهن فقير سخت كش / ‏زير پتك و آتش است او سرخ و خوش
entries.english: That iron is the dervish who bears hardship: under the hammer and the fire he is red and happy.

entries.farsi: بى‏حجاب آب و فرزندان آب / پختگى ز آتش نيابند و خطاب
entries.english: He is the fire’s chamberlain in immediate touch: he goes into the heart of the fire without link.

entries.farsi: ‏زير پتك و آتش است او سرخ و خوش / ‏در دل آتش رود بى‏رابطه
entries.english: Without some screen, water and water’s children get no cooking or conversation from the fire.

entries.farsi: واسطه ديگى بود يا تابه‏اى / ‏همچو پا را در روش پا تابه‏اى
entries.english: The medium is a pot or a pan— as for the foot in walking a sock

entries.farsi: يا مكانى در ميان تا آن هوا / مى‏شود سوزان و مى‏آرد بما
entries.english: Or a space between, so that the air becomes burning hot and brings to the water

entries.farsi: پس فقير آن است كاو بى‏واسطه ست / ‏شعله‏ها را با وجودش رابطه ست
