entries.english: When it makes chak chak, say you to it, “Death and woe,” in order that this hell, your fleshly soul, may become cold,

entries.farsi: تا نسوزد او گلستان تو را / تا نسوزد عدل و احسان تو را
entries.english: So that it may not burn your rose-garden; so that it may not burn your justice and well-doing

entries.farsi: باز پهنا مى‏رويم از راه راست / ‏باز گرد اى خواجه راه ما كجاست
entries.english: After that, anything that you sow will yield fruit; it will yield anemones and wild roses and thyme.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن چيزى كه كارى بردهد / الله و نسرين و سيسنبردهد
entries.english: Once more we are going wide of the straight way: turn back, O master—where is our way?

entries.farsi: اندر آن تقرير بوديم اى حسود / كه خرت لنگ است و منزل دور زود
entries.english: We were showing, O envious one, that your ass is lame and the place of alighting far off, quick.

entries.farsi: سال بى‏گه گشت وقت كشت نى / ‏جز سيه رويى و فعل زشت نى
entries.english: The year has turned late; it is not sowing-time, nothing except black shame’ and foul deeds

entries.farsi: كرم در بيخ درخت تن فتاد / بايدش بر كند و در آتش نهاد
entries.english: The worm has entered the root of the body’s tree: it must be dug up and put in the fire.

entries.farsi: هين و هين اى راه رو بى‏گاه شد / آفتاب عمر سوى چاه شد
entries.english: Hark and hark, O wayfarer! It is late; life’s sun is gone towards the pit.

entries.farsi: اين دو روزك را كه زورت هست زود / پر افشانى بكن از راه جود
entries.english: these two brief days when you have strength, quick, flap your wings generously.

entries.farsi: اين قدر تخمى كه مانده ستت بباز / تا برويد زين دو دم عمر دراز
entries.english: Devote this amount of seed that you have remaining, in order that long life may grow from these two moments.

entries.farsi: تا نمرده ست اين چراغ با گهر / هين فتيله‏اش ساز و روغن زودتر
entries.english: While this jewelled lamp is not extinguished, see you trim its wick and oil immediately.

entries.farsi: هين مگو فردا كه فرداها گذشت / ‏تا به كلى نگذرد ايام كشت
entries.english: Beware! Do not say “Tomorrow “—for tomorrows have passed. Let not the days of sowing pass away altogether.

entries.farsi: پند من بشنو كه تن بند قوى است / ‏كهنه بيرون كن گرت ميل نوى است
entries.english: Listen to my admonition that the body is a strong bond: put off the old, if you have desire for newness.

entries.farsi: لب ببند و كف پر زر بر گشا / بخل تن بگذار و پيش آور سخا
entries.english: Shut the lips and open the palm filled with gold: leave off being a miser with the body, exhibit munificence.

entries.farsi: ترك شهوتها و لذتها سخاست / ‏هر كه در شهوت فرو شد بر نخاست
entries.english: Generosity is the abandonment of lusts and pleasures; no one who is sunken in lust rises up.

entries.farsi: اين سخا شاخى است از سرو بهشت / ‏واى او كز كف چنين شاخى بهشت
entries.english: This generosity is a branch of the cypress of Paradise: woe to him that lets such a branch go from his hand!

entries.farsi: عروة الوثقى است اين ترك هوا / بر كشد اين شاخ جان را بر سما
entries.english: This abandonment of sensuality is the firmest handle: this branch draws the spirit up to Heaven.

entries.farsi: تا برد شاخ سخا اى خوب كيش / ‏مر ترا بالا كشان تا اصل خويش
entries.english: That the branch of generosity, O righteous man, drawing you aloft may bear you to its origin

entries.farsi: يوسف حسنى و اين عالم چو چاه / ‏وين رسن صبر است بر امر اله
entries.english: You are Joseph of beauty, and this world is as the well, and this rope is patience with the command of God.

entries.farsi: يوسفا آمد رسن در زن دو دست / ‏از رسن غافل مشو بى‏گه شده ست
entries.english: O Joseph, the rope is come: put your two hands upon it. Do not neglect the rope, it has grown late.

entries.farsi: حمد هلل كين رسن آويختند / فضل و رحمت را بهم آميختند
entries.english: Praise be to God, that this rope has been dangled; grace and mercy have been blended together,

entries.farsi: تا ببينى عالم جان جديد / عالم بس آشكار ناپديد
entries.english: So that you may behold the world of the new spirit, a world very manifest, invisible.

entries.farsi: اين جهان نيست چون هستان شده / ‏و آن جهان هست بس پنهان شده
entries.english: This world of non-existence has become like existence, while that world of existence has become hidden.

entries.farsi: خاك بر باد است و بازى مى‏كند / كژنمايى پرده سازى مى‏كند
entries.english: The dust is on the wind: it is playing; it is making a false show and forming a veil.

entries.farsi: اينكه بر كار است بى‏كار است و پوست / ‏و انكه پنهان است مغز و اصل اوست
entries.english: This, which is busy, is idle and a husk; and that which is hidden is its core and origin.

entries.farsi: خاك همچون آلتى در دست باد / باد را دان عالى و عالى نژاد
entries.english: The dust is as a tool in the hand of the wind: deem the wind high and of high descent

entries.farsi: چشم خاكى را به خاك افتد نظر / باد بين چشمى بود نوعى دگر
entries.english: The gaze of the eye of dust falls on the dust; an eye that sees the wind is of another sort.

entries.farsi: اسب داند اسب را كاو هست يار / هم سوارى داند احوال سوار
entries.english: A horse knows a horse, because it is associated; likewise a rider knows the things appertaining to a rider.

entries.farsi: چشم حس اسب است و نور حق سوار / بى‏سواره اسب خود نايد به كار
entries.english: The sensuous eye is the horse, and the Light of God is the rider: without the rider the horse itself is useless.

entries.farsi: پس ادب كن اسب را از خوى بد / ور نه پيش شاه باشد اسب رد
entries.english: Therefore train the horse of bad habits; else the horse will be rejected before the King.

entries.farsi: چشم اسب از چشم شه رهبر بود / چشم او بى‏چشم شه مضطر بود
entries.english: The horse’s eye finds the way from the King’s eye: without the King’s eye its eye is in desperate plight.

entries.farsi: چشم اسبان جز گياه و جز چرا / هر كجا خوانى بگويد نه چرا
entries.english: The eye of horses, whithersoever you call it except to grass and pasture, says, “Nay, why?”

entries.farsi: نور حق بر نور حس راكب شود / آن گهى جان سوى حق راغب شود
entries.english: The Light of God mounts on the sensuous eye, and then the soul yearns after God.

entries.farsi: اسب بى‏راكب چه داند رسم راه / ‏شاه بايد تا بداند شاه راه
entries.english: How should the riderless horse know the marks of the road? The King is needed in order that it may know the King’s road.

entries.farsi: سوى حسى رو كه نورش راكب است / ‏حس را آن نور نيكو صاحب است
entries.english: Go towards a sense on which the Light is riding: that Light is a good companion for the sense.

entries.farsi: نور حس را نور حق تزيين بود / معنى نُورٌ عَلى‏ نُورٍ اين بود
entries.english: The Light of God is an ornament to the light of sense: this is the meaning of light upon light.

entries.farsi: نور حسى مى‏كشد سوى ثرى / ‏نور حقش مى‏برد سوى على
entries.english: The light of sense draws towards earth; the Light of God bears him aloft,

entries.farsi: ز انكه محسوسات دونتر عالمى است / ‏نور حق دريا و حس چون شبنمى است
entries.english: Because sensible things are a lower world: the Light of God is the sea, and the sense as a dew-drop.

entries.farsi: ليك پيدا نيست آن راكب بر او / جز به آثار و به گفتار نكو
entries.english: But that which rides on it is not manifested save by good effects and words.

entries.farsi: نور حسى كاو غليظ است و گران / ‏هست پنهان در سواد ديده‏گان
entries.english: The sensuous light, which is gross and heavy, is hidden in the black of the eyes

entries.farsi: نور حس با اين غليظى مختفى است / ‏چون خفى نبود ضيايى كان صفى است
entries.english: Inasmuch as you are not seeing the light of sense with eye, how should you see the light of that religious one with eye?

entries.farsi: چون كه نور حس نمى‏بينى ز چشم / ‏چون ببينى نور آن دينى ز چشم
entries.english: The light of sense is hidden notwithstanding this grossness: how should not that radiance be hidden which is pure?

entries.farsi: اين جهان چون خس به دست باد غيب / ‏عاجزى پيش گرفت و داد غيب
entries.english: This world, like straws in the hand of the wind, the unseen, has adopted helplessness as its resource; and the dispensation of the Unseen

entries.farsi: گه بلندش مى‏كند گاهيش پست / گه درستش مى‏كند گاهى شكست
entries.english: Makes it now lofty, now low; makes it now sound, now broken;

entries.farsi: گه يمينش مى‏برد گاهى يسار / گه گلستانش كند گاهيش خار
entries.english: Now carries it to the right, now to the left; now makes it roses, now thorns.

entries.farsi: دست پنهان و قلم بين خط گزار / اسب در جوالن و ناپيدا سوار
entries.english: See the Hand hidden, while the pen is writing; the horse careering, while the Rider is invisible.

entries.farsi: تير پران بين و ناپيدا كمان / ‏جانها پيدا و پنهان جان جان
entries.english: See the arrow flying, and the Bow not in sight; the souls manifest, and the Soul of souls hidden.

entries.farsi: تير را مشكن كه اين تير شهى است / ‏تير پرتابى ز شصت آگهى است
entries.english: Do not break the arrow, for it is the arrow of a King; it is not shot at long range, it is from the thumb-stall of One who knows.

entries.farsi: ما رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رَمَيْتَ گفت حق / ‏كار حق بر كارها دارد سبق
entries.english: God said, “You did not throw when thou threw”: the action of God has precedence over actions.

entries.farsi: خشم خود بشكن تو مشكن تير را / چشم خشمت خون شمارد شير را
entries.english: Break your own anger, do not break the arrow: the eye of your anger reckons milk blood.

entries.farsi: بوسه ده بر تير و پيش شاه بر / تير خون آلود از خون تو تر
entries.english: Give the arrow a kiss and bring it to the King— the bloodstained arrow, wet with your blood.

entries.farsi: آن چه پيدا عاجز و بسته و زبون / ‏و آن چه ناپيدا چنان تند و حرون
entries.english: That which is seen is helpless and confined and feeble; and that which is unseen is so fierce and uncontrollable.

entries.farsi: ما شكاريم اين چنين دامى كراست / ‏گوى چوگانيم چوگانى كجاست
entries.english: We are the prey: to whom belongs such a snare? We are the ball of the polo-bat—and where is the Batsman?

entries.farsi: مى‏درد مى‏دوزد اين خياط كو / مى‏دمد مى‏سوزد اين نفاط كو
entries.english: He tears, He sews: where is this Tailor? He blows, He burns: where is this Fire-kindler?

entries.farsi: ساعتى كافر كند صديق را / ساعتى زاهد كند زنديق را
entries.english: At one hour He makes the true saint an unbeliever; at another hour He makes the deist an ascetic;

entries.farsi: ز انكه مخلص در خطر باشد ز دام / ‏تا ز خود خالص نگردد او تمام
entries.english: For the mukhlis is in danger of the snare until he becomes entirely purged of self,

entries.farsi: ز انكه در راهست و ره زن بى‏حد است / ‏آن رهد كاو در امان ايزد است
entries.english: Because he is on the Way, and the brigands are numberless: he escapes who is under God’s protection.

entries.farsi: آينه‏ى خالص نگشت او مخلص است / ‏مرغ را نگرفته است او مقنص است
entries.english: He has not become a pure mirror, he is mukhlis: he has not caught the bird, he is hunting;

entries.farsi: چون كه مخلص گشت مخلص باز رست / ‏در مقام امن رفت و برد دست
entries.english: When the pure intention has become the place of cleanliness, he is de­ livered: he has reached the place of safety and has won the victory.

entries.farsi: هيچ آيينه دگر آهن نشد / هيچ نانى گندم خرمن نشد
entries.english: No mirror became iron again; no bread became the wheat in the stack.

entries.farsi: هيچ انگورى دگر غوره نشد / هيچ ميوه‏ى پخته با كوره نشد
entries.english: No full-grown grape became a young grape; no mature fruit became premature fruit.

entries.farsi: پخته گرد و از تغير دور شو / رو چو برهان محقق نور شو
entries.english: Become mature and be far from change for the worse: go, become the Light, like Burhan-i Mubaqqiq

entries.farsi: چون ز خود رستى همه برهان شدى / ‏چون كه بنده نيست شد سلطان شدى
entries.english: When you have escaped from self, you have become wholly the proof: when the slave has become naught, you have become the King.

entries.farsi: ور عيان خواهى صالح دين نمود / ديده‏ها را كرد بينا و گشود
entries.english: And if you wish to behold plainly, Salibu’ddin has shown it forth; he has made the eyes to see and has opened.

entries.farsi: ديده‏ها را كرد بينا و گشود / ديد هر چشمى كه دارد نور هو
entries.english: From his eyes and bearing every eye that has the Light of Hu has discerned poverty.

entries.farsi: شيخ فعال است بى‏آلت چو حق / ‏با مريدان داده بى‏گفتى سبق
entries.english: The Shaykh is one who, like God, acts without instrument, giving lessons to his disciples without anything said.

entries.farsi: دل به دست او چو موم نرم رام / ‏مهر او گه ننگ سازد گاه نام
entries.english: In his hand the heart is submissive like soft wax: his seal makes now shame, now fame.

entries.farsi: مهر مومش حاكى انگشترى است / ‏باز آن نقش نگين حاكى كيست
entries.english: The seal impressed on his wax is telling of the seal-ring; of whom, again, does the device tell, graven on the stone of the ring?

entries.farsi: حاكى انديشه‏ى آن زرگر است / ‏سلسله‏ى هر حلقه اندر ديگر است
entries.english: It tells of the thought of the Goldsmith— is a chain, every link in another.

entries.farsi: اين صدا در كوه دلها بانگ كى ست / ‏گه پرست از بانگ اين كه گه تهى است
entries.english: Whose voice is this echo in the mountains of hearts? Sometimes this mountain is full of the voice, sometimes it is empty.

entries.farsi: هر كجا هست او حكيم است اوستاد / بانگ او زين كوه دل خالى مباد
entries.english: Wherever he is, he is the Sage, the Master— may his voice not forsake this mountain!

entries.farsi: هست كه كاوا مثنا مى‏كند / هست كه كآواز صد تا مى‏كند
entries.english: There is a mountain that doubles the voice; there is a mountain that makes it hundredfold.

entries.farsi: مى‏زهاند كوه از آن آواز و قال / ‏صد هزاران چشمه‏ى آب زالل
entries.english: At that voice and speech the mountain gushes forth hundreds of thousands of springs of clear water.

entries.farsi: چون ز كوه آن لطف بيرون مى‏شود / آبها در چشمه‏ها خون مى‏شود
entries.english: Inasmuch as that grace emanates from the mountain, the waters in the springs become blood.

entries.farsi: ز آن شهنشاه همايون نعل بود / كه سراسر طور سينا لعل بود
entries.english: It was on account of that monarch of auspicious gait that Mount Sinai was rubies from end to end.

entries.farsi: جان پذيرفت و خرد اجزاى كوه / ‏ما كم از سنگيم آخر اى گروه
entries.english: The parts of the mountain received life and intelligence— after all, are we inferior to stone, O people?

entries.farsi: نه ز جان يك چشمه جوشان مى‏شود / نه بدن از سبز پوشان مى‏شود
entries.english: Neither is there gushing from the soul a single spring, nor is the body becoming one of those clad in green;

entries.farsi: نه صداى بانگ مشتاقى در او / نه صفاى جرعه‏ى ساقى در او
entries.english: Neither is there in it the echo of the cry of longing, nor the purity of the draught of the Cup-bearer.

entries.farsi: نه صفاى جرعه‏ى ساقى در او / اين چنين كه را بكلى بر كنند
entries.english: Where is zeal, that they should entirely dig up such a mountain as this with axe and with pick?—

entries.farsi: بو كه بر اجزاى او تابد مهى / ‏بو كه در وى تاب مه يابد رهى
entries.english: Maybe a Moon will shine upon its particles; maybe the radiance of the Moon will find a way into it.

entries.farsi: اين قيامت ز آن قيامت كى كم است / ‏آن قيامت زخم و اين چون مرهم است
entries.english: Inasmuch as the Resurrection shall dig up the mountains, how shall it cast the shadow over us?

entries.farsi: چون قيامت كوهها را بر كند / پس قيامت اين كرم كى مى‏كند
entries.english: How is this Resurrection inferior to that Resurrection? That Resurrection is the wound, and this Resurrection is as the plaster.

entries.farsi: هر كه ديد اين مرهم از زخم ايمن است / ‏هر بدى كاين حسن ديد او محسن است
entries.english: Every one that has seen this plaster is safe from the wound : every evil one that has seen this good is a well-doer.

entries.farsi: اى خنك زشتى كه خويش شد حريف / ‏و اى گل رويى كه جفتش شد خريف
entries.english: Oh, happy is the ugly one to whom the beauteous one has become a companion; alas for one of rosy countenance with whom autumn has consorted!

entries.farsi: نان مرده چون حريف جان شود / زنده گردد نان و عين آن شود
entries.english: When lifeless bread is companioned with life, the bread becomes living and is turned into the substance of that.

entries.farsi: هيزم تيره حريف نار شد / تيرگى رفت و همه انوار شد
entries.english: Dark twigs become the companions of fire: the darkness departed, and all was turned into light.

entries.farsi: در نمك‏الن چون خر مرده فتاد / آن خرى و مردگى يك سو نهاد
entries.english: When the dead ass fell into the salt-mine, it put aside asininity and mortality

entries.farsi: صبغة اهلل هست خم رنگ هو / پيسها يك رنگ گردد اندر او
entries.english: The baptism of Allah is the dyeing-vat of Hu: therein piebald things become of one colour.

entries.farsi: چون در آن خم افتد و گوييش قم / ‏از طرب گويد منم خم ال تلم
entries.english: When he falls into the vat, and you say to him, “Arise,” he says in rapture,” I am the vat: do not blame.”

entries.farsi: آن منم خم خود انا الحق گفتن است / ‏رنگ آتش دارد الا آهن است
entries.english: That “I am the vat” is the saying “I am God ”: he has the colour of the fire, albeit he is iron.

entries.farsi: رنگ آهن محو رنگ آتش است / ‏ز آتشى مى‏الفد و خامش‏وش است
entries.english: The colour of the iron is negated in the colour of the fire: it boasts of fieriness, though it is like one who keeps silence.

entries.farsi: چون به سرخى گشت همچون زر كان / ‏پس انا النار است الفش بى‏زبان
entries.english: When it has become like gold of the mine in redness, then without tongue its boast is “I am the fire.”

entries.farsi: شد ز رنگ و طبع آتش محتشم / ‏گويد او من آتشم من آتشم
entries.english: It has become glorified by the colour and nature of the fire: it says, “I am the fire, I am the fire.

entries.farsi: آتشم من گر ترا شك است و ظن / ‏آزمون كن دست را بر من بزن
entries.english: I am the fire; if you have doubt and suspicion, make trial, put your hand upon me.

entries.farsi: آتشم من بر تو گر شد مشتبه / ‏روى خود بر روى من يك دم بنه
entries.english: I am the fire; if it seems doubtful to you, lay your face upon my face for one moment.”

entries.farsi: آدمى چون نور گيرد از خدا / هست مسجود ماليك ز اجتبا
entries.english: When Man receives light from God, he is worshipped by the angels because of his being chosen.

entries.farsi: نيز مسجود كسى كاو چون ملك / ‏رسته باشد جانش از طغيان و شك
entries.english: Also, worshipped by that one whose spirit, like the angel, has been freed from insolence and doubt.

entries.farsi: آتش چه آهن چه لب ببند / ريش تشبيه مشبه را مخند
entries.english: What fire? What iron? Close your lips: do not laugh at the beard of the assimilator’s simile.

entries.farsi: پاى در دريا منه كم گوى از آن / بر لب دريا خمش كن لب گزان
entries.english: Do not set foot in the Sea, speak not of It: on the shore of the Sea keep silence, biting your lips.

entries.farsi: گر چه صد چون من ندارد تاب بحر / ليك مى‏نشكيبم از غرقاب بحر
entries.english: Though a hundred like me would not have the strength to bear the Sea, yet I cannot refrain from the drowning waters of the Sea.

entries.farsi: جان و عقل من فداى بحر باد / خونبهاى عقل و جان اين بحر داد
entries.english: May my soul and mind be a sacrifice to the Sea: this Sea has paid the blood-price of mind and soul.

entries.farsi: تا كه پايم مى‏رود رانم در او / چون نماند پا چو بطانم در او
entries.english: I will march in It as long as my feet move; when feet remain not, I am in It, like ducks.

entries.farsi: بى‏ادب حاضر ز غايب خوشتر است / ‏حلقه گر چه كژ بود نه بر در است
entries.english: A disrespectful person present is better than one absent: though the ring be crooked, is it not on the door?

entries.farsi: اى تن آلوده به گرد حوض گرد / پاك كى گردد برون حوض مرد
entries.english: O defiled in body, frequent the tank: outside of the tank, how shall a man be cleansed?

entries.farsi: پاك كاو از حوض مهجور اوفتاد / او ز پاكى خويش هم دور اوفتاد
entries.english: The pure one who has been banished from the tank becomes far also from his purity.

entries.farsi: پاكى اين حوض بى‏پايان بود / پاكى اجسام كم ميزان بود
entries.english: The purity of this tank is infinite; the purity of bodies is of little weight,

entries.farsi: ز انكه دل حوض است ليكن در كمين / ‏سوى دريا راه پنهان دارد اين
entries.english: Because the heart is a tank, yet in ambush it has a hidden channel to the Sea.

entries.farsi: پاكى محدود تو خواهد مدد / ور نه اندر خرج كم گردد عدد
entries.english: Your finite purity wants reinforcement; otherwise, number is diminished in expenditure.

entries.farsi: آب گفت آلوده را در من شتاب / ‏گفت آلوده كه دارم شرم از آب
entries.english: The water said to the defiled one, “Hasten into me.” The defiled one said, “I feel shame before the water.”

entries.farsi: گفت آب اين شرم بى‏من كى رود / بى‏من اين آلوده زايل كى شود
entries.english: Said the water, “Without me how shall this shame go? Without me how shall this defilement be removed?”

entries.farsi: ز آب هر آلوده كاو پنهان شود / الحياء يمنع اإليمان بود
entries.english: Every defiled one who hides from the water is “Shame hinders Faith”

entries.farsi: دل ز پايه‏ى حوض تن گلناك شد / تن ز آب حوض دلها پاك شد
entries.english: The heart is muddied by the steps of the body’s tank; the body is cleansed by the water of the heart’s tanks.

entries.farsi: گرد پايه‏ى حوض دل گرد اى پسر / هان ز پايه‏ى حوض تن مى‏كن حذر
entries.english: Haunt the steps of the heart’s tank, O son; take heed and always beware of the steps of the body’s tank.

entries.farsi: بحر تن بر بحر دل بر هم زنان / ‏در ميانشان بَرْزَخٌ ال يبغيان
entries.english: The sea of the body is dashing against the sea of the heart; between them is a barrier which they shall not cross.

entries.farsi: گر تو باشى راست ور باشى تو كژ / پيشتر مى‏غژ بدو واپس مغژ
entries.english: Whether you are straight or crooked, always creep forwards to Him; do not creep backwards.

entries.farsi: شاه چون شيرين‏تر از شكر بود / جان به شيرينى رود خوشتر بود
entries.english: If in the presence of kings there be danger to life, yet they that aspire cannot refrain from Him.

entries.farsi: پيش شاهان گر خطر باشد به جان / ‏ليك نشكيبد از او با همتان
entries.english: Since the King is sweeter than sugar, it is better that life should go to sweetness.

entries.farsi: اى مالمت گر سالمت مر ترا / ‏اى سالمت جو تويى واهى العرى
entries.english: O blamer, safety be yours! O seeker of safety, you are infirm.

entries.farsi: جان من كوره ست با آتش خوش است / ‏كوره را اين بس كه خانه‏ى آتش است
entries.english: My soul is a furnace: it is happy with the fire: it is enough for the furnace that it is the fire’s house.

entries.farsi: همچو كوره عشق را سوزيدنى است / ‏هر كه او زين كور باشد كوره نيست
entries.english: For Love, as the furnace, there is something to be burned: any one that is blind to this is not a furnace.

entries.farsi: برگ بى‏برگى ترا چون برگ شد / جان باقى يافتى و مرگ شد
entries.english: When the provision of unprovidedness has become your provision, you have gained life everlasting, and death is gone.

entries.farsi: چون ترا غم شادى افزودن گرفت / ‏روضه‏ى جانت گل و سوسن گرفت
entries.english: When the pain has begun to increase your joy, roses and lilies have taken possession of the garden of your soul.

entries.farsi: آن چه خوف ديگران آن امن تست / ‏بط قوى از بحر و مرغ خانه سست
entries.english: That which is the dread of others is your safety: the duck is strong by the river, the domestic fowl weak.

entries.farsi: باز ديوانه شدم من اى طبيب / ‏باز سودايى شدم من اى حبيب
entries.english: Once more have I become mad, O Physician! Once more have I become frenzied, O Beloved!

entries.farsi: حلقه‏هاى سلسله‏ى تو ذو فنون / ‏هر يكى حلقه دهد ديگر جنون
entries.english: The rings of Your chain are multiform: every single ring gives a different madness.

entries.farsi: داد هر حلقه فنونى ديگر است / ‏پس مرا هر دم جنونى ديگر است
entries.english: The gift of every ring is different forms: therefore I have a different madness at every moment.

entries.farsi: پس فنون باشد جنون اين شد مثل / ‏خاصه در زنجير اين مير اجل
entries.english: So “Madness is of different forms “—this has become a proverb; especially as regards the chain of this most glorious Prince.

entries.farsi: آن چنان ديوانگى بگسست بند / كه همه ديوانگان پندم دهند
entries.english: Such madness has broken the bonds that all madmen would give me admonition.

sections.title_fa: ‏آمدن دوستان به بيمارستان جهت پرسش ذو النون مصرى
sections.title_en: How friends came to the madhouse for Dhu’l-Nun —may God sanctify his honoured spirit!

entries.farsi: اين چنين ذو النون مصرى را فتاد / كاندر او شور و جنونى نو بزاد
entries.english: It so happened to Dhu ‘l-Nun the Egyptian that a new agitation and madness was born within him.

entries.farsi: شور چندان شد كه تا فوق فلك / ‏مى‏رسيد از وى جگرها را نمك
entries.english: His agitation became so great that salt from it was reaching hearts up to above the sky.

entries.farsi: خلق را تاب جنون او نبود / آتش او ريشهاشان مى‏ربود
entries.english: Beware, O salty soil, do not put your agitation beside the agitation of the holy lords.

entries.farsi: هين منه تو شور خود اى شوره خاك / ‏پهلوى شور خداوندان پاك
entries.english: The people could not endure his madness: his fire was carrying off their beards.

entries.farsi: چون كه در ريش عوام آتش فتاد / بند كردندش به زندانى نهاد
entries.english: When fire fell on the beards of the vulgar, they bound him and put him in a prison.

entries.farsi: نيست امكان واكشيدن اين لگام / ‏گر چه زين ره تنگ مى‏آيند عام
entries.english: There is no possibility of pulling back this rein, though the vulgar be distressed by this way,

entries.farsi: ديده اين شاهان ز عامه خوف جان / ‏كاين گره كورند و شاهان بى‏نشان
entries.english: These kings have seen danger of their lives from the vulgar; for this multitude are blind, and the kings without mark.

entries.farsi: چون كه حكم اندر كف رندان بود / الجرم ذو النون در زندان بود
entries.english: When authority is in the hands of profligates, Dhu ‘l-Nun is inevitably in prison

entries.farsi: يك سواره مى‏رود شاه عظيم / ‏در كف طفالن چنين در يتيم
entries.english: The great king rides alone! Such a unique pearl in the hands of children!

entries.farsi: در چه دريا نهان در قطره‏اى / ‏آفتابى مخفى اندر ذره‏اى
entries.english: What pearl? The Sea hidden in a drop; a Sun concealed in a mote.

entries.farsi: آفتابى خويش را ذره نمود / و اندك اندك روى خود را بر گشود
entries.english: A Sun showed itself as a mote, and little by little uncovered its face.

entries.farsi: جمله‏ى ذرات در وى محو شد / عالم از وى مست گشت و صحو شد
entries.english: All motes vanished in it; the world became intoxicated by it and became sober.

entries.farsi: چون قلم در دست غدارى بود / بى‏گمان منصور بر دارى بود
entries.english: When the pen is in the hand of a traitor, unquestionably Mansur is on a gibbet.

entries.farsi: چون سفيهان راست اين كار و كيا / الزم آمد يَقْتُلُونَ األنبياء
entries.english: When this affair belongs to the foolish, the necessary consequence is they kill the prophets.

entries.farsi: انبيا را گفته قومى راه گم / ‏از سفه إِنَّا تَطَ يَّرْنا بكم
entries.english: Through folly the people who had lost the way said to the prophets, “Lo, we augur ill from you.”

entries.farsi: جهل ترسا بين امان انگيخته / ‏ز آن خداوندى كه گشت آويخته
entries.english: See the ignorance of the Christian appealing for protection to the Lord who was suspended!

entries.farsi: چون به قول اوست مصلوب جهود / پس مر او را امن كى تاند نمود
entries.english: Since, according to his belief, He was crucified by the Jews, how then can He protect him?

entries.farsi: چون دل آن شاه ز ايشان خون بود / عصمت وَ أَنْتَ فِيهِمْ چون بود
entries.english: Inasmuch as the heart of that King bleeds on account of them, how should there be the inviolable defense of while you are among them?

entries.farsi: زر خالص را و زرگر را خطر / باشد از قالب خاين بيشتر
entries.english: To the pure gold and to the goldsmith the danger from the false counterfeiter is greater.

entries.farsi: يوسفان از رشك زشتان مخفيند / كز عدو خوبان در آتش مى‏زيند
entries.english: Josephs are concealed on account of the jealousy of the ugly, for because of enemies the beautiful live in the fire.

entries.farsi: از حسد بر يوسف مصرى چه رفت / ‏اين حسد اندر كمين گرگى است زفت
entries.english: Josephs are in the pit through the guile of brethren, who from envy give Joseph to the wolves.

entries.farsi: يوسفان از مكر اخوان در چه‏اند / كز حسد يوسف به گرگان مى‏دهند
entries.english: What happened to Joseph of Egypt on account of envy? This envy is a big wolf in covert.

entries.farsi: الجرم زين گرگ يعقوب حليم / ‏داشت بر يوسف هميشه خوف و بيم
entries.english: Of necessity, kind Jacob had always feared for Joseph because of this wolf.

entries.farsi: گرگ ظاهر گرد يوسف خود نگشت / ‏اين حسد در فعل از گرگان گذشت
entries.english: The outward wolf, indeed, did not prowl about Joseph; this envy has actually surpassed wolves.

entries.farsi: رحم كرد اين گرگ و ز عذر لبق / ‏آمده كه إِنَّا ذَهَبْنا نستبق
entries.english: This wolf dealt its wound, and by way of specious excuse came, “Lo, we went to compete with one another.”

entries.farsi: صد هزاران گرگ را اين مكر نيست / ‏عاقبت رسوا شود اين گرگ بيست
entries.english: Hundreds of thousands of wolves have not this cunning; this wolf will at last be put to shame—stop!

entries.farsi: ز انكه حشر حاسدان روز گزند / بى‏گمان بر صورت گرگان كنند
entries.english: Because on the Day of Bale the envious will without doubt be brought together in the shape of wolves.

entries.farsi: حشر پر حرص خس مردار خوار / صورت خوكى بود روز شمار
entries.english: The resurrection of the greedy vile eater of carrion will be in the shape of a hog on the Day of Reckoning.

entries.farsi: زانيان را گند اندام نهان / ‏خمر خواران را بود گند دهان
entries.english: The adulterer’s hidden parts will smell; wine-drinkers will have stinking mouths.

entries.farsi: گند مخفى كان به دلها مى‏رسيد / گشت اندر حشر محسوس و پديد
entries.english: The hidden stench that was reaching to hearts will become sensible and manifest at the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: بيشه‏اى آمد وجود آدمى / ‏بر حذر شو زين وجود ار ز آن دمى
entries.english: The being of Man is a jungle: be on your guard against this being, if you are of that Breath.

entries.farsi: در وجود ما هزاران گرگ و خوك / ‏صالح و ناصالح و خوب و خشوك
entries.english: In our being there are thousands of wolves and hogs; godly and ungodly and fair and foul.

entries.farsi: حكم آن خور است كان غالبتر است / ‏چون كه زر بيش از مس آيد آن زر است
entries.english: To the disposition that is preponderant belongs the decision: when the gold is more than the copper, it is gold.

entries.farsi: ‏چون كه زر بيش از مس آيد آن زر است / ‏هم بر آن تصوير حشرت واجب است
entries.english: The manner of acting that preponderates in your nature— in that same form you must needs rise.

entries.farsi: ساعتى گرگى در آيد در بشر / ساعتى يوسف رخى همچون قمر
entries.english: At one hour a wolf enters into Man, at another hour a moonlike beauty with the face of Joseph.

entries.farsi: مى‏رود از سينه‏ها در سينه‏ها / از ره پنهان صالح و كينه‏ها
entries.english: Good and hateful qualities pass from bosoms into bosoms by a hidden way;

entries.farsi: بلكه خود از آدمى در گاو و خر / مى‏رود دانايى و علم و هنر
entries.english: Nay, indeed, wisdom and knowledge and excellence pass from Man into the ox and the ass.

entries.farsi: اسب سكسك مى‏شود رهوار و رام / ‏خرس بازى مى‏كند بر هم سالم
entries.english: The stumbling horse becomes smooth-paced and docile; the bear dances, the goat also salaams.

entries.farsi: رفت اندر سگ ز آدميان هوس / ‏تا شبان شد يا شكارى يا حرس
entries.english: Volition has passed from human beings into the dog, so that he has become a shepherd or a hunter or a guardian.

entries.farsi: در سگ اصحاب خوبى ز ان وفود / رفت تا جوياى اللهَّ گشته بود
entries.english: Into the dog of the Companions there passed from those Sleepers a disposition, so that he had become a seeker of God.

entries.farsi: هر زمان در سينه نوعى سر كند / گاه ديو و گه ملك گه دام و دد
entries.english: At every moment a species bursts up in the breast: now a devil, and now an angel, now wild beasts.

entries.farsi: ز آن عجب بيشه كه شير آگه است / ‏تا به دام سينه‏ها پنهان ره است
entries.english: From that marvellous Jungle with which every lion is acquainted, there is a hidden way to the breasts which ensnare.

entries.farsi: دزديى كن از درون مرجان جان / ‏اى كم از سگ از درون عارفان
entries.english: O you, who are less than a dog, steal the spiritual pearl from within— from within them that know God.

entries.farsi: چون كه دزدى بارى آن در لطيف / ‏چون كه حامل مى‏شوى بارى شريف
entries.english: As you steal, at any rate that lovely pearl; as you are going to bear a burden, at any rate a noble one.

sections.title_fa: فهم كردن مريدان كه ذو النون ديوانه نشده است قاصد كرده است
sections.title_en: How the disciples understood that Dhu ‘l Nun had not
become mad, had acted with intention

entries.farsi: دوستان در قصه‏ى ذو النون شدند / سوى زندان و در آن رايى زدند
entries.english: The friends went to the prison about the story of Dhu ‘l-Nun, and expressed an opinion concerning it,

entries.farsi: كاين مگر قاصد كند يا حكمتى است / ‏او در اين دين قبله‏اى و آيتى است
entries.english: Saying, “Perchance he does this purposely, or there is some Wisdom; he is an exemplar and a shining light in this religion.

entries.farsi: دور دور از عقل چون درياى او / تا جنون باشد سفه فرماى او
entries.english: Far, far be it from his sea-like intelligence that madness should prompt him to folly!

entries.farsi: حاش هلل از كمال جاه او / كابر بيمارى بپوشد ماه او
entries.english: God forbid, in view of the perfection of his attainments, that the cloud of sickness should cover his moon!

entries.farsi: او ز شر عامه اندر خانه شد / او ز ننگ عاقالن ديوانه شد
entries.english: He has gone into the house from the wickedness of the vulgar: he has become mad on account of the infamy of the sane.

entries.farsi: او ز عار عقل كند تن پرست / ‏قاصدا رفته ست و ديوانه شده ست
entries.english: From the disgrace of the dull body-serving intellect, he has purposely gone and become mad,

entries.farsi: كه ببنديدم قوى و ز ساز گاو / بر سر و پشتم بزن وين را مكاو
entries.english: Saying, ‘Bind me fast and with the tail of a cow smite me on head and back, and do not dispute this,

entries.farsi: تا ز زخم لخت يابم من حيات / ‏چون قتيل از گاو موسى اى ثقات
entries.english: That from the stroke of the part I may gain life, as the murdered man from the cow of Moses, O trusty ones;

entries.farsi: تا ز زخم لخت گاوى خوش شوم / ‏همچو كشته‏ى گاو موسى گش شوم
entries.english: That I may be made happy by the stroke of a part of the cow; become well, like the murdered man of the cow of Moses’. ”

entries.farsi: زنده شد كشته ز زخم دم گاو / همچو مس از كيميا شد زر ساو
entries.english: The murdered man was revived by the stroke of the cow’s tail: he became pure gold, even as copper by the elixir.

entries.farsi: كشته بر جست و بگفت اسرار را / وا نمود آن زمره‏ى خون‏خوار را
entries.english: The murdered man sprang up and told the secrets: he revealed that bloodthirsty gang.

entries.farsi: گفت روشن كاين جماعت كشته‏اند / كاين زمان در خصمى‏ام آشفته‏اند
entries.english: He said plainly, “ I was killed by these people who are now disturbed in contending against me.”

entries.farsi: چون كه كشته گردد اين جسم گران / ‏زنده گردد هستى اسرار دان
entries.english: When this gross body is killed, the essence that knows secrets is brought to life.

entries.farsi: جان او بيند بهشت و نار را / باز داند جمله‏ى اسرار را
entries.english: Its spirit beholds Paradise and Hell-fire and discerns all the mysteries.

entries.farsi: وا نمايد خونيان ديو را / وا نمايد دام خدعه و ريو را
entries.english: It reveals the devilish murderers; it reveals the snare of deceit and guile.

entries.farsi: گاو كشتن هست از شرط طريق / ‏تا شود از زخم دمش جان مفيق
entries.english: To kill the cow is the stipulation of the Path, in order that the spirit may be restored to consciousness by the stroke of her tail.

entries.farsi: گاو نفس خويش را زوتر بكش / ‏تا شود روح خفى زنده و بهش
entries.english: Kill most quickly the cow, your nafs, so that the hidden spirit may become alive and conscious.

sections.title_fa: رجوع به حكايت ذو النون
sections.title_en: Resumption of the story of Dhu’l-Nun, may God sanctify his spirit!

entries.farsi: چون رسيدند آن نفر نزديك او / بانگ بر زد هى كيانيد اتقوا
entries.english: When those persons arrived near him, he shouted, “Hey, who are you? Take care!”

entries.farsi: با ادب گفتند ما از دوستان / ‏بهر پرسش آمديم اينجا به جان
entries.english: They said respectfully, “We are some of your friends: we have come here in devotion to ask about you.

entries.farsi: چونى اى درياى عقل ذو فنون / ‏اين چه بهتان است بر عقلت جنون
entries.english: How are you? O Sea of manifold intelligence? What slander on your intelligence is this madness?

entries.farsi: دود گلخن كى رسد در آفتاب / ‏چون شود عنقا شكسته از غراب
entries.english: How should the smoke of the bath-stove reach the Sun? How should the Anqa be crushed by the crow?

entries.farsi: مر محبان را نشايد دور كرد / يا به رو پوش و دغل مغرور كرد
entries.english: Do not withhold from us: explain this matter; we are lovers; do not behave to us in this fashion.

entries.farsi: وامگير از ما بيان كن اين سخن / ‏ما محبانيم با ما اين مكن
entries.english: One ought not to drive lovers away or dupe them by mask and false pretence.

entries.farsi: راز را اندر ميان آور شها / رو مكن در ابر پنهانى مها
entries.english: Communicate the secret, O King: do not hide your face in the cloud, O Moon!

entries.farsi: ما محب و صادق و دل خسته‏ايم / ‏در دو عالم دل به تو در بسته‏ايم
entries.english: We are loving and true and with wounded hearts: in the two worlds we have fixed our hearts on thee.”

entries.farsi: فحش آغازيد و دشنام از گزاف / ‏گفت او ديوانگانه زى و قاف
entries.english: He began foul words and bad names recklessly: he spoke gibberish like madmen.

entries.farsi: بر جهيد و سنگ پران كرد و چوب / ‏جملگى بگريختند از بيم كوب
entries.english: He jumped up and let fly stones and sticks; the whole party fled for fear of blows.

entries.farsi: قهقهه خنديد و جنبانيد سر / گفت باد ريش اين ياران نگر
entries.english: He laughed loudly and tossed his head. “Look,” said he, “at the vain bluster of these friends!

entries.farsi: دوستان بين، كو نشان دوستان / ‏دوستان را رنج باشد همچو جان
entries.english: See the friends! Where is the sign of friends? To friends pain is as life.”

entries.farsi: كى كران گيرد ز رنج دوست دوست / ‏رنج مغز و دوستى آن را چو پوست
entries.english: How should a friend turn aside from the pain inflicted by his friend? Pain is the kernel, and friendship is as the husk to it.

entries.farsi: نه نشان دوستى شد سر خوشى / ‏در بال و آفت و محنت كشى
entries.english: Has not joy in tribulation and calamity and suffering become the sign of friendship?

entries.farsi: دوست همچون زر بال چون آتش است / ‏زر خالص در دل آتش خوش است
entries.english: A friend is like gold, tribulation is like the fire: the pure gold is glad in the heart of the fire.

sections.title_fa: امتحان كردن خواجه‏ى لقمان زيركى لقمان را
sections.title_en: How Luqman’s master tested his sagacity.

entries.farsi: نه كه لقمان را كه بنده‏ى پاك بود / روز و شب در بندگى چالاك بود
entries.english: Not that to Luqman, who was a pure slave, and day and night was brisk in service?

entries.farsi: خواجه‏اش مى‏داشتى در كار پيش / ‏بهترش ديدى ز فرزندان خويش
entries.english: His master used to prefer him in the work and deem him better than his own sons,

entries.farsi: ز انكه لقمان گر چه بنده زاد بود / خواجه بود و از هوا آزاد بود
entries.english: Because Luqman, though he was slave-born, was the master and was free from sensual desire

entries.farsi: گفت شاهى شيخ را اندر سخن / ‏چيزى از بخشش ز من درخواست كن
entries.english: A certain king said to the Shaykh in conversation, “Ask me to bestow some bounty upon thee.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى شه شرم نايد مر ترا / كه چنين گويى مرا زين برتر آ
entries.english: He answered, “O King, are not you ashamed to say such a thing to me? Come higher!

entries.farsi: من دو بنده دارم و ايشان حقير / و آن دو بر تو حاكمانند و امير
entries.english: I have two slaves, and they are vile, and those two are rulers and lords over you.”

entries.farsi: گفت شه آن دو چه‏اند اين زلت است / ‏گفت آن يك خشم و ديگر شهوت است
entries.english: The king said, “Who are they? That is a mistake.” He replied, “The one is anger and the other is lust.”

entries.farsi: شاه آن دان كاو ز شاهى فارغ است / ‏بى‏مه و خورشيد نورش بازغ است
entries.english: Regard as a king him that is unconcerned with kingship, him whose light shines forth without moon or sun.

entries.farsi: مخزن آن دارد كه مخزن ذات اوست / ‏هستى او دارد كه با هستى عدوست
entries.english: That one whose essence is the treasury possesses the treasury: he that is an enemy to existence possesses existence.

entries.farsi: خواجه‏ى لقمان به ظاهر خواجه‏وش / ‏در حقيقت بنده، لقمان خواجه‏اش
entries.english: Luqman’s master like a master outwardly; in reality his master Luqman’s slave’.

entries.farsi: در جهان باژگونه زين بسى است / ‏در نظرشان گوهرى كم از خسى است
entries.english: In the topsy-turvy world there is many a one of this: a pearl is less than a straw in their sight.

entries.farsi: مر بيابان را مفازه نام شد / نام و رنگى عقلشان را دام شد
entries.english: Every desert has been named waterless country: a name and specious form has ensnared their understanding.

entries.farsi: يك گره را خود معرف جامه است / ‏در قبا گويند كاو از عامه است
entries.english: In the case of one class of people, the dress makes known: in a djellaba they say that he belongs to the vulgar.

entries.farsi: يك گره را ظاهر سالوس زهد / نور بايد تا بود جاسوس زهد
entries.english: In the case of one class of people the hypocritical appearance of asceticism; light is needed, that may be a spy on asceticism.

entries.farsi: نور بايد پاك از تقليد و غول / ‏تا شناسد مرد را بى‏فعل و قول
entries.english: Light is needed, purged of whining and poison, that he may know a man without deed or word,

entries.farsi: در رود در قلب او از راه عقل / ‏نقد او بيند نباشد بند نقل
entries.english: And enter into his heart by way of the intellect, and behold his real state and not be confined to tradition.

entries.farsi: بندگان خاص عالم الغيوب / ‏در جهان جان جواسيس القلوب
entries.english: The chosen servants of the Knower of things unseen are, in the spiritual world, the spies on hearts.

entries.farsi: در درون دل در آيد چون خيال / ‏پيش او مكشوف باشد سر حال
entries.english: He enters within the heart like a fancy: the mystery of state is unveiled to him.

entries.farsi: در تن گنجشك چه بود برگ و ساز / كه شود پوشيده آن بر عقل باز
entries.english: In the body of the sparrow what power and faculty is there that is hidden from the intellect of the falcon?

entries.farsi: آن كه واقف گشت بر اسرار هو / سر مخلوقات چه بود پيش او
entries.english: He who has become acquainted with the secrets of Hu, what to him is the secret of created beings?

entries.farsi: آن كه بر افلاک رفتارش بود / بر زمين رفتن چه دشوارش بود
entries.english: He whose walk is on the spheres, how should it be hard for him to walk on the earth?

entries.farsi: در كف داود كاهن گشت موم / ‏موم چه بود در كف او اى ظلوم
entries.english: Since iron became wax in the hand of David, what should wax be in his hand, O man of iniquity?

entries.farsi: بود لقمان بنده شكلى خواجه‏اى / ‏بندگى بر ظاهرش ديباجه‏اى
entries.english: Luqman was a slave in appearance, a master: servitude was a frontispiece on his outside.

entries.farsi: چون رود خواجه به جاى ناشناس / ‏در غالم خويش پوشاند لباس
entries.english: When the master goes to a place where he is not known, he puts clothes on his slave.

entries.farsi: او بپوشد جامه‏هاى آن غالم / ‏مر غالم خويش را سازد امام
entries.english: He puts on the slave’s clothes and makes his slave the leader.

entries.farsi: در پيش چون بندگان در ره شود / تا نبايد زو كسى آگه شود
entries.english: He goes behind him on the road, as slaves do, lest any one should recognize him.

entries.farsi: گويد اى بنده تو رو بر صدر شين / ‏من بگيرم كفش چون بنده‏ى كهين
entries.english: “O slave,” says he,” go you and sit in the place of honour: I will take shoes, like the meanest slave.

entries.farsi: ترك خدمت خدمت تو داشتم / ‏تا به غربت تخم حيلت كاشتم
entries.english: Treat roughly and revile me; do not bestow any respect on me.

entries.farsi: تو درشتى كن مرا دشنام ده / ‏مر مرا تو هيچ توقيرى منه
entries.english: I hold neglect of service to be your service, since I have sown the seed of contrivance by dwelling in a foreign land.”

entries.farsi: خواجگان اين بندگيها كرده‏اند / تا گمان آيد كه ايشان برده‏اند
entries.english: Masters have performed these slavish offices in order that it might be thought that they were slaves.

entries.farsi: چشم پر بودند و سير از خواجگى / ‏كارها را كرده‏اند آمادگى
entries.english: They had their fill of leadership and were sated: They have made ready for the work.

entries.farsi: وين غالمان هوا بر عكس آن / ‏خويشتن بنموده خواجه‏ى عقل و جان
entries.english: On the contrary, these slaves of sensuality have represented themselves as masters of intellect and spirit.

entries.farsi: آيد از خواجه ره افكندگى / ‏نايد از بنده بغير بندگى
entries.english: On the contrary, these slaves of sensuality have represented themselves as masters of intellect and spirit.

entries.farsi: پس از آن عالم بدين عالم چنان / ‏تعبيت‏ها هست بر عكس اين بدان
entries.english: So topsy-turvy, then, are the arrangements between that world and this world. Know this.

entries.farsi: خواجه‏ى لقمان از اين حال نهان / ‏بود واقف ديده بود از وى نشان
entries.english: Luqman’s master was aware of this hidden state; he had seen signs in him.

entries.farsi: راز مى‏دانست و خوش مى‏راند خر / از براى مصلحت آن راهبر
entries.english: That traveller knew the secret, quietly pursued his way for the sake of the good.

entries.farsi: مر و را آزاد كردى از نخست / ‏ليك خشنودى لقمان را بجست
entries.english: He would have set him free at the first, but he sought to content Luqman,

entries.farsi: ز انكه لقمان را مراد اين بود تا / ‏كس نداند سر آن شير و فتى
entries.english: Because it was Luqman’s desire, in order that none might know the secret of that brave and generous youth

entries.farsi: چه عجب گر سر ز بد پنهان كنى / ‏اين عجب كه سر ز خود پنهان كنى
entries.english: What wonder that you should hide your secret from the evil ? The wonder is this, that you should hide the secret from yourself.

entries.farsi: خويش را تسليم كن بر دام مزد / و انگه از خود بى‏ز خود چيزى بدزد
entries.english: Hide your work from your own eyes, that your work may be safe from the evil eye.

entries.farsi: كار پنهان كن تو از چشمان خود / تا بود كارت سليم از چشم بد
entries.english: Yield yourself up to the snare of the reward, and then, beside yourself, steal something from yourself.

entries.farsi: مى‏دهند افيون به مرد زخم‏مند / تا كه پيكان از تنش بيرون كنند
entries.english: They give opium to the wounded man, in order that they may extract the point from his body.

entries.farsi: وقت مرگ از رنج او را مى‏درند / او بدان مشغول شد جان مى‏برند
entries.english: At the hour of death he is torn with pain; he becomes preoccupied with that, and his spirit is taken away.

entries.farsi: چون به هر فكرى كه دل خواهى سپرد / از تو چيزى در نهان خواهند برد
entries.english: Inasmuch as, to whatever thought you give up your mind, something will secretly be taken away from you,

entries.farsi: هر چه انديشى و تحصيلى كنى / ‏مى‏درآيد دزد از آن سو كايمنى
entries.english: Whatever you may cogitate an acquisition you may make, the thief will enter from the side where you feel safe,

entries.farsi: پس بدان مشغول شو كان بهتر است / ‏تا ز تو چيزى برد كان بهتر است
entries.english: Become occupied, therefore, with that which is better, in order that the thief may take away from you something that is less.

entries.farsi: بار بازرگان چو در آب اوفتد / دست اندر كاله‏ى بهتر زند
entries.english: When the trader’s bales fall into the water, he lays his hands upon the better merchandise.

entries.farsi: چون كه چيزى فوت خواهد شد در آب / ‏ترك كمتر گوى و بهتر را بياب
entries.english: Since something will be lost in the water, take leave of the less and gain the better.

sections.title_fa: ظاهر شدن فضل و زيركى لقمان پيش امتحان كنندگان
sections.title_en: How the excellence and sagacity of Luqman became manifest

entries.farsi: هر طعامى كاوريدندى به وى / ‏كس سوى لقمان فرستادى ز پى
entries.english: to those who made trial.

entries.farsi: تا كه لقمان دست سوى آن برد / قاصدا تا خواجه پس خوردش خورد
entries.english: Whatever food they brought to him, he would send some one to Luqman after,

entries.farsi: سور او خوردى و شور انگيختى / هر طعامى كاو نخوردى ريختى
entries.english: That Luqman might put his hand to it, on purpose that the master might eat his leavings.

entries.farsi: خربزه آورده بودند ارمغان / ‏گفت رو فرزند لقمان را بخوان
entries.english: He would eat his remnants and be enraptured: any food that he did not taste, he would throw away;

entries.farsi: ور بخوردى بى‏دل و بى‏اشتها / اين بود پيوندى بى‏انتها
entries.english: Or if he ate without heart and without appetite: this is affinity without end.

entries.farsi: چون بريد و داد او را يك برين / همچو شكر خوردش و چون انگبين
entries.english: They had brought a melon as a present. “Go,” said he, “call my son, Luqman.”

entries.farsi: از خوشى كه خورد داد او را دوم / ‏تا رسيد آن گرچها تا هفدهم
entries.english: When he cut it and gave him a slice, he ate it as if it were sugar and honey.

entries.farsi: ماند گرچى گفت اين را من خورم / ‏تا چه شيرين خربزه ست اين بنگرم
entries.english: On account of the pleasure with which he ate, he gave him a second, till the slices reached the seventeenth.

entries.farsi: او چنين خوش مى‏خورد كز ذوق او / طبعها شد مشتهى و لقمه جو
entries.english: One slice remained. He said, “I will eat this, so that I may see what a sweet melon this is.

entries.farsi: چون بخورد از تلخيش آتش فروخت / ‏هم زبان كرد آبله هم حلق سوخت
entries.english: He eats it with such pleasure that from his delight natures have become eager and craving the morsel.”

entries.farsi: ساعتى بى‏خود شد از تلخى آن / ‏بعد از آن گفتش كه اى جان و جهان
entries.english: As soon as he ate it, by its sourness there was kindled fire blistered his tongue and burnt his throat.

entries.farsi: نوش چون كردى تو چندين زهر را / لطف چون انگاشتى اين قهر را
entries.english: He became beside himself for a while on account of its sourness; after that, he said to him, “O soul and world,

entries.farsi: اين چه صبر است اين صبورى از چه روست / يا مگر پيش تو اين جانت عدوست
entries.english: How did you make all this poison an antidote? How did you deem this cruelty to be kindness?

entries.farsi: چون نياوردى به حيلت حجتى / ‏كه مرا عذرى است بس كن ساعتى
entries.english: What patience is this? For what reason is this great fortitude? Or, perchance, in your opinion this life of yours is an enemy.

entries.farsi: ‏كه مرا عذرى است بس كن ساعتى / خورده‏ام چندان كه از شرمم دو تو
entries.english: Why did not you cunningly bring a plea, saying, I have an excuse: desist for a while’.”

entries.farsi: شرمم آمد كه يكى تلخ از كفت / ‏من ننوشم اى تو صاحب معرفت
entries.english: Luqman said, “From your kindness hand I have eaten somuch that I am double with shame.
I was ashamed not to eat one bitter thing from thy hand, O you who are possessed of knowledge.

entries.farsi: چون همه اجزام از انعام تو / رسته‏اند و غرق دانه و دام تو
entries.english: Since all parts of me have grown from your bounty and are plunged in your bait and snare

entries.farsi: گر ز يك تلخى كنم فرياد و داد / خاك صد ره بر سر اجزام باد
entries.english: If I make outcry and complaint because of one bitter thing, may the dust of a hundred roads be on parts of me!

entries.farsi: لذت دست شكر بخشت بداشت / ‏اندر اين بطيخ تلخى كى گذاشت
entries.english: It had the enjoyment of your sugar-bestowing hand: how could it leave any bitterness in this melon?”

entries.farsi: از محبت تلخها شيرين شود / از محبت مسها زرين شود
entries.english: By love bitter things become sweet; by love pieces of copper become golden;

entries.farsi: از محبت دردها صافى شود / از محبت دردها شافى شود
entries.english: By love dregs become clear; by love pains become healing;

entries.farsi: از محبت مرده زنده مى‏كنند / از محبت شاه بنده مى‏كنند
entries.english: By love the dead is made living; by love the king is made a slave.

entries.farsi: اين محبت هم نتيجه‏ى دانش است / ‏كى گزافه بر چنين تختى نشست
entries.english: This love, moreover, is the result of knowledge: who sat in foolishness’ on such a throne?

entries.farsi: دانش ناقص كجا اين عشق زاد / عشق زايد ناقص اما بر جماد
entries.english: On what occasion did deficient knowledge give birth to this love? Deficient gives birth to love, but for that which is lifeless.

entries.farsi: بر جمادى رنگ مطلوبى چو ديد / از صفيرى بانگ محبوبى شنيد
entries.english: When it sees in a lifeless being the colour of a desired one, it heard the voice of a beloved in a whistle.

entries.farsi: دانش ناقص نداند فرق را / الجرم خورشيد داند برق را
entries.english: Deficient knowledge cannot discriminate: of necessity it deems the lightning to be the sun.

entries.farsi: ز انكه ناقص تن بود مرحوم رحم / ‏نيست بر مرحوم اليق لعن و زخم
entries.english: When the Prophet called the “deficient” accursed, as interpreted was “deficiency of mind,”

entries.farsi: چون كه ملعون خواند ناقص را رسول / ‏بود در تاويل نقصان عقول
entries.english: Because one whose body is deficient is the object of mercy: cursing and repulse against the object of mercy are improper.

entries.farsi: نقص عقل است آن كه بد رنجورى است / ‏موجب لعنت سزاى دورى است
entries.english: It is deficiency of mind that is the bad disease: it is the cause of curse and merits banishment,

entries.farsi: ز انكه تكميل خردها دور نيست / ‏ليك تكميل بدن مقدور نيست
entries.english: Forasmuch as the perfecting of minds is not remote, but the perfecting of the body is not within our power.

entries.farsi: كفر و فرعونى هر گبر بعيد / جمله از نقصان عقل آمد پديد
entries.english: The villainy and Pharaoh-like pride of every infidel who is far have all been produced by deficiency of mind.

entries.farsi: بهر نقصان بدن آمد فرج / ‏در نبى كه ما على الاعمى حرج
entries.english: Relief for bodily deficiency has come in the Qur’an— it is no crime in the blind man.

entries.farsi: برق آفل باشد و بس بى‏وفا / آفل از باقى ندانى بى‏صفا
entries.english: Lightning is transient and very faithless: without clearness you will not know the transient from the permanent.

entries.farsi: برق خندد بر كه مى‏خندد بگو / بر كسى كه دل نهد بر نور او
entries.english: The lightning laughs: say, at whom is it laughing? At him that sets his heart upon its light.

entries.farsi: نورهاى چرخ ببريده پى است / ‏آن چو ال شرقى و ال غربى كى است
entries.english: The lights of the sky are hamstrung: how are they like neither of the east nor of the west?

entries.farsi: برق را چون يخطف األبصار دان / ‏نور باقى را همه انصار دان
entries.english: Regard the lightning as takes away the sight; Regard the everlasting Light as entirely Helpers.’

entries.farsi: بر كف دريا فرس را راندن / ‏نامه‏اى در نور برقى خواندن
entries.english: To ride horse upon the foam of the sea, to read a letter in a flash of lightning,

entries.farsi: از حريصى عاقبت ناديدن است / ‏بر دل و بر عقل خود خنديدن است
entries.english: Is, to fail, because of covetousness, to see the end; it is, to laugh at your own mind and intellect.

entries.farsi: عاقبت بين است عقل از خاصيت / ‏نفس باشد كاو نبيند عاقبت
entries.english: Intellect, by its proper nature, is a seer of the end; it is the fleshly soul that does not see the end.

entries.farsi: عقل كاو مغلوب نفس او نفس شد / مشترى مات زحل شد نحس شد
entries.english: The intellect that is vanquished by the flesh becomes the flesh: Jupiter is checkmated by Saturn and becomes inauspicious.

entries.farsi: هم درين نحسى بگردان اين نظر / در كسى كه كرد نحست درنگر
entries.english: Still, turn this gaze upon this bad omen, look on that One who made you ill-starred.

entries.farsi: آن نظر كه بنگرد اين جر و مد / او ز نحسى سوى سعدى نقب زد
entries.english: The gaze that surveys this ebb and flow pierces from the inauspicious influence to the auspicious.

entries.farsi: ز آن همى‏گرداندت حالى به حال / ‏ضد به ضد پيدا كنان در انتقال
entries.english: He continually turns you from one state to another, manifesting opposite by means of opposite in the change,

entries.farsi: تا كه خوفت زايد از ذات الشمال / ‏لذت ذات اليمين يرجى الرجال
entries.english: For the purpose that fear of the left hand side may bring to birth in you the delight of “the men are led to hope for the right hand side,”

entries.farsi: تا دو پر باشى كه مرغ يك پره / ‏عاجز آيد از پريدن اى سره
entries.english: So that you may have two wings; for the bird that has one wing is unable to fly, O excellent.

entries.farsi: يا رها كن تا نيايم در كالم / ‏يا بده دستور تا گويم تمام
entries.english: Either let me not come to speech, or give me leave to tell to the end.

entries.farsi: ور نه اين خواهى نه آن فرمان تراست / ‏كس چه داند مر ترا مقصد كجاست
entries.english: But if You will neither this nor that, it is Yours to command: how should any one know what You intend?

entries.farsi: جان ابراهيم بايد تا به نور / بيند اندر نار فردوس و قصور
entries.english: One needs to have the spirit of Abraham to see in the fire Paradise and its palaces by the light;

entries.farsi: پايه پايه بر رود بر ماه و خور / تا نماند همچو حلقه بند در
entries.english: And mount step by step above the moon and the sun, lest he remain like the door-ring fastened on the door;

entries.farsi: اين جهان تن غلط انداز شد / جز مر آن را كاو ز شهوت باز شد
entries.english: And, like the Friend’, pass beyond the Seventh Heaven, saying, “I love not them that set.”

entries.farsi: چون خليل از آسمان هفتمين / ‏بگذرد كه ال أُحِبُّ اآلفلين
entries.english: This bodily world is deceptive, save to him that has escaped from lust.

sections.title_fa: ‏تتمه‏ى حسد آن حشم بر آن غالم خاص
sections.title_en: Conclusion of how the retainers envied the King’s favourite slave.

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى شاه و اميران و حسد / بر غالم خاص و سلطان خرد
entries.english: The story of the King and the Amirs and their envy of the favourite slave and lord of wisdom

entries.farsi: دور ماند از جر جرار كالم / ‏باز بايد گشت و كرد آن را تمام
entries.english: This tale been left far on account of the powerful attraction of the discourse. We must turn back and conclude it.

entries.farsi: باغبان ملك با اقبال و بخت / ‏چون درختى را نداند از درخت
entries.english: The happy and fortunate gardener of the kingdom— how should not he know one tree from another?

entries.farsi: آن درختى را كه تلخ و رد بود / و آن درختى كه يكش هفصد بود
entries.english: The tree that is bitter and damned and the tree whose one is seven hundred-

entries.farsi: كى برابر دارد اندر تربيت / ‏چون ببيندشان به چشم عاقبت
entries.english: How, in rearing, should he deem equal, when he beholds them with the eye of the end,

entries.farsi: كان درختان را نهايت چيست بر / گر چه يكسانند اين دم در نظر
entries.english: What fruit those trees will ultimately bear, though at this moment they are alike in appearance’?

entries.farsi: شيخ كاو ينظر بنور اهلل شد / از نهايت وز نخست آگاه شد
entries.english: The Shaykh who has become seeing by the light of God has become acquainted with the end and the beginning.

entries.farsi: چشم آخر بين ببست از بهر حق / ‏چشم آخر بين گشاد اندر سبق
entries.english: He has shut for God’s sake the eye that sees the stable; he has opened, in priority, the eye that sees the end.

entries.farsi: آن حسودان بد درختان بوده‏اند / تلخ گوهر شور بختان بوده‏اند
entries.english: Those envious ones were bad trees; they were ill-fortuned ones of bitter stock.

entries.farsi: از حسد جوشان و كف مى‏ريختند / در نهانى مكر مى‏انگيختند
entries.english: They were boiling and foaming with envy, and were starting plots in secret,

entries.farsi: تا غالم خاص را گردن زنند / بيخ او را از زمانه بر كنند
entries.english: That they might behead the favourite slave and tear up his root from the world;

entries.farsi: شاه از آن اسرار واقف آمده / ‏همچو بو بكر ربابى تن زده
entries.english: How should he perish, since the King was his soul, and his root was under the protection of God?

entries.farsi: چون شود فانى چو جانش شاه بود / بيخ او در عصمت اهلل بود
entries.english: The King had become aware of those secret thoughts, like Bu Bakr-i Rababi he kept silence.

entries.farsi: در تماشاى دل بد گوهران / ‏مى‏زدى خنبك بر آن كوزه‏گران
entries.english: In the spectacle of the hearts of evil-natured ones he was clapping his hands at those potters.

entries.farsi: مكر مى‏سازند قومى حيله‏مند / تا كه شه را در فقاعى در كنند
entries.english: Some cunning people devise stratagems to get the King into a beer keg;

entries.farsi: پادشاهى بس عظيمى بى‏كران / ‏در فقاعى كى بگنجد اى خران
entries.english: a King exceedingly grand and illimitable— how should He be contained in a beer barrel, O donkeys?

entries.farsi: از براى شاه دامى دوختند / آخر اين تدبير از او آموختند
entries.english: They knitted a net for the King; after all, they learned this contrivance from Him.

entries.farsi: نحس شاگردى كه با استاد خويش / ‏همسرى آغازد و آيد به پيش
entries.english: Ill-starred is the pupil that begins rivalry with his master and comes forward.

entries.farsi: با كدام استاد استاد جهان / ‏پيش او يكسان و هويدا و نهان
entries.english: With what master? The master of the world, to whom the manifest and the occult are alike;

entries.farsi: چشم او ينظر بنور اللهَّ شده / ‏پرده‏هاى جهل را خارق بده
entries.english: Whose eyes have become seeing by the light of God and have rent the veils of ignorance.

entries.farsi: از دل سوراخ چون كهنه گليم / ‏پرده‏اى بندد به پيش آن حكيم
entries.english: a veil of heart, full of holes as an old blanket, he puts it on in the presence of that Sage.

entries.farsi: پرده مى‏خندد بر او با صد دهان / ‏هر دهانى گشته اشكافى بر آن
entries.english: The veil laughs at him with a hundred mouths, every mouth having become a slit to that.

entries.farsi: گويد آن استاد مر شاگرد را / اى كم از سگ نيستت با من وفا
entries.english: The master says to the disciple, “O you, who are less than a dog, have you no faithfulness to me?

entries.farsi: خود مرا استا مگير آهن گسل / ‏همچو خود شاگرد گير و كوردل
entries.english: Even suppose I am not a master and an iron-breaker, suppose I am a disciple like yourself and blind of heart,

entries.farsi: نه از منت يارى است در جان و روان / ‏بى‏منت آبى نمى‏گردد روان
entries.english: Have not you help in spirit and mind from me? Without me no water is set flowing for you

entries.farsi: پس دل من كارگاه بخت تست / چه شكنى اين كارگاه اى نادرست
entries.english: Therefore my heart is the factory of your fortune: why would you break this factory, O unrighteous one?”

entries.farsi: گويى‏اش پنهان زنم آتش زنه / ‏نه به قلب از قلب باشد روزنه
entries.english: You may say that you kindle the flame’ against him in secret; but is there not a window between heart and heart?

entries.farsi: آخر از روزن ببيند فكر تو / دل گواهى مى‏دهد زين ذكر تو
entries.english: After all, he sees your thought through the window: your heart gives testimony as to what you are meditating.

entries.farsi: گير در رويت نمالد از كرم / ‏هر چه گويى خندد و گويد نعم
entries.english: Suppose that, from kindness, he does not rebuke you to your face, whatever you say, he smiles and says “Yes”

entries.farsi: او نمى‏خندد ز ذوق مالشت / ‏او همى‏خندد بر آن اسگالشت
entries.english: He does not smile from pleasure at your stroking; he smiles at that thought of yours.

entries.farsi: پس خداعى را خداعى شد جزا / كاسه زن كوزه بخور اينك سزا
entries.english: So a deceit is paid with a deceit: strike with a cup, get struck with a jug—serve you right!

entries.farsi: گر بدى با تو و را خنده‏ى رضا / صد هزاران گل شكفتى مر ترا
entries.english: Were his smile at you one of approval, hundreds of thousands of flowers would blossom for you.

entries.farsi: چون دل او در رضا آرد عمل / ‏آفتابى دان كه آيد در حمل
entries.english: When his heart works in approval, deem it a sun entering Aries,

entries.farsi: زو بخندد هم نهار و هم بهار / در هم آميزد شكوفه و سبزه‏زار
entries.english: Because of whom’ both the day and the spring smile, and blossoms and green fields are mingled together,

entries.farsi: صد هزاران بلبل و قمرى نوا / افكنند اندر جهان بى‏نوا
entries.english: And myriads of nightingales and ringdoves pour their song into the unfilled world.

entries.farsi: چون كه برگ روح خود زرد و سياه / ‏مى‏ببينى چون ندانى خشم شاه
entries.english: When you see the leaves of your spirit yellow and black, how know you not the anger of the King?

entries.farsi: آفتاب شاه در برج عتاب / ‏مى‏كند روها سيه همچون كباب
entries.english: The King’s sun, in the sign of reproach, makes faces black as a piece of roasted meat.

entries.farsi: آن عطارد را ورقها جان ماست / ‏آن سپيدى و آن سيه ميزان ماست
entries.english: Our souls are leaves for that Mercury: that white and black is our standard.

entries.farsi: باز منشورى نويسد سرخ و سبز / تا رهند ارواح از سودا و عجز
entries.english: Again, he writes a patent in red and green that spirits may be delivered from melancholy and despair.

entries.farsi: سرخ و سبز افتاد نسخ نو بهار / چون خط قوس و قزح در اعتبار
entries.english: Red and green are spring’s cancellation; in regard like the lines of the rainbow.

sections.title_fa: عكس تعظيم پيغام سليمان عليه السلام در دل بلقيس از صورت حقير هدهد
sections.title_en: How reverence for the message of Solomon, on whom be peace, was reflected in the heart of Bilqis from the despicable form of the hoopoe.

entries.farsi: رحمت صد تو بر آن بلقيس باد / كه خدايش عقل صد مرده بداد
entries.english: Hundredfold mercy on that Bilqis to whom God gave the intellect of a hundred men!

entries.farsi: خواند او آن نكتهاى با شمول / ‏با حقارت ننگريد اندر رسول
entries.english: A hoopoe brought the letter with the sign-manual from Solomon— a few eloquent words.

entries.farsi: هدهدى نامه بياورد و نشان / ‏از سليمان چند حرفى با بيان
entries.english: She read those pregnant sayings, she did not look with contempt on the messenger.

entries.farsi: جسم هدهد ديد و جان عنقاش ديد / حس چو كفى ديد و دل درياش ديد
entries.english: Her eye saw him as a hoopoe, her spirit saw him as the ‘Anqa; her senses saw him as a fleck of foam, her heart saw him as the sea.

entries.farsi: عقل با حس زين طلسمات دو رنگ / ‏چون محمد با ابو جهالن به جنگ
entries.english: Because of these two-coloured talismans the intellect is at war with the senses, as Mohammed with the likes of Abu Jahl.

entries.farsi: كافران ديدند احمد را بشر / چون نديدند از وى انْشَقَّ القمر
entries.english: The infidels regarded Ahmad as a man, since they did not see in him the moon was cleft asunder.

entries.farsi: خاك زن در ديده‏ى حس بين خويش / ‏ديده‏ى حس دشمن عقل است و كيش
entries.english: Throw dust on your sense-perceiving eye: the sensuous eye is the enemy of intellect and religion.

entries.farsi: ديده‏ى حس را خدا اعماش خواند / بت پرستش گفت و ضد ماش خواند
entries.english: God has called the sensuous eye blind; He has said that it is an idolater and our foe,

entries.farsi: ز انكه او كف ديد و دريا را نديد / ز انكه حالى ديد و فردا را نديد
entries.english: Because it saw the foam and not the sea, because it saw the present and not to-morrow

entries.farsi: خواجه‏ى فردا و حالى پيش او / او نمى‏بيند ز گنجى جز تسو
entries.english: The master of tomorrow and of the present before it; of a treasure it sees not a penny.

entries.farsi: ذره‏اى ز آن آفتاب آرد پيام / ‏آفتاب آن ذره را گردد غالم
entries.english: A mote bring a message from yonder Sun, the sun would become a slave to that mote.

entries.farsi: قطره‏اى كز بحر وحدت شد سفير / هفت بحر آن قطره را باشد اسير
entries.english: The drop that has become an envoy from the Sea of Unity— the seven seas would be captive to that drop.

entries.farsi: گر كف خاكى شود چالاك او / پيش خاكش سر نهد افلاک او
entries.english: If a handful of earth becomes His courier, His heavens will lay their heads before His earth.

entries.farsi: خاك آدم چون كه شد چالاك حق / ‏پيش خاكش سر نهند امالك حق
entries.english: When the earth of Adam became God’s courier, God’s angels laid their heads before His earth.

entries.farsi: السَّماءُ انْشَقَّتْ آخر از چه بود / از يكى چشمى كه خاكى بر گشود
entries.english: Wherefore, pray, that heaven was rent asunder? Because of one eye that an earthly creature opened

entries.farsi: خاك از دردى نشيند زير آب / ‏خاك بين كز عرش بگذشت از شتاب
entries.english: Earth, from its grossness, settles beneath water; see how earth has sped beyond the empyrean!

entries.farsi: آن لطافت پس بدان كز آب نيست / ‏جز عطاى مبدع وهاب نيست
entries.english: Know, then, that the subtlety is not from the water: it is only the gift of the Bounteous Originator.

entries.farsi: گر كند سفلى هوا و نار را / ور ز گل او بگذراند خار را
entries.english: If He make air and fire low, and if He lets the thorn surpass the rose,

entries.farsi: حاكم است و يَفْعَلُ اللهَُّ ما يشاء / كاو ز عين درد انگيزد دوا
entries.english: He is the Ruler God does what He wills: from the very self of pain He raises the remedy.

entries.farsi: گر هوا و نار را سفلى كند / تيرگى و دردى و ثقلى كند
entries.english: If He make air and fire low, and make darkness and grossness and heaviness,

entries.farsi: ور زمين و آب را علوى كند / راه گردون را بپا مطوى كند
entries.english: And if He makes earth and water high, and makes the path of heaven traversed by foot

entries.farsi: پس يقين شد كه تُعِزُّ مَنْ تشاء / خاكيى را گفت پرها بر گشا
entries.english: Then it has become certain that You exalt whom you will: He said to an earthly creature, “Unfold your wings.”

entries.farsi: آتشى را گفت رو ابليس شو / زير هفتم خاك با تلبيس شو
entries.english: To the creature of fire He said, “ Go, become Iblis: go under the Seventh Earth with imposture!

entries.farsi: آدم خاكى برو تو بر سها / ‏اى بليس آتشى رو تا ثرى
entries.english: O earthly Adam, go above Venus ; O fiery Iblis, go to the Earth.

entries.farsi: چار طبع و علت اولى نى‏ام / ‏در تصرف دايما من باقى‏ام
entries.english: I am not the four temperaments or the first cause; I am ever remaining in control.

entries.farsi: كار من بى‏علت است و مستقيم / ‏هست تقديرم نه علت اى سقيم
entries.english: My action is uncaused and upright: I have pre-determination, no cause, O infirm one.

entries.farsi: عادت خود را بگردانم به وقت / ‏اين غبار از پيش بنشانم به وقت
entries.english: I alter My custom at the time: at the time I lay the dust in front.

entries.farsi: بحر را گويم كه هين پر نار شو / گويم آتش را كه رو گلزار شو
entries.english: I say to the sea, Listen, be full of fire!’ I say to the fire, Go, be a rose-garden!’

entries.farsi: كوه را گويم سبك شو همچو پشم / ‏چرخ را گويم فرو در پيش چشم
entries.english: I say to the mountain, ‘Be light as wool!’ I say to heaven, ‘ Be rent asunder before the eye!’

entries.farsi: چشمه‏ى خورشيد را سازيم خشك / ‏چشمه‏ى خون را به فن سازيم مشك
entries.english: I say, ‘ O sun, be joined to the moon!’ I make them both like two black clouds.

entries.farsi: گويم اى خورشيد مقرون شو به ماه / ‏هر دو را سازم چو دو ابر سياه
entries.english: We make dry the fountain of the sun: by Our are We turn into musk the fountain of blood.”

entries.farsi: آفتاب و مه چو دو گاو سياه / ‏يوغ بر گردن ببنددشان اله
entries.english: Sun and moon like two black oxen : God will fasten a yoke upon their necks.

sections.title_fa: انكار فلسفى بر قرائت إِنْ أَصْ بَحَ ماؤُكُمْ غَ وْرا
sections.title_en: How a philosopher showed disbelief at the recitation of, “if your water shall have sunk into the ground.”

entries.farsi: مقريى مى‏خواند از روى كتاب / ‏ماؤُكُمْ غَوْراً ز چشمه بندم آب
entries.english: A teacher of Qur’an-recitation was reading from the page of the Book, “your water sunk into the ground: I stop the water from the spring,

entries.farsi: آب را در غورها پنهان كنم / ‏چشمه‏ها را خشك و خشكستان كنم
entries.english: And hide the water in the depths, and make the springs dry and a place of drought,

entries.farsi: آب را در چشمه كى آرد دگر / جز من بى‏مثل با فضل و خطر
entries.english: Who shall bring the water to the spring again except Me who have no like, the Gracious, the Glorious?”

entries.farsi: فلسفى منطقى مستهان / ‏مى‏گذشت از سوى مكتب آن زمان
entries.english: A contemptible philosopher and logician was passing beside the school at that moment.

entries.farsi: چون كه بشنيد آيت او از ناپسند / گفت آريم آب را ما با كلند
entries.english: When he heard the verse, he said in disapproval, “We bring the water with a mattock;

entries.farsi: شب بخفت و ديد او يك شير مرد / زد طپانچه هر دو چشمش كور كرد
entries.english: With strokes of the spade and the sharpness of the axe we bring the water up from below.”

entries.farsi: ما بزخم بيل و تيزى تبر / آب را آريم از پستى ز بر
entries.english: At night he fell asleep and saw a lion-hearted man gave a blow on the face and blinded both his eyes,

entries.farsi: گفت زين دو چشمه‏ى چشم اى شقى / ‏با تبر نورى بر آر ار صادقى
entries.english: And said, “O wretch, if you are speaking the truth, bring up some light with an axe from these two springs of vision.”

entries.farsi: روز بر جست و دو چشم كور ديد / نور فايض از دو چشمش ناپديد
entries.english: At day he jumped up and found two blind eyes: from both his eyes the overflowing light had vanished.

entries.farsi: گر بناليدى و مستغفر شدى / ‏نور رفته از كرم ظاهر شدى
entries.english: If he had moaned and asked pardon, the departed light would have appeared through kindness;

entries.farsi: ليك استغفار هم در دست نيست / ‏ذوق توبه نقل هر سر مست نيست
entries.english: But asking pardon, also, is not in hands: the savour of repentance is not the dessert of every drunk.

entries.farsi: زشتى اعمال و شومى جحود / راه توبه بر دل او بسته بود
entries.english: The wickedness of actions and the disastrousness of denial had barred the way of repentance to his heart.

entries.farsi: دل به سختى همچو روى سنگ گشت / ‏چون شكافد توبه آن را بهر كشت
entries.english: His heart became in hardness as the face of a rock: how should repentance cleave it for sowing?

entries.farsi: چون شعيبى كو كه تا او را دعا / بهر كشتن خاك سازد كوه را
entries.english: Where is one like Shu’ayb, that by prayer he may make the mountain earth for sowing?

entries.farsi: از نياز و اعتقاد آن خليل / ‏گشت ممكن امر صعب و مستحيل
entries.english: Through the supplication and belief of that Friend the thing that was hard and impossible became possible.

entries.farsi: يا به دريوزه‏ى مقوقس از رسول / ‏سنگ‏الخى مزرعى شد با اصول
entries.english: Or, through the Muqawqis’ begging the Prophet a stony ground became a good wheat field

entries.farsi: همچنين بر عكس آن انكار مرد / مس كند زر را و صلحى را نبرد
entries.english: So, contrariwise, a man’s disbelief turns gold into copper and peace into war.

entries.farsi: كهرباى مسخ آمد اين دغا / خاك قابل را كند سنگ و حصا
entries.english: This falseness draws after it an evil transformation: it turns ground capable into stones and pebbles.

entries.farsi: هر دلى را سجده هم دستور نيست / ‏مزد رحمت قسم هر مزدور نيست
entries.english: Nor is it granted to every heart to fall down in prayer: the wages of mercy are not the portion of every hireling.

entries.farsi: مى‏ببايد تاب و آبى توبه را / شرط شد برق و سحابى توبه را
entries.english: Beware! Do not commit crime and sin in reliance on, “I will repent and come to refuge”

entries.farsi: هين بپشت آن مكن جرم و گناه / ‏كه كنم توبه در آيم در پناه
entries.english: For repentance, there must be a glow and a flood: lightning and clouds are the condition indispensable to repentance.

entries.farsi: آتش و آبى ببايد ميوه را / واجب آيد ابر و برق اين شيوه را
entries.english: There must be fire and water for the fruit: clouds and lightning are necessary for this accomplishment.

entries.farsi: تا نباشد برق دل و ابر دو چشم / ‏كى نشيند آتش تهديد و خشم
entries.english: Until there is the lightning of the heart and the rain-clouds of the two eyes, how shall the fire of menace and wrath be allayed?

entries.farsi: كى برويد سبزه‏ى ذوق وصال / ‏كى بجوشد چشمه‏ها ز آب زالل
entries.english: How shall the herbage grow, of the delight of union? How shall the fountains of clear water gush forth?

entries.farsi: كى گلستان راز گويد با چمن / ‏كى بنفشه عهد بندد با سمن
entries.english: How shall the rose-beds tell their secret to the garden? How shall the violet make an engagement with the jasmine?

entries.farsi: كى چنارى كف گشايد در دعا / كى درختى سر فشاند در هوا
entries.english: How shall a plane-tree open its hands in prayer? How shall any tree toss its head in the air?

entries.farsi: كى شكوفه آستين پر نثار / بر فشاندن گيرد ايام بهار
entries.english: How shall the blossoms begin to shake out their sleeves full of largesse in the days of spring?

entries.farsi: كى فروزد الله را رخ همچو خون / ‏كى گل از كيسه بر آرد زر برون
entries.english: How shall the cheeks of the anemone flame like blood? How shall the rose bring gold out of its purse?

entries.farsi: كى بيايد بلبل و گل بو كند / كى چو طالب فاخته كوكو كند
entries.english: How shall the nightingale come and smell the rose? How shall the dove say “coo, coo,” as one that seeks?

entries.farsi: كى بگويد لكلك آن لك لك به جان / ‏لك چه باشد ملك تست اى مستعان
entries.english: How shall the stork utter with its soul the cry lak, lak? What is lak? “Yours is the kingdom, O You whose help is invoked.”

entries.farsi: كى نمايد خاك اسرار ضمير / كى شود بى‏آسمان بستان منير
entries.english: How shall the earth show forth the secrets of its inmost mind? How shall the garden become radiant as the sky?

entries.farsi: آن لطافتها نشان شاهدى است / ‏آن نشان پاى مرد عابدى است
entries.english: From where have they gathered those garments? Of them from One who is Bounteous and Merciful.

entries.farsi: از كجا آورده‏اند آن حله‏ها / من كريم من رحيم كلها
entries.english: Those graces are the signs of Fitness: they are the foot- prints of a man devoted to service.

entries.farsi: آن شود شاد از نشان كاو ديد شاه / ‏چون نديد او را نباشد انتباه
entries.english: That person that has seen the King is gladdened by the sign; when one has not seen Him, there is no recognition.

entries.farsi: ُروح آن كس كاو به هنگام أَ لَسْ ت / ‏ديد رب خويش و شد بى‏خويش و مست
entries.english: The spirit of that one who at the time of Am not I? Saw his Lord and became beside himself and intoxicated

entries.farsi: او شناسد بوى مى كاو مى بخورد / چون نخورد او مى چه داند بوى كرد
entries.english: He knows the scent of the wine because he drank it; when he has not drunk it, he cannot scent it.

entries.farsi: ز انكه حكمت همچو ناقه‏ى ضاله است / ‏همچو دالله شهان را داله است
entries.english: For Wisdom is like a stray camel: like a go-between, it guides to kings.

entries.farsi: تو ببينى خواب در يك خوش لقا / كاو دهد وعده و نشانى مر ترا
entries.english: You behold in dream a person of pleasing countenance, who gives you a promise and a sign

entries.farsi: كه مراد تو شود اينك نشان / ‏كه بپيش آيد ترا فردا فالن
entries.english: That your desire will come to pass; here is the sign— such and such a person will meet you to-morrow.

entries.farsi: يك نشانى آن كه او باشد سوار / يك نشانى كه ترا گيرد كنار
entries.english: One sign is that he will be riding; one sign is that he will clasp you to his breast;

entries.farsi: يك نشانى كه بخندد پيش تو / يك نشان كه دست بندد پيش تو
entries.english: One sign is that he will smile before you; one sign is that he will fold his hands in your presence;

entries.farsi: يك نشانى آن كه اين خواب از هوس / ‏چون شود فردا نگويى پيش كس
entries.english: One sign is that when the morrow comes you will not tell this dream to any one, though you would want to.

entries.farsi: ز ان نشان با والد يحيى بگفت / ‏كه نيايى تا سه روز اصال به گفت
entries.english: Concerning that sign, He said to the father of Yahya, “You shall not begin to speak at all till three days.

entries.farsi: تا سه شب خامش كن از نيك و بدت / ‏اين نشان باشد كه يحيى آيدت
entries.english: For three nights keep silence as to your good and ill: this will be the sign that Yahya will come to you.

entries.farsi: دم مزن سه روز اندر گفت‏وگو / كاين سكوت است آيت مقصود تو
entries.english: During three days do not breathe a word, for this silence is the sign of your purpose.

entries.farsi: هين مياور اين نشان را تو به گفت / ‏وين سخن را دار اندر دل نهفت
entries.english: Beware! Do not speak of this sign, and keep this matter hidden in your heart.”

entries.farsi: اين نشانها گويدش همچون شكر / اين چه باشد صد نشانى دگر
