entries.english: He will sweetly tell these signs to him. What are these signs? a hundred signs besides.

entries.farsi: اين نشان آن بود كان ملك و جاه / ‏كه همى‏جويى بيابى از اله
entries.english: This is the sign that you will gain from God the kingdom and power that you are seeking

entries.farsi: آن كه مى‏گريى به شبهاى دراز / و انكه مى‏سوزى سحرگه در نياز
entries.english: That you weep continually in the long nights, and that you are always ardent in supplication at the hour of dawn;

entries.farsi: آن كه بى‏آن روز تو تاريك شد / همچو دوكى گردنت باريك شد
entries.english: That, in the absence of that, your day has become dark; your neck has become thin as a spindle;

entries.farsi: و آن چه دادى هر چه دارى در زكات / ‏چون زكات پاك بازان رختهات
entries.english: And what you have given in alms all that you possess, your belongings like the alms of those who gamble all away;

entries.farsi: رختها دادى و خواب و رنگ رو / سر فدا كردى و گشتى همچو مو
entries.english: You have given up your belongings and sleep and the colour of your face, and sacrificed your head and become as a hair;

entries.farsi: چند در آتش نشستى همچو عود / چند پيش تيغ رفتى همچو خود
entries.english: You have sat—how often!--in the fire, like aloes-wood; that you have gone—how often!—to meet the sword, like a helmet.

entries.farsi: زين چنين بى‏چارگيها صد هزار / خوى عشاق است و نايد در شمار
entries.english: A hundred thousand such acts of helplessness are habitual to lovers, and cannot be reckoned.

entries.farsi: چون كه شب اين خواب ديدى روز شد / از اميدش روز تو پيروز شد
entries.english: After you have had this dream at night, the day breaks; through hope thereof your day becomes triumphant.

entries.farsi: چشم گردان كرده‏اى بر چپ و راست / ‏كان نشان و آن عالمتها كجاست
entries.english: You have turned your eye to left and right, where is that sign and those tokens.

entries.farsi: بر مثال برگ مى‏لرزى كه واى / ‏گر رود روز و نشان نايد به جاى
entries.english: You are trembling like a leaf, “Alas, if the day depart and the sign come not to pass!”

entries.farsi: مى‏دوى در كوى و بازار و سرا / چون كسى كاو گم كند گوساله را
entries.english: You are running in street and market and into houses, like one that should lose a calf.

entries.farsi: خواجه خير است اين دوادو چيستت / ‏گم شده اينجا كه دارى كيستت
entries.english: “Is it good, Sir? Why are you running to and fro? Who belonging to you is it that you have lost here?”

entries.farsi: گر بگويم نك نشانم فوت شد / چون نشان شد فوت وقت موت شد
entries.english: “It is good,” you tell him, “but none may know my good except myself.

entries.farsi: گويى‏اش خير است ليكن خير من / ‏كس نشايد كه بداند غير من
entries.english: If I tell it, lo, my sign is missed, and when the sign is missed, the hour of death is come.”

entries.farsi: بنگرى در روى هر مرد سوار / گويدت منگر مرا ديوانه‏وار
entries.english: You peer into the face of every rider: he says to you, “Do not look at me like a madman.”

entries.farsi: گويى‏اش من صاحبى گم كرده‏ام / ‏رو به جستجوى او آورده‏ام
entries.english: You say to him, “I have lost a friend; I have set out to seek him.

entries.farsi: دولتت پاينده بادا اى سوار / رحم كن بر عاشقان معذور دار
entries.english: May your fortune be lasting, O rider! Have pity on lovers and excuse.”

entries.farsi: چون طلب كردى به جد آمد نظر / جد خطا نكند چنين آمد خبر
entries.english: When you have made search looking has been in earnest— earnest endeavour does not fail: so the Tradition has come down

entries.farsi: ناگهان آمد سوارى نيك بخت / ‏پس گرفت اندر كنارت سخت سخت
entries.english: Suddenly comes a blessed rider; then he clasps you very closely to his breast.

entries.farsi: تو شدى بى‏هوش و افتادى به طاق / ‏بى‏خبر گفت اينت سالوس و نفاق
entries.english: You become senseless and fall to vaunting; the ignorant man says, “Here is fraud and hypocrisy.”

entries.farsi: او چه مى‏بيند در او اين شور چيست / ‏او نداند كان نشان وصل كيست
entries.english: How does he see what this enthusiasm in him is? He knows not with whom that is the sign of union.

entries.farsi: اين نشان در حق او باشد كه ديد / آن دگر را كى نشان آيد پديد
entries.english: This sign concerns him that has seen: how should the sign appear to the other one?

entries.farsi: هر زمان كز وى نشانى مى‏رسيد / شخص را جانى به جانى مى‏رسيد
entries.english: Every moment that a sign was coming from Him, a spirit was coming into that person’s spirit.

entries.farsi: ماهى بى‏چاره را پيش آمد آب / ‏اين نشانها تِلْكَ آياتُ الكتاب
entries.english: Water has reached the helpless fish. These signs are those are the signs of the Book.

entries.farsi: پس نشانيها كه اندر انبياست / ‏خاص آن جان را بود كاو آشناست
entries.english: Hence the signs which are in the prophets are peculiar to him who is a friend.

entries.farsi: اين سخن ناقص بماند و بى‏قرار / دل ندارم بى‏دلم معذور دار
entries.english: This discourse remains imperfect and unsettled; I have no heart, I am out of my mind: excuse me.

entries.farsi: ذره‏ها را كى تواند كس شمرد / خاصه آن كاو عشق عقل او ببرد
entries.english: How can any one number the motes, especially that one whose understanding has been transported by Love?

entries.farsi: مى‏شمارم برگهاى باغ را / مى‏شمارم بانگ كبك و زاغ را
entries.english: Shall I number the leaves of the garden? Shall I number the cries of the partridge and the crow?

entries.farsi: در شمار اندر نيايد ليك من / ‏مى‏شمارم بهر رشد ممتحن
entries.english: They come not into computation, but I enumerate them for the guidance of him that is put to trial.

entries.farsi: نحس كيوان يا كه سعد مشترى / ‏نايد اندر حصر گر چه بشمرى
entries.english: The sinister influence of Saturn and the auspicious influence of Jupiter come not into computation, though you may enumerate;

entries.farsi: ليك هم بعضى از اين هر دو اثر / شرح بايد كرد يعنى نفع و ضر
entries.english: But still, some of these two effects must be explained— that is, the benefit and injury

entries.farsi: تا شود معلوم آثار قضا / شمه اى مر اهل سعد و نحس را
entries.english: In order that some little part of the effects of the decree may be made known to the good-fortuned and the ill-starred

entries.farsi: طالع آن كس كه باشد مشترى / ‏شاد گردد از نشاط و سرورى
entries.english: He whose ascendant is Jupiter will be rejoiced by vivacity and eminence;

entries.farsi: و انكه را طالع زحل از هر شرور / احتياطش الزم آيد در امور
entries.english: And it will be necessary for him whose ascendant is Saturn to take precautions against every mischief in his affairs.

entries.farsi: گر بگويم آن زحل استاره را / ز آتشش سوزد مر آن بى‏چاره را
entries.english: If I should speak to one whose planet is Saturn of his fire, it would burn that unfortunate man.

entries.farsi: اذْكُرُوا اللهََّ شاه ما دستور داد / اندر آتش ديد ما را نور داد
entries.english: Our King has given permission,, “Commemorate Allah”: He saw us in the fire and gave us light.

entries.farsi: گفت اگر چه پاكم از ذكر شما / نيست اليق مر مرا تصويرها
entries.english: He has said, “ Although I far transcend your commemoration, the pictorial ideas are not suitable to Me,

entries.farsi: ذكر جسمانه خيال ناقص است / ‏وصف شاهانه از آنها خالص است
entries.english: Yet he that is intoxicated with imagination and fancy will never apprehend My essence without similes.”

entries.farsi: ليك هرگز مست تصوير و خيال / ‏در نيابد ذات ما را بى‏مثال
entries.english: Bodily commemoration is an imperfect imaginings: the Kingly attributes are remote from those.

entries.farsi: شاه را گويد كسى جواله نيست / ‏اين چه مدح است اين مگر آگاه نيست
entries.english: If any one says of a king, “He is not a weaver,” what praise is this? He is surely ignorant.

sections.title_fa: ‏انكار كردن موسى عليه السلام بر مناجات شبان
sections.title_en: How Moses, on whom be peace, took offence at the prayer of the shepherd.

entries.farsi: ديد موسى يك شبانى را به راه / ‏كاو همى‏گفت اى خدا و اى اله
entries.english: Moses saw a shepherd on the way, who was saying, “O God who chooses,

entries.farsi: تو كجايى تا شوم من چاكرت / ‏چارقت دوزم كنم شانه سرت
entries.english: Where are You, that I may become Your servant and sew Your shoes and comb Your head?

entries.farsi: جامه‏ات شويم شپشهايت كشم / ‏شير پيشت آورم اى محتشم
entries.english: That I may wash Your clothes and kill Your lice and bring milk to You, O worshipful One;

entries.farsi: دستكت بوسم بمالم پايكت / ‏وقت خواب آيد بروبم جايكت
entries.english: That I may kiss Your little hand and rub Your little foot, bedtime comes I may sweep Your little room,

entries.farsi: اى فداى تو همه بزهاى من / ‏اى به يادت هيهى و هيهاى من
entries.english: O You to whom all my goats be a sacrifice, O You in remembrance of whom are my cries of ay and ah!”

entries.farsi: اين نمط بى‏هوده مى‏گفت آن شبان / ‏گفت موسى با كى است اين اى فالن
entries.english: The shepherd was speaking foolish words in this wise. Moses said, “Man, to whom is this?”

entries.farsi: گفت با آن كس كه ما را آفريد / اين زمين و چرخ از او آمد پديد
entries.english: He answered, “To that One who created us; by whom this earth and sky were brought to sight.”

entries.farsi: گفت موسى هاى خيره‏سر شدى / ‏خود مسلمان ناشده كافر شدى
entries.english: “Listen!” said Moses, “ you have become very backsliding; indeed you have not become a Moslem, you have become an infidel.

entries.farsi: اين چه ژاژست و چه كفر است و فشار / پنبه‏اى اندر دهان خود فشار
entries.english: What babble is this? What blasphemy and raving? Stuff some cotton into your mouth!

entries.farsi: گند كفر تو جهان را گنده كرد / كفر تو ديباى دين را ژنده كرد
entries.english: The stench of your blasphemy has made the world stinking: your blasphemy has turned the silk robe of religion into rags.

entries.farsi: چارق و پا تابه اليق مر تراست / ‏آفتابى را چنينها كى رواست
entries.english: Shoes and socks are fitting for you, how are such things right for a Sun?

entries.farsi: گر نبندى زين سخن تو حلق را / آتشى آيد بسوزد خلق را
entries.english: If you do not stop your throat from these words, a fire will come and burn up the people.

entries.farsi: آتشى گر نامده ست اين دود چيست / ‏جان سيه گشته روان مردود چيست
entries.english: If a fire has not come) what is this smoke? Why has your soul become black and your spirit rejected?

entries.farsi: گر همى‏دانى كه يزدان داور است / ‏ژاژ و گستاخى ترا چون باور است
entries.english: If you know that God is the Judge, how is it right for you this doting talk and familiarity?

entries.farsi: دوستى بى‏خرد خود دشمنى است / ‏حق تعالى زين چنين خدمت غنى است
entries.english: Truly, the friendship of a witless man is enmity: the high God is not in want of suchlike service.

entries.farsi: با كه مى‏گويى تو اين با عم و خال / ‏جسم و حاجت در صفات ذو الجلال
entries.english: To whom are you saying this? To your paternal and maternal uncles? Are the body and its needs among the attributes of the Lord of glory?

entries.farsi: شير او نوشد كه در نشو و نماست / چارق او پوشد كه او محتاج پاست
entries.english: He that is waxing and growing drinks milk: he that has need of feet puts on shoes.

entries.farsi: ور براى بنده‏ش است اين گفت‏وگو / آن كه حق گفت او من است و من خود او
entries.english: And if these words are for His servant, of whom God said, ‘He is I and I myself am he’;

entries.farsi: آن كه گفت انى مرضت لم تعد / من شدم رنجور او تنها نشد
entries.english: Of whom He said, ‘Truly, I was sick and you did not visit Me’, ‘ I became ill, not he alone;

entries.farsi: آن كه بى‏يسمع و بى‏يبصر شده ست / ‏در حق آن بنده اين هم بى‏هده ست
entries.english: Who has become seeing by Me and hearing by Me This is foolish nonsense even in regard to that servant.

entries.farsi: بى‏ادب گفتن سخن با خاص حق / ‏دل بميراند سيه دارد ورق
entries.english: To speak irreverently to one chosen of God causes the heart to perish and keeps the page black.

entries.farsi: گر تو مردى را بخوانى فاطمه / ‏گر چه يك جنسند مرد و زن همه
entries.english: If you should call a man Fatima — though men and women are all of one kind

entries.farsi: قصد خون تو كند تا ممكن است / ‏گر چه خوش خو و حليم و ساكن است
entries.english: He will seek to murder you, so far as it is possible, albeit he is good-natured and forbearing and quiet.

entries.farsi: فاطمه مدح است در حق زنان / ‏مرد را گويى بود زخم سنان
entries.english: Fatima is praise in regard to women, if you address it to a man; it is the blow of a spearhead.

entries.farsi: دست و پا در حق ما استايش است / ‏در حق پاكى حق آاليش است
entries.english: Hand and foot are praise in relation to us; in relation to the holiness of God they are pollution.

entries.farsi: لَمْ يَلِدْ لَمْ يُولَدْ او را اليق است / ‏والد و مولود را او خالق است
entries.english: He begat not, He was not begotten are appropriate to Him: He is the Creator of begetter and begotten.

entries.farsi: هر چه جسم آمد والدت وصف اوست / ‏هر چه مولود است او زين سوى جوست
entries.english: Birth is the attribute of everything that is body: whatever is born is on this side of the river,

entries.farsi: ز انكه از كون و فساد است و مهين / ‏حادث است و محدثى خواهد يقين
entries.english: Because it is of becoming and decay and contemptible: it is originated and certainly requires an Originator.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى موسى دهانم دوختى / ‏و ز پشيمانى تو جانم سوختى
entries.english: He said, “O Moses, you have closed my mouth and you have burned my soul with repentance.”

entries.farsi: جامه را بدريد و آهى كرد تفت / ‏سر نهاد اندر بيابانى و رفت
entries.english: He rent his garment and heaved a sigh, and hastily turned his head towards the desert and went.

sections.title_fa: ‏عتاب كردن حق تعالى با موسى عليه السلام از بهر آن شبان
sections.title_en: How the high God rebuked Moses, on whom be peace, on account of the shepherd.

entries.farsi: وحى آمد سوى موسى از خدا / بنده‏ى ما را ز ما كردى جدا
entries.english: A revelation came to Moses from God— “You have parted My servant from Me.

entries.farsi: تو براى وصل كردن آمدى / نى براى فصل كردن آمدى
entries.english: Did you come to unite, or did you come to sever?

entries.farsi: تا توانى پا منه اندر فراق / ‏أبغض األشياء عندي الطالق
entries.english: So far as you canst, do not set foot in separation: of things the most hateful to Me is divorce.

entries.farsi: در حق او مدح و در حق تو ذم / ‏در حق او شهد و در حق تو سم
entries.english: I have bestowed on every one a way of acting: I have given to every one a form of expression.

entries.farsi: هر كسى را سيرتى بنهاده‏ام / ‏هر كسى را اصطالحى داده‏ام
entries.english: In regard to him it is praise, and in regard to you it is blame: in regard to him honey, and in regard to you poison.

entries.farsi: ما برى از پاك و ناپاكى همه / ‏از گران جانى و چالاكى همه
entries.english: I am independent of all purity and impurity, of all slothfulness and alacrity.

entries.farsi: من نكردم امر تا سودى كنم / ‏بلكه تا بر بندگان جودى كنم
entries.english: I did not ordain that I might make any profit; no, but that I might do a kindness to servants.

entries.farsi: هندوان را اصطالح هند مدح / ‏سنديان را اصطالح سند مدح
entries.english: In the Hindus the idiom of Hind is praiseworthy; in the Sindians the idiom of Sind is praiseworthy.

entries.farsi: من نگردم پاك از تسبيحشان / ‏پاك هم ايشان شوند و در فشان
entries.english: I am not sanctified by their glorification; it is they that become sanctified and pearl-scattering.

entries.farsi: ما زبان را ننگريم و قال را / ما روان را بنگريم و حال را
entries.english: I look not at the tongue and the speech; I look at the inward and the state.

entries.farsi: ناظر قلبيم اگر خاشع بود / گر چه گفت لفظ ناخاضع رود
entries.english: I gaze into the heart whether it be humble, though the words uttered be not humble,

entries.farsi: ز انكه دل جوهر بود گفتن عرض / ‏پس طفيل آمد عرض جوهر غرض
entries.english: Because the heart is the substance, speech the accident; so the form is subservient, the substance is the object.

entries.farsi: چند ازين الفاظ و اضمار و مجاز / سوز خواهم سوز با آن سوز ساز
entries.english: How much of these phrases and conceptions and metaphors? I want burning, burning: become friendly with that burning!

entries.farsi: آتشى از عشق در جان بر فروز / سربه‏سر فكر و عبارت را بسوز
entries.english: Light up a fire of love in your soul, burn thought and expression entirely!

entries.farsi: موسيا آداب دانان ديگرند / سوخته جان و روانان ديگرند
entries.english: O Moses, they that know the conventions are of one sort, they whose souls and spirits burn are of another sort.”

entries.farsi: عاشقان را هر نفس سوزيدنى ست / ‏بر ده ويران خراج و عشر نيست
entries.english: To lovers there is a burning at every moment: tax and tithe are not on a ruined village.

entries.farsi: گر خطا گويد و را خاطى مگو / گر بود پر خون شهيد او را مشو
entries.english: If he speaks faultily, do not call him faulty; and if he be bathed in blood, do not wash martyrs.

entries.farsi: خون شهيدان را ز آب اولى‏تر است / ‏اين خطا از صد ثواب اولى‏تر است
entries.english: For martyrs, blood is better than water: this fault is better than a hundred right actions.

entries.farsi: در درون كعبه رسم قبله نيست / ‏چه غم ار غواص را پاچيله نيست
entries.english: Within the Ka`ba the rule of the qibla does not exist: what matter if the diver has no snow-shoes?

entries.farsi: تو ز سر مستان قالووزى مجو / جامه چاكان را چه فرمايى رفو
entries.english: Do not seek guidance from the drunken: why do you order those whose garments are torn in pieces to mend them?

entries.farsi: ملت عشق از همه دينها جداست / ‏عاشقان را ملت و مذهب خداست
entries.english: The religion of Love is apart from all religions: for lovers, the religion and creed is—God.

entries.farsi: لعل را گر مهر نبود باك نيست / ‏عشق در درياى غم غمناك نيست
entries.english: If the ruby has not a seal, it is no harm: Love in the sea of sorrow is not sorrowful.

sections.title_fa: ‏وحى آمدن موسى را عليه السلام در عذر آن شبان
sections.title_en: How the revelation came to Moses, on whom be peace, excusing that shepherd.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن در سر موسى حق نهفت / ‏رازهايى كان نمى‏آيد به گفت
entries.english: After that, God hid in the inmost heart of Moses mysteries which cannot be spoken.

entries.farsi: بر دل موسى سخنها ريختند / ديدن و گفتن به هم آميختند
entries.english: Words were poured upon his heart: vision and speech were mingled together.

entries.farsi: چند بى‏خود گشت و چند آمد به خود / چند پريد از ازل سوى ابد
entries.english: How oft did he become beside himself and how oft return to himself! How oft did he fly from eternity to everlastingness!

entries.farsi: بعد از اين گر شرح گويم ابلهى است / ‏ز انكه شرح اين وراى آگهى است
entries.english: If I should unfold after this, it is foolishness, because the explanation of this is beyond understanding;

entries.farsi: ور بگويم عقلها را بر كند / ور نويسم بس قلمها بشكند
entries.english: And if I should speak it would root up minds; and if I should write, it would shatter many pens.

entries.farsi: چون كه موسى اين عتاب از حق شنيد / در بيابان در پى چوپان دويد
entries.english: When Moses heard these reproaches from God, he ran into the desert in quest of the shepherd.

entries.farsi: بر نشان پاى آن سر گشته راند / گرد از پرده‏ى بيابان بر فشاند
entries.english: He pushed on over the footprints of the bewildered man, he scattered dust from the skirt of the desert.

entries.farsi: گام پاى مردم شوريده خود / هم ز گام ديگران پيدا بود
entries.english: The footstep of a man distraught is, in truth, distinct from the footsteps of others:

entries.farsi: يك قدم چون رخ ز بالا تا نشيب / ‏يك قدم چون پيل رفته بر وريب
entries.english: One step like the rook from top to bottom; one step he goes crossways, like the bishop;

entries.farsi: گاه چون موجى بر افرازان علم / ‏گاه چون ماهى روانه بر شكم
entries.english: Now lifting his crest like a wave; now going on his belly like a fish;

entries.farsi: گاه بر خاكى نبشته حال خود / همچو رمالى كه رملى بر زند
entries.english: Now writing his state on some dust, like a geomancer who takes an omen by drawing lines.

entries.farsi: عاقبت دريافت او را و بديد / گفت مژده ده كه دستورى رسيد
entries.english: At last he overtook and beheld him; the giver of glad news said, “Permission has come”.

entries.farsi: هيچ آدابى و ترتيبى مجو / هر چه مى‏خواهد دل تنگت بگو
entries.english: Do not seek any rules or method; say whatsoever your distressful heart desires.

entries.farsi: كفر تو دين است و دينت نور جان / ‏ايمنى و ز تو جهانى در امان
entries.english: Your blasphemy is religion, and your religion is the light of the spirit: you are saved, and through you a world is in salvation.

entries.farsi: اى معاف يَفْعَلُ اللهَُّ ما يشاء / بى‏محابا رو زبان را بر گشا
entries.english: O you who are made secure by God does whatever He wills, go, loose your tongue without regard.”

entries.farsi: من ز سدره‏ى منتهى بگذشته‏ام / ‏صد هزاران ساله ز آن سو رفته‏ام
entries.english: He said, “O Moses, I have passed beyond that: I am now bathed in heart’s blood.

entries.farsi: گفت اى موسى از آن بگذشته‏ام / ‏من كنون در خون دل آغشته‏ام
entries.english: I have passed beyond the Lote-tree of the farthest limit, I have gone a hundred thousand years’ journey on the other side.

entries.farsi: تازيانه بر زدى اسبم بگشت / ‏گنبدى كرد و ز گردون بر گذشت
entries.english: You applied the lash, and my horse shied, made a bound, and passed beyond the sky.

entries.farsi: محرم ناسوت ما الهوت باد / آفرين بر دست و بر بازوت باد
entries.english: May the Divine Nature be intimate with my human nature— blessings be on Your hand and on your arm!

entries.farsi: حال من اكنون برون از گفتن است / اين چه مى‏گويم نه احوال من است
entries.english: Now my state is beyond telling: what I am telling is not my state.”

entries.farsi: اين چه مى‏گويم نه احوال من است / ‏نقش تست آن نقش آن آيينه نيست
entries.english: You behold the image which is in a mirror: it is your image; it is not the image of the mirror.

entries.farsi: دم كه مرد نايى اندر ناى كرد / در خور ناى است نه در خورد مرد
entries.english: The breath which the flute-player puts into the flute— does it belong to the flute? No, it belongs to the man.

entries.farsi: هان و هان گر حمد گويى گر سپاس / ‏همچو نافرجام آن چوپان شناس
entries.english: Take good heed! Whether you speak praise or thanksgiving, know that it is even as the unseemly of that shepherd.

entries.farsi: حمد تو نسبت بدان گر بهتر است / ‏ليك آن نسبت به حق هم ابتر است
entries.english: Though your praise is better in comparison with that, yet in relation to God it too is maimed.

entries.farsi: چند گويى چون غطا برداشتند / كاين نبوده ست آن كه مى‏پنداشتند
entries.english: How often will you say, when the lid has been raised, “This was not what they were thinking!”

entries.farsi: اين قبول ذكر تو از رحمت است / ‏چون نماز مستحاضه رخصت است
entries.english: This acceptance of your praise is from mercy: it is an indulgence, like the prayers of a woman suffering from a heavy period.

entries.farsi: با نماز او بيالوده ست خون / ‏ذكر تو آلوده‏ى تشبيه و چون
entries.english: Her prayers are stained with blood; your praise is stained with assimilation and qualification.

entries.farsi: خون پليد است و به آبى مى‏رود / ليك باطن را نجاستها بود
entries.english: Blood is foul, and it goes by water; but the inward part has impurities

entries.farsi: كان به غير آب لطف كردگار / كم نگردد از درون مرد كار
entries.english: Which fail not from the interior of the man of works except by the water of the grace of the Maker.

entries.farsi: در سجودت كاش رو گردانى‏اى / ‏معنى سبحان ربى دانى‏اى
entries.english: Would that in your bowing low in prayer you would turn your face and apprehend the meaning of “Glory to my Lord!”

entries.farsi: كاى سجودم چون وجودم ناسزا / مر بدى را تو نكويى ده جزا
entries.english: Saying, “Oh, my prostration, like my existence, is unworthy: give good in return for evil!”

entries.farsi: اين زمين از حلم حق دارد اثر / تا نجاست برد و گلها داد بر
entries.english: This earth has the mark of God’s clemency, in that it got filth and gave flowers as the produce;

entries.farsi: تا بپوشد او پليديهاى ما / در عوض بر رويد از وى غنچه‏ها
entries.english: In that it covers our pollutions, buds grow up from it in exchange.

entries.farsi: پس چو كافر ديد كاو در داد و جود / كمتر و بى‏مايه تر از خاك بود
entries.english: Therefore, when the infidel saw that in giving and lavishing he was meaner and poorer than the earth,

entries.farsi: از وجود او گل و ميوه نرست / ‏جز فساد جمله پاكيها نجست
entries.english: Flowers and fruit did not grow from his being; he sought nothing but the corruption of all purities,

entries.farsi: گفت واپس رفته‏ام من در ذهاب / ‏حسرتا يا ليتني كنت تراب
entries.english: He said, “I have gone backwards in course. Alas! would that I had been earth!

entries.farsi: كاش از خاكى سفر نگزيدمى / ‏همچو خاكى دانه‏اى مى‏چيدمى
entries.english: Would that I had not chosen to travel away from earthiness, like a clod of earth I had gathered some grain!

entries.farsi: چون سفر كردم مرا راه آزمود / زين سفر كردن ره آوردم چه بود
entries.english: When I travelled, the Way tried me: what was the present I brought from this travelling?”

entries.farsi: ز آن همه ميلش سوى خاك است كاو / در سفر سودى نبيند پيش رو
entries.english: It is from all that propensity of his towards earth that he sees before him no profit in the journey.

entries.farsi: روى واپس كردنش آن حرص و آز / روى در ره كردنش صدق و نياز
entries.english: His turning his face back is that greed and avarice; his turning his face to the Way is sincerity and supplication.

entries.farsi: هر گيا را كش بود ميل عال / هر گيا را كش بود ميل عال
entries.english: Every herb that has a propensity for upwards is in increase and life and growth;

entries.farsi: چون كه گردانيد سر سوى زمين / ‏در كمى و خشكى و نقص و غبين
entries.english: When it has turned its head towards the earth, in decrease and dryness and failure and disappointment

entries.farsi: ميل روحت چون سوى بالا بود / در تزايد مرجعت آن جا بود
entries.english: When the propensity of your spirit is upwards, in increase, and that place is the place to which you will return;

entries.farsi: ور نگون سارى سرت سوى زمين / ‏آفلى حق ال يحب اآلفلين
entries.english: But if you are upside down, your head towards the earth, you are one that sinks: God loves not them that sink.

sections.title_fa: پرسيدن موسى عليه السلام از حق تعالى سر غلبه‏ى ظالمان
sections.title_en: How Moses, on whom be peace, asked the high God the secret of the predominance of the unjust.

entries.farsi: گفت موسى اى كريم كارساز / اى كه يك دم ذكر تو عمر دراز
entries.english: Moses said, “O Bounteous Disposer, O You whom to commemorate for one moment is a long life,

entries.farsi: نقش كژمژ ديدم اندر آب و گل / چون ماليك اعتراضى كرد دل
entries.english: I have seen the crooked, misshapen image in water and clay, and like the angels, my heart has raised an objection,

entries.farsi: كه چه مقصود است نقشى ساختن / ‏و اندر او تخم فساد انداختن
entries.english: As to what is the purpose of making an image and casting within it the seed of corruption?

entries.farsi: آتش ظلم و فساد افروختن / ‏مسجد و سجده كنان را سوختن
entries.english: To kindle the fire of iniquity and corruption; to burn the mosque and those who bend low in prayer;

entries.farsi: مايه‏ى خونابه و زردآبه را / جوش دادن از براى البه را
entries.english: To set boiling the source of bloody tears for the sake of humble entreaties

entries.farsi: من يقين دانم كه عين حكمت است / ‏ليك مقصودم عيان و رويت است
entries.english: I know for certain that it is the essence of wisdom, but my aim is actual seeing and vision.

entries.farsi: آن يقين مى‏گويدم خاموش كن / ‏حرص رويت گويدم نه جوش كن
entries.english: That certainty says to me, ‘keep silence’; the craving for vision says to me, ‘make a stir.’

entries.farsi: مر ماليك را نمودى سر خويش / ‏كاين چنين نوشى همى‏ارزد به نيش
entries.english: You have shown Your secret to the angels, that such honey as this is worth the sting.

entries.farsi: عرضه كردى نور آدم را عيان / ‏بر ماليك گشت مشكلها بيان
entries.english: You have displayed the Light of Adam manifestly to the angels, the difficulties were explained.

entries.farsi: حشر تو گويد كه سر مرگ چيست / ‏ميوه‏ها گويند سر برگ چيست
entries.english: Your Resurrection declares what is the secret of death: the fruits declare what is the secret of the leaves.”

entries.farsi: سر خون و نطفه حسن آدمى است / ‏سابق هر بيشيى آخر كمى است
entries.english: The secret of blood and seed is the excellence of Man; after all, inferiority is antecedent to every superiority.

entries.farsi: لوح را اول بشويد بى‏وقوف / ‏آن گهى بروى نويسد او حروف
entries.english: The ignorant first washes the tablet, and then he writes the letters upon it.

entries.farsi: خون كند دل را و اشك مستهان / ‏بر نويسد بر وى اسرار آن گهان
entries.english: He turns the heart into blood and abject tears, and then He writes the mysteries upon it.

entries.farsi: وقت شستن لوح را بايد شناخت / ‏كه مر آن را دفترى خواهند ساخت
entries.english: At the time of washing the tablet one must recognise that it will be made into a book.

entries.farsi: چون اساس خانه‏اى مى‏افگنند / اولين بنياد را بر مى‏كنند
entries.english: When they lay the foundation of a house, they dig up the first foundation.

entries.farsi: گل بر آرند اول از قعر زمين / ‏تا به آخر بر كشى ماء معين
entries.english: People first fetch up clay from the depths of the earth in order that at last you may draw up flowing water.

entries.farsi: از حجامت كودكان گريند زار / كه نمى‏دانند ايشان سر كار
entries.english: Children weep piteously at cupping, for they know not the secret of the matter;

entries.farsi: مرد خود زر مى‏دهد حجام را / مى‏نوازد نيش خون آشام را
entries.english: A man, in truth, gives the cupper gold and fondles the blood-drinking lancet.

entries.farsi: مى‏دود حمال زى بار گران / ‏مى‏ربايد بار را از ديگران
entries.english: The porter runs to the heavy load: he snatches the load from others.

entries.farsi: جنگ حمالان براى بار بين / ‏اين چنين است اجتهاد كار بين
entries.english: Behold the struggle of the porters for the load! Such is the endeavour of him that sees,

entries.farsi: چون گرانيها اساس راحت است / ‏تلخها هم پيشواى نعمت است
entries.english: Inasmuch as burdens are the foundation of ease, and bitter things, too, are the forerunners of enjoyment.

entries.farsi: تخم مايه‏ى آتشت شاخ تر است / ‏سوخته‏ى آتش قرين كوثر است
entries.english: Paradise is compassed about with the things we dislike; the fires are compassed about with our lusts.

entries.farsi: حفت الجنة بمكروهاتنا / حفت النيران من شهواتنا
entries.english: The seed of the substance of your fire is the fresh bough; he that is burned by the fire is the comrade of Kawthar.

entries.farsi: هر كه در زندان قرين محنتى است / ‏آن جزاى لقمه‏اى و شهوتى است
entries.english: Whoever is the comrade of affliction in prison— that is the retribution for a mouthful and a lust.

entries.farsi: هر كه در قصرى قرين دولتى است / ‏آن جزاى كارزار و محنتى است
entries.english: Whoever is the comrade of a high fortune in a palace— that is the reward for some battle-field and sore trial.

entries.farsi: هر كه را ديدى به زر و سيم فرد / دان كه اندر كسب كردن صبر كرد
entries.english: Whomever you have seen unrivalled in gold and silver— know that he has been patient in earning.

entries.farsi: بى‏سبب بيند چو ديده شد گذار / تو كه در حسى سبب را گوش دار
entries.english: When the eye has become piercing, he sees without causes. You who are in sense-perception, pay you heed to causes!

entries.farsi: آن كه بيرون از طبايع جان اوست / ‏منصب خرق سببها آن اوست
entries.english: He whose spirit is beyond natural properties— to him belongs the position of power to split causes.

entries.farsi: بى‏سبب بيند نه از آب و گيا / چشم چشمه‏ى معجزات انبيا
entries.english: The eye regards the fountain of the miracles of the prophets as without cause, not as from water and herbage.

entries.farsi: اين سبب همچون طبيب است و عليل / ‏اين سبب همچون چراغ است و فتيل
entries.english: These causes are like the physician and the sick: these causes are like the lamp and the wick.

entries.farsi: شب چراغت را فتيل نو بتاب / ‏پاك دان زينها چراغ آفتاب
entries.english: Twist a new wick for your night-lamp; know that the lamp of the sun transcends these things.

entries.farsi: رو تو كهگل ساز بهر سقف خان / ‏سقف گردون را ز كهگل پاك دان
entries.english: Go you and make plaster for the roof of your house, know that the roof of the sky is undefiled by plaster.

entries.farsi: اه كه چون دل دار ما غم سوز شد / خلوت شب در گذشت و روز شد
entries.english: Alas that, after our Beloved had burned our pain, the night-time of being alone passed away and became day!

entries.farsi: جز به شب جلوه نباشد ماه را / جز به درد دل مجو دل خواه را
entries.english: Except at night there is no unveiling of the moon: except through heartache do not seek your heart’s desire.

entries.farsi: ترك عيسى كرده خر پرورده‏اى / ‏الجرم چون خر برون پرده‏اى
entries.english: Forsaking Jesus, you have fostered the donkey: of necessity, like the donkey, you are outside of the curtain.

entries.farsi: طالع عيسى است علم و معرفت / ‏طالع خر نيست اى تو خر صفت
entries.english: Knowledge and gnosis are the fortune of Jesus; they are not the fortune of the donkey, O you asinine one!

entries.farsi: ناله‏ى خر بشنوى رحم آيدت / ‏پس ندانى خر خرى فرمايدت
entries.english: You listen to the moaning of the donkey, and pity comes over you; then you, know not the donkey commands you to be asinine.

entries.farsi: رحم بر عيسى كن و بر خر مكن / ‏طبع را بر عقل خود سرور مكن
entries.english: Have pity on Jesus and have no pity on the donkey: do not make the nature lord over your intellect.

entries.farsi: طبع را هل تا بگريد زار زار / تو از او بستان و وام جان گزار
entries.english: Let the nature weep sore and bitterly: do you take from it and pay the debt of the soul.

entries.farsi: سالها خربنده بودى بس بود / ز انكه خربنده ز خر واپس بود
entries.english: For years you have been the donkey’s slave. It is enough, for the donkey’s slave is behind the donkey.

entries.farsi: ز اخروهن مرادش نفس تست / ‏كاو به آخر بايد و عقلت نخست
entries.english: The thing meant by “put them behind” is your nafs; for it must be last and your intellect first.

entries.farsi: هم مزاج خر شده ست اين عقل پست / ‏فكرش اين كه چون علف آرم بدست
entries.english: This base intellect has become of the same temperament as the ass: its thought is how it shall get hold of fodder.

entries.farsi: آن خر عيسى مزاج دل گرفت / ‏در مقام عاقالن منزل گرفت
entries.english: The ass of Jesus took the temperament of the spirit: it took its abode in the place of the intelligent,

entries.farsi: اين خر پژمرده گشته ست اژدها / ‏هم از او صحت رسد او را مهل
entries.english: Because intellect was ruling, and the ass weak — the donkey is made lean by a strong rider

entries.farsi: ز انكه غالب عقل بود و خر ضعيف / ‏از سوار زفت گردد خر نحيف
entries.english: While from the weakness of your intellect, O you who have the value of a donkey, this worn-out donkey has become a dragon.

entries.farsi: و ز ضعيفى عقل تو اى خر بها / اين خر پژمرده گشته ست اژدها
entries.english: If through Jesus you have become heartsick, health too comes from him: do not leave him.

entries.farsi: چونى اى عيساى عيسى دم ز رنج / ‏كه نبود اندر جهان بى‏مار گنج
entries.english: How are you as to affliction, O Jesus who has the breath of Jesus? For there never was in the world a treasure without a snake

entries.farsi: چونى اى عيسى ز ديدار جهود / چونى اى يوسف ز مكار حسود
entries.english: How are you, O Jesus, at the sight of the Jews? How are you, O Joseph, in respect of the envious plotter?

entries.farsi: تو شب و روز از پى اين قوم غمر / چون شب و روزى مدد بخشاى عمر
entries.english: Night and day for the sake of this foolish people you, like night and day, art a renewer of life.

entries.farsi: چونى از صفراييان بى‏هنر / چه هنر زايد ز صفرا درد سر
entries.english: Alas for those bile filled ones who are without excellence! What excellence is born from bile? Headache.

entries.farsi: تو همان كن كه كند خورشيد شرق / ‏ما نفاق و حيله و دزدى و زرق
entries.english: Do you the same thing that the sun of the east does with hypocrisy and craft and thieving and dissimulation.

entries.farsi: تو عسل ما سركه در دنيا و دين / ‏دفع اين صفرا بود سركنگبين
entries.english: You are honey, we are vinegar in this world and in religion; the removing this bile is oxymel.

entries.farsi: سركه افزوديم ما قوم زحير / تو عسل بفزا كرم را وامگير
entries.english: We folk who suffer from colic have added more and more vinegar; add more and more honey, withhold not Your bounty.

entries.farsi: اين سزيد از ما چنان آمد ز ما / ريگ اندر چشم چه فزايد عما
entries.english: This was meet in us; such issued from us: what is increased by sand in the eye? Blindness.

entries.farsi: آن سزد از تو أيا كحل عزيز / كه بيابد از تو هر ناچيز چيز
entries.english: It is proper in you, O precious eye salve, that every nothing should gain from you something.

entries.farsi: ز آتش اين ظالمانت دل كباب / ‏از تو جمله اهد قومى بد خطاب
entries.english: Your heart is roasted by the fire of these unrighteous men, all your appeal has been, “Guide my people!”

entries.farsi: كان عودى در تو گر آتش زنند / اين جهان از عطر و ريحان آگنند
entries.english: You are a mine of aloes-wood: if they set you afire, they will fill this world with attar of roses and sweet basil.

entries.farsi: تو نه آن عودى كز آتش كم شود / تو نه آن روحى كه اسير غم شود
entries.english: You art not that aloes-wood that is diminished by the fire: you art not that spirit that is made captive by grief.

entries.farsi: عود سوزد كان عود از سوز دور / باد كى حمله برد بر اصل نور
entries.english: Aloes-wood burns, the mine of aloes-wood is far from burning: how should the wind assail the source of light?

entries.farsi: اى ز تو مر آسمانها را صفا / اى جفاى تو نكوتر از وفا
entries.english: Oh, it is from you the heavens have purity; oh, Your unkindness is better than kindness,

entries.farsi: ز انكه از عاقل جفايى گر رود / از وفاى جاهالن آن به بود
entries.english: Because if an unkindness come from the wise it is better than the kindness of the ignorant

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر عداوت از خرد / بهتر از مهرى كه از جاهل رسد
entries.english: The Prophet said, “Enmity from wisdom is better than the love that comes from a fool.’

sections.title_fa: رنجانيدن اميرى خفته‏اى را كه مار در دهانش رفته بود
sections.title_en: How an Amir harassed a sleeping man into whose mouth a snake had gone.

entries.farsi: عاقلى بر اسب مى‏آمد سوار / در دهان خفته‏اى مى‏رفت مار
entries.english: A wise man was riding along a snake was going into the mouth of a man asleep.

entries.farsi: آن سوار آن را بديد و مى‏شتافت / ‏تا رماند مار را فرصت نيافت
entries.english: The rider saw that, and was hurrying to scare away the snake, he got no chance.

entries.farsi: چون كه از عقلش فراوان بد مدد / چند دبوسى قوى بر خفته زد
entries.english: Since he had an abundant supply of intelligence, he struck the sleeper several powerful blows with a mace.

entries.farsi: برد او را زخم آن دبوس سخت / زو گريزان تا به زير يك درخت
entries.english: The strokes of the hard mace drove him in flight from him to beneath a tree.

entries.farsi: سيب پوسيده بسى بد ريخته / ‏گفت از اين خور اى به درد آويخته
entries.english: There were many rotten apples which had dropped: he said, “Eat of these, O you in the grip of pain!”

entries.farsi: سيب چندان مر و را در خورد داد / كز دهانش باز بيرون مى‏فتاد
entries.english: He gave the man so many apples to eat that they were falling out of his mouth again.

entries.farsi: بانگ مى‏زد كاى امير آخر چرا / قصد من كردى تو ناديده جفا
entries.english: He was crying, “O Amir, pray, why have you set on me? What have I done to you?

entries.farsi: گر ترا ز اصل است با جانم ستيز / تيغ زن يك بارگى خونم بريز
entries.english: If you have an inveterate and mortal feud with me, strike with your sword and shed my blood at once.

entries.farsi: شوم ساعت كه شدم بر تو پديد / اى خنك آن را كه روى تو نديد
entries.english: Ill-omened the hour I came into your sight: oh, happy he’ that never saw your face!

entries.farsi: بى‏جنايت بى‏گنه بى‏بيش و كم / ‏ملحدان جايز ندارند اين ستم
entries.english: Without guilt, without sin, without anything great or small-- the heretics hold not such oppression allowable.

entries.farsi: مى‏جهد خون از دهانم با سخن / ‏اى خدا آخر مكافاتش تو كن
entries.english: Blood gushes from my mouth together with words. O God, I beseech You, give him the retribution!”

entries.farsi: هر زمان مى‏گفت او نفرين نو / اوش مى‏زد كاندر اين صحرا بدو
entries.english: Every instant he was uttering a new curse, he kept beating him and saying, “Run in this plain.”

entries.farsi: زخم دبوس و سوار همچو باد / مى‏دويد و باز در رو مى‏فتاد
entries.english: Blows of the mace, and the rider as the wind! He went on running and again falling on his face.

entries.farsi: ممتلى و خوابناك و سست بد / پا و رويش صد هزاران زخم شد
entries.english: He was full-fed and sleepy and fatigued: his feet and face became a hundred thousand wounds.

entries.farsi: تا شبانگه مى‏كشيد و مى‏گشاد / تا ز صفرا قى شدن بر وى فتاد
entries.english: Till nightfall he drove to and fro’, until vomiting caused by bile overtook him.

entries.farsi: زو بر آمد خورده‏ها زشت و نكو / مار با آن خورده بيرون جست از او
entries.english: All the things he had eaten, bad or good, came up from him: the snake shot forth from him along with what he had eaten.

entries.farsi: چون بديد از خود برون آن مار را / سجده آورد آن نكو كردار را
entries.english: When he saw the snake outside of him, he fell on his knees before that beneficent man.

entries.farsi: سهم آن مار سياه زشت زفت / ‏چون بديد آن دردها از وى برفت
entries.english: As soon as he saw the horror of that black, ugly, big snake, those griefs departed from him.

entries.farsi: گفت خود تو جبرييل رحمتى / ‏يا خدايى كه ولى نعمتى
entries.english: “Truly,” said he, “you are the Gabriel of mercy, or you are God, for you are the lord of bounty.

entries.farsi: اى مبارك ساعتى كه ديدى‏ام / ‏مرده بودم جان نو بخشيدى‏ام
entries.english: Oh, blessed the hour that you saw me: I was dead, you have given me new life.

entries.farsi: تو مرا جويان مثال مادران / ‏من گريزان از تو مانند خران
entries.english: You seeking me like mothers; I fleeing from you like asses.

entries.farsi: خر گريزد از خداوند از خرى / ‏صاحبش در پى ز نيكو گوهرى
entries.english: The donkey flees from his master because of donkey nature; his owner after because of good-nature.

entries.farsi: نه از پى سود و زيان مى‏جويدش / ليك تا در گرگش ندرد يا ددش
entries.english: He seeks him, not on account of profit or loss, but in order that a wolf or wild beast may not tear him.

entries.farsi: اى خنك آن را كه بيند روى تو / يا در افتد ناگهان در كوى تو
entries.english: Oh, happy he that espies your face or suddenly lights upon your abode.

entries.farsi: اى روان پاك بستوده ترا / چند گفتم ژاژ و بى‏هوده ترا
entries.english: O you whom the pure spirit hath praised, how many foolish and idle words have I spoken to you!

entries.farsi: اى خداوند و شهنشاه و امير / من نگفتم جهل من گفت آن مگير
entries.english: O lord and emperor and Amir, I spoke not, my folly spoke: do not punish that.

entries.farsi: شمه‏اى زين حال اگر دانستمى / ‏گفتن بى‏هوده كى تانستمى
entries.english: If I had known a little of this matter, how could I have spoken foolish words?

entries.farsi: بس ثنايت گفتمى اى خوش خصال / ‏گر مرا يك رمز مى‏گفتى ز حال
entries.english: I should have spoken much praise of you, O man of good qualities, if you had given me a single hint as to the case;

entries.farsi: شد سرم كاليوه عقل از سر بجست / ‏خاصه اين سر را كه مغزش كمتر است
entries.english: But you, keeping silence, showed perturbation and silently continued to beat me on the head.

entries.farsi: ليك خامش كرده مى‏آشوفتى / ‏خامشانه بر سرم مى‏كوفتى
entries.english: My head became dizzy; the wits flew out of my head— especially as this head has little brain.

entries.farsi: عفو كن اى خوب روى خوب كار / آن چه گفتم از جنون اندر گذار
entries.english: Pardon, O man of goodly countenance and goodly behaviour: let pass that which I said in frenzy.”

entries.farsi: گفت اگر من گفتمى رمزى از آن / ‏زهره‏ى تو آب گشتى آن زمان
entries.english: He answered, “If I had uttered a hint of it, your gall would instantly have turned to water

entries.farsi: مصطفى فرمود اگر گويم به راست / ‏شرح آن دشمن كه در جان شماست
entries.english: Had I told you the qualities of the snake, terror would have made you give up the ghost’.”

entries.farsi: گر ترا من گفتمى اوصاف مار / ترس از جانت بر آوردى دمار
entries.english: Mustafa said, ‘ If I should tell correctly the description of the enemy which is in your souls,

entries.farsi: زهره‏هاى پر دالن هم بر درد / نه رود ره نه غم كارى خورد
entries.english: The gall-bladders even of courageous men would burst: he would neither go his way nor care for any work.

entries.farsi: نه دلش را تاب ماند در نياز / نه تنش را قوت روزه و نماز
entries.english: Neither would there remain to his heart endurance in supplication, nor to his body strength for fasting and prayer.

entries.farsi: همچو موشى پيش گربه ال شود / همچو بره پيش گرگ از جا رود
entries.english: He would become nothing as a mouse before a cat; he would be distraught as a lamb before a wolf.

entries.farsi: اندر او نه حيله ماند نه روش / ‏پس كنم ناگفته تان من پرورش
entries.english: No power to plan or move would remain in him: therefore I tend you without speaking.

entries.farsi: همچو بو بكر ربابى تن زنم / ‏دست چون داود در آهن زنم
entries.english: I am mute, like Bu Bakr-i Rababi; I handle the iron, like David,

entries.farsi: تا محال از دست من حالى شود / مرغ پر بركنده را بالى شود
entries.english: So that by my hand the impossible is brought to pass, and wings are restored to the bird whose plumes were torn away.

entries.farsi: چون يَدُ اللهَِّ فَوْقَ أَيْدِيهِمْ بود / دست ما را دست خود فرمود احد
entries.english: Since there is the hand of God is above their hands, the One has declared our hand to be His hand.

entries.farsi: پس مرا دست دراز آمد يقين / ‏بر گذشته ز آسمان هفتمين
entries.english: Therefore mine is surely a long hand that has passed beyond the Seventh Heaven.

entries.farsi: دست من بنمود بر گردون هنر / مقريا بر خوان كه انْشَقَّ القمر
entries.english: My hand showed cunning upon the sky: O teacher of the Qur’an, recite the moon hath been cleft asunder.’

entries.farsi: اين صفت هم بهر ضعف عقلهاست / ‏با ضعيفان شرح قدرت كى رواست
entries.english: This characteristic, moreover, is on account of the weakness of understandings: how is it possible to explain the omnipotence to the weak?

entries.farsi: خود بدانى چون بر آرى سر ز خواب / ‏ختم شد و اهلل أعلم بالصواب
entries.english: You will surely know when you lift your head from sleep. It is the end, and God knows best what is right.

entries.farsi: مر ترا نه قوت خوردن بدى / ‏نه ره و پرواى قى كردن بدى
entries.english: “You would not have been able to eat, nor would you have been capable of vomiting or cared.

entries.farsi: مى‏شنيدم فحش و خر مى‏راندم / ‏رب يسر زير لب مى‏خواندم
entries.english: I heard abuse and went on with my work; I kept repeating under my lip , `O Lord, make easy !’

entries.farsi: از سبب گفتن مرا دستور نه / ‏ترك تو گفتن مرا مقدور نه
entries.english: I had not permission to speak of the cause, and I had not power to abandon you.

entries.farsi: سجده‏ها مى‏كرد آن رسته ز رنج / ‏كاى سعادت اى مرا اقبال و گنج
entries.english: From the grief in my heart I was saying continually, ‘Guide my people; truly, they know not’ .”

entries.farsi: هر زمان مى‏گفتم از درد درون / ‏اهد قومي إنهم ال يعلمون
entries.english: The man that had been delivered from woe was falling on his knees and saying, “O my bliss, O my fortune and treasure,

entries.farsi: از خدا يابى جزاها اى شريف / ‏قوت شكرت ندارد اين ضعيف
entries.english: You will get rewards from God, O noble one; this weakling has not the power to thank you.

entries.farsi: شكر حق گويد ترا اى پيشوا / آن لب و چانه ندارم و آن نوا
entries.english: God will say thanks to you, O leader; I have not the lips and the chin and the voice for that.”

entries.farsi: دشمنى عاقالن زين‏سان بود / زهر ايشان ابتهاج جان بود
entries.english: Of this fashion is the enmity of the wise: their poison is gladness to the soul.

entries.farsi: دوستى ابله بود رنج و ضالل / ‏اين حكايت بشنو از بهر مثال
entries.english: The friendship of the fool is woe and perdition: hear this tale as a parable.

sections.title_fa: اعتماد كردن بر تملق و وفاى خرس
sections.title_en: On putting trust in the false friendship and good faith of the bear

entries.farsi: اژدهايى خرس را در مى‏كشيد / شير مردى رفت و فريادش رسيد
entries.english: A dragon was pulling a bear; a valiant man went and rescued it.

entries.farsi: بانگ مظلومان ز هر جا بشنوند / آن طرف چون رحمت حق مى‏دوند
entries.english: The valiant men are a help in the world when the wail of the oppressed reaches.

entries.farsi: شير مردانند در عالم مدد / آن زمان كافغان مظلومان رسد
entries.english: From every quarter they hear the cry of the oppressed and run in that direction, like the mercy of God.

entries.farsi: آن ستونهاى خللهاى جهان / ‏آن طبيبان مرضهاى نهان
entries.english: Those buttresses for the breaches of the world, those physicians for hidden maladies,

entries.farsi: محض مهر و داورى و رحمتند / همچو حق بى‏علت و بى‏رشوتند
entries.english: Are pure love and justice and mercy; even as God, they are flawless and uncorrupted.

entries.farsi: اين چه يارى مى‏كنى يك بارگيش / ‏گويد از بهر غم و بى‏چارگيش
entries.english: “Why do you give him this aid all at once?” he says, “On account of his grief and helplessness.”

entries.farsi: مهربانى شد شكار شير مرد / در جهان دارو نجويد غير درد
entries.english: Loving kindness is fallen a prey to the valiant man, medicine seeks nothing in the world but the pain.

entries.farsi: هر كجا دردى دوا آن جا رود / هر كجا پستى است آب آن جا دود
entries.english: Wherever a pain is, the remedy goes there: wherever lowland is, the water runs there.

entries.farsi: آب رحمت بايدت رو پست شو / و آن گهان خور خمر رحمت مست شو
entries.english: If thou want the water of mercy, go, become lowly, and then drink the wine of mercy and become drunk.

entries.farsi: رحمت اندر رحمت آمد تا به سر / بر يكى رحمت فروماى اى پسر
entries.english: Mercy upon mercy comes up to the head; do not come down to a single mercy, O son!

entries.farsi: پنبه‏ى وسواس بيرون كن ز گوش / ‏تا به گوشت آيد از گردون خروش
entries.english: Bring the sky under your feet, O brave one! Hear from above the firmament the noise of the music!

entries.farsi: چرخ را در زير پا آر اى شجاع / ‏بشنو از فوق فلك بانگ سماع
entries.english: Put out of your ear the cotton of evil suggestion that the cries from heaven may come into your ear.

entries.farsi: پاك كن دو چشم را از موى عيب / ‏تا ببينى باغ و سروستان غيب
entries.english: Purge your two eyes from the hair of defect, that you may behold the garden and cypress-plot of the world unseen.

entries.farsi: دفع كن از مغز و از بينى زكام / ‏تا كه ريح اللهَّ در آيد در مشام
entries.english: Eject the phlegm from your brain and nose that the wind of God may come into the centres of your sense of smell.

entries.farsi: هيچ مگذار از تب و صفرا اثر / تا بيابى از جهان طعم شكر
entries.english: Do not leave any trace of fever and bile that you may get from the world the taste of sugar.

entries.farsi: داروى مردى كن و عنين مپوى / ‏تا برون آيند صد گون خوب روى
entries.english: Cure your manliness; don’t be impotent, so that a hundred beautiful ladies will want you.

entries.farsi: كنده‏ى تن را ز پاى جان بكن / ‏تا كند جوالن به گرد آن چمن
entries.english: Tear the fetter, which is the body, from the foot of your soul, so that it may race round the arena.

entries.farsi: غل بخل از دست و گردن دور كن / ‏بخت نو درياب در چرخ كهن
entries.english: Take off the shackle of avarice from your hands and neck: seize a new fortune in the old heaven.

entries.farsi: ور نمى‏تانى به كعبه‏ى لطف پر / عرضه كن بى‏چارگى بر چاره‏گر
entries.english: And if you art unable fly to the Ka`ba of grace: lay your helplessness before the Helper.

entries.farsi: زارى و گريه قوى سرمايه‏اى است / ‏رحمت كلى قوى‏تر دايه‏اى است
entries.english: Lamentation and weeping are mighty stock-in-trade; the Universal Mercy is the mightiest nurse.

entries.farsi: دايه و مادر بهانه جو بود / تا كه كى آن طفل او گريان شود
entries.english: The nurse and mother seeks a pretext: when her child will begin to weep.

entries.farsi: طفل حاجات شما را آفريد / تا بناليد و شود شيرش پديد
entries.english: He created the child, your wants, in order that it might moan and that milk might be produced for it.

entries.farsi: گفت ادْعُوا اللهََّ بى‏زارى مباش / ‏تا بجوشد شيرهاى مهرهاش
entries.english: He said, “Call upon God!”Do not refrain not from lamentation, in order that the milk of His loving kindnesses may flow.

entries.farsi: هوى هوى باد و شير افشان ابر / در غم مااند يك ساعت تو صبر
entries.english: The howling of the wind and the pouring forth of milk from the cloud are for care of us: patience one moment!

entries.farsi: فِي السَّماءِ رِزْقُكُمْ بشنيده‏اى / ‏اندر اين پستى چه بر چفسيده‏اى
entries.english: Have not you heard “in the sky is your daily bread”? Why have you stuck to this low place?

entries.farsi: ترس و نوميديت دان آواز غول / ‏مى‏كشد گوش تو تا قعر سفول
entries.english: Deem your fear and despair to be the voice of the ghoul drawing your ear to the abyss of degradation.

entries.farsi: هر ندايى كه ترا بالا كشيد / آن ندا مى‏دان كه از بالا رسيد
entries.english: Every call that draws you upward— know that that call has come from on high.

entries.farsi: هر ندايى كه ترا حرص آورد / بانگ گرگى دان كه او مردم درد
entries.english: Every call that excites greed in you— know that it is the howl of the wolf which tears men.

entries.farsi: اين بلندى نيست از روى مكان / ‏اين بلنديهاست سوى عقل و جان
entries.english: This height is not in respect of position; these “heights” are towards mind and spirit.

entries.farsi: آن فالنى فوق آن سركش نشست / ‏گر چه در صورت به پهلويش نشست
entries.english: Every cause is higher than the effect: the stone and iron are superior to the sparks.

entries.farsi: هر سبب بالاتر آمد از اثر / سنگ و آهن فايق آمد بر شرر
entries.english: Such and such a person is seated above that one who lifts up his head so haughtily, though in appearance he is seated beside him.

entries.farsi: فوقى آن جاست از روى شرف / ‏جاى دور از صدر باشد مستخف
entries.english: The superiority of that place is in respect of nobility; the place far from the seat of honour is held in slight regard.

entries.farsi: سنگ و آهن زين جهت كه سابق است / ‏در عمل فوقى اين دو اليق است
entries.english: Forasmuch as the stone and iron are prior in action, the superiority of these two is proper;

entries.farsi: و آن شرر از روى مقصودى خويش / ‏ز آهن و سنگ است زين رو پيش و بيش
entries.english: But those sparks, in respect of their being the final cause, are from this point of view far in front of the iron and stone.

entries.farsi: سنگ و آهن اول و پايان شرر / ليك اين هر دو تنند و جان شرر
entries.english: The stone and iron are first; and the sparks last; but these two are the body, and the sparks are the soul.

entries.farsi: آن شرر گر در زمان واپس‏تر است / ‏در صفت از سنگ و آهن برتر است
entries.english: If those sparks are posterior in time, in quality they are higher than the stone and iron.

entries.farsi: در زمان شاخ از ثمر سابق‏تر است / ‏در هنر از شاخ او فايق‏تر است
entries.english: The bough is prior to the fruit in time; it is superior to the bough in excellence.

entries.farsi: چون كه مقصود از شجر آمد ثمر / پس ثمر اول بود و آخر شجر
entries.english: Since the fruit is the final cause of the tree, it follows that the fruit is first, the tree last.

entries.farsi: خرس چون فرياد كرد از اژدها / شير مردى كرد از چنگش جدا
entries.english: When the bear cried out for help against the dragon, a valiant man rescued it from the claws.

entries.farsi: حيلت و مردى بهم دادند پشت / ‏اژدها را او بدين قوت بكشت
entries.english: Cunning and courage gave support to each other: by this strength he slew the dragon.

entries.farsi: اژدها را هست قوت حيله نيست / ‏نيز فوق حيله‏ى تو حيله‏اى است
entries.english: The dragon has strength it has not cunning; again, there is a cunning above your cunning.

entries.farsi: حيله‏ى خود را چو ديدى باز رو / كز كجا آمد سوى آغاز رو
entries.english: When you have regarded your own cunning, go back from where it came: go to the origin.

entries.farsi: هر چه در پستى است آمد از عال / چشم را سوى بلندى نه هال
entries.english: Whatever is below has come from above: come on, turn your eye towards the height.

entries.farsi: روشنى بخشد نظر اندر على / ‏گر چه اول خيرگى آرد بلى
entries.english: Looking aloft gives light; though at first it produces bewilderment as a trial

entries.farsi: چشم را در روشنايى خوى كن / ‏گر نه خفاشى نظر آن سوى كن
entries.english: Accustom your eye to the light; if you are not a bat, look in that direction.

entries.farsi: عاقبت بينى نشان نور تست / ‏شهوت حالى حقيقت گور تست
entries.english: Vision of the end is the sign of your light; the lust of the moment is in truth your grave.

entries.farsi: عاقبت بينى كه صد بازى بديد / مثل آن نبود كه يك بازى شنيد
entries.english: The man with vision of the end, who has seen a hundred artifices, is not like him that has heard of one artifice,

entries.farsi: ز آن يكى بازى چنان مغرور شد / كز تكبر ز اوستادان دور شد
entries.english: He has been so befooled by that one artifice that in his pride he has become alienated from the masters

entries.farsi: سامرى‏وار آن هنر در خود چو ديد / او ز موسى از تكبر سر كشيد
entries.english: Like Samiri, when he has seen in himself that skill, through pride he has rebelled against Moses.

entries.farsi: او ز موسى آن هنر آموخته / ‏وز معلم چشم را بر دوخته
entries.english: He has learned that skill from Moses and closed his eyes to his teacher.

entries.farsi: الجرم موسى دگر بازى نمود / تا كه آن بازى و جانش را ربود
entries.english: Moses, of course, exhibited another artifice, so that it swept away that artifice and his life.

entries.farsi: اى بسا دانش كه اندر سر دود / تا شود سرور بدان خود سر رود
entries.english: Oh, many is the knowledge that runs in the head that he should become eminent—in truth, through that his head goes.

entries.farsi: سر نخواهى كه رود تو پاى باش / ‏در پناه قطب صاحب راى باش
entries.english: If you do not wish your head to be lost, be a foot: be under the protection of the Qtub who is possessed of discernment.

entries.farsi: گر چه شاهى خويش فوق او مبين / ‏گر چه شهدى جز نبات او مچين
entries.english: Though you are a king, do not deem yourself above him: though you be honey, gather nothing but his sugar-cane.

entries.farsi: فكر تو نقش است و فكر اوست جان / ‏نقد تو قلب است و نقد اوست كان
entries.english: Your thought is the outward form, and his thought is the soul: your coin is false, and his coin is the mine.

entries.farsi: او تويى خود را بجو در اوى او / كو و كو گو فاخته شو سوى او
entries.english: You are he: seek yourself in his “he”. Say coo, coo: become a dove towards him.

entries.farsi: ور نخواهى خدمت ابناى جنس / ‏در دهان اژدهايى همچو خرس
entries.english: And if you are unwilling to serve the men of kind, you are in the dragon’s mouth, like the bear.

entries.farsi: بو كه استادى رهاند مر ترا / و ز خطر بيرون كشاند مر ترا
entries.english: It may be that a Master will deliver you and pull you out of danger.

entries.farsi: ارزيى مى‏كن چو زورت نيست هين / ‏چون كه كورى سر مكش از راه بين
entries.english: As you have no strength, keep making a lamentation; since you are blind, take care, do not turn your head away from him that sees the road.

entries.farsi: تو كم از خرسى نمى‏نالى ز درد / خرس رست از درد چون فرياد كرد
entries.english: You are less than the bear, you are not wailing at the pain. The bear was freed from pain when it made an outcry.

entries.farsi: اى خدا اين سنگ دل را موم كن / ‏ناله‏ى ما را خوش و مرحوم كن
entries.english: O God, make our stony hearts wax; make our wailing sweet and an object of mercy!

sections.title_fa: ‏گفتن نابيناى سائل كه دو كورى دارم
sections.title_en: How a sightless beggar said,” I have two types of blindness.”

entries.farsi: بود كورى كاو همى‏گفت الامان / ‏من دو كورى دارم اى اهل زمان
entries.english: There was a blind man who used to say, “Pity! I have two forms of blindness, O people of the time.

entries.farsi: پس دو باره رحمتم آريد هان / ‏چون دو كورى دارم و من در ميان
entries.english: Therefore, listen, show me twice as much compassion, since I have two forms of blindness, and I between.

entries.farsi: گفت يك كوريت مى‏بينيم ما / آن دگر كورى چه باشد وانما
entries.english: “We see one type of your blindness: what may the other blindness be? Explain.”

entries.farsi: گفت زشت آوازم و ناخوش نوا / زشت آوازى و كورى شد دوتا
entries.english: He answered, “ I have an ugly voice and unpleasing tones: ugliness of voice and blindness are double.

entries.farsi: بانگ زشتم مايه‏ى غم مى‏شود / مهر خلق از بانگ من كم مى‏شود
entries.english: My ugly cry becomes the source of annoyance: the people’s love is lessened by my cry.

entries.farsi: زشت آوازم به هر جا كه رود / مايه‏ى خشم و غم و كين مى‏شود
entries.english: Wherever my ugly voice goes, it becomes the source of anger and annoyance and hatred.

entries.farsi: بر دو كورى رحم را دوتا كنيد / اين چنين ناگنج را گنجا كنيد
entries.english: Double your compassion for two forms of blindness : make room for one who gets so little room.”

entries.farsi: زشتى آواز كم شد زين گله / ‏خلق شد بر وى به رحمت يك دله
entries.english: The ugliness of voice was diminished by this plaint: the people became of one mind in compassion for him.

entries.farsi: كرد نيكو چون بگفت او راز را / لطف آواز دلش آواز را
entries.english: When he had told the secret, his voice was made beautiful by the graciousness of the voice of his heart ;

entries.farsi: و انكه آواز دلش هم بد بود / آن سه كورى دورى سرمد بود
entries.english: But that one whose heart’s voice also is bad-- those three forms of blindness are banishment everlasting

entries.farsi: ليك وهابان كه بى‏علت دهند / بو كه دستى بر سر زشتش نهند
entries.english: Yet it may be that the bounteous, who give without cause, will lay a hand upon his ugly head.

entries.farsi: چون كه آوازش خوش و مظلوم شد / زو دل سنگين دالن چون موم شد
entries.english: Since his voice became sweet and pitiable, the hearts of the stony-hearted were made as wax .

entries.farsi: ناله‏ى كافر چو زشت است و شهيق / ‏ز آن نمى‏گردد اجابت را رفيق
entries.english: Inasmuch as the infidel’s lament is ugly and braying, for that it meets with no response.

entries.farsi: اخْ سَؤُا بر زشت آواز آمده ست / ‏كاو ز خون خلق چون سگ بود مست
entries.english: “Be silent” has come down against the ugly-voiced, for he was drunken with the people’s blood, like a dog

entries.farsi: چون كه ناله‏ى خرس رحمت كش بود / ناله‏ات نبود چنين ناخوش بود
entries.english: Inasmuch as the lament of the bear attracts compassion, your lament is not like this, is unpleasing,

entries.farsi: توبه كن و ز خورده استفراغ كن / ‏ور جراحت كهنه شد رو داغ كن
entries.english: Know that you have behaved with wolfishness to Joseph, or have drunk of the blood of an innocent.

entries.farsi: دان كه با يوسف تو گرگى كرده‏اى / ‏يا ز خون بى‏گناهى خورده‏اى
entries.english: Repent, and empty yourself of what you have drunk; and if your wound is old, go, cauterize.

sections.title_fa: تتمه‏ى حكايت خرس و آن ابله كه بر وفاى او اعتماد كرده بود
sections.title_en: Continuation of the story of the bear and of the fool who had put trust in its good faith.

entries.farsi: خرس هم از اژدها چون وارهيد / و آن كرم ز آن مرد مردانه بديد
entries.english: The bear, too, when it was delivered from the dragon and received such kindness from that brave man

entries.farsi: چون سگ اصحاب كهف آن خرس زار / شد مالزم در پى آن بردبار
entries.english: Like the dog of the Men of the Cave, that poor bear became an attendant at the heels of him that bore the burden.

entries.farsi: آن مسلمان سر نهاد از خستگى / ‏خرس حارس گشت از دل بستگى
entries.english: That Moslem, from fatigue, laid down his head; the bear, from devotion, became guard.

entries.farsi: آن يكى بگذشت و گفتش حال چيست / ‏اى برادر مر ترا اين خرس كيست
entries.english: A certain man passed by and said to him, “What has happened? O brother, who is this bear to you?”

entries.farsi: قصه واگفت و حديث اژدها / گفت بر خرسى منه دل ابلها
entries.english: He recounted the adventure, and the story of the dragon. The other said, “Do not set your heart on a bear, O fool!

entries.farsi: دوستى ابله بتر از دشمنى است / ‏او بهر حيله كه دانى راندنى است
entries.english: The friendship of a fool is worse than enmity: it ought to be driven away by every means you know.”

entries.farsi: گفت و اهلل از حسودى گفت اين / ‏ور نه خرسى چه نگرى اين مهر بين
entries.english: He said, “By God, he has said this from envy; otherwise, “Why do you look at the bearishness? Behold this affection!”

entries.farsi: گفت مهر ابلهان عشوه‏ده است / ‏اين حسودى من از مهرش به است
entries.english: “The affection of fools,” said the other, “is beguiling; this envy of mine is better than its affection.

entries.farsi: هى بيا با من بران اين خرس را / خرس را مگزين مهل هم جنس را
entries.english: Hey, come with me and drive away this bear: do not choose the bear, do not forsake one of your own kind!”

entries.farsi: گفت رو رو كار خود كن اى حسود / گفت كارم اين بد و رزقت نبود
entries.english: “Go, go,” said he, “ mind your own business, O envious man!” Said the other, “This was my business, and it was not your fortune.

entries.farsi: من كم از خرسى نباشم اى شريف / ‏ترك او كن تا منت باشم حريف
entries.english: I am not less than a bear, O noble sir: abandon it, in order that I may be your comrade.

entries.farsi: بر تو دل مى‏لرزدم ز انديشه‏اى / ‏با چنين خرسى مرو در بيشه‏اى
entries.english: My heart is trembling with anxiety for you: do not go into a forest with a bear like this.

entries.farsi: اين دلم هرگز نلرزيد از گزاف / ‏نور حق است اين نه دعوى و نه الف
entries.english: This heart of mine has never trembled in vain; this is the Light of God, not pretence or idle brag.

entries.farsi: مومنم ينظر بنور اللهَّ شده / ‏هان و هان بگريز از اين آتشكده
entries.english: I am the true believer who has become seeing by the Light of God. Beware and beware! Flee from this fire-temple!”

entries.farsi: اين همه گفت و به گوشش در نرفت / ‏بد گمانى مرد را سدى است زفت
entries.english: He said all this, and it entered not his ear: suspicion is a thick barrier to a man.

entries.farsi: دست او بگرفت و دست از وى كشيد / گفت رفتم چون نه‏اى يار رشيد
entries.english: He took his hand, and he withdrew his hand from him. The other said, “I will go, since you are not a well-guided friend.”

entries.farsi: گفت رو بر من تو غم خواره مباش / ‏بو الفضوال معرفت كمتر تراش
entries.english: “Go,” cried he; “be not troubled for me; don’t carve so much wisdom, O meddlesome one!”

entries.farsi: باز گفتش من عدوى تو نى‏ام / ‏لطف باشد گر بيايى در پى‏ام
entries.english: He answered him, “I am not your enemy: it would be a kindness if you would come after me.”

entries.farsi: گفت خوابستم مرا بگذار و رو / گفت آخر يار را منقاد شو
entries.english: “I am sleepy,” said he;” let me alone, go!” He replied, “Pray, give in to your friend,

entries.farsi: تا بخسبى در پناه عاقلى / ‏در جوار دوستى صاحب دلى
entries.english: So that you may sleep under the safeguard of a sage, under the protection of one loved, a man of heart.”

entries.farsi: در خيال افتاد مرد از جد او / خشمگين شد زود گردانيد رو
entries.english: The man was thrown by his earnestness into a fancy: he became angry and quickly averted his face,

entries.farsi: كاين مگر قصد من آمد خونى است / ‏يا طمع دارد گدا و تونى است
entries.english: Thinking, “Perhaps this man has come to attack me—he is a murderer; or he has hope—he is a beggar and a tout;

entries.farsi: يا گرو بسته ست با ياران بدين / ‏كه بترساند مرا زين هم نشين
entries.english: Or he has wagered with his friends that he will make me afraid of this companion.”

entries.farsi: خود نيامد هيچ از خبث سرش / ‏يك گمان نيك اندر خاطرش
entries.english: From the wickedness of his heart, not one good surmise came into his thoughts at all.

entries.farsi: ظن نيكش جملگى بر خرس بود / او مگر مر خرس را هم جنس بود
entries.english: His good opinions were wholly for the bear: to be sure, he was of the same kind as the bear.

entries.farsi: عاقلى را از سگى تهمت نهاد / خرس را دانست اهل مهر و داد
entries.english: Through currishness, he suspected a sage and deemed a bear affectionate and just.

sections.title_fa: ‏گفتن موسى عليه السلام گوساله پرست را كه آن خيال انديشى و حزم تو كجاست
sections.title_en: How Moses, on whom be peace, said to one who worshipped the calf, “Where is Your vain skepticism and precaution’?”

entries.farsi: گفت موسى با يكى مست خيال / ‏كاى بد انديش از شقاوت وز ضالل
entries.english: Moses said to one drunken with fancy, “O you who thinks evil because of your unblessed condition and perdition,

entries.farsi: صد هزاران معجزه ديدى ز من / ‏صد خيالت مى‏فزود و شك و ظن
entries.english: You have had a hundred suspicions concerning my prophet-hood, notwithstanding these proofs and this noble nature.

entries.farsi: صد گمانت بود در پيغمبريم / ‏با چنين برهان و اين خلق كريم
entries.english: You have seen from me hundreds of thousands of miracles, a hundred fancies and doubts and opinions were growing in you.

entries.farsi: از خيال و وسوسه تنگ آمدى / ‏طعن بر پيغمبرى‏ام مى‏زدى
entries.english: You wert sorely pressed by fancy and devilish suggestion, you were sneering at my prophethood.

entries.farsi: گرد از دريا بر آوردم عيان / ‏تا رهيديت از شر فرعونيان
entries.english: I raised up dust from the sea before your eyes, so that you might be delivered from the wickedness of the people of Pharaoh.

entries.farsi: ز آسمان چل سال كاسه و خوان رسيد / وز دعايم جويى از سنگى دويد
entries.english: During forty years the platter and tray came from heaven, and at my prayer the river ran from a rock.

entries.farsi: اين و صد چندين و چندين گرم و سرد / از تو اى سرد آن توهم كم نكرد
entries.english: These and a hundred times as many, and all these diverse, did not make that vain imagination fade away from you, O cold man!

entries.farsi: بانگ زد گوساله‏اى از جادويى / ‏سجده كردى كه خداى من تويى
entries.english: Through sorcery a calf lowed; you bowed down to worship, saying, “You are my God.’

entries.farsi: آن توهمهات را سيالب برد / زيركى باردت را خواب برد
entries.english: Those imaginations were swept away by a flood, and your silly shrewdness went to sleep.

entries.farsi: چون نبودى بد گمان در حق او / چون نهادى سر چنان اى زشت رو
entries.english: How were you not suspicious in regard to him? Why did you lay your head like that, O ugly one?

entries.farsi: چون خيالت نامد از تزوير او / وز فساد سحر احمق‏گير او
entries.english: How did no idea come to you of his imposition and of the corruptness of his fool-catching magic?

entries.farsi: سامريى خود كه باشد اى سگان / ‏كه خدايى بر تراشد در جهان
entries.english: Who, indeed, is a Samiri, O ye curs, that he should hew up a God in the world?

entries.farsi: چون در اين تزوير او يكدل شدى / ‏وز همه اشكالها عاطل شدى
entries.english: How did you become of one mind as to this imposture of his, and become devoid of all perplexities?

entries.farsi: گاو مى‏شايد خدايى را به الف / ‏در رسولى‏ام تو چون كردى خالف
entries.english: Is a cow worthy to be deified on a vain boast, a hundred disputes as to the prophetic mission of one like me?

entries.farsi: پيش گاوى سجده كردى از خرى / ‏گشت عقلت صيد سحر سامرى
entries.english: Through asinine dullness you did cast yourself down in worship before a cow; your understanding fell a prey to Samiri’s magic.

entries.farsi: چشم دزديدى ز نور ذو الجلال / ‏اينت جهل وافر و عين ضالل
entries.english: You did steal your eye away from the Light of the Glorious: here is plenteous folly for you and the essence of damnation!

entries.farsi: شه بر آن عقل و گزينش كه تراست / ‏چون تو كان جهل را كشتن سزاست
entries.english: Fie upon such an understanding and choice as you have! It was fitting to kill a mine of foolishness like you.

entries.farsi: گاو زرين بانگ كرد آخر چه گفت / ‏كاحمقان را اين همه رغبت شگفت
entries.english: The golden calf uttered a cry; what did it say, that all this desire blossomed in the fools?

entries.farsi: ز آن عجبتر ديده‏ايد از من بسى / ‏ليك حق را كى پذيرد هر خسى
entries.english: You have seen from me many a thing more wonderful than that, but how should every rascal accept God?”

entries.farsi: باطالن را چه ربايد باطلى / ‏عاطالن را چه خوش آيد عاطلى
entries.english: What carries away worthless folk? Worthlessness. What pleases futile folk? Futility,

entries.farsi: ز انكه هر جنسى ربايد جنس خود / گاو سوى شير نر كى رو نهد
entries.english: Because every kind is carried away by its own kind: how should the ox turn its face towards the fierce lion?

entries.farsi: جز مگر از مكر تا او را خورد / چون سگ كهف از بنى آدم شود
entries.english: How should the wolf bear love for Joseph, unless, perchance, through cunning, in order to devour him?

entries.farsi: گرگ بر يوسف كجا عشق آورد / جز مگر از مكر تا او را خورد
entries.english: When it is delivered from wolfness, it will become familiar; like the dog of the Cave, it will become one of the sons of Adam.

entries.farsi: چون ابو بكر از محمد برد بو / ‏گفت هذا ليس وجه كاذب
entries.english: When Abu Bakr smelt Mohammed, he said, “This is not a face that lies;”

entries.farsi: دردمندى كش ز بام افتاد طشت / ‏زو نهان كرديم حق پنهان نگشت
entries.english: Since Bu Jahl was not of those in sympathy, he saw a hundred splittings of the moon, and believed not.

entries.farsi: چون نبد بو جهل از اصحاب درد / ديد صد شق قمر باور نكرد
entries.english: The sorrowful, whose bowl has fallen from the roof— we hide the Truth from him, it does not become hid;

entries.farsi: و انكه او جاهل بد از دردش بعيد / چند بنمودند و او آن را نديد
entries.english: While he that is ignorant and a stranger to His sorrow— how often has it been shown, and he has not seen it!

entries.farsi: آينه‏ى دل صاف بايد تا در او / واشناسى صورت زشت از نكو
entries.english: The mirror of the heart must be clear, in order that you may know therein the ugly form from the beautiful.

sections.title_fa: ترك گفتن آن مرد ناصح بعد از مبالغه‏ى پند مغرور خرس را
sections.title_en: How the man of sincere counsel, after having done his utmost in admonition, took leave of him who was deluded by the bear.

entries.farsi: آن مسلمان ترك ابله كرد و تفت / ‏زير لب الحول‏گويان باز رفت
entries.english: That Moslem left the foolish man and quickly, saying La hawl under his lip, went back.

entries.farsi: گفت چون از جد و پندم وز جدال / ‏در دل او بيش مى‏زايد خيال
entries.english: He said, “Inasmuch as from my earnestness in admonition and from disputing, vain fancies are being born in his mind more ,

entries.farsi: پس ره پند و نصيحت بسته شد / امر أَعْ رِضْ عَنْهُمْ پيوسته شد
entries.english: Therefore the road of admonition and counsel has become barred: the command, ‘Turn aside from them,’ has arrived.”

entries.farsi: چون دوايت مى‏فزايد درد پس / چون دوايت مى‏فزايد درد پس
entries.english: When your remedy increases the disease, then tell your story to one that seeks. Read ‘Abasa :

entries.farsi: چون كه اعمى طالب حق آمده ست / ‏بهر فقر او را نشايد سينه خست
entries.english: “As the blind man has come seeking the Truth’, it is not proper to wound his breast on account of his poverty.

entries.farsi: احمدا ديدى كه قومى از ملوك / ‏مستمع گشتند گشتى خوش كه بوك
entries.english: You art eager for the great ones to take the right way, in order that the common folk may learn from the rulers.

entries.farsi: تو حريصى بر رشاد مهتران / ‏تا بياموزند عام از سروران
entries.english: O Ahmad, you have seen that a company of princes have become ready to listen, and you are pleased that, maybe,

entries.farsi: اين رئيسان يار دين گردند خوش / ‏بر عرب اينها سرند و بر حبش
entries.english: These chieftains will become good friends of the Religion; they are lords over the Arabs and the Abyssinians,

entries.farsi: بگذرد اين صيت از بصره و تبوك / ‏ز انكه الناس على دين الملوك
entries.english: The fame of this will pass beyond Basra and Tabuk, since people follow the religion of their kings.’

entries.farsi: زين سبب تو از ضرير مهتدى / ‏رو بگردانيدى و تنگ آمدى
entries.english: For this cause you have averted your face from the blind man that was led into the right way, and have become vexed,

entries.farsi: مزدحم مى‏گرديم در وقت تنگ / ‏اين نصيحت مى‏كنم نه از خشم و جنگ
entries.english: Saying, this gathering seldom falls out so opportunely, you are one of my friends, and your time is ample.

entries.farsi: ‏رو بگردانيدى و تنگ آمدى / ‏تو ز يارانى و وقت تو فراخ
entries.english: You are urgent with me at an inconvenient time. I give you this admonition, not in anger and strife.’

entries.farsi: احمدا نزد خدا اين يك ضرير / بهتر از صد قيصر است و صد وزير
entries.english: O Ahmad, in the sight of God this one blind man is better than a hundred emperors and a hundred viziers.

entries.farsi: ياد الناس معادن هين بيار / معدنى باشد فزون از صد هزار
entries.english: Take heed, remember, Men are mines: one mine may be more than a hundred thousand.

entries.farsi: معدن لعل و عقيق مكتنس / ‏بهتر است از صد هزاران كان مس
entries.english: The mine of lurking ruby and cornelian is better than myriads of mines of copper.

entries.farsi: احمدا اينجا ندارد مال سود / سينه بايد پر ز عشق و درد و دود
entries.english: O Ahmad, here riches have no use; a breast is wanted, full of love and pain and sighs.

entries.farsi: اعمى روشن دل آمد در مبند / پند او را ده كه حق اوست پند
entries.english: The blind man of enlightened heart is come: do not shut the door; give him counsel, for counsel is his right.

entries.farsi: گر دو سه ابله ترا منكر شدند / تلخ كى گردى چو هستى كان قند
entries.english: If two or three fools have disbelieved in you, how should you be soured when you art a mine of candy?

entries.farsi: گر دو سه ابله ترا تهمت نهند / حق براى تو گواهى مى‏دهد
entries.english: If two or three fools impute falsehood to you, God is giving testimony on your behalf.”

entries.farsi: گفت از اقرار عالم فارغم / ‏آن كه حق باشد گواه او را چه غم
entries.english: He said, “ I am unconcerned with the acknowledgment of the world: what care hath he whose witness is God?

entries.farsi: گر خفاشى را ز خورشيدى خورى است / ‏آن دليل آمد كه آن خورشيد نيست
entries.english: If a bat receives anything agreeable from a sun, it is a proof that that is not the sun.

entries.farsi: نفرت خفاشكان باشد دليل / ‏كه منم خورشيد تابان جليل
entries.english: The disgust of the wretched bats is proof that I am the shining glorious sun.

entries.farsi: گر گالبى را جعل راغب شود / آن دليل ناگالبى مى‏كند
entries.english: If the beetle feels a desire for some rose-water, that constitutes a proof of its not being rose-water.

entries.farsi: گر شود قلبى خريدار محك / ‏در محكى‏اش در آيد نقص و شك
entries.english: If any false coin is eager for the touchstone, uncertainty and doubt enter into its being a touchstone.

entries.farsi: دزد شب خواهد نه روز اين را بدان / ‏شب نى‏ام روزم كه تابم در جهان
entries.english: The thief wants night, not day—mark this! I am not night, I am day, for I shine throughout the world.

entries.farsi: فارقم فاروقم و غلبيروار / تا كه كاه از من نمى‏يابد گذار
entries.english: I am discerning, I am exceedingly discriminating and sieve like, so that the chaff finds no passage through me.

entries.farsi: آرد را پيدا كنم من از سبوس / ‏تا نمايم كاين نقوش است آن نفوس
entries.english: I make the flour distinct from the bran, in order to show that this is the forms, and that the souls.

entries.farsi: من چو ميزان خدايم در جهان / ‏وانمايم هر سبك را از گران
entries.english: I am as the scales of God in the world: I reveal every light thing from the heavy.

entries.farsi: گاو را داند خدا گوساله‏اى / ‏خر خريدارى و در خور كاله‏اى
entries.english: A calf deems the cow God; the ass one who is fond and that which accords with its desires’.

entries.farsi: من نه گاوم تا كه گوساله‏م خرد / من نه خارم كاشترى از من چرد
entries.english: I am not a cow, that the calf should be fond of me; I am not thistles that a camel should browse on me.

entries.farsi: او گمان دارد كه با من جور كرد / بلكه از آيينه‏ى من روفت گرد
entries.english: He supposes that he has done me an injury; no, he has wiped away the dust from my mirror.”

sections.title_fa: تملّق كردن ديوانه جالينوس را و ترسيدن جالينوس
sections.title_en: How the madman sought to ingratiate himself with Jalinus, and how Jalinus was afraid.

entries.farsi: گفت جالينوس با اصحاب خود / مر مرا تا آن فالن دارو دهد
entries.english: Jalinus said to his companions, “Let give me such-and-such a medicine.”

entries.farsi: دور از عقل تو اين ديگر مگو / گفت در من كرد يك ديوانه رو
