entries.english: Then said that person to him, “O master of sciences, this medicine is sought for madness.

entries.farsi: پس بدو گفت آن يكى اى ذو فنون / ‏اين دوا خواهند از بهر جنون
entries.english: Far be this from your intellect! Say no more.” He replied, “A madman turned his face to me,

entries.farsi: ساعتى در روى من خوش بنگريد / چشمكم زد آستين من دريد
entries.english: Looked pleasantly on my face for a while, made little eyes at me, and plucked my sleeve.

entries.farsi: گر نه جنسيت بدى در من از او / كى رخ آوردى به من آن زشت رو
entries.english: Had there not been in me congeniality with him, how would that ill-favoured man have turned his face towards me?

entries.farsi: گر نديدى جنس خود كى آمدى / ‏كى به غير جنس خود را بر زدى
entries.english: Had he not seen one of his own kind, how should he have approached? How should he have thrown himself upon one of another kind? “

entries.farsi: چون دو كس بر هم زند بى‏هيچ شك / ‏در ميانشان هست قدر مشترك
entries.english: When two persons come into touch with each other, without any doubt there is something in common between them.

entries.farsi: كى پرد مرغى مگر با جنس خود / صحبت ناجنس گور است و لحد
entries.english: How should a bird fly except with its own kind? The society of the uncongenial is the grave and the tomb.

sections.title_fa: سبب پريدن و چريدن مرغى با مرغى كه جنس او نبود
sections.title_en: The cause of a bird’s flying and feeding with a bird that is not of its own kind.

entries.farsi: آن حكيمى گفت ديدم هم تكى / ‏در بيابان زاغ را با لكلكى
entries.english: Said a certain sage, “I saw a crow running about with a stork”

entries.farsi: در عجب ماندم بجستم حالشان / ‏تا چه قدر مشترك يابم نشان
entries.english: I marvelled long, and I investigated their case, in order that I might find the clue what it was that they had in common.

entries.farsi: چون شدم نزديك، من حيران و دنگ / ‏خود بديدم هر دوان بودند لنگ
entries.english: When, amazed and bewildered, I approached them, indeed I saw that both of them were lame.”

entries.farsi: خاصه شهبازى كه او عرشى بود / با يكى جغدى كه او فرشى بود
entries.english: In particular, a royal falcon, which is of the highest heaven, with an owl, which is of the low earth?

entries.farsi: آن يكى خورشيد عليين بود / وين دگر خفاش كز سجين بود
entries.english: That one is the sun of while the other is a bat which belongs to Sijjin.

entries.farsi: آن يكى نورى ز هر عيبى برى / ‏وين يكى كورى گداى هر درى
entries.english: That one is a luminary, free from every defect, while this one is a blind man begging at every door.

entries.farsi: آن يكى ماهى كه بر پروين زند / وين يكى كرمى كه در سرگين زيد
entries.english: That one is a moon that strikes upon the Pleiades, while this one is a worm that cleaves to dung.

entries.farsi: آن يكى يوسف رخى عيسى نفس / ‏وين يكى گرگى و يا خر با جرس
entries.english: That one has the face of a Joseph, the breath of a Jesus, while this one is a wolf or an ass with a bell.

entries.farsi: آن يكى پران شده در ال مكان / ‏وين يكى در كاهدان همچون سگان
entries.english: That one has flown to Spacelessness, while this one is in the straw-barn, like the dogs.

entries.farsi: با زبان معنوى گل با جعل / ‏اين همى‏گويد كه اى گنده بغل
entries.english: With the tongue of meaning the rose is saying to the beetle this—“O stinking,

entries.farsi: گر گريزانى ز گلشن بى‏گمان / ‏هست آن نفرت كمال گلستان
entries.english: If you art fleeing from the rose-bed, doubtless that aversion is the perfection of the rose-garden.

entries.farsi: غيرت من بر سر تو دور باش / ‏مى‏زند كاى خس از اينجا دور باش
entries.english: My jealousy strikes you on the head with a baton, keep far away from here, O vile one;

entries.farsi: بلبالن را جاى مى‏زيبد چمن / ‏مر جعل را در چمين خوشتر وطن
entries.english: For if, base wretch, you should mix with me, it will be thought that you art of my stock.

entries.farsi: ور بياميزى تو با من اى دنى / ‏اين گمان آيد كه از كان منى
entries.english: For nightingales the garden is the proper place; for the beetle the best home is in excrement.”

entries.farsi: حق مرا چون از پليدى پاك داشت / ‏چون سزد بر من پليدى را گماشت
entries.english: Since God has kept me pure from filth, how were it seemly to appoint a foul one for me?

entries.farsi: يك رگم ز ايشان بد و آن را بريد / در من آن بد رگ كجا خواهد رسيد
entries.english: I had a vein of them. He cut it out: how will he with the vein of evil attain unto me?

entries.farsi: يك نشان آدم آن بود از ازل / ‏كه ماليك سر نهندش از محل
entries.english: One mark of Adam from eternity was this, that the angels should lay their heads before him, because it was his place.

entries.farsi: يك نشان ديگر آن كه آن بليس / ‏ننهدش سر كه منم شاه و رئيس
entries.english: Another mark was that Iblis, saying, “I am the king and chief,” should not lay down his head before him.

entries.farsi: هم سجود هر ملك ميزان اوست / ‏هم جحود آن عدو برهان اوست
entries.english: But if Iblis too had become a worshipper, he would not have been Adam: he would have been another.

entries.farsi: پس اگر ابليس هم ساجد شدى / ‏او نبودى آدم او غيرى بدى
entries.english: At once the worship of every angel is the test of him and the denial by that enemy is the proof of him.

entries.farsi: هم گواه اوست اقرار ملك / هم گواه اوست اقرار ملك
entries.english: At once the acknowledgment by the angels is witness for him, and the disbelief of that petty cur is witness for him.

sections.title_fa: ‏تتمّه اعتماد آن مغرور بر تملق خرس
sections.title_en: Conclusion of the trust of that deluded man in the fawningness of the bear.

entries.farsi: شخص خفت و خرس مى‏راندش مگس / ‏وز ستيز آمد مگس زو باز پس
entries.english: The man fell asleep, and the bear kept driving the flies away, but in spite of him they soon came back again.

entries.farsi: چند بارش راند از روى جوان / ‏آن مگس زو باز مى‏آمد دوان
entries.english: Several times he drove them from the youth’s face, but soon they came hurrying back once more.

entries.farsi: خشمگين شد با مگس خرس و برفت / ‏بر گرفت از كوه سنگى سخت زفت
entries.english: The bear was enraged with the flies and went off. He picked up a very big stone from the mountain-side.

entries.farsi: سنگ آورد و مگس را ديد باز / بر رخ خفته گرفته جاى ساز
entries.english: He fetched the stone, and saw the flies again settled comfortably on the face of the sleeper.

entries.farsi: بر گرفت آن آسيا سنگ و بزد / بر مگس تا آن مگس واپس خزد
entries.english: He took up that millstone and struck at the flies, in order that they might retire.

entries.farsi: سنگ روى خفته را خشخاش كرد / اين مثل بر جمله عالم فاش كرد
entries.english: The stone made powder of the sleeping man’s face, and published to the whole world this adage—.

entries.farsi: مهر ابله مهر خرس آمد يقين / ‏كين او مهر است و مهر اوست كين
entries.english: “The love of a fool is for sure the love of a bear: his hate is love and his love is hate.”

entries.farsi: عهد او سست است و ويران و ضعيف / ‏گفت او زفت و وفاى او نحيف
entries.english: His promise is infirm and corrupt and feeble; his word stout and his performance lean.

entries.farsi: گر خورد سوگند هم باور مكن / گر خورد سوگند هم باور مكن
entries.english: Do not believe him, even if he takes an oath: the man whose speech is false will break his oath.

entries.farsi: چون كه بى‏سوگند گفتش بد دروغ / ‏تو ميفت از مكر و سوگندش به دوغ
entries.english: Inasmuch as, without the oath, his word was a lie, is he not entrapped by his deceit and oath?

entries.farsi: نفس او مير است و عقل او اسير / صد هزاران مصحفش خود خورده‏گير
entries.english: His nafs is in command, and his intellect captive; even suppose that he has sworn on a hundred thousand Qurans,

entries.farsi: چون كه بى‏سوگند پيمان بشكند / گر خورد سوگند هم آن بشكند
entries.english: Since without an oath he will break his pact— if he takes an oath, he will break that too;

entries.farsi: ز آن كه نفس آشفته‏تر گردد از آن / ‏كه كنى بندش به سوگند گران
entries.english: Because the nafs becomes the more infuriated by your binding it with a heavy oath

entries.farsi: چون اسيرى بند بر حاكم نهد / حاكم آن را بر درد بيرون جهد
entries.english: When a captive puts bonds upon a governor, the governor will burst them and leap out;

entries.farsi: بر سرش كوبد ز خشم آن بند را / مى‏زند بر روى او سوگند را
entries.english: He will bang those bonds on his head in wrath, dashing the oath in his face.

entries.farsi: تو ز اوفوا بالعقودش دست شو / احْ فَظُ وا أَيْمانَكُمْ با او مگو
entries.english: Wash your hands of his, “Fulfill your promises”; do not say to him, “Keep your oaths.”

sections.title_en: 2140 But he that knows to whom he makes the promise

entries.farsi: و آن كه حق را ساخت در پيمان سند / تن كند چون تار و گرد او تند
entries.english: will make his body as a thread and wind about Him

sections.title_fa: ‏رفتن مصطفى عليه السلام به عيادت صحابى و بيان فايده عيادت
sections.title_en: How Mustafa, on whom be peace, went to visit the sick Companion; and an exposition of the profit of visiting the sick.

entries.farsi: از صحابه خواجه‏اى بيمار شد / و اندر آن بيماريش چون تار شد
entries.english: A notable amongst the Companions fell ill, and in that illness of his became as a thread.

entries.farsi: مصطفى آمد عيادت سوى او / چون همه لطف و كرم بد خوى او
entries.english: Mustafa went to visit him, since his nature was all kindness and generosity.

entries.farsi: در عيادت رفتن تو فايده است / ‏فايده آن باز با تو عايده است
entries.english: There is profit in your visiting the sick: the profit is returning to you again.

entries.farsi: فايده اوّل كه آن شخص عليل / ‏بوك قطبى باشد و شاه جليل
entries.english: The first profit is that the sick person may perchance be a Qutb and a glorious king;

entries.farsi: ور عدو باشد همين احسان نكوست / ‏كه به احسان بس عدو گشته است دوست
entries.english: And if he be not a Qutb, he may be a friend of the Way; if he be not the king, he may be the cavalier of the host.

entries.farsi: ور نباشد قطب يار ره بود / شه نباشد فارس اسپه بود
entries.english: Deem it, then, incumbent to attach yourselves to the friends of the Way, whosoever it may be, and whether footman or rider.

entries.farsi: پس صله ياران ره الزم شمار / هر كه باشد گر پياده گر سوار
entries.english: And if he be a foe, still this kindness is good, for by kindness many a foe has been made a friend;

entries.farsi: ور نگردد دوست كينش كم شود / ز آن كه احسان كينه را مرهم شود
entries.english: And though he does not become a friend, his enmity is lessened, because kindness becomes the balm for enmity.

entries.farsi: بس فوايد هست غير اين و ليك / ‏از درازى خايفم اى يار نيك
entries.english: There are many profits besides these, but I am afraid of being tedious, good friend.

entries.farsi: حاصل اين آمد كه يار جمع باش / ‏هم چو بتگر از حجر يارى تراش
entries.english: The gist is this: be the friend of the whole community: like the idol-maker, carve a friend out of the stone,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه انبوهى و جمع كاروان / ‏ره زنان را بشكند پشت و سنان
entries.english: Because the throng and multitude of a caravan will break the backs and spears of the highwaymen

entries.farsi: چون دو چشم دل ندارى اى عنود / كه نمى‏دانى تو هيزم را ز عود
entries.english: Inasmuch as you have not the heart’s two eyes, O rebellious man, so that you cannot distinguish firewood from aloes-wood,

entries.farsi: چون كه گنجى هست در عالم مرنج / ‏هيچ ويران را مدان خالى ز گنج
entries.english: Since there exists a treasure in the world, do not grieve: deem no ruined place empty of treasure.

entries.farsi: قصد هر درويش مى‏كن از گزاف / ‏چون نشان يابى بجد مى‏كن طواف
entries.english: Take yourself to every dervish at random, and when you find the mark, frequent assiduously.

entries.farsi: چون تو را آن چشم باطن بين نبود / گنج مى‏پندار اندر هر وجود
entries.english: As the inward-seeing eye was not to you, think always the treasure in everybody.

sections.title_fa: ‏وحى كردن حق تعالى به موسى عليه السلام كه چرا به عيادت من نيامدى
sections.title_en: How the high God revealed to Moses, on whom be peace, “Why did you not visit me when I was ill?”

entries.farsi: آمد از حق سوى موسى اين عتاب / ‏كاى طلوع ماه ديده تو ز جيب
entries.english: To Moses there came from God this reproach: “O you who have seen the rising of the moon from your bosom,

entries.farsi: مشرقت كردم ز نور ايزدى / ‏من حقم رنجور گشتم نامدى
entries.english: You whom I have illumined with the Divine Light! I am God, I fell sick, you came not.”

entries.farsi: گفت سبحانا تو پاكى از زيان / ‏اين چه رمز است اين بكن يا رب بيان
entries.english: Moses said,” O You transcendent in Your glory, You are clear of loss. What mystery is this? Explain this, O Lord!”

entries.farsi: باز فرمودش كه در رنجوريم / ‏چون نپرسيدى تو از روى كرم
entries.english: God said unto him again, “Why in My sickness did you not kindly ask after Me? “

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب نيست نقصانى تو را / عقل گم شد اين سخن را بر گشا
entries.english: He answered, “O Lord, You have no imperfection. Understanding is lost: unfold these words.”

entries.farsi: گفت آرى بنده خاص گزين / ‏گشت رنجور او منم نيكو ببين
entries.english: God said, “Yea; a favourite and chosen slave fell sick. I am he. Consider well!

entries.farsi: هست معذوريش معذورى من / ‏هست رنجوريش رنجورى من
entries.english: His excusability is My excusability, his sickness is My sickness.”

entries.farsi: هر كه خواهد همنشينى خدا / تا نشيند در حضور اوليا
entries.english: Whoever wishes to sit with God let him sit in the presence of the saints.

entries.farsi: از حضور اوليا گر بسكلى / ‏تو هالكى ز آن كه جزوى بى‏كلى
entries.english: If you are broken off from the presence of the saints, you are in perdition, because you are a part without the whole.

entries.farsi: هر كه را ديو از كريمان وابرد / بى‏كسش يابد سرش را او خورد
entries.english: Whomever the Devil cuts off from the noble, he finds him without any one, and he devours his head.

entries.farsi: يك بدست از جمع رفتن يك زمان / ‏مكر ديو است بشنو و نيكو بدان
entries.english: To go for one moment a single span apart from the community is the Devil’s guile. listen, and know well.

sections.title_fa: تنها كردن باغبان صوفى و فقيه و علوى را از همديگر
sections.title_en: How the gardener isolated the Sufi, the jurist,
and the descendant of ‘ Ali from one another.

entries.farsi: باغبانى چون نظر در باغ كرد / ديد چون دزدان به باغ خود سه مرد
entries.english: A gardener, when he looked into the orchard, saw three men in his orchard, as thieves:

entries.farsi: يك فقيه و يك شريف و صوفيى / ‏هر يكى شوخى بدى اليوفيى
entries.english: A jurist and a Sharif and a Sufi: each one an impudent, knavish and perfidious rogue.

entries.farsi: گفت با اينها مرا صد حجت است / ‏ليك جمع‏اند و جماعت قوت است
entries.english: He said, “ I have a hundred arguments against these fellows, but they are united, and a united party is strength.

entries.farsi: بر نيايم يك تنه با سه نفر / پس ببرمشان نخست از همدگر
entries.english: I cannot cope singly with three persons, so first I will separate them from one another.

entries.farsi: هر يكى را من به سويى افكنم / ‏چون كه تنها شد سبيلش بر كنم
entries.english: I will isolate each one from the others, and when each is alone, I will tear out his moustache.”

entries.farsi: حيله كرد و كرد صوفى را به راه / ‏تا كند يارانش را با او تباه
entries.english: He employed a ruse and got the Sufi away, that he might poison his friends against him.

entries.farsi: گفت صوفى را برو سوى وثاق / ‏يك گليم آور براى اين رفاق
entries.english: He said to the Sufi “Go to the house and fetch a rug for these companions.”

entries.farsi: رفت صوفى گفت خلوت با دو يار / تو فقيهى وين شريف نامدار
entries.english: The Sufi departed, he said in private to the two friends, “You are a jurist, and this is a renowned Sharif.

entries.farsi: ما به فتوى تو نانى مى‏خوريم / ‏ما به پر دانش تو مى‏پريم
entries.english: It is according to your legal decision that we eat a loaf: it is by the wings of your knowledge that we fly.

entries.farsi: وين دگر شه زاده و سلطان ماست / ‏سيد است از خاندان مصطفاست
entries.english: And this other is our prince and sovereign: he is a Sayyid; he is of the House of ‘Mustafa.

entries.farsi: كيست آن صوفى شكم خوار خسيس / ‏تا بود با چون شما شاهان جليس
entries.english: Who is this gluttonous vile Sufi that he should associate with kings like you?

entries.farsi: چون بيايد مر و را پنبه كنيد / هفته‏اى بر باغ و راغ من زنيد
entries.english: When he comes, drive him away and take possession of my orchard and field for a week.

entries.farsi: باغ چه بود جان من آن شماست / ‏اى شما بوده مرا چون چشم راست
entries.english: What is my orchard? My life is yours, O you who have been as my right eye.”

entries.farsi: وسوسه كرد و مر ايشان را فريفت / ‏آه كز ياران نمى‏بايد شكيفت
entries.english: He made evil suggestions and beguiled them. Ah, one must not patiently suffer the loss of friends.

entries.farsi: چون به ره كردند صوفى را و رفت / ‏خصم شد اندر پيش با چوب زفت
entries.english: When they had turned the Sufi away and he was gone, the enemy went after him with a stout cudgel.

entries.farsi: گفت اى سگ صوفيى باشد كه تيز / اندر آيى باغ ما تو از ستيز
entries.english: “O dog,” he cried, “is it Sufism that of a sudden you come into my orchard in spite?

entries.farsi: اين جنيدت ره نمود و بايزيد / از كدامين شيخ و پيرت اين رسيد
entries.english: Did Junayd or Bayazid direct you to behave in this way? From what Shaykh and Pir did this reach you?”

entries.farsi: كوفت صوفى را چو تنها يافتش / ‏نيم كشتش كرد و سر بشكافتش
entries.english: He beat the Sufi when he found him alone; he half killed him and cracked his head.

entries.farsi: گفت صوفى آن من بگذشت ليك / ‏اى رفيقان پاس خود داريد نيك
entries.english: Said the Sufi, “My life is over, but O comrades, take good care of yourselves!

entries.farsi: مر مرا اغيار دانستيد هان / ‏نيستم اغيارتر زين قلتبان
entries.english: You regarded me as an alien. Look out! I am not more alien than this scoundrel.

entries.farsi: اين چه من خوردم شما را خوردنى است / ‏وين چنين شربت جزاى هر دنى است
entries.english: That which I have drunk must be drunk by you, and such a draught as this is the due of every cad.”

entries.farsi: اين جهان كوه است و گفت‏وگوى تو / از صدا هم باز آيد سوى تو
entries.english: This world is the mountain, and your words come back to you from the echo.

entries.farsi: چون ز صوفى گشت فارغ باغبان / ‏يك بهانه كرد ز آن پس جنس آن
entries.english: After the gardener had finished with the Sufi, he invented a pretext of the same kind as the former,

entries.farsi: كاى شريف من برو سوى وثاق / ‏كه ز بهر چاشت پختم من رقاق
entries.english: Saying, “O my Sharif, go to the house, for I have baked thin cakes of bread for breakfast

entries.farsi: بر در خانه بگو قيماز را / تا بيارد آن رقاق و قاز را
entries.english: At the house-door tell Qaymaz to fetch those cakes and the goose.”

entries.farsi: چون به ره كردش بگفت اى تيز بين / ‏تو فقيهى ظاهر است اين و يقين
entries.english: Having sent him off, he said, “O keen-sighted one, you are a jurist; this is manifest and sure.

entries.farsi: او شريفى مى‏كند دعوى سرد / مادر او را كه داند تا كه كرد
entries.english: He a Sharif! It is an absurd claim he is making. Who knows who committed adultery with his mother?

entries.farsi: بر زن و بر فعل زن دل مى‏نهيد / عقل ناقص و آن گهانى اعتماد
entries.english: Will you set your hearts on woman and the deeds of woman? Weak mind, and then trust?

entries.farsi: خويشتن را بر على و بر نبى / ‏بسته است اندر زمانه بس غبى
entries.english: He has tacked himself on to ‘Ali and to the Prophet— and there is many a fool in the world.”

entries.farsi: هر كه باشد از زنا و زانيان / ‏اين برد ظن در حق ربانيان
entries.english: Whoever is of adultery and of the adulterous will think this concerning the men of God.

entries.farsi: هر كه بر گردد سرش از چرخها / همچو خود گردنده بيند خانه را
entries.english: Any one whose head is made giddy by gyrations sees the house turning round like himself.

entries.farsi: آن چه گفت آن باغبان بو الفضول / ‏حال او بد، دور از اوالد رسول
entries.english: What that vain talker, the gardener, said was his condition; far from the descendants of the Prophet.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى او نتيجه مرتدان / ‏كى چنين گفتى براى خاندان
entries.english: If he had not been the issue of apostates, how should he have spoken thus as regards House?

entries.farsi: خواند افسونها شنيد آن را فقيه / ‏در پيش رفت آن ستمكار سفيه
entries.english: He used spells, and the jurist listened to them. That insolent bully went after him.

entries.farsi: گفت اى خر اندر اين باغت كه خواند / دزدى از پيغمبرت ميراث ماند
entries.english: He said, “O ass, who invited you into this orchard? Has robbery been left to you as an inheritance by the Prophet?

entries.farsi: شير را بچه همى‏ماند بدو / تو به پيغمبر به چه مانى بگو
entries.english: The lion’s cub resembles him: in what do you resemble the Prophet? Tell!”

entries.farsi: با شريف آن كرد مرد ملتجى / ‏كه كند با آل ياسين خارجى
entries.english: The man who had sought refuge did to the Sharif that which a Kharijite would do to the Family of Ya-sin.

entries.farsi: تا چه كين دارند دايم ديو و غول / ‏چون يزيد و شمر با آل رسول
entries.english: What hatred devils and ghouls like Yazid and Shimr always have towards the Prophet’s Family?

entries.farsi: شد شريف از زخم آن ظالم خراب / ‏با فقيه او گفت ما جستيم از آب
entries.english: The Sharif was devastated by the blows of that ruffian. He said to the jurist, “I have jumped out of the water.

entries.farsi: پاى دار اكنون كه ماندى فرد و كم / ‏چون دهل شو زخم مى‏خور بر شكم
entries.english: Stand fast, now that you are left alone and deprived. Be as a drum, suffer blows on the belly!

entries.farsi: گر شريف و اليق و هم دم نى‏ام / ‏از چنين ظالم تو را من كم نى‏ام
entries.english: If I am not a Sharif and worthy and a bosom-friend, I am no worse for you than such a ruffian as this.”

entries.farsi: شد از او فارغ بيامد كاى فقيه / ‏چه فقيهى اى تو ننگ هر سفيه
entries.english: He finished with him, and came up, saying, “O jurist, what jurist are you, O you disgrace to every fool?

entries.farsi: فتوى‏ات اين است اى ببريده دست / ‏كاندر آيى و نگويى امر هست
entries.english: Is it your legal opinion, O convicted thief, that you may come without asking leave’?

entries.farsi: اين چنين رخصت بخواندى در وسيط / يا بدست اين مسئله اندر محيط
entries.english: Have you read such a license in the Wasit, or has this question been in the Muhit?”

entries.farsi: گفت حق استت بزن دستت رسيد / اين سزاى آن كه از ياران بريد
entries.english: “You are right,” he replied; “beat: you have got the upper hand. This is the fit penalty for him that parts from friends.”

sections.title_fa: ‏رجعت به قصه مريض و عيادت پيغامبر عليه السلام
sections.title_en: Returning to the story of the sick man and the visit paid by the Prophet, God bless him and grant him peace!

entries.farsi: اين عيادت از براى اين صله است / ‏وين صله از صد محبت حامله است
entries.english: This visiting of the sick is for the sake of this attachment, and this attachment is pregnant with a hundred lovingkindnesses.

entries.farsi: در عيادت شد رسول بى‏نديد / آن صحابى را به حال نزع ديد
entries.english: The peerless Prophet went to visit the sick man; he found that Companion at the last gasp.

entries.farsi: چون شوى دور از حضور اوليا / در حقيقت گشته‏اى دور از خدا
entries.english: When you become far from the presence of the saints, you have in reality become far from God.

entries.farsi: چون نتيجه هجر همراهان غم است / ‏كى فراق روى شاهان ز آن كم است
entries.english: Inasmuch as the result of parting from fellow-travellers is sorrow, how is separation from the countenance of the kings less than that?

entries.farsi: سايه شاهان طلب هر دم شتاب / ‏تا شوى ز آن سايه بهتر ز آفتاب
entries.english: Hasten every moment to seek the shadow of kings, that by means of that shadow you may become superior to the sun.

entries.farsi: گر سفر دارى بدين نيت برو / ور حضر باشد از اين غافل مشو
entries.english: If you have a journey, go with this intention; and if it be at home, neglect not this.

sections.title_fa: گفتن شيخى بايزيد را كه كعبه منم گرد من طوافى مى‏كن
sections.title_en: How a certain Shaykh said to Bayazid,” I am the Ka`ba: perform a circumambulation round me.”

entries.farsi: سوى مكه شيخ امت بايزيد / از براى حج و عمره مى‏دويد
entries.english: Bayazid, the Shaykh of the community, was hurrying to Mecca for the greater pilgrimage and the lesser.

entries.farsi: او به هر شهرى كه رفتى از نخست / ‏مر عزيزان را بكردى باز جست
entries.english: In every city to which he went he would at first make search after the venerable.

entries.farsi: گرد مى‏گشتى كه اندر شهر كيست / ‏كاو بر اركان بصيرت متكى‏است
entries.english: He would roam about, asking, “Who is there in the city that is relying on insight?”

entries.farsi: گفت حق اندر سفر هر جا روى / ‏بايد اول طالب مردى شوى
entries.english: God has said, “Wherever you go in your travels, you must first seek after a man.”

entries.farsi: قصد گنجى كن كه اين سود و زيان / ‏در تبع آيد تو آن را فرع دان
entries.english: Go in quest of a treasure, for profit and loss come second: regard them as the branch.

entries.farsi: هر كه كارد قصد گندم باشدش / ‏كاه خود اندر تبع مى‏آيدش
entries.english: Whoever sows is in quest of wheat; the chaff comes to him indeed, secondarily.

entries.farsi: قصد كعبه كن چو وقت حج بود / چون كه رفتى مكه هم ديده شود
entries.english: If you sow chaff, no wheat will come up: seek a man, seek a man, a man!

entries.farsi: كه بكارى بر نيايد گندمى / ‏مردمى جو مردمى جو مردمى
entries.english: When it is the season of pilgrimage, go in quest of the Ka`ba; when you have gone, Mecca also will be seen.

entries.farsi: قصد در معراج ديد دوست بود / در تبع عرش و ماليك هم نمود
entries.english: In the Miraj the quest was vision of the Beloved; it was but secondarily that the empyrean and the angels were also shown.

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت
sections.title_en: Story.

entries.farsi: خانه‏ى نو ساخت روزى نو مريد / پير آمد خانه‏ى او را بديد
entries.english: A novice one day built a new house; the Pir came saw his house.

entries.farsi: گفت شيخ آن نو مريد خويش را / امتحان كرد آن نكو انديش را
entries.english: The Shaykh said to his new disciple— he put to the test him that had good thoughts

entries.farsi: روزن از بهر چه كردى اى رفيق / ‏گفت تا نور اندر آيد زين طريق
entries.english: “Why have you made a window, O comrade?” Said he, “In order that light may come in by this way”

entries.farsi: گفت آن فرع است اين بايد نياز / تا از اين ره بشنوى بانگ نماز
entries.english: He said, “That is the branch; want must be this, that through this channel you may hear the call to prayer.”

entries.farsi: بايزيد اندر سفر جستى بسى / ‏تا بيابد خضر وقت خود كسى
entries.english: Bayazid, on his journey, sought much to find some one that was the Khizr of his time.

entries.farsi: ديد پيرى با قدى همچون هالل / ‏ديد در وى فر و گفتار رجال
entries.english: He spied an old man with a stature like the new moon; he saw in him the majesty and speech of men;

entries.farsi: ديده نابينا و دل چون آفتاب / ‏همچو پيلى ديده هندستان به خواب
entries.english: His eyes sightless and his heart as the sun: like an elephant dreaming of Hindustan.

entries.farsi: چشم بسته خفته بيند صد طرب / ‏چون گشايد آن نبيند اى عجب
entries.english: With closed eyes, asleep, he beholds a hundred delights; when he opens, he sees not those—oh, wonderful!

entries.farsi: بس عجب در خواب روشن مى‏شود / دل درون خواب روزن مى‏شود
entries.english: Many a wonder is made manifest in sleep: in sleep the heart becomes a window.

entries.farsi: آن كه بيدار است و بيند خواب خوش / ‏عارف است او خاك او در ديده كش
entries.english: One that is awake and dreams fair dreams, he is the knower: smear your eyes with his dust.

entries.farsi: پيش او بنشست و مى‏پرسيد حال / ‏يافتش درويش و هم صاحب عيال
entries.english: He sat down before him and asked about his condition; he found him to be a dervish and also a family man.

entries.farsi: گفت عزم تو كجا اى بايزيد / رخت غربت را كجا خواهى كشيد
entries.english: He said, “Where are you going, O Bayazid? To what place would you take the baggage of travel in a strange land?”

entries.farsi: گفت قصد كعبه دارم از پگه / ‏گفت هين با خود چه دارى زاد ره
entries.english: Bayazid answered, “ I start for the Ka`ba at daybreak.” “Eh, cried the other, “what have you as provisions for the road?”

entries.farsi: گفت دارم از درم نقره دويست / ‏نك ببسته سخت در گوشه‏ى ردى است
entries.english: “I have two hundred silver dirhems,” said he; “look, tied fast in the corner of my cloak.”

entries.farsi: و آن درمها پيش من نه‏اى جواد / دان كه حج كردى و حاصل شد مراد
entries.english: He said, “ Make a circuit round me seven times, and reckon this better than the circumambulation in the pilgrimage;

entries.farsi: گفت طوفى كن به گردم هفت بار / وين نكوتر از طواف حج شمار
entries.english: And lay those dirhems before me, O generous one. Know that you have made the greater pilgrimage and that your desire has been achieved;

entries.farsi: عمره كردى عمر باقى يافتى / ‏صاف گشتى بر صفا بشتافتى
entries.english: You have performed the lesser pilgrimage and gained the life everlasting; you have become pure and sped up purity.

entries.farsi: حق آن حقى كه جانت ديده است / ‏كه مرا بر بيت خود بگزيده است
entries.english: By the truth of the Truth whom your soul has seen, that He has chosen me above His House.

entries.farsi: تا بكرد آن كعبه را در وى نرفت / ‏و اندر اين خانه بجز آن حى نرفت
entries.english: Although the Ka`ba is the House of His religious service, my form too, in which I was created, is the House of His inmost consciousness.

entries.farsi: كعبه هر چندى كه خانه‏ى بر اوست / ‏خلقت من نيز خانه‏ى سر اوست
entries.english: Never since God made the Ka`ba has He gone into it, and none but the Living has ever gone into this House.

entries.farsi: چون مرا ديدى خدا را ديده‏اى / ‏گرد كعبه‏ى صدق بر گرديده‏اى
entries.english: When you have seen me, you have seen God: you have circled round the Ka`ba of Sincerity.

entries.farsi: خدمت من طاعت و حمد خداست / ‏تا نپندارى كه حق از من جداست
entries.english: To serve me is to obey and glorify God: beware you think not that God is separate from me.

entries.farsi: چشم نيكو باز كن در من نگر / تا ببينى نور حق اندر بشر
entries.english: Open your eyes well and look on me, that you may behold the Light of God in man.”

entries.farsi: بايزيد آن نكته‏ها را هوش داشت / ‏همچو زرين حلقه‏اش در گوش داشت
entries.english: Bayazid gave heed to those mystic sayings, and put them in his ear as a golden ring.

entries.farsi: آمد از وى بايزيد اندر مزيد / منتهى در منتها آخر رسيد
entries.english: Through him, Bayazid came into an increase: the adept at last attained unto the end.

sections.title_fa: دانستن پيغامبر صلى اللهَّ عليه و آله كه سبب رنجورى آن شخص گستاخى بوده است در دعا
sections.title_en: How the Prophet—God bless and save him! —perceived that the cause of that person’s sickness was irreverence in prayer.

entries.farsi: چون پيمبر ديد آن بيمار را / خوش نوازش كرد يار غار را
entries.english: When the Prophet saw the sick man, he dealt sweetly and tenderly with that familiar friend.

entries.farsi: زنده شد او چون پيمبر را بديد / گوييا آن دم مر او را آفريد
entries.english: He became alive when he saw the Prophet: you might say that moment created him.

entries.farsi: گفت بيمارى مرا اين بخت داد / كامد اين سلطان بر من بامداد
entries.english: He said, “Sickness has given me this good fortune, that this Sultan has come to my side at morn,

entries.farsi: تا مرا صحت رسيد و عاقبت / ‏از قدوم اين شه بى‏حاشيت
entries.english: So that health and well-being have accrued to me from the arrival of this King who is without retinue.

entries.farsi: اى خجسته رنج و بيمارى و تب / ‏اى مبارك درد و بيدارى شب
entries.english: O happy pain and sickness and fever! O blessed anguish and wakefulness in the night!

entries.farsi: نك مرا در پيرى از لطف و كرم / ‏حق چنين رنجوريى داد و سقم
entries.english: Lo, in old age God of His grace and bounty has bestowed on me such a sickness and malady!

entries.farsi: درد پشتم داد هم تا من ز خواب / ‏بر جهم هر نيم شب ال بد شتاب
entries.english: He too has given me pain in the back, so that every midnight I cannot help springing up quickly from sleep.

entries.farsi: تا نخسبم جمله شب چون گاوميش / ‏دردها بخشيد حق از لطف خويش
entries.english: In order that I may not slumber all night like a buffalo, God of His grace bath given me pains.

entries.farsi: زين شكست آن رحم شاهان جوش كرد / دوزخ از تهديد من خاموش كرد
entries.english: Through this infirmity the mercy of Kings has been aroused, and Hell’s threatening of me has been silenced.”

entries.farsi: رنج گنج آمد كه رحمتها در اوست / ‏مغز تازه شد چو بخراشيد پوست
entries.english: Pain is a treasure, for there are mercies in it: the kernel becomes fresh when you scrape off the rind.

entries.farsi: اى برادر موضع تاريك و سرد / صبر كردن بر غم و سستى و درد
entries.english: O brother, a dark and cold place, to endure patiently sorrow and weakness and pain,

entries.farsi: چشمه‏ى حيوان و جام مستى است / ‏كان بلنديها همه در پستى است
entries.english: Is the Fountain of Life and the cup of intoxication, for those heights are all in lowliness.

entries.farsi: آن بهاران مضمر است اندر خزان / ‏در بهار است آن خزان مگريز از آن
entries.english: That spring is implied in autumn, and that autumn is in the spring: do not flee from it

entries.farsi: همره غم باش و با وحشت بساز / مى‏طلب در مرگ خود عمر دراز
entries.english: Be a fellow-traveller with grief, agree with desolation, and seek long life in your death.

entries.farsi: آن چه گويد نفس تو كاينجا بد است / ‏مشنوش چون كار او ضد آمده ست
entries.english: Do not listen to what your nafs says that this place is bad, inasmuch as her doings are contrary.

entries.farsi: تو خالفش كن كه از پيغمبران / ‏اين چنين آمد وصيت در جهان
entries.english: Oppose her, for such injunction has come from the prophets in the world.

entries.farsi: مشورت در كارها واجب شود / تا پشيمانى در آخر كم بود
entries.english: It becomes necessary to take counsel concerning things to be done, so that there may not be repentance in the end.

entries.farsi: گفت امت مشورت با كى كنيم / ‏انبيا گفتند با عقل اميم
entries.english: The community said, “With whom shall we take counsel?” The prophets answered, “ With intellect, the Imam.”

entries.farsi: گفت گر كودك در آيد يا زنى / ‏كاو ندارد عقل و راى روشنى
entries.english: He said, “If a child should come in, or a woman who has no judgment or clear understanding.”

entries.farsi: گفت با او مشورت كن و انچه گفت / ‏تو خالف آن كن و در راه افت
entries.english: “Take counsel with her,” said he, “and do the contrary of what she bids, and go your way.”

entries.farsi: نفس خود را زن شناس از زن بتر / ز انكه زن جزوى است نفست كل شر
entries.english: Know that your nafs is woman and worse than woman, because woman is a part, but your nafs is evil entire.

entries.farsi: مشورت با نفس خود گر مى‏كنى / ‏هر چه گويد كن خالف آن دنى
entries.english: If you take counsel with your nafs, oppose that vile one whatsoever she may say.

entries.farsi: گر نماز و روزه مى‏فرمايدت / ‏نفس مكار است مكرى زايدت
entries.english: If she bid you pray and fast—the nafs is a great plotter, she will bring some plot against you to birth.

entries.farsi: مشورت با نفس خويش اندر فعال / ‏هر چه گويد عكس آن باشد كمال
entries.english: Counsel with your nafs concerning actions— whatsoever she tells, the reverse of that is perfectly right.

entries.farsi: بر نيايى با وى و استيز او / رو بر يارى بگير آميز او
entries.english: You cannot cope with her and her rebellious nature, go to a friend and mix with him.

entries.farsi: عقل قوت گيرد از عقل دگر / نى شكر كامل شود از نيشكر
entries.english: Mind gains strength from another mind: the sugar-cane is made perfect by the sugar-cane.

entries.farsi: من ز مكر نفس ديدم چيزها / كاو برد از سحر خود تمييزها
entries.english: I have seen things from the deceitfulness of the nafs, for by her magic she takes away the faculties of discernment.

entries.farsi: وعده‏ها بدهد ترا تازه به دست / ‏كه هزاران بار آنها را شكست
entries.english: She will proffer afresh to you promises that she has broken thousands of times.

entries.farsi: عمر اگر صد سال خود مهلت دهد / اوت هر روزى بهانه‏ى نو نهد
entries.english: If your life be prolonged’ even for a hundred years, she will lay before you a new pretext every day.

entries.farsi: گرم گويد وعده‏هاى سرد را / جادويى مردى ببندد مرد را
entries.english: She utters cold promises warm; a witch, she ties up the manhood of a man.

entries.farsi: اى ضياء الحق حسام الدين بيا / كه نرويد بى‏تو از شوره گيا
entries.english: O radiance of God, Husamu’ddin, come! For without you no herbs will grow from the brackish soil.

entries.farsi: از فلك آويخته شد پرده‏اى / ‏از پى نفرين دل آزرده‏اى
entries.english: A curtain has been let down from heaven because of the curse of one whose heart is sorely vexed.

entries.farsi: اين قضا را هم قضا داند عالج / ‏عقل خلقان در قضا گيج است گيج
entries.english: This destiny only the destiny can cure: the understanding of His creatures is stunned at His destiny, stunned.

entries.farsi: اژدها گشته ست آن مار سياه / ‏آن كه كرمى بود افتاده به راه
entries.english: The black serpent that was a worm fallen on the road has become a dragon;

entries.farsi: اژدها و مار اندر دست تو / شد عصا اى جان موسى مست تو
entries.english: In your hand, O you with whom the soul of Moses is intoxicated, the dragon or serpent became the rod.

entries.farsi: حكم خذها ال تخف دادت خدا / تا به دستت اژدها گردد عصا
entries.english: God gave you command, saying, “Take it; fear not, so that the dragon may become a rod in your hand.”

entries.farsi: هين يد بيضا نما اى پادشاه / ‏صبح نو بگشا ز شبهاى سياه
entries.english: Hark, show the white hand, O King: out of the black nights reveal a new dawn!

entries.farsi: دوزخى افروخت در وى دم فسون / ‏اى دم تو از دم دريا فزون
entries.english: A hell has blazed forth; breathe enchantment over it, O thou whose breath is more than the breath of the sea.

entries.farsi: بحر مكار است بنموده كفى / ‏دوزخ است از مكر بنموده تفى
entries.english: She is the guileful sea that shows foam; she is the hell that from guile shows a heat.

entries.farsi: ز آن نمايد مختصر در چشم تو / تا زبون بينيش جنبد خشم تو
entries.english: She appears weak in your eyes, to the end that you may regard her as feeble and that your wrath may be stirred;

entries.farsi: همچنان كه لشكر انبوه بود / مر پيمبر را به چشم اندك نمود
entries.english: Even as there was a dense host, to the Prophet’s eye it seemed small,

entries.farsi: تا بر ايشان زد پيمبر بى‏خطر / ور فزون ديدى از آن كردى حذر
entries.english: So that the Prophet set upon them without the danger; but if he had deemed more, he would have acted cautiously in that.

entries.farsi: آن عنايت بود و اهل آن بدى / ‏احمدا ور نه تو بد دل مى‏شدى
entries.english: That was the Divine favour, and you wert worthy of it, O Ahmad; else, you wouldst have become fainthearted.

entries.farsi: كم نمود او را و اصحاب و را / آن جهاد ظاهر وباطن خدا
entries.english: God caused the outward and inward warfare to seem little to him and his Companions,

entries.farsi: تا ميسر كرد يسرى را بر او / تا ز عسرى او بگردانيد رو
entries.english: In order that He might make it easy for him to gain ease, and that he might not turn away his face from difficulty.

entries.farsi: كم نمودن مر و را پيروز بود / كه حقش يار و طريق آموز بود
entries.english: For him, causing to seem little was victory, since God was his friend and taught him the way.

entries.farsi: آن كه حق پشتش نباشد از ظفر / واى اگر گربش نمايد شير نر
entries.english: He that has not God for his victorious support, alas if the fierce lion seem to him a cat

entries.farsi: واى اگر صدرا يكى بيند ز دور / تا به چالش اندر آيد از غرور
entries.english: Alas if from afar he sees a hundred as one, so that in vain confidence he enters on the fray.

entries.farsi: ز آن نمايد ذو الفقارى حربه‏اى / ‏ز آن نمايد شير نر چون گربه‏اى
entries.english: He causes a Prophet’s sword to seem a dart and makes the fierce lion appear as a cat,

entries.farsi: تا دلير اندر فتد احمق به جنگ / ‏و اندر آردشان بدين حيلت به چنگ
entries.english: To the end that the fool may fall boldly to fighting, and that He may catch them by this device;

entries.farsi: تا به پاى خويش باشند آمده / ‏آن فليوان جانب آتش‏كده
entries.english: In order that those dolts may have come towards the fire-temple by means of their own feet

entries.farsi: كاه برگى مى‏نمايد تا تو زود / پف كنى كاو را برانى از وجود
entries.english: He is showing a blade of straw, in order that you may quickly give a puff so as to make it vanish out of existence.

entries.farsi: هين كه آن كه كوهها بر كنده است / ‏زو جهان گريان و او در خنده است
entries.english: Beware! For that straw has uprooted mountains: through it the world is weeping, while it is in laughter.

entries.farsi: مى‏نمايد تا به كعب اين آب جو / صد چو عاج ابن عنق شد غرق او
entries.english: He makes this river-water seem up to the ankle, a hundred such as Aj son of `Unuq have been drowned there.

entries.farsi: مى‏نمايد موج خونش تل مشك / ‏مى‏نمايد قعر دريا خاك خشك
entries.english: He makes the wave of blood seem to him a mound of musk: He makes the bottom of the sea seem dry land.

entries.farsi: خشك ديد آن بحر را فرعون كور / تا در او راند از سر مردى و زور
entries.english: Blind Pharaoh deemed that sea dry, so that in manhood and strength he drove into it.

entries.farsi: چون در آيد در تگ دريا بود / ديده‏ى فرعون كى بينا بود
entries.english: When he enters, he is at the bottom of the sea: how should the eye of Pharaoh be seeing?

entries.farsi: ديده بينا از لقاى حق شود / حق كجا هم راز هر احمق شود
entries.english: The eye is made seeing by meeting with God: how should God become the confidant of every fool?

entries.farsi: قند بيند خود شود زهر قتول / ‏راه بيند خود بود آن بانگ غول
entries.english: He sees candy : it is, in sooth, deadly poison; he sees the road: it is, in sooth, the cry of the ghoul.

entries.farsi: اى فلك در فتنه‏ى آخر زمان / ‏تيز مى‏گردى بده آخر زمان
entries.english: O skies, in the tribulation of latter days you are revolving swiftly; pray, give time.

entries.farsi: خنجر تيزى تو اندر قصد ما / نيش زهر آلوده‏اى در فصد ما
entries.english: You are a keen dagger to assail us; you are a poisoned lancet to bleed us.

entries.farsi: اى فلك از رحم حق آموز رحم / ‏بر دل موران مزن چون مار زخم
entries.english: O sky, learn mercy from the Mercy of God: do not, like a snake, inflict wounds on the hearts of ants.

entries.farsi: حق آن كه چرخه‏ى چرخ ترا / كرد گردان بر فراز اين سرا
entries.english: By the truth of Him who has set the wheel of your sphere turning above this abode,

entries.farsi: كه دگرگون گردى و رحمت كنى / ‏پيش از آن كه بيخ ما را بر كنى
entries.english: Revolve in another wise and show mercy, before you uproot us.

entries.farsi: حق آن كه دايگى كردى نخست / ‏تا نهال ما ز آب و خاك رست
entries.english: The truth of your having fostered us at first, so that our shoot grew up from water and earth;

entries.farsi: حق آن شه كه ترا صاف آفري / كرد چندان مشعله در تو پديد
entries.english: By the truth of that King who created you pure and displayed so many lamps in you,

entries.farsi: آن چنان معمور و باقى داشتت / ‏تا كه دهرى از ازل پنداشتت
entries.english: Who has kept you so flourishing and lasting that the materialist has thought you from eternity.

entries.farsi: شكر دانستيم آغاز ترا / انبيا گفتند آن راز ترا
entries.english: Thanks, we have come to know your beginning: the prophets have told that secret of yours.

entries.farsi: آدمى داند كه خانه حادث است / ‏عنكبوتى نه كه در وى عابث است
entries.english: A man knows that a house is made; the spider which plays idly in it not.

entries.farsi: پشه كى داند كه اين باغ از كى است / ‏كاو بهاران زاد و مرگش در دى است
entries.english: How should the gnat know of what date this garden is? —for it was born in spring, and its death is in the winter

entries.farsi: كرم كاندر چوب زايد سست حال / ‏كى بداند چوب را وقت نهال
entries.english: The worm that is born miserably in wood— how should it know the wood at the time when it was a shoot?

entries.farsi: ور بداند كرم از ماهيتش / ‏عقل باشد كرم باشد صورتش
entries.english: And if the worm should know, it would be intellect in its essential substance; the worm would be its form.

entries.farsi: عقل خود را مى‏نمايد رنگها / چون پرى دور است از آن فرسنگ‏ها
entries.english: Intellect shows itself guises, like the Jinn is leagues removed from them.

entries.farsi: از ملك بالاست چه جاى پرى / ‏تو مگس پرى به پستى مى‏پرى
entries.english: It is above the angels—what occasion is there for the Jinn? You have the wings of a gnat, you are flying downwards.

entries.farsi: گر چه عقلت سوى بالا مى‏پرد / گر چه عقلت سوى بالا مى‏پرد
entries.english: Although your intellect is flying upward, the bird of your conventional notions is feeding below.

entries.farsi: علم تقليدى وبال جان ماست / ‏عاريه ست و ما نشسته كان ماست
entries.english: Conventional knowledge is the bane of our souls; it is a borrowed thing, but we rest that it is ours.

entries.farsi: زين خرد جاهل همى بايد شدن / ‏دست در ديوانگى بايد زدن
entries.english: It behooves us to become ignorant of this wisdom; must we clutch at madness.

entries.farsi: هر چه بينى سود خود ز آن مى‏گريز / زهر نوش و آب حيوان را بريز
entries.english: Always flee from whatever you deem profitable to your nafs: drink poison and spill the water of life.

entries.farsi: هر كه بستاید ترا دشنام ده / ‏سود و سرمايه به مفلس وام ده
entries.english: Revile any one that praises you: lend interest and capital to the destitute.

entries.farsi: ايمنى بگذار و جاى خوف باش / ‏بگذر از ناموس و رسوا باش و فاش
entries.english: Let safety go, and dwell in the place of fear: leave reputation behind and be disgraced and notorious.

entries.farsi: آزمودم عقل دور انديش را / بعد از اين ديوانه سازم خويش را
entries.english: I have tried far-thinking intellect; henceforth I will make myself mad.

sections.title_fa: عذر گفتن دلقك با سيد كه چرا فاحشه را نكاح كرد
sections.title_en: How Dalqak excused himself to the Sayyid-i Ajall why he had married a harlot

entries.farsi: گفت با دلقك شبى سيد اجل / ‏قحبه‏اى را خواستى تو از عجل
entries.english: One night the Sayyid-i Ajall said to Dalqak, “You have married a harlot in haste.

entries.farsi: با من اين را باز مى‏بايست گفت / ‏تا يكى مستور كرديميت جفت
entries.english: You ought to have disclosed this to me, so that we might have made a chaste your wife.”

entries.farsi: گفت نه مستور صالح خواستم / ‏قحبه گشتند و ز غم تن كاستم
entries.english: Dalqak replied, “I have married nine chaste and virtuous women: they became harlots, and I wasted away with grief.

entries.farsi: خواستم اين قحبه را بى‏معرفت / ‏تا ببينم چون شود اين عاقبت
entries.english: I married this harlot without acquaintance, in order to see how this one would turn out in the end.

entries.farsi: عقل را من آزمودم هم بسى / ‏زين سپس جويم جنون را مغرسى
entries.english: Often have I tried intelligence; henceforth I will seek a nursery for insanity.”

sections.title_fa: به حيلت در سخن آوردن سائل آن بزرگ را كه خود را ديوانه ساخته بود
sections.title_en: How an inquirer managed to draw into conversation an eminent man

entries.farsi: آن يكى مى‏گفت خواهم عاقلى / ‏مشورت آرم بدو در مشكلى
entries.english: who had feigned to be mad.

entries.farsi: آن يكى گفتش كه اندر شهر ما / نيست عاقل جز كه آن مجنون‏نما
entries.english: A certain man was saying, “I want some one of intelligence, I may consult him about a difficulty.”

entries.farsi: بر نيى گشته سواره نك فالن / ‏مى‏دواند در ميان كودكان
entries.english: One said to him, “In our city there is nobody of intelligence except yonder man who appears to be mad.

entries.farsi: صاحب راى است و آتش پاره‏اى / ‏آسمان قدر است و اختر باره‏اى
entries.english: Look, there is so-and-so: mounted on a cane, he rides amongst the children.

entries.farsi: فر او كروبيان را جان شده ست / ‏او در اين ديوانگى پنهان شده ست
entries.english: He is possessed of judgment and a spark of fire; he is as the sky in dignity, and as the stars in high estate.

entries.farsi: ليك هر ديوانه را جان نشمرى / ‏سر منه گوساله را چون سامرى
entries.english: His glory has become the soul of the Cherubim; he has become concealed in this madness.”

entries.farsi: چون وليى آشكارا با تو گفت / ‏صد هزاران غيب و اسرار نهفت
entries.english: But you must not account every madman a soul: do not, like Sirniri, lay down your head to a calf.

entries.farsi: مر ترا آن فهم و آن دانش نبود / واندانستى تو سرگين را ز عود
entries.english: When a manifest saint has declared unto you hundreds of thousands of unseen things and hidden mysteries,
And you have not had the understanding and knowledge; you have not distinguished dung from aloes-wood

entries.farsi: از جنون خود را ولى چون پرده ساخت / مر و را اى كور كى خواهى شناخت
entries.english: How, when the saint has made for himself a veil of madness, will you recognise him, O blind one?

entries.farsi: گر ترا باز است آن ديده‏ى يقين / ‏زير هر سنگى يكى سرهنگ بين
entries.english: If your eye of intuitive certainty is open, behold a captain under every stone.

entries.farsi: ‏پيش آن چشمى كه باز و رهبر است / ‏هر گليمى را كليمى در بر است
entries.english: To the eye that is open and a guide, every dervish-cloak has a Moses in its embrace.

entries.farsi: مر ولى را هم ولى شهره كند / هر كه را او خواست با بهره كند
entries.english: It is only the saint that makes the saint known and makes fortunate whomever he will.

entries.farsi: كس نداند از خرد او را شناخت / ‏چون كه او مر خويش را ديوانه ساخت
entries.english: No one can recognise him by means of wisdom when he has feigned to be mad.

entries.farsi: چون بدزدد دزد بينايى ز كور / هيچ يابد دزد را او در عبور
entries.english: When a seeing thief steals from a blind man, can he at all detect the thief in the act of passing?

entries.farsi: كور نشناسد كه دزد او كه بود / گر چه خود بر وى زند دزد عنود
entries.english: The blind man does not know who it was that robbed him, even though the wicked thief may knock against him.

entries.farsi: چون گزد سگ كور صاحب ژنده را / كى شناسد آن سگ درنده را
entries.english: When a dog bites a blind ragged mendicant, how should he recognise that ferocious dog?

sections.title_fa: حمله بردن سگ بر كور گدا
sections.title_en: How the dog attacked the mendicant who was blind.

entries.farsi: يك سگى در كوى بر كور گدا / حمله مى‏آورد چون شير وغا
entries.english: A dog was attacking, as a warlike lion, a blind mendicant in a certain street.

entries.farsi: سگ كند آهنگ درويشان به خشم / ‏در كشد مه خاك درويشان به چشم
entries.english: The dog rushes angrily at dervishes; the moon smears her eyes with dust of dervishes.

entries.farsi: كاى امير صيد و اى شير شكار / دست دست تست دست از من بدار
entries.english: The blind man was made helpless by the dog’s barking and by fear of the dog; the blind man began to pay honour to the dog,

entries.farsi: كور عاجز شد ز بانگ و بيم سگ / ‏اندر آمد كور در تعظيم سگ
entries.english: Saying, “O prince of the chase, and O lion of the hunt, yours is the hand: refrain your hand from me!”—

entries.farsi: كز ضرورت دم خر را آن حكيم / ‏كرد تعظيم و لقب دادش كريم
entries.english: For, by necessity, that philosopher paid honour to the tail of an ass, and gave him the title of “noble.”

entries.farsi: گفت او هم از ضرورت كاى اسد / از چو من الغر شكارت چه رسد
entries.english: He too, of necessity, said, “ O lion, what will come to you from such a meager prey as I am?

entries.farsi: گور مى‏گيرند يارانت به دشت / ‏كور مى‏گيرى تو در كوچه به گشت
entries.english: Your friends are catching wild asses in the desert; you are catching a blind man in the street: this is bad.

entries.farsi: گور مى‏جويند يارانت به صيد / كور مى‏جويى تو در كوچه به كيد
entries.english: Your friends seek wild donkeys by hunting; you in malice seeks a blind man in the street.”

entries.farsi: آن سگ عالم شكار گور كرد / وين سگ بى‏مايه قصد كور كرد
entries.english: The knowing dog has made the wild donkey his prey, while this worthless dog has attacked a blind man.

entries.farsi: سگ چو عالم گشت شد چالاك زحف / ‏سگ چو عارف گشت شد ز اصحاب كهف
entries.english: When the dog has learned the knowledge, he has escaped from error: he hunts lawful prey in the jungles.

entries.farsi: علم چون آموخت سگ رست از ضالل / ‏مى‏كند در بيشه‏ها صيد حالل
entries.english: When the dog has become knowing, he marches briskly; when the dog has become a knower of God he becomes the Men of the Cave.

entries.farsi: سگ شناسا شد كه مير صيد كيست / ‏اى خدا آن نور اشناسنده چيست
entries.english: The dog has come to know who the Master of the hunt is. O God, what is that knowing light?

entries.farsi: كور نشناسد نه از بى‏چشمى است / ‏بلكه اين ز آن است كز جهل است مست
entries.english: The blind man knows it not, it is not from having no eye; nay, it is because he is drunken with ignorance.

entries.farsi: نيست خود بى‏چشم تر كور از زمين / ‏اين زمين از فضل حق شد خصم بين
entries.english: Truly, the blind man is not more eyeless than the earth; and this earth, by the grace of God, has become a seer of enemies.

entries.farsi: نور موسى ديد و موسى را نواخت / ‏خسف قارون كرد و قارون را شناخت
entries.english: It saw the Light of Moses and showed kindness to Moses; Qarun it engulfed, it knew Qarun.

entries.farsi: رجف كرد اندر هالك هر دعى / ‏فهم كرد از حق كه يا أَرْضُ ابلعي
entries.english: It quaked for the destruction of every false pretender: it understood from God, “O earth, swallow!”

entries.farsi: خاك و آب و باد و نار با شرر / بى‏خبر با ما و با حق با خبر
entries.english: Earth and water and air and sparking fire are unacquainted with us, but acquainted with God.

entries.farsi: ما بعكس آن ز غير حق خبير / بى‏خبر از حق و از چندين نذير
entries.english: Contrariwise, we are aware of other than God, unaware of God and of so many prophets.

entries.farsi: الجرم أَشْ فَقْنَ مِنْها جمله‏شان / ‏كند شد ز آميز حيوان حمله‏شان
entries.english: As a necessary consequence, they all shrank from it: their impulse to partake of life was blunted.

entries.farsi: گفته بيزاريم جمله زين حيات / ‏كاو بود با خلق حى با حق موات
entries.english: They said, “We all are averse to this life, that one should be living in relation to created beings and dead in relation to God.”

entries.farsi: چون بماند از خلق گردد او يتيم / ‏انس حق را قلب مى‏بايد سليم
entries.english: When he remains away from created beings, he is orphaned: for intimacy with God, the heart must be free.

entries.farsi: چون ز كورى دزد دزدد كاله‏اى / ‏مى‏كند آن كور عميا ناله‏اى
entries.english: When a thief steals some article of property from a blind man, the blind man is blindly lamenting.

entries.farsi: تا نگويد دزد او را كان منم / ‏كز تو دزديدم كه دزد پر فنم
entries.english: Until the thief say to him, “ ‘It is I that stole from you, for I am an artful thief,”

entries.farsi: كى شناسد كور دزد خويش را / چون ندارد نور چشم و آن ضيا
entries.english: How should the blind man know his thief, since he has not the eye’s light and that radiance?

entries.farsi: چون بگويد هم بگير او را تو سخت / ‏تا بگويد او عالمتهاى رخت
entries.english: When he speaks, at once take tight hold of him, that he may tell the marks of the goods.

entries.farsi: پس جهاد اكبر آمد عصر دزد / تا بگويد كه چه دزديده است مزد
entries.english: The Greater Jihad, then, consists in squeezing the thief to the dregs, in order that he may tell what he has stolen and what he has carried off.

entries.farsi: اوال دزديد كحل ديده‏ات / ‏چون ستانى باز يابى تبصرت
entries.english: First, he has stolen your eye-salve; when you take it, you will regain insight.

entries.farsi: كاله‏ى حكمت كه گم كرده‏ى دل است / ‏پيش اهل دل يقين آن حاصل است
entries.english: The goods of wisdom, which have been lost by heart, are certainly to be found with the man of heart.

entries.farsi: كوردل با جان و با سمع و بصر / مى‏نداند دزد شيطان را ز اثر
entries.english: The blind of heart, notwithstanding life and hearing and sight, never knows the devilish thief by the evidence.

entries.farsi: ز اهل دل جو از جماد آن را مجو / كه جماد آمد خاليق پيش او
entries.english: Seek from the man of heart; do not seek it from the inanimate, for people are inanimate beside him.

entries.farsi: مشورت جوينده آمد نزد او / كاى اب كودك شده رازى بگو
entries.english: The seeker of counsel approached him, saying, “O father who have become a child, tell a secret.”

entries.farsi: گفت رو زين حلقه كاين در باز نيست / ‏باز گرد امروز روز راز نيست
entries.english: He answered, “Be gone from this door-ring, for this door is not open. Turn back: to-day is not the day for secrets.

entries.farsi: گر مكان را ره بدى در المكان / ‏همچو شيخان بودمى من بر دكان
entries.english: If the spatial had access to the non-spatial, I should be on the bench, like the Shaykhs.”

sections.title_fa: خواندن محتسب مست خراب افتاده را به زندان
sections.title_en: How the Police Inspector summoned the man who had fallen dead-drunk to prison.

entries.farsi: محتسب در نيم شب جايى رسيد / در بن ديوار مستى خفته ديد
entries.english: The Inspector came at midnight to a certain place: he saw a man lying at the bottom of a wall.

entries.farsi: گفت هى مستى چه خورده ستى بگو / گفت از اين خوردم كه هست اندر سبو
entries.english: He cried, “Hey, you are drunk: tell, what have you been drinking?” Said the man, “I have drunk of this which is in the jar”

entries.farsi: گفت آخر در سبو واگو كه چيست / ‏گفت از آن كه خورده‏ام گفت اين خفى است
entries.english: “Pray,” said he, “explain what is in the jar.” He replied, “Some of what I have drunk.” Said the Inspector, “this is hidden.”

entries.farsi: گفت آن چه خورده‏اى آن چيست آن / ‏گفت آن كه در سبو مخفى است آن
entries.english: He asked, “What is it that you have drunk?” He rejoined, “That which is hidden in the jar.”

entries.farsi: دور مى‏شد اين سؤال و اين جواب / ‏ماند چون خر محتسب اندر خالب
entries.english: These questions and answers were becoming a circle. The Inspector was left in the mud, like an ass.

entries.farsi: گفت او را محتسب هين آه كن / ‏مست هو هو كرد هنگام سخن
entries.english: The Inspector said to him, “Come now, say ‘Ah‘”; the drunken man, at the moment of utterance, said “Hu, HU.”

entries.farsi: گفت گفتم آه كن هو مى‏كنى / ‏گفت من شاد و تو از غم دم زنى
entries.english: “I told you to say `Ah’,” said he; “you are saying ‘Hu‘.” I am glad,” he replied, “while you are bent with grief.

entries.farsi: آه از درد و غم و بى‏دادى است / ‏هوى هوى مى خوران از شادى است
entries.english: ‘Ah’ is on account of pain and grief and injustice; the ‘Hu’ of the wine-drinkers is from joy.”

entries.farsi: محتسب گفت اين ندانم خيز خيز / معرفت متراش و بگذار اين ستيز
entries.english: The Inspector said, “I know nothing about this. Get up, get up! Don’t retail mystic lore, and leave off this wrangling.”

entries.farsi: گفت رو تو از كجا من از كجا / گفت مستى خيز تا زندان بيا
entries.english: “Go away,” said the man;” what have you to do with me?” “You are drunk,” the Inspector said. “Get up and come to prison.”

entries.farsi: گفت مست اى محتسب بگذار و رو / از برهنه كى توان بردن گرو
entries.english: Said the drunken man, “O Inspector, let me alone and go away. How is it possible to carry off pledges from one that is naked?

entries.farsi: گر مرا خود قوت رفتن بدى / ‏خانه‏ى خود رفتمى وين كى شدى
entries.english: If indeed I had had the power to walk, I should have gone to my house—and how would this have occurred?

entries.farsi: من اگر با عقل و با امكانمى / ‏همچو شيخان بر سر دكانمى
entries.english: Were I possessed of understanding and of contingent existence, I should be on the bench, like the Shaykhs.”

sections.title_fa: دوم بار در سخن كشيدن سايل آن بزرگ را تا حال او معلوم تر گردد
sections.title_en: How the inquirer, for the second time, drew that eminent into conversation, in order that his condition might be made better known.

entries.farsi: گفت آن طالب كه آخر يك نفس / ‏اى سواره بر نى اين سو ران فرس
entries.english: That seeker said, “O you mounted on the cane, pray, ride your horse this way for one moment.”

entries.farsi: راند سوى او كه هين زوتر بگو / كاسب من بس توسن است و تند خو
entries.english: He rode towards him, crying, “Listen, say as quickly as you can, for my horse is very restive and fierce-tempered.

entries.farsi: تا لگد بر تو نكوبد زود باش / ‏از چه مى‏پرسى بيانش كن تو فاش
entries.english: Be quick, lest he kick you: explain clearly what you are asking about.”

entries.farsi: او مجال راز دل گفتن نديد / زو برون شو كرد و در الغش كشيد
entries.english: He saw no opportunity to tell his heart’s secret: he at once made an evasion and drew him into jesting talk.

entries.farsi: گفت سه گونه زن‏اند اندر جهان / ‏آن دو رنج و اين يكى گنج روان
entries.english: He said, “I wish to marry a woman in this street: who is suitable for one like me?”

entries.farsi: گفت مى‏خواهم در اين كوچه زنى / ‏كيست اليق از براى چون منى
entries.english: “There are three kinds of women in the world,” said he: “two of those are a sorrow, and one is the soul’s treasure.

entries.farsi: آن يكى را چون بخواهى كل تراست / ‏و آن دگر نيمى ترا نيمى جداست
entries.english: The first, when you marry her, is wholly yours; and the second is half yours and half separate;

entries.farsi: و آن سوم هيچ او ترا نبود بدان / ‏اين شنودى دور شو رفتم روان
entries.english: And the third, know she is not yours at all. You have heard this. Away!—I start in a moment.

entries.farsi: تا ترا اسبم نپراند لگد / كه بيفتى بر نخيزى تا ابد
entries.english: Lest my horse let fly a kick at you, so that you fall and never rise up”

entries.farsi: شيخ راند اندر ميان كودكان / ‏بانگ زد بار دگر او را جوان
entries.english: The Shaykh rode off amongst the children, the young man shouted to him once more,

entries.farsi: كه بيا آخر بگو تفسير اين / ‏اين زنان سه نوع گفتى بر گزين
entries.english: “Come, please declare the exposition of this. You have said that these women are of three kinds: pick out.”

entries.farsi: راند سوى او و گفتش بكر خاص / ‏كل ترا باشد ز غم يابى خالص
entries.english: He rode towards him and said to him, “The virgin of your choice will be wholly yours, and you will gain freedom from sorrow;

entries.farsi: و انكه نيمى آن تو بيوه بود / و انكه هيچست آن عيال با ولد
entries.english: And she that is half yours is the widow; and she that is nothing is the married woman with a child :

entries.farsi: چون ز شوى اولش كودك بود / مهر و كل خاطرش آن سو رود
entries.english: When she has a child by her first husband, her love and whole heart will go there.

entries.farsi: دور شو تا اسب نندازد لگد / سم اسب توسنم بر تو رسد
entries.english: Get away, lest my horse launch a kick, and the hoof of my restive horse land upon you.”

entries.farsi: هاى و هويى كرد شيخ و باز راند / كودكان را باز سوى خويش خواند
entries.english: The Shaykh gave a loud cry of jubilation and rode back: he again called the children to him.

entries.farsi: باز بانگش كرد آن سايل بيا / يك سؤالم ماند اى شاه كيا
entries.english: That inquirer shouted to him once more, “Come, I have one question left, O sovereign king.”

entries.farsi: باز راند اين سو بگو زودتر چه بود / كه ز ميدان آن بچه گويم ربود
entries.english: He rode back in this direction. “Say what it is,” he cried, “as quick as you can, for yonder child has enraptured my heart.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى شه با چنين عقل و ادب / ‏اين چه شيداست اين چه فعل است اى جب
entries.english: Said the other, “O king, with such intelligence and erudition, what dissimulation is this? What acting is this? Oh, it is a marvel!

entries.farsi: تو وراى عقل كلى در بيان / ‏آفتابى در جنون چونى نهان
entries.english: You transcend the Universal Intellect in elucidation. You are a sun: how are you hid in madness?”

entries.farsi: گفت اين اوباش رايى مى‏زنند / تا در اين شهر خودم قاضى كنند
entries.english: He replied, “These rascals are proposing to make me Cadi in this their city.

entries.farsi: دفع مى‏گفتم مرا گفتند نى / ‏نيست چون تو عالمى صاحب فنى
entries.english: I raised objections, they said to me, ‘No, there is none so learned and accomplished as you.

entries.farsi: با وجود تو حرام است و خبيث / ‏كه كم از تو در قضا گويد حديث
entries.english: While you are in existence, it is unlawful and wicked that any one inferior to you should cite Prophetic Traditions in the office of Cadi.

entries.farsi: در شريعت نيست دستورى كه ما / كمتر از تو شه كنيم و پيشوا
entries.english: Permission is not in the Law, that we should appoint one less than you as prince and leader.

entries.farsi: زين ضرورت گيج و ديوانه شدم / ‏ليك در باطن همانم كه بدم
entries.english: By this necessity I was made distraught and mad, but inwardly I am just the same as I was.

entries.farsi: عقل من گنج است و من ويرانه‏ام / ‏گنج اگر پيدا كنم ديوانه‏ام
entries.english: My intelligence is the treasure, and I am the ruin; if I display the treasure, I am mad.

entries.farsi: اوست ديوانه كه ديوانه نشد / اين عسس را ديد و در خانه نشد
entries.english: The madman is he that has not gone mad, he that has seen this night-patrol and has not gone home.

entries.farsi: دانش من جوهر آمد نه عرض / ‏اين بهايى نيست بهر هر غرض
entries.english: My knowledge is substantial, not accidental; and this precious is not for every interest.

entries.farsi: كان قندم نيستان شكرم / ‏هم ز من مى‏رويد و من مى‏خورم
entries.english: I am a mine of candy, I am a plantation of sugar-canes: it is growing from me, and at the same time I am eating.

entries.farsi: علم تقليدى و تعليمى است آن / ‏كز نفورش مستمع دارد فغان
entries.english: Knowledge is conventional and acquired, when he laments because the hearer is averse to it.

entries.farsi: چون پى دانه نه بهر روشنى است / ‏همچو طالب علم دنياى دنى است
entries.english: Since it is as bait, not for the sake of enlightenment, he is just as the seeker of vile worldly knowledge;

entries.farsi: طالب علم است بهر عام و خاص / ‏نى كه تا يابد از اين عالم خالص
entries.english: He is seeking knowledge on account of the vulgar and the noble, not in order that he may win release from this world.

entries.farsi: همچو موشى هر طرف سوراخ كرد / چون كه نورش راند از در گشت سرد
entries.english: Like a mouse, he has burrowed in every direction, since the light drove him from the door and said, ‘Away!’

entries.farsi: چون كه سوى دشت و نورش ره نبود / هم در آن ظلمات جهدى مى‏نمود
entries.english: Inasmuch as he had no way to the open country and the light, he continued to make an exertion even in that darkness.

entries.farsi: گر خدايش پر دهد پر خرد / برهد از موشى و چون مرغان پرد
entries.english: If God give him wings, the wings of Wisdom, he will escape from mousiness and will fly like the birds;

entries.farsi: ور نجويد پر بماند زير خاك / ‏نااميد از رفتن راه سماك
entries.english: But if he does not seek wings, he will remain underground with no hope of traversing the path to Simak.

entries.farsi: علم گفتارى كه آن بى‏جان بود / عاشق روى خريداران بود
entries.english: Dialectic knowledge, which is soulless, is in love with the countenance of customers;

entries.farsi: گر چه باشد وقت بحث علم زفت / ‏چون خريدارش نباشد مرد و رفت
entries.english: Although it is robust at the time of disputation, it is dead and gone when it has no customer.

entries.farsi: مشترى من خداى است او مرا / ‏مى‏كشد بالا كه اللهَّ اشترى
entries.english: My purchaser is God: He is drawing me aloft, for God has purchased.

entries.farsi: خونبهاى من جمال ذو الجلال / ‏خونبهاى خود خورم كسب حالل
entries.english: My blood payment is the beauty of the Glorious One: I enjoy my blood payment lawful earnings.

entries.farsi: اين خريداران مفلس را بهل / ‏چه خريدارى كند يك مشت گل
entries.english: Abandon these insolvent customers: what purchase can be made by a handful of clay?

entries.farsi: گِل مخور گِل را مخر گِل را مجو / ز انكه گل خوار است دايم زرد رو
entries.english: Do not eat clay, do not buy clay, and do not seek clay, because the eater of clay is always pale-faced.

entries.farsi: دل بخور تا دايما باشى جوان / ‏از تجلى چهره‏ات چون ارغوان
entries.english: Eat your heart, that you may be young always, your face with Divine illumination, like the arghawan.”

entries.farsi: يا رب اين بخشش نه حد كار ماست / ‏لطف تو لطف خفى را خود سزاست
entries.english: O Lord, this gift is not the compass of our work: verily, Your grace is according to Your mysterious grace.

entries.farsi: دست گير از دست ما ما را بخر / پرده را بردار و پرده‏ى ما مدر
entries.english: Take our hands; buy us from our hands; lift the veil, and do not tear our veil.

entries.farsi: باز خر ما را از اين نفس پليد / كاردش تا استخوان ما رسيد
entries.english: Redeem us from this filthy self: its knife has reached our bones.

entries.farsi: از چو ما بى‏چارگان اين بند سخت / ‏كى گشايد اى شه بى‏تاج و تخت
entries.english: Who will loose these strong chains from helpless ones like us, O king uncrowned and unthroned?

entries.farsi: اين چنين قفل گران را اى ودود / كى تواند جز كه فضل تو گشود
entries.english: Who except Your bounty, O Loving One, can loose such a heavy lock?

entries.farsi: اين دعا هم بخشش و تعليم تست / ‏گر نه در گلخن گلستان از چه رست
entries.english: Let us turn our heads from ourselves towards You, inasmuch as You are nearer unto us than we.

entries.farsi: ما ز خود سوى كه گردانيم سر / چون تويى از ما به ما نزديكتر
entries.english: Even this prayer is Your gift and lesson; else, when has a rose-bed grown in an ash-pit?

entries.farsi: در ميان خون و روده فهم و عقل / ‏جز ز اكرام تو نتوان كرد نقل
entries.english: Save through Your munificence, it is impossible to convey understanding and reason into the midst of blood and entrails.

entries.farsi: از دو پاره‏ى پيه اين نور روان / موج نورش مى‏زند بر آسمان
entries.english: This flowing light from two pieces of fat: their waves of light reach up to the sky.

entries.farsi: گوشت پاره كه زبان آمد از او / مى‏رود سيالب حكمت همچو جو
entries.english: The piece of flesh which is the tongue— from it the flood of Wisdom is flowing, like a stream,

entries.farsi: سوى سوراخى كه نامش گوشهاست / ‏تا بباغ جان كه ميوه‏اش هوشهاست
entries.english: Towards a cavity, whereof the name is “ears,” up to the orchard of the soul, whereof the fruit is intellections.

entries.farsi: شاه راه باغ جانها شرع اوست / ‏باغ و بستانهاى عالم فرع اوست
entries.english: Its main course is the highway of the orchard of souls; the orchards and gardens of the world are its branches.

entries.farsi: اصل و سرچشمه‏ى خوشى آن است آن / ‏زود تَجْ رِي تَحْ تَهَا الأَْنْهارُ خوان
entries.english: That, that, is the source and fountainhead of joy: quick, recite, beneath which flow the rivers.

sections.title_fa: تتمه‏ى نصيحت رسول صلى اللهَّ عليه و آله بيمار را
sections.title_en: Conclusion of the admonishment given by the Prophet, God bless and save him, to the sick man

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر مر آن بيمار را / چون عيادت كرد يار زار را
entries.english: The Prophet said to the sick man, when he visited suffering friend,

entries.farsi: كه مگر نوعى دعايى كرده‏اى / ‏از جهالت زهربايى خورده‏اى
entries.english: “Maybe you have made a prayer of some sort, and from ignorance have eaten some poisoned food.

entries.farsi: ياد آور چه دعا مى‏گفته‏اى / ‏چون ز مكر نفس مى‏آشفته‏اى
entries.english: Bring to mind what a prayer you said when you were being vexed by the guile of the nafs.”

entries.farsi: گفت يادم نيست الا همتى / ‏دار با من يادم آيد ساعتى
entries.english: He answered, “I do not remember; but direct an influence towards me, and it will come to my memory in a moment.”

entries.farsi: از حضور نور بخش مصطفا / پيش خاطر آمد او را آن دعا
entries.english: Through the light-giving presence of Mustafa, that prayer came into his mind;

entries.farsi: همت پيغمبر روشن‏كده / پيش خاطر آمدش آن گم شده
entries.english: The aspiration of the Prophet who dwells in light there came into his mind that which had been lost;

entries.farsi: تافت ز آن روزن كه از دل تا دل است / روشنى كه فرق حق و باطل است
entries.english: Through the window which is between heart and heart flashed the light that separates truth and falsehood.

entries.farsi: گفت اينك يادم آمد اى رسول / ‏آن دعا كه گفته‏ام من بو الفضول
entries.english: He said, “Look now, I have remembered, O Prophet, the prayer which I, impertinent fool, spoke.

entries.farsi: چون گرفتار گنه مى‏آمدم / ‏غرقه دست اندر حشايش مى‏زدم
entries.english: When I was being caught in sin and drowning was clutching at straws

entries.farsi: از تو تهديد و وعيدى مى‏رسيد / مجرمان را از عذاب بس شديد
entries.english: There was coming from you to sinners a threat and menace of punishment exceedingly severe

entries.farsi: مضطرب مى‏گشتم و چاره نبود / بند محكم بود و قفل ناگشود
entries.english: I was becoming agitated, and there was no help, there were chains made fast and a lock not opened:

entries.farsi: نى مقام صبر و نه راه گريز / نى اميد توبه نه جاى ستيز
entries.english: Neither room for patience nor means of flight, neither hope of repenting nor opportunity for rebelling

entries.farsi: من چو هاروت و چو ماروت از حزن / ‏آه مى‏كردم كه اى خالق من
entries.english: I, like Harut and Marut, was crying in sorrow, ‘Alas!’ and saying, ‘O my Creator!’”

entries.farsi: از خطر هاروت و ماروت آشكار / چاه بابل را بكردند اختيار
entries.english: Because of the dangers Harut and Marut openly chose the pit of Babylon,

entries.farsi: تا عذاب آخرت اينجا كشند / گربزند و عاقل و ساحروش‏اند
entries.english: So that they might suffer here the punishment of the world to come; and they are cunning and intelligent and magician-like.

entries.farsi: نيك كردند و بجاى خويش بود / سهلتر باشد ز آتش رنج دود
entries.english: They acted well, and it was fitly done: the pain of smoke is lighter than fire.

entries.farsi: حد ندارد وصف رنج آن جهان / ‏سهل باشد رنج دنيا پيش آن
entries.english: The pain of that world is beyond description; light is the pain of this world beside it.

entries.farsi: اى خنك آن كاو جهادى مى‏كند / بر بدن زجرى و دادى مى‏كند
entries.english: Oh, happy he that wages a holy war, and puts a restraint upon the body and deals justice,

entries.farsi: تا ز رنج آن جهانى وارهد / بر خود اين رنج عبادت مى‏نهد
entries.english: And, in order that he may be delivered from the pain of that world, lays upon himself this pain of serving God.

entries.farsi: من همى‏گفتم كه يا رب آن عذاب / ‏هم در اين عالم بران بر من شتاب
entries.english: “I was saying, O Lord, quickly inflict on me in this world that punishment,

entries.farsi: تا در آن عالم فراغت باشدم / ‏در چنين درخواست حلقه مى‏زدم
entries.english: So that I may have exemption in yonder world: About such-like a request was I knocking at the door.

entries.farsi: اين چنين رنجوريى پيدام شد / جان من از رنج بى‏آرام شد
entries.english: Such a sickness as this appeared in me: through pain my soul was deprived of rest.

entries.farsi: مانده‏ام از ذكر و از اوراد خود / بى‏خبر گشتم ز خويش و نيك و بد
entries.english: I have been left without power to perform my dhikr and litanies: I have become unconscious of myself and of good and ill.

entries.farsi: گر نمى‏ديدم كنون من روى تو / اى خجسته وى مبارك بوى تو
entries.english: If I had not now beheld your face, O you whose scent is fortunate and blessed,

entries.farsi: مى‏شدم از دست من يك بارگى / ‏كرديم شاهانه اين غم خوارگى
entries.english: I should have passed altogether from the bondage. You in kingly fashion have bestowed on me this sympathy.”

entries.farsi: گفت هى‏هى اين دعا ديگر مكن / ‏بر مكن تو خويش را از بيخ و بن
entries.english: Said the Prophet, “Hey! Do not offer this prayer again: do not dig yourself up from root and base.

entries.farsi: تو چه طاقت دارى اى مور نژند / كه نهد بر تو چنان كوه بلند
entries.english: What strength do you have, O wretched ant, to endure that He should lay on you such a lofty mountain?”

entries.farsi: گفت توبه كردم اى سلطان كه من / ‏از سر جلدى نه الفم هيچ فن
entries.english: He answered, “O Sultan, I repent that in no wise will I brag recklessly.

entries.farsi: اين جهان تيه است و تو موسى و ما / از گنه در تيه مانده مبتال
entries.english: This world is the Desert, and you are Moses and we because of sin remain in the Desert in tribulation.

entries.farsi: سالها ره مى‏رويم و در اخير / همچنان در منزل اول اسير
entries.english: We are wayfaring for years, and at the end we are still held captive in the first stage.

entries.farsi: ور به كل بيزار بودى او ز ما / كى رسيدى خوانمان هيچ از سما
entries.english: If the heart of Moses were pleased with us, there would be shown the way through the Desert and the border;

entries.farsi: گر دل موسى ز ما راضى بدى / ‏تيه را راه و كران پيدا شدى
entries.english: And if he were wholly disgusted with us, how by any means would trays of food come to us from heaven?

entries.farsi: كى ز سنگى چشمه‏ها جوشان شدى / ‏در بيابان‏مان امان جان شدى
entries.english: How would springs gush from a rock, would there be security for our lives in the desert?

entries.farsi: بل به جاى خوان خود آتش آمدى / ‏اندر اين منزل لهب بر ما زدى
entries.english: Nay, truly, fire would come instead of trays: flames would beat upon us in this abode.

entries.farsi: چون دو دل شد موسى اندر كار ما / گاه خصم ماست گاهى يار ما
entries.english: For as much as Moses has become of two minds concerning us, he is sometimes our enemy and sometimes our friend.

entries.farsi: خشمش آتش مى‏زند در رخت ما / حلم او رد مى‏كند تير بال
entries.english: His anger sets fire to our goods; his clemency becomes a shield against affliction.

entries.farsi: كى بود كه حلم گردد خشم نيز / نيست اين نادر ز لطفت اى عزيز
entries.english: When may it be that anger shall again become clemency? This is not extraordinary from your grace, O venerable one.

entries.farsi: مدح حاضر وحشت است از بهر اين / ‏نام موسى مى‏برم قاصد چنين
entries.english: To praise any one present is embarrassment; on this account I am purposely using the name of Moses, like this.

entries.farsi: ور نه موسى كى روا دارد كه من / ‏پيش تو ياد آورم از هيچ تن
entries.english: Otherwise, how should Moses deem it right that I make mention of anybody before you?

entries.farsi: عهد ما بشكست صد بار و هزار / عهد تو چون كوه ثابت برقرار
entries.english: Our covenant has been broken hundreds and thousands of times; Your covenant, like a mountain, stands firm and stable.

entries.farsi: عهد ما كاه و به هر بادى زبون / ‏عهد تو كوه و ز صد كه هم فزون
entries.english: Our covenant is straw and subject to every wind; Your covenant is a mountain, and even more than a hundred mountains.

entries.farsi: حق آن قوت كه بر تلوين ما / رحمتى كن اى امير لونها
entries.english: By the truth of that power, have some mercy upon our mutability, O Ruler of mutations!

entries.farsi: خويش را ديديم و رسوايى خويش / ‏امتحان ما مكن اى شاه بيش
entries.english: We have seen ourselves and our shame. Put us not to further trial, O King,

entries.farsi: بى‏حدى تو در جمال و در كمال / ‏در كژى ما بى‏حديم و در ضالل
entries.english: So that You wilt have concealed other disgraces’, O Bounteous One whose help we implore!

entries.farsi: تا فضيحت‏هاى ديگر را نهان / ‏كرده باشى اى كريم مستعان
entries.english: You are infinite in beauty and perfection; we are infinite in wrongness and error.

entries.farsi: بى‏حدى خويش بگمار اى كريم / ‏بر كژى بى‏حد مشتى لئيم
entries.english: Direct Your infinity, O Bounteous One, upon the infinite wrongness of a handful of vile wretches.

entries.farsi: هين كه از تقطيع ما يك تار ماند / مصر بوديم و يكى ديوار ماند
entries.english: Oh, come, for of our cloth-piece a single thread remains: we were a city, and a single wall remains.

entries.farsi: البقيه البقيه اى خديو / تا نگردد شاد كلى جان ديو
entries.english: The remnant, the remnant, O Sovereign that the soul of the devil may not rejoice entirely

entries.farsi: بهر ما نه بهر آن لطف نخست / ‏كه تو كردى گمرهان را باز جست
entries.english: Not for our sakes, for the sake of the primal grace through which You did seek out them that had lost the way.

entries.farsi: چون نمودى قدرتت بنماى رحم / ‏اى نهاده رحمها در لحم و شحم
entries.english: As You have shown Your power, show Your mercy, O You who have implanted feelings of mercy in flesh and fat.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كادم بيفتاد از بهشت / ‏رجعتش دادى كه رست از ديو زشت
entries.english: If this prayer increases Your wrath, teach us to pray, O Lord,

entries.farsi: اين دعا گر خشم افزايد ترا / تو دعا تعليم فرما مهترا
entries.english: Even as, Adam fell from Paradise, You gave him to turn toward You, so that he escaped from the ugly devil.”

entries.farsi: ديو كه بود كاو ز آدم بگذرد / بر چنين نطعى از او بازى برد
entries.english: Who is the devil that he should surpass Adam and win the game from him on such a board?

entries.farsi: بازيى ديد و دو صد بازى نديد / پس ستون خانه‏ى خود را بريد
entries.english: In truth, it all turned out to Adam’s advantage: that guile became a curse to the envious one.

entries.farsi: در حقيقت نفع آدم شد همه / ‏لعنت حاسد شده آن دمدمه
entries.english: He saw one game; he did not see two hundred games: therefore he cut down the supports of his own house.
