entries.farsi: آتشى زد شب به كشت ديگران / ‏باد آتش را به كشت او بران
entries.english: He set fire by night to the cornfield of others; the wind carrying the fire into his own field.

entries.farsi: چشم بندى بود لعنت ديو را / تا زيان خصم ديد آن ريو را
entries.english: The curse was a blind to the Devil, so that he regarded that trickery as harm to enemy.

entries.farsi: لعنت اين باشد كه كژبينش كند / حاسد و خود بين و پر كينش كند
entries.english: The curse is that which makes him see falsely, and makes him envious, self-conceited, and malicious,

entries.farsi: تا نداند كه هر آن كه كرد بد / عاقبت باز آيد و بر وى زند
entries.english: To the end that he may not know that whoever does evil, it will at last come back and smite him.

entries.farsi: جمله فرزين بندها بيند بعكس / ‏مات بر وى گردد و نقصان و وكس
entries.english: He sees all the master-moves backwards: they result in check-mate to him and failure and defeat.

entries.farsi: ز انكه گر او هيچ بيند خويش را / مهلك و ناسور بيند ريش را
entries.english: Because, if he regard himself as naught, he regard the wound as deadly and festering,

entries.farsi: درد خيزد زين چنين ديدن درون / ‏درد او را از حجاب آرد برون
entries.english: Pain will arise from such looking within, and the pain will bring him out from the veil.

entries.farsi: تا نگيرد مادران را درد زه / ‏طفل در زادن نيابد هيچ ره
entries.english: Until mothers are overtaken by the pains of childbirth, the child finds no way to be born.

entries.farsi: اين امانت در دل و دل حامله ست / ‏اين نصيحتها مثال قابله ست
entries.english: This trust is in the heart, and the heart is pregnant: these counsels are like the midwife.

entries.farsi: قابله گويد كه زن را درد نيست / ‏درد بايد درد كودك را رهى است
entries.english: The midwife may say that the woman has no pain; pain is necessary, pain is a way for the child.

entries.farsi: آن كه او بى‏درد باشد ره زن است / ‏ز انكه بى‏دردى انا الحق گفتن است
entries.english: He that is without pain is a brigand, because to be without pain is to say “I am God.”

entries.farsi: آن انا بى‏وقت گفتن لعنت است / ‏آن انا در وقت گفتن رحمت است
entries.english: To say that “I” out of the time is a curse; to say that “I” at the time is a mercy

entries.farsi: آن انا منصور رحمت شد يقين / ‏آن انا فرعون لعنت شد ببين
entries.english: The “I” of Mansur certainly became a mercy; the “I” of Pharaoh became a curse. Mark!

entries.farsi: الجرم هر مرغ بى‏هنگام را / سر بريدن واجب است اعالم را
entries.english: Consequently, it is incumbent to behead every untimely bird, in order to give notice.

entries.farsi: سر بريدن چيست كشتن نفس را / در جهاد و ترك گفتن نفس را
entries.english: What is “beheading”? Killing the nafs in the holy war, and renouncing heat

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه نيش كژدم بر كنى / ‏تا كه يابد او ز كشتن ايمنى
entries.english: Just as you would extract the scorpion’s sting in order that it might be saved from being killed,

entries.farsi: بر كنى دندان پر زهرى ز مار / تا رهد مار از بالى سنگسار
entries.english: Pull out the venomous fang of a snake, in order that the snake might escape from the calamity of being stoned.

entries.farsi: هيچ نكشد نفس را جز ظل پير / دامن آن نفس كش را سخت گير
entries.english: Nothing will slay the nafs except the shadow of the Pir: grasp tightly the skirt of that slayer of the flesh.

entries.farsi: چون بگيرى سخت آن توفيق هوست / ‏در تو هر قوت كه آيد جذب اوست
entries.english: When you grasp tightly, that is the aid of Him: whatever strength comes into you is His drawing.

entries.farsi: ما رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رَمَيْتَ راست دان / ‏هر چه كارد جان بود از جان جان
entries.english: Know that true is you didst not throw when you threw: whatever the soul sows is from the Soul of the soul.

entries.farsi: دست گيرنده وى است و بردبار / دم‏به‏دم آن دم از او اميد دار
entries.english: He is the One that takes the hand, and burden-bearing: have hope, from moment to moment, of that breath from Him.

entries.farsi: نيست غم گر دير بى‏او مانده‏اى / ‏ديرگير و سخت‏گيرش خوانده‏اى
entries.english: ‘It is no harm if you have remained long without Him: you have read that He is long in gripping, gripping tight.

entries.farsi: دير گيرد سخت گيرد رحمتش / ‏يك دمت غايب ندارد حضرتش
entries.english: His Mercy is long in gripping, grips tight: His Presence does not keep you absent for one moment.

entries.farsi: گر تو خواهى شرح اين وصل و وال / ‏از سر انديشه مى‏خوان و الضحى
entries.english: If you desire the explanation of this union and friendship, read thoughtfully Wa’l-Duha.

entries.farsi: ور تو گويى هم بديها از وى است / ‏ليك آن نقصان فضل او كى است
entries.english: And if you say that evils too are from Him, but how is it a defect in His grace?

entries.farsi: آن بدى دادن كمال اوست هم / ‏من مثالى گويمت اى محتشم
entries.english: Bestowing this evil is even His perfection: I will tell you a parable, O respected one.

entries.farsi: كرد نقاشى دو گونه نقشها / نقشهاى صاف و نقشى بى‏صفا
entries.english: A painter made two kinds of pictures— beautiful pictures and pictures devoid of beauty.

entries.farsi: نقش يوسف كرد و حور خوش سرشت / ‏نقش عفريتان و ابليسان زشت
entries.english: He painted Joseph and fair-formed houris; he painted ugly monsters and devils.

entries.farsi: هر دو گونه نقش استادى اوست / ‏زشتى او نيست آن رادى اوست
entries.english: Both kinds of pictures are his mastery: those are not his ugliness; they are his bounty.

entries.farsi: زشت را در غايت زشتى كند / جمله زشتيها به گردش بر تند
entries.english: He makes the ugly of extreme ugliness— it is invested with all ugliness-

entries.farsi: تا كمال دانشش پيدا شود / منكر استادى‏اش رسوا شود
entries.english: In order that the perfection of his skill may be displayed the denier of his mastery may be put to shame.

entries.farsi: ور نداند زشت كردن ناقص است / ‏زين سبب خالق گبر و مخلص است
entries.english: And if he cannot make the ugly, he is deficient: hence He is the Creator of the infidel and the sincere.

entries.farsi: پس از اين رو كفر و ايمان شاهداند / بر خداونديش و هر دو ساجداند
entries.english: From this point of view, then, infidelity and faith are bearing witness: both are bowing down in worship before His Lordliness.

entries.farsi: ليك مومن دان كه طوعا ساجد است / ‏ز انكه جوياى رضا و قاصد است
entries.english: But know that the faithful bows down willingly, because he is seeking pleasure and aiming.

entries.farsi: هست كرها گبر هم يزدان پرست / ‏ليك قصد او مرادى ديگر است
entries.english: The infidel too is a worshipper of God, unwillingly; but his aim is another object of desire.

entries.farsi: قلعه‏ى سلطان عمارت مى‏كند / ليك دعوى امارت مى‏كند
entries.english: He keeps the King’s fortress in good repair; but he is claiming to be in command.

entries.farsi: گشته ياغى تا كه ملك او بود / عاقبت خود قلعه سلطانى شود
entries.english: He has become a rebel, to the intent that it may be his domain; truly, in the end the fortress comes to be the King’s.

entries.farsi: مومن آن قلعه براى پادشاه / ‏مى‏كند معمور نه از بهر جاه
entries.english: The faithful believer keeps that fortress in good repair for the sake of the King, not for place.

entries.farsi: زشت گويد اى شه زشت آفرين / ‏قادرى بر خوب و بر زشت مهين
entries.english: The ugly one says, “O King who creates the ugly, You are able for the beautiful as well as the despicable ugly.”

entries.farsi: خوب گويد اى شه حسن و بها / گفت پيغمبر مر آن بيمار را
entries.english: The beautiful one says, “O King of beauty and comeliness, You have made me free from defects.”

sections.title_fa: ‏وصيت كردن پيغامبر صلى اللهَّ عليه و آله مر آن بيمار را و دعا آموزانيدنش
sections.title_en: How the Prophet, God bless and save him, gave injunctions to the sick man

entries.farsi: اين بگو كاى سهل كن دشوار را / آتنا فى دار دنيانا حسن
entries.english: and taught him to pray.

entries.farsi: ‏آتنا فى دار عقبانا حسن / راه را بر ما چو بستان كن لطيف
entries.english: The Prophet said to the sick man: “Say thus:— ‘O You that makes easy that which is hard,

entries.farsi: ‏منزل ما خود تو باشى اى شريف / مومنان در حشر گويند اى ملك
entries.english: Give good to us in our present abode, and give us good in our future abode!

entries.farsi: ‏نى كه دوزخ بود راه مشترك / مومن و كافر بر او يابد گذار
entries.english: Make the way agreeable to us as a garden: You indeed, O Glorious One, are our goal.’”

entries.farsi: ما نديديم اندر اين ره دود و نار / نك بهشت و بارگاه ايمنى
entries.english: At the Gathering the true believers will say, “O angel, is it not that Hell is the common road,

entries.farsi: ‏پس كجا بود آن گذرگاه دنى / پس ملك گويد كه آن روضه‏ى خضر
entries.english: True believer and infidel pass by it? We saw no smoke or fire in this road.

entries.farsi: كه فالن جا ديده‏ايد اندر گذر / دوزخ آن بود و سياستگاه سخت
entries.english: Lo, here is Paradise and the Court of safety: where, then, was that vile place of passage? “

entries.farsi: ‏بر شما شد باغ و بستان و درخت / چون شما اين نفس دوزخ خوى را
entries.english: Then the angel will say, “The garden of greenery which you saw in a certain spot as you passed

entries.farsi: آتشى گبر فتنه جوى را / جهدها كرديد و او شد پر صفا
entries.english: That was Hell and the terrible place of chastisement, to you it became gardens and pleasantness and trees.

entries.farsi: نار را كشتيد از بهر خدا / آتش شهوت كه شعله مى‏زدى
entries.english: Inasmuch as with this soul of hellish nature, miscreant fiery one that seeks temptation,

entries.farsi: ‏سبزه‏ى تقوى شد و نور هدى / آتش خشم از شما هم حلم شد
entries.english: You have striven, and it has become full of purity, and you have quenched the fire for God’s sake ;

entries.farsi: ظلمت جهل از شما هم علم شد / آتش حرص از شما ايثار شد
entries.english: The fire of lust, which was flaming, has become the verdure of piety and the light of guidance;

entries.farsi: و آن حسد چون خار بد گلزار شد / چون شما اين جمله آتشهاى خويش
entries.english: At once the fire of anger in you has turned to forbearance, and the darkness of ignorance in you has turned to knowledge;

entries.farsi: ‏بهر حق كشتيد جمله پيش پيش / نفس نارى را چو باغى ساختيد
entries.english: The fire of greed in you has turned to unselfishness, and that envy was like thorns has turned to roses;

entries.farsi: اندر او تخم وفا انداختيد / بلبالن ذكر و تسبيح اندر او
entries.english: Inasmuch as you formerly extinguished all these fires of your own for God’s sake;
And made the fiery soul like an orchard and cast in it the seed of fealty,

entries.farsi: خوش سرايان در چمن بر طرف جو / داعى حق را اجابت كرده‏ايد
entries.english: There the nightingales of commemoration and glorification of God singing sweetly in the garden by the river-side;

entries.farsi: در جحيم نفس آب آورده‏ايد / دوزخ ما نيز در حق شما
entries.english: You have answered the call of God and have brought water into the blazing hell of your soul

entries.farsi: سبزه گشت و گلشن و برگ و نوا / چيست احسان را مكافات اى پسر
entries.english: Our Hell also in regard to you has become greenery and roses and plenty and riches.”

entries.farsi: لطف و احسان و ثواب معتبر / نى شما گفتيد ما قربانى‏ايم
entries.english: What is the requital for well-doing, O son? Kindness and well-doing and valued recompense

entries.farsi: ‏پيش اوصاف بقا ما فانى‏ايم / ما اگر قالش و گر ديوانه‏ايم
entries.english: “Did not you say, ‘ We are devoted, we are passing away before the attributes of Everlastingness?

entries.farsi: ‏مست آن ساقى و آن پيمانه‏ايم / بر خط و فرمان او سر مى‏نهيم
entries.english: We, whether we be cunning or mad, are intoxicated with that Cupbearer and that cup.

entries.farsi: ‏جان شيرين را گروگان مى‏دهيم / تا خيال دوست در اسرار ماست
entries.english: We lay our heads upon His writ and mandate: we give our sweet lives in pawn.

entries.farsi: ‏چاكرى و جان سپارى كار ماست / هر كجا شمع بال افروختند
entries.english: So long as the thought of the Friend is in our inmost hearts, our work is to serve and yield up our lives.’”

entries.farsi: شمع روى يار را پروانه‏اند / اى دل آن جا رو كه با تو روشن‏اند
entries.english: Wherever the candle of tribulation has been lighted, hundreds of thousands of loving souls are burnt.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران جان عاشق سوختند / عاشقانى كز درون خانه‏اند
entries.english: Those lovers that are within the house are moths to the candle of the face of the Friend.

entries.farsi: وز بالها مر ترا چون جوشن‏اند / ز آن ميان جان ترا جا مى‏كنند
entries.english: O heart, go where they are bright toward you and are as a coat of mail to you against afflictions,

entries.farsi: تا ترا پر باده چون جامى كنند / در ميان جان ايشان خانه گير
entries.english: And give you a place within their souls, that they may fill you with wine, like a cup.

entries.farsi: در فلك خانه كن اى بدر منير / چون عطارد دفتر دل واكنند
entries.english: Take your abode within their souls: O radiant full-moon, make your home in the sky!

entries.farsi: تا كه بر تو سرها پيدا كنند / پيش خويشان باش چون آواره‏اى
entries.english: Like Mercury, they will open the book of the heart, that they may reveal mysteries unto you.

entries.farsi: ‏بر مه كامل زن ار مه پاره‏اى / جزو را از كل خود پرهيز چيست
entries.english: Stay beside your kinsfolk—how are you roaming abroad? Cleave to the perfect Moon if you are a piece of the moon.

entries.farsi: ‏با مخالف اين همه آميز چيست / جنس را بين نوع گشته در روش
entries.english: What is the part’s keeping aloof from its whole? What is all this mixture with diverse?

entries.farsi: ‏غيبها بين گشته عين از پرتوش / تا چون زن عشوه خرى اى بى‏خرد
entries.english: Behold how the genus has become species in the process: behold how the unseen things have become visible in emanation.

entries.farsi: از دروغ و عشوه كى يابى مدد / چاپلوس و لفظ شيرين و فريب
entries.english: So long as you would happily be cajoled like a woman, O man without wisdom, how will you be helped by lies and cajoling?

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏ستانى مى‏نهى چون زر به جيب / مر ترا دشنام و سيلى شهان
entries.english: You are taking flattery and sweet words and cajoling and putting them like gold in your bosom.

entries.farsi: ‏بهتر آيد از ثناى گمرهان / صفع شاهان خور مخور شهد خسان
entries.english: For you the reviling and blows of the kings would be better than the praise of the unrighteous.

entries.farsi: ‏تا كسى گردى ز اقبال كسان / ز آنك از ايشان خلعت و دولت رسد
entries.english: Swallow the slaps of the kings; do not swallow the honey of the rabble, to the end that through the fortune of personages you may become a personage;

entries.farsi: در پناه روح جان گردد جسد / هر كجا بينى برهنه و بى‏نوا
entries.english: Because from them comes felicity and robes of honour: under the shelter of the spirit, body becomes soul.

entries.farsi: دان كه او بگريخته ست از اوستا / تا چنان گردد كه مى‏خواهد دلش
entries.english: Wherever you see one naked and destitute, know that he has fled from the master,

entries.farsi: ‏آن دل كور بد بى‏حاصلش / گر چنان گشتى كه استا خواستى
entries.english: In order that he may become such as his heart desires— that blind, wicked, worthless heart of him

entries.farsi: ‏خويش را و خويش را آراستى / هر كه از استا گريزد در جهان
entries.english: If he had become such as his master desired, he would have graced himself and his kindred.

entries.farsi: ‏او ز دولت مى‏گريزد اين بدان / پيشه‏اى آموختى در كسب تن
entries.english: Whoever in the world flees from his master is fleeing from felicity. Know this!

entries.farsi: ‏چنگ اندر پيشه‏ى دينى بزن / در جهان پوشيده گشتى و غنى
entries.english: You have learned a trade to earn a livelihood for the body: set your hand to a religious trade.

entries.farsi: ‏اندر آيد دخل كسب مغفرت / آن جهان شهرى است پر بازار و كسب
entries.english: In this world you have become clothed and rich: when you come forth from here, how will you do?

entries.farsi: ‏چون برون آيى از اينجا چون كنى / پيشه‏اى آموز كاندر آخرت
entries.english: Learn such a trade that after the earning of God’s forgiveness may come in as revenue.

entries.farsi: ‏تا نپندارى كه كسب اينجاست حسب / حق تعالى گفت كاين كسب جهان
entries.english: Yonder world is a city full of trafficking and earning: think not that the earnings here are a sufficient.

entries.farsi: ‏پيش آن كسب است لعب كودكان / همچو آن طفلى كه بر طفلى تند
entries.english: The high God has said that beside those earnings these earnings in the world are children’s play—

entries.farsi: شكل صحبت كن مساسى مى‏كند / كودكان سازند در بازى دكان
entries.english: As a child that embraces another child attend to touching and rubbing, pretending to talk

entries.farsi: ‏سود نبود جز كه تعبير زبان / شب شود در خانه آيد گرسنه
entries.english: Children at play set up a shop; it is of no use except as a pastime.

entries.farsi: ‏كودكان رفته بمانده يك تنه / اين جهان بازى‏گه است و مرگ شب
entries.english: Night falls, and he comes home hungry: the children are gone, and he is left alone.

entries.farsi: ‏باز گردى كيسه خالى پر تعب / كسب دين عشق است و جذب اندرون
entries.english: This world is a playground, and death is the night: you return with an empty purse, tired out.

entries.farsi: ‏قابليت نور حق دان اى حرون / كسب فانى خواهدت اين نفس خس
entries.english: The earnings of religion are love and inward rapture— capacity to receive the Light of God, O you obstinate one!

entries.farsi: ‏چند كسب خس كنى بگذار بس / نفس خس گر جويدت كسب شريف
entries.english: This vile nafs desires you to earn that which passes away: how long will you earn what is vile? Let it go! Enough!
If the vile nafs desires you to earn what is noble, there is some trick and plot behind it.

sections.title_fa: بيدار كردن ابليس معاويه را كه خيز وقت نماز است
sections.title_en: How Iblis awakened Mu’awiya—may God be well-pleased with him!—saying, “Arise, it is time for prayer.”

entries.farsi: در خبر آمد كه آن معاويه / ‏خفته بد در قصر در يك زاويه
entries.english: It is related in Tradition that Mu’awiya was asleep in a nook of the palace.

entries.farsi: قصر را از اندرون در بسته بود / كز زيارتهاى مردم خسته بود
entries.english: The palace-door was fastened from the inside, for he was fatigued by people’s visits.

entries.farsi: ناگهان مردى و را بيدار كرد / چشم چون بگشاد پنهان گشت مرد
entries.english: Suddenly he was awakened by a man, when he opened his eyes the man vanished.

entries.farsi: گفت اندر قصر كس را ره نبود / كيست كاين گستاخى و جرات نمود
entries.english: He said, “No one had entrance to the palace: who is he that has shown such impudence and boldness?”

entries.farsi: گرد برگشت و طلب كرد آن زمان / ‏تا بيابد ز آن نهان گشته نشان
entries.english: Then he went round and searched in order to find the trace of that one who had become hidden.

entries.farsi: از پس در مدبرى را ديد كاو / در در و پرده نهان مى‏كرد رو
entries.english: Behind the door he espied a luckless man who was hiding his face in the door and the curtain.

entries.farsi: گفت هى تو كيستى نام تو چيست / ‏گفت نامم فاش ابليس شقى است
entries.english: “Hey,” he cried, “who are you? What is your name? “plainly,” said he, “my name is Iblis the damned.”

entries.farsi: گفت بيدارم چرا كردى به جد / راست گو با من مگو بر عكس و ضد
entries.english: He asked, “Why did you take pains to awaken me? Tell the truth; don’t tell me what is reverse and contrary.”

sections.title_fa: از خر افكندن ابليس معاويه را و رو پوش و بهانه كردن و جواب گفتن عاويه او را
sections.title_en: How Iblis gave Mu’awiya, may God be well-pleased with him, a fall, and practiced dissimulation and pretence, and how Mu’awiya answered him.

entries.farsi: عجلوا الطاعات قبل الفوت گفت / ‏مصطفى چون در معنى مى‏بسفت
entries.english: He said, “The time for prayer is come to an end: you must run quickly to the mosque.

entries.farsi: گفت هنگام نماز آخر رسيد / سوى مسجد زود مى‏بايد دويد
entries.english: Mustafa said, boring the pearl of the idea, Make haste to perform your devotions before the time is past.’”

entries.farsi: گفت نى نى اين غرض نبود ترا / كه به خيرى رهنما باشى مرا
entries.english: He said, “Nay, nay; it is not your purpose to be my guide unto any good.

entries.farsi: دزد آيد از نهان در مسكنم / ‏گويدم كه پاسبانى مى‏كنم
entries.english: If a thief comes secretly into my dwelling-place and says to me, ‘ I am keeping watch,’

entries.farsi: من كجا باور كنم آن دزد را / دزد كى داند ثواب و مزد را
entries.english: How shall I believe that thief? How should a thief know the recompense and reward for good works?”

sections.title_fa: باز جواب گفتن ابليس معاويه را
sections.title_en: How Iblis again made answer to Mu’awiya.

entries.farsi: گفت ما اول فرشته بوده‏ايم / ‏راه طاعت را به جان پيموده‏ايم
entries.english: He said, “At first I was an angel: I traversed the way of obedience with soul.

entries.farsi: سالكان راه را محرم بديم / ‏ساكنان عرش را هم دم بديم
entries.english: I was the confidant of them that follow the path: I was familiar with them that dwell by the Throne of God.

entries.farsi: پيشه‏ى اول كجا از دل رود / مهر اول كى ز دل بيرون شود
entries.english: How should first calling go out of mind? How should first love go forth from heart?

entries.farsi: در سفر گر روم بينى يا ختن / ‏از دل تو كى رود حب الوطن
entries.english: If in travel you see Anatolia or Khutan, how should love of your own country go from your heart?

entries.farsi: ما هم از مستان اين مى بوده‏ايم / ‏عاشقان درگه وى بوده‏ايم
entries.english: I too have been one of those drunken with this wine: I have been a lover at His court.

entries.farsi: ناف ما بر مهر او ببريده‏اند / عشق او در جان ما كاريده‏اند
entries.english: They cut my navel in love of Him: they sowed love of Him in my heart.

entries.farsi: روز نيكو ديده‏ايم از روزگار / آب رحمت خورده‏ايم اندر بهار
entries.english: I have seen good days from Fortune: I have drunk the water of Mercy in spring-time.

entries.farsi: نه كه ما را دست فضلش كاشته ست / ‏از عدم ما را نه او برداشته ست
entries.english: Was it not the hand of His bounty that sowed me? Was it not He that raised me up from non-existence?

entries.farsi: اى بسا كز وى نوازش ديده‏ايم / ‏در گلستان رضا گرديده‏ايم
entries.english: Oh, many is the time I have received kindness from Him and walked in the rose-garden of approval.

entries.farsi: بر سر ما دست رحمت مى‏نهاد / چشمه‏هاى لطف از ما مى‏گشاد
entries.english: He would lay the hand of mercy on my head, He would open from me the fountains of grace.

entries.farsi: وقت طفلى‏ام كه بودم شير جو / گاهوارم را كه جنبانيد او
entries.english: Who found milk for me in the season of my infancy? Who rocked my cradle? He

entries.farsi: از كه خوردم شير غير شير او / كى مرا پرورد جز تدبير او
entries.english: From whom did I drink milk other than His milk? Who nourished me except His providence?

entries.farsi: خوى كان با شير رفت اندر وجود / كى توان آن را ز مردم واگشود
entries.english: The disposition which has entered with the milk into being— how can it be discharged from folk?

entries.farsi: گر عتابى كرد درياى كرم / ‏بسته كى گردند درهاى كرم
entries.english: If the Sea of Bounty has given a rebuke, how have the doors of Bounty been shut?

entries.farsi: اصل نقدش داد و لطف و بخشش است / ‏قهر بر وى چون غبارى از غش است
entries.english: Giving and grace and favour are the fundamental substance of His coin: wrath is as a speck of alloy on it.

entries.farsi: از براى لطف عالم را بساخت / ‏ذره‏ها را آفتاب او نواخت
entries.english: He made the world for kindness’ sake: His sun caressed the motes.

entries.farsi: فرقت از قهرش اگر آبستن است / ‏بهر قدر وصل او دانستن است
entries.english: If separation is big with His wrath, it is for the sake of knowing the worth of union with Him,

entries.farsi: تا دهد جان را فراقش گوشمال / ‏جان بداند قدر ايام وصال
entries.english: So that separation from Him may give the soul chastisement, the soul may know the value of the days of union.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه حق فرموده است / ‏قصد من از خلق احسان بوده است
entries.english: The Prophet has declared that God said, my purpose in creating was to do good:

entries.farsi: آفريدم تا ز من سودى كنند / تا ز شهدم دست‏آلودى كنند
entries.english: I created to the intent that they might draw some gain from Me, and that they might smear their hands with My honey;

entries.farsi: نى براى آن كه تا سودى كنم / ‏و ز برهنه من قبايى بر كنم
entries.english: Not to the end that I might draw some gain, and that I might tear off a coat from one naked

entries.farsi: چند روزى كه ز پيشم رانده است / ‏چشم من در روى خوبش مانده است
entries.english: During the short while since He drove me from His presence, my eye has remained upon His beautiful face;

entries.farsi: كز چنان رويى چنين قهر اى عجب / ‏هر كسى مشغول گشته در سبب
entries.english: ‘Such wrath from such a face! Oh, wonderful!’ every one has become occupied with the cause.

entries.farsi: من سبب را ننگرم كان حادث است / ‏ز انكه حادث حادثى را باعث است
entries.english: I do not look at the cause, which is temporal, inasmuch as the temporal produces something temporal.

entries.farsi: ترك سجده از حسد گيرم كه بود / آن حسد از عشق خيزد نز جحود
entries.english: I am regarding precedent mercy: whatsoever is temporal I rend in twain.

entries.farsi: لطف سابق را نظاره مى‏كنم / هر چه آن حادث دو پاره مى‏كنم
entries.english: Grant that my declining to worship was from envy; that envy arises from love, not from denial.

entries.farsi: هر حسد از دوستى خيزد يقين / ‏كه شود با دوست غيرى همنشين
entries.english: It is certain; all envy arises from love, lest another become the companion of the beloved.

entries.farsi: هست شرط دوستى غيرت پزى / ‏همچو شرط عطسه گفتن دير زى
entries.english: Brooding jealousy is the necessary consequence of love, just as saying ‘ Live long!’ must follow the sneeze.

entries.farsi: چون كه بر نطعش جز اين بازى نبود / گفت بازى كن چه دانم در فزود
entries.english: Since there was no play but this on His board, and He said, ’Play,’ what more can I do?

entries.farsi: آن يكى بازى كه بد من باختم / ‏خويشتن را در بال انداختم
entries.english: I played the one play that there was, and cast myself into woe.

entries.farsi: در بال هم مى‏چشم لذات او / مات اويم مات اويم مات او
entries.english: Even in woe I taste His delights: I am mated by Him, mated by Him, mated by Him!

entries.farsi: چون رهاند خويشتن را اى سره / ‏هيچ كس در شش جهت از شش دره
entries.english: How shall any one, O noble sir, in six directions deliver himself from the shashdara?

entries.farsi: جزو شش از كل شش چون وارهد / خاصه كه بى‏چون مر او را كژ نهد
entries.english: How shall the part of the six escape from the whole of the six, especially when the Unconditioned sets it wrong?

entries.farsi: هر كه در شش او درون آتش است / ‏اوش برهاند كه خالق شش است
entries.english: Whoever is in the six is in the fire; He that is the creator of the six will deliver him.

entries.farsi: خود اگر كفر است و گر ايمان او / گفت امير او را كه اينها راست است
entries.english: Truly, whether it be infidelity or faith in Him, he is the hand-loom of the Lord and belongs to Him.”

sections.title_fa: باز تقرير كردن معاويه با ابليس مكر او را
sections.title_en: How Mu’awiya again exposed the deceitfulness of Iblis.

entries.farsi: ‏ليك بخش تو ازينها كاست است / صد هزاران را چو من تو ره زدى
entries.english: The Amir said to him, “These things are true, but your share in these things is wanting.

entries.farsi: ‏حفره كردى در خزينه آمدى / آتشى از تو نسوزم چاره نيست
entries.english: You have waylaid hundreds of thousands like me: you have made a hole and have come into the treasure-house.

entries.farsi: ‏كيست كز دست تو جامه‏ش پاره نيست / طبعت اى آتش چو سوزانيدنى است
entries.english: You are fire and naphtha: you burn, you cannot help it. Who is there whose raiment is not torn to pieces by your hand?

entries.farsi: ‏تا نسوزانى تو چيزى چاره نيست / لعنت اين باشد كه سوزانت كند
entries.english: Inasmuch as it is your nature, O fire, to be a cause of burning, there is no help but you must burn something.

entries.farsi: اوستاد جمله دزدانت كند / با خدا گفتى شنيدى رو برو
entries.english: This is God’s curse, that He makes you burn and makes you the master of all thieves.

entries.farsi: بانگ مرغانى است ليكن مرغ گير / صد هزاران مرغ را آن ره زده ست
entries.english: You have spoken with God and heard face to face: what should I be before your deceit, O enemy?

entries.farsi: من چه باشم پيش مكرت اى عدو / معرفتهاى تو چون بانگ صفير
entries.english: Your stock of knowledge is like the sound of whistle: it is the cry of birds, but it is bird-ensnaring.

entries.farsi: ‏مرغ غره كاشنايى آمده ست / در هوا چون بشنود بانگ صفير
entries.english: That has waylaid myriads of birds, the bird being duped that a friend is come.

entries.farsi: از هوا آيد شود اينجا اسير / قوم نوح از مكر تو در نوحه‏اند
entries.english: When it hears in the air the sound of the whistle, it comes from the air and is made captive here.

entries.farsi: دل كباب و سينه شرحه شرحه‏اند / عاد را تو باد دادى در جهان
entries.english: Through your deceit the people of Noah are in lamentation: they have hearts charred and bosoms to shreds.

entries.farsi: ‏در فگندى در عذاب و اندهان / از تو بود آن سنگسار قوم لوط
entries.english: You gave ‘Ad in this world to the wind: you cast into torment and sorrows.

entries.farsi: در سياه آبه ز تو خوردند غوط / مغز نمرود از تو آمد ريخته
entries.english: Through you was the stoning of the people of Lot: through you were they sunk in the black rain-water.

entries.farsi: ‏اى هزاران فتنه‏ها انگيخته / عقل فرعون ذكى فيلسوف
entries.english: Through you was the brain of Nimrod crumbled, O you that have raised thousands of sorts of turmoil!

entries.farsi: ‏كور گشت از تو نيابيد او وقوف / بو لهب هم از تو نااهلى شده
entries.english: Through you the intelligence of Pharaoh, the acute and sage, became blinded, he found no understanding

entries.farsi: ‏بو الحكم هم از تو بو جهلى شده / اى بر اين شطرنج بهر ياد را
entries.english: Through you also Bu Lahab became an unworthy one; through you also Bu ‘l-Hakam became a Bu Jahl.

entries.farsi: مات كرده صد هزار استاد را / اى ز فرزين بندهاى مشكلت
entries.english: O you that on this chessboard, for the sake of remembrance, have checkmated hundreds of thousands of masters,

entries.farsi: ‏سوخته دلها سيه گشته دلت / بحر مكرى تو خاليق قطره‏اى
entries.english: O you by whose difficult attacking moves hearts have been burned and your heart has been blackened,

entries.farsi: ‏تو چو كوهى وين سليمان ذره‏اى / كى رهد از مكر تو اى مختصم
entries.english: You are the sea of cunning, the creatures a drop: you are like a mountain and simple ones a mote.

entries.farsi: ‏غرق طوفانيم الا من عصم / بس ستاره‏ى سعد از تو محترق
entries.english: Who shall escape from your cunning, O adversary? We are drowned in the flood, except them that are protected.

entries.farsi: ‏بس سپاه و جمع از تو مفترق / گفت ابليسش گشاى اين عقد را
entries.english: By you many a fortunate star has been burned: by you many an army and host have been scattered.”

sections.title_fa: باز جواب گفتن ابليس معاويه را
sections.title_en: How Iblis again replied to Mu’awiya.

entries.farsi: من محكم قلب را و نقد را / امتحان شير و كلبم كرد حق
entries.english: Iblis said to him, “ Unravel this knot: I am the touchstone for the false coin and the true.

entries.farsi: ‏امتحان نقد و قلبم كرد حق / قلب را من كى سيه رو كرده‏ام
entries.english: God has made me the test of lion and cur, God has made me the test of genuine coin and counterfeit.

entries.farsi: ‏شاخه‏هاى خشك را بر مى‏كنم / اين علفها مى‏نهم از بهر چيست
entries.english: When have I blackened the false coin’s face? I am the money-changer: I have valued it.

entries.farsi: ‏صيرفى‏ام قيمت او كرده‏ام / نيكوان را ره نمايى مى‏كنم
entries.english: To the good I act as guide, the dry branches I rip off.

entries.farsi: ‏تا پديد آيد كه حيوان جنس كيست / گرگ از آهو چو زايد كودكى
entries.english: I lay these sorts of fodder—for what purpose? In order that it may be seen of what kind the animal is.

entries.farsi: ‏هست در گرگيش و آهويى شكى / تو گياه و استخوان پيشش بريز
entries.english: When a wolf bears young to an antelope, and there is some doubt whether it has the nature of wolf or antelope,

entries.farsi: تا كدامين سو كند او گام تيز / گر به سوى استخوان آيد سگ است
entries.english: Drop you grass and bones in front of it to which side it quickly steps

entries.farsi: ‏ور گيا خواهد يقين آهو رگ است / قهر و لطفى جفت شد با همدگر
entries.english: If it comes towards the bones, it is canine; and if it craves the grass, it is assuredly of the antelope race.

entries.farsi: زاد از اين هر دو جهانى خير و شر / تو گياه و استخوان را عرضه كن
entries.english: A wrath and a mercy were wedded to one another: from these two was born the world of good and evil.

entries.farsi: ‏قوت نفس و قوت جان را عرضه كن / گر غذاى نفس جويد ابتر است
entries.english: Offer grass and bones; offer the food of the flesh and the food of the spirit.

entries.farsi: ‏ور غذاى روح خواهد سرور است / گر كند او خدمت تن هست خر
entries.english: If he seeks the food of the flesh, he is bobtailed, and if he desires the food of the spirit, he is a chief.

entries.farsi: ور رود در بحر جان يابد گهر / گر چه اين دو مختلف خير و شراند
entries.english: If he serves the body, he is an ass; and if he goes into the sea of the spirit, he will find pearls.

entries.farsi: ليك اين هر دو به يك كار اندراند / انبيا طاعات عرضه مى‏كنند
entries.english: Although these two—good and evil—are different, yet these two are in one work.

entries.farsi: دشمنان شهوات عرضه مى‏كنند / نيك را چون بد كنم يزدان نى‏ام
entries.english: The prophets offer devotions, the enemies offer lusts.

entries.farsi: ‏داعيم من خالق ايشان نى‏ام / خوب را من زشت سازم رب نه‏ام
entries.english: How should I make the good man bad? I am not God. I am a prompter, I am not their creator.

entries.farsi: ‏زشت را و خوب را آيينه‏ام / سوخت هندو آينه از درد را
entries.english: Should I make the fair foul? I am not the Lord. I am a mirror for the foul and the fair.

entries.farsi: كاين سيه رو مى‏نمايد مرد را / او مرا غماز كرد و راست گو
entries.english: The Hindu burnt a mirror in vexation, saying, this causes a man to look black-faced.’

entries.farsi: تا بگويم زشت كو و خوب كو / من گواهم بر گوا زندان كجاست
entries.english: He has made me an informer and truth-teller, that I may tell where the ugly one is and where the beautiful.

entries.farsi: تربيتها مى‏كنم من دايه‏وار / هر كجا بينم درخت تلخ و خشك
entries.english: I am a witness: how is prison for a witness? I do not deserve prison, God is the witness.

entries.farsi: ‏اهل زندان نيستم ايزد گواست / هر كجا بينم نهال ميوه‏دار
entries.english: Wherever I see a fruitful sapling, I foster diligently like a nurse.

entries.farsi: مر مرا چه مى‏برى سر بى‏خطا / باغبان گويد خمش اى زشت خو
entries.english: Wherever I see a sour and dry tree, I cut it down, in order that the musk may be delivered from the dung.

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏برم تا وارهد از پشك مشك / خشك گويد باغبان را كاى فتى
entries.english: The dry says to the gardener, ‘O young man, why do you cut off my head without fault?’

entries.farsi: بس نباشد خشكى تو جرم تو / خشك گويد راستم من كژ نى‏ام
entries.english: The gardener says, Be silent, O evil-natured one! Is not your dryness sin enough in you?’

entries.farsi: ‏تو چرا بى‏جرم مى‏برى پيم / باغبان گويد اگر مسعودى‏اى
entries.english: The dry says “I am straight, I am not crooked: why are you cutting me without guilt?’

entries.farsi: كاشكى كژ بودى‏اى تر بودى‏اى / جاذب آب حياتى گشته‏اى
entries.english: The gardener says, Had you been blessed, would that you were crooked you were moist.

entries.farsi: ‏اندر آب زندگى آغشتى‏اى / تخم تو بد بوده است و اصل تو
entries.english: You would have drawn the Water of Life: you would have been steeped in the Water of Life.

entries.farsi: با درخت خوش نبوده وصل تو / شاخ تلخ ار با خوشى وصلت كند
entries.english: Your seed and your root were bad, and you have not been joined to a good tree.

entries.farsi: آن خوشى اندر نهادش بر زند / گفت امير اى راه زن حجت مگو
entries.english: If the sour branch be joined to a sweet one, that sweetness will strike on its nature.’”

sections.title_fa: ‏عنف كردن معاويه با ابليس
sections.title_en: How Mu’awiya dealt sternly with Iblis.

entries.farsi: مر ترا ره نيست در من ره مجو / ره زنى و من غريب و تاجرم
entries.english: Said the Amir, “O brigand, do not argue: there is no way for you into me, do not seek the way.

entries.farsi: ‏هر لباساتى كه آرى كى خرم / گرد رخت من مگرد از كافرى
entries.english: You are a brigand, and I am a stranger and merchant: how should I purchase any garments that you may bring?

entries.farsi: ‏تو نه اى رخت كسى را مشترى / مشترى نبود كسى را راه زن
entries.english: Do not prowl about my property, infidel as you are: you are not one to buy the property of anybody.

entries.farsi: ‏ور نمايد مشترى مكر است و فن / تا چه دارد اين حسود اندر كدو
entries.english: The brigand is not a buyer for any person, and if he seems to be a buyer, it is deceit and artfulness.

entries.farsi: اى خدا فرياد ما را زين عدو / گر يكى فصلى دگر در من دمد
entries.english: I wonder what this envier has in his gourd! O God, help us against this enemy!

entries.farsi: در ربايد از من اين ره زن نمد / اين حديثش همچو دود است اى اله
entries.english: If he pronounces one more speech over me, this brigand will rob me of the mantle.

sections.title_fa: ‏ناليدن معاويه به حضرت حق تعالى از ابليس و نصرت خواستن
sections.title_en: How Mu’awiya complained of Iblis to the most high God and besought His aid.

entries.farsi: ‏دست گير ار نه گليمم شد سياه / من به حجت بر نيايم با بليس
entries.english: O God, this talk of his is like smoke: take my hand, or else my raiment is blackened.

entries.farsi: ‏كاوست فتنه‏ى هر شريف و هر خسيس / آدمى كه علم الاسما بك است
entries.english: I cannot prevail in argument with Iblis, for he leads every one, noble and base, into temptation.

entries.farsi: ‏در تك چون برق اين سگ بى‏تك است / از بهشت انداختش بر روى خاك
entries.english: Adam, who is the lord of He taught the Names, is powerless before the lightning-like onset of this cur.

entries.farsi: ‏چون سمك در شست او شد از سماك / نوحه‏ى إنا ظلمنا مى‏زدى
entries.english: He cast him from Paradise upon the face of the earth: he fell from Simak into his net, like a fish,

entries.farsi: ‏نيست دستان و فسونش را حدى / اندرون هر حديث او شر است
entries.english: Crying in lamentation, ‘verily, we have wronged.’ There is no bound to his guile and imposture

entries.farsi: ‏صد هزاران سحر در وى مضمر است / مردى مردان ببندد در نفس
entries.english: In his every saying there is mischief: myriads of enchantments are concealed in his mind.

entries.farsi: ‏در زن و در مرد افروزد هوس / اى بليس خلق سوز فتنه جو
entries.english: He castrates men in a moment: he kindles vain desire in man and woman.

entries.farsi: بر چى‏ام بيدار كردى راست گو / گفت هر مردى كه باشد بد گمان
entries.english: O Iblis, you that consume the people and seek to tempt them, on what ground did you awaken me? Tell the truth!”

sections.title_fa: باز تقرير ابليس تلبيس خود را
sections.title_en: How Iblis once more exhibited his deceit.

entries.farsi: چون دليل آرى خيالش بيش شد / چون سخن دروى رود علت شود
entries.english: He said, “No man that thinks evil would hearken to the truth notwithstanding a hundred signs.

entries.farsi: ‏نشنود او راست را با صد نشان / هر درونى كه خيال‏انديش شد
entries.english: Every mind that has conceived fancies— when you bring forward proof, its fancy is increased.

entries.farsi: تيغ غازى دزد را آلت شود / پس جواب او سكوت است و سكون
entries.english: When words enter it, they become a disease: the holy warrior’s sword becomes a tool for the thief.

entries.farsi: ‏هست با ابله سخن گفتن جنون / تو ز من با حق چه نالى اى سليم
entries.english: Therefore the answer to him is silence and rest: to talk with a fool is madness.

entries.farsi: ‏تو بنال از شر آن نفس لئيم / تو خورى حلوا تو را دنبل شود
entries.english: Why do you complain to God of me, O simpleton? Complain of the wickedness of that vile nafs.

entries.farsi: تب بگيرد طبع تو مختل شود / بى‏گنه لعنت كنى ابليس را
entries.english: You eat halwa, boils break out in you, fever lays hold of you, your health is disordered.

entries.farsi: چون نبينى از خود آن تلبيس را / نيست از ابليس از تست اى غوى
entries.english: You curse Iblis, guiltless. How do you not see that deception from yourself?

entries.farsi: ‏كه چو روبه سوى دنبه مى‏دوى / چون كه در سبزه ببينى دنبه را
entries.english: It is not of Iblis, it is of yourself, O misguided one that you are running like a fox towards the sheep’s fat tail.

entries.farsi: دام باشد اين ندانى تو چرا / ز آن ندانى كت ز دانش دور كرد
entries.english: When you see the fat tails in the green field, it is a snare. Why are you ignorant of this?

entries.farsi: ميل دنبه چشم و عقلت كور كرد / حبك األشياء يعميك يصم
entries.english: You are ignorant because desire for the fat tail has made you far from knowledge and has blinded your eye and intelligence.

entries.farsi: ‏نفسك السودا جنت ال تختصم / تو گنه بر من منه كژ مژ مبين
entries.english: Your love of things makes you blind and deaf; your black fleshly soul is the culprit: do not quarrel.

entries.farsi: ‏من ز بد بيزارم و از حرص و كين / من بدى كردم پشيمانم هنوز
entries.english: Do not put the guilt on me, do not see upside down. I am averse to evil and greed and enmity.

entries.farsi: انتظارم تا شبم آيد به روز / متهم گشتم ميان خلق من
entries.english: I did an evil deed and am still repenting: I am waiting that my night may turn to day.

entries.farsi: ‏فعل خود بر من نهد هر مرد و زن / گرگ بى‏چاره اگر چه گرسنه است
entries.english: I have become suspect amongst mankind: every man and woman lay their actions on me.

entries.farsi: ‏متهم باشد كه او در طنطنه است / از ضعيفى چون نتاند راه رفت
entries.english: The helpless wolf, though he is hungry, is suspected of being in luxury.

entries.farsi: ‏خلق گويد تخمه است از لوت زفت / گفت غير راستى نرهاندت
entries.english: When, because of feebleness, he cannot go his way, people say it is indigestion from gross food.”

sections.title_fa: باز الحاح كردن معاويه ابليس را
sections.title_en: How Mu’awiya once more pressed Iblis hard.

entries.farsi: ‏داد سوى راستى مى‏خواندت / راست گو تا وارهى از چنگ من
entries.english: He said, “Nothing but the truth will save you: justice is calling you to the truth.

entries.farsi: ‏مكر ننشاند غبار جنگ من / گفت چون دانى دروغ و راست را
entries.english: Tell the truth, so that you may be delivered from my hand: cunning will not lay the dust of my war”

entries.farsi: اى خيال‏انديش پر انديشه‏ها / گفت پيغمبر نشانى داده است
entries.english: He said, “How do you know falsehood and truth, O thinker of vain fancies, filled with thoughts?”

entries.farsi: ‏قلب و نيكو را محك بنهاده است / گفته است الكذب ريب فى القلوب
entries.english: He answered, “ The Prophet has given an indication: he has laid down the touchstone for the base coin and the good.

entries.farsi: ‏گفت الصدق طمانين طروب / دل نيارامد ز گفتار دروغ
entries.english: He has said, ‘Falsehood is disquiet in hearts; he has said, ‘Truth is a joyous tranquility.’

entries.farsi: ‏آب و روغن هيچ نفروزد فروغ / در حديث راست آرام دل است
entries.english: The heart is not comforted by lying words: water and oil kindle no light.

entries.farsi: ‏راستيها دانه‏ى دام دل است / دل مگر رنجور باشد بد دهان
entries.english: In truthful speech is there comfort for the heart : truths are the bait that entraps the heart.

entries.farsi: ‏كه نداند چاشنى اين و آن / چون شود از رنج و علت دل سليم
entries.english: Sick, surely, and ill-savoured is the heart that knows not the taste of this and that.

entries.farsi: ‏طعم كذب و راست را باشد عليم / حرص آدم چون سوى گندم فزود
entries.english: When the heart becomes whole of pain and disease, it will recognise the flavour of falsehood and truth.

entries.farsi: از دل آدم سليمى را ربود / پس دروغ و عشوه‏ات را گوش كرد
entries.english: When Adam’s greed for the wheat waxed great, it robbed Adam’s heart of health.

entries.farsi: غره گشت و زهر قاتل نوش كرد / كژدم از گندم ندانست آن نفس
entries.english: Then he gave ear to your lies and enticements: he was befooled and drank the killing poison.

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏پرد تمييز از مست هوس / خلق مست آرزويند و هوا
entries.english: At that moment he knew not scorpion from wheat: discernment flies from one that is drunken with vain desire.

entries.farsi: ز آن پذيرايند دستان ترا / هر كه خود را از هوا خود باز كرد
entries.english: The people are drunk with cupidity and desire: hence they are accepting your cheating.

entries.farsi: چشم خود را آشناى راز كرد / قاضيى بنشاندند او مى‏گريست
entries.english: Whoever has rid his nature of vain desire has made his eve familiar with the secret.

sections.title_fa: شكايت قاضى از آفت قضا و جواب گفتن نايب او را
sections.title_en: How a cadi complained of the calamity of the office of cadi, and how his deputy answered him.

entries.farsi: ‏گفت نايب قاضيا گريه ز چيست / اين نه وقت گريه و فرياد تست
entries.english: They installed a cadi, he wept. The deputy said, ‘O cadi, what are you weeping for?

entries.farsi: ‏وقت شادى و مبارك باد تست / گفت اه چون حكم راند بى‏دلى
entries.english: This is not the time for you to weep and lament: it is the time for you to rejoice and receive felicitations.’

entries.farsi: ‏در ميان آن دو عالم جاهلى / آن دو خصم از واقعه‏ى خود واقفند
entries.english: Ah,’ said he, ‘how shall a man without insight pronounce judgment— an ignorant man between two who know?

entries.farsi: قاضى مسكين چه داند ز آن دو بند / جاهل است و غافل است از حالشان
entries.english: Those two adversaries are acquainted with their own case: what should the poor cadi know of those two tangles?

entries.farsi: ‏چون رود در خونشان و مالشان / گفت خصمان عالمند و علتى
entries.english: He is ignorant and unaware of their state: how should he proceed concerning their lives and property?’

entries.farsi: ‏جاهلى تو ليك شمع ملتى / ز انكه تو علت ندارى در ميان
entries.english: He said, ‘ The litigants know and are unsound; you are ignorant, but you are the luminary of the whole body,

entries.farsi: ‏آن فراغت هست نور ديده‏گان / و آن دو عالم را غرضشان كور كرد
entries.english: Because you have no prejudice to interfere, and that freedom is light to the eves;

entries.farsi: علم را علت كژ و ظالم كند / تا تو رشوت نستدى بيننده‏اى
entries.english: While those two who know are blinded by their self-interest: prejudice has put their knowledge into the grave.

entries.farsi: علمشان را علت اندر گور كرد / جهل را بى‏علتى عالم كند
entries.english: An open mind makes ignorance wise; prejudice makes knowledge perverse and iniquitous.

entries.farsi: ‏چون طمع كردى ضرير و بنده‏اى / از هوا من خوى را واكرده‏ام
entries.english: So long as you accept no bribe, you are seeing; when you act covetously, you are blind and enslaved.

entries.farsi: ‏لقمه‏هاى شهوتى كم خورده‏ام / چاشنى گير دلم شد با فروغ
entries.english: I have turned my nature away from vain desire: I have not eaten delicious morsels.

entries.farsi: ‏راست را داند حقيقت از دروغ / تو چرا بيدار كردى مر مرا
entries.english: My heart, which tastes, has become bright: it really knows truth from falsehood.

sections.title_fa: به اقرار آوردن معاويه ابليس را
sections.title_en: How Mu’awiya—may God be well-pleased with him!—induced Iblis to confess.

entries.farsi: دشمن بيداريى تو اى دغا / همچو خشخاشى همه خواب آورى
entries.english: Why did you awaken me? You are the enemy of wakefulness, O trickster.

entries.farsi: ‏همچو خمرى عقل و دانش را برى / چار ميخت كرده‏ام هين راست گو
entries.english: You are like poppy-seeds: you put every one to sleep. You are like wine: you take away understanding and knowledge.

entries.farsi: راست را دانم تو حيلتها مجو / من ز هر كس آن طمع دارم كه او
entries.english: I have impaled you. Come, tell the truth. I know what is true: do not seek evasions.

entries.farsi: صاحب آن باشد اندر طبع و خو / من ز سركه مى‏نجويم شكرى
entries.english: I expect from every person that of which by nature and disposition he is the owner.

entries.farsi: ‏مر مخنث را نگيرم لشكرى / همچو گبران من نجويم از بتى
entries.english: I do not look for any sugar from vinegar; I do not take the catamite for a soldier.

entries.farsi: ‏كاو بود حق يا خود از حق آيتى / من ز سرگين مى‏نجويم بوى مشك
entries.english: I do not, like infidels, seek from an idol that it should be God or even a sign from God.

entries.farsi: ‏من در آب جو نجويم خشت خشك / من ز شيطان اين نجويم كاوست غير
entries.english: I do not seek the smell of musk from dung; I do not seek dry bricks in river-water.

entries.farsi: كه مرا بيدار گرداند به خير / گفت بسيار آن بليس از مكر و غدر
entries.english: From Satan, who is other, I do not look for this— that he should awaken me with good.”

sections.title_fa: ‏راست گفتن ابليس ضمير خود را به معاويه
sections.title_en: How Iblis told truly his hidden thought to Mu’awiya— may God be well-pleased with him!

entries.farsi: مير از او نشنيد كرد استيز و صبر / از بن دندان بگفتش بهر آن
entries.english: Iblis spoke many words of deceit and treachery, the Amir hearkened not to him and strove and showed fortitude.

entries.farsi: ‏كردمت بيدار مى‏دان اى فالن / تا رسى اندر جماعت در نماز
entries.english: With the bitterest pangs’ he said: “O such-and-such, know that I awakened you for the purpose

entries.farsi: از پى پيغمبر دولت فراز / گر نماز از وقت رفتى مر ترا
entries.english: That you might join the congregation in praying after the Prophet of high estate

entries.farsi: اين جهان تاريك گشتى بى‏ضيا / از غبين و درد رفتى اشكها
entries.english: If the time of prayers had passed, this world would have become dark to you and without a gleam of light;

entries.farsi: از دو چشم تو مثال مشكها / ذوق دارد هر كسى در طاعتى
entries.english: From disappointment and grief tears would have flowed from your two eyes in the fashion of water-skins,

entries.farsi: ‏الجرم نشكيبد از وى ساعتى / آن غبين و درد بودى صد نماز
entries.english: Every one has delight in some act of devotion and consequently cannot bear to miss it for a short while.
That disappointment and grief would have been a hundred prayers: what is prayer in comparison with the glow of humble supplication?”

sections.title_fa: ‏فضيلت حسرت خوردن آن مخلص بر فوت نماز جماعت
sections.title_en: The excellence of the remorse felt by one who was sincere for having missed the congregational prayers

entries.farsi: آن يكى مى‏رفت در مسجد درون / مردم از مسجد همى‏آمد برون
entries.english: A certain man was going into the mosque the people were coming out of the mosque.

entries.farsi: گفت پرسان كه جماعت را چه بود / كه ز مسجد مى‏برون آيند زود
entries.english: He began to ask, saying, “What ails the congregation that they are coming out of the mosque soon?”

entries.farsi: آن يكى گفتش كه پيغمبر نماز / با جماعت كرد و فارغ شد ز راز
entries.english: That person said to him, “The Prophet has prayed with the congregation and finished communion.

entries.farsi: تو كجا در مى‏روى اى مرد خام / ‏چون كه پيغمبر بداده ست السلام
entries.english: How art you going in, O foolish man, when the Prophet has given the blessing?”

entries.farsi: گفت آه و دود از آن اه شد برون / ‏آه او مى‏داد از دل بوى خون
entries.english: He cried, “Ah!” and smoke issued from that sigh: his sigh was giving forth the smell of blood from his heart.

entries.farsi: آن يكى از جمع گفت اين آه را / تو به من ده و آن نماز من ترا
entries.english: One of the congregation said, “Give me this sigh, and my prayers are yours.”

entries.farsi: گفت دادم آه و پذرفتم نماز / او ستد آن آه را با صد نياز
entries.english: He answered, “I give the sigh and accept the prayers.” He took that sigh with a hundred yearnings.

entries.farsi: شب به خواب اندر بگفتش هاتفى / كه خريدى آب حيوان و شفا
entries.english: At night, whilst asleep, a Voice said to him, “You have bought the Water of Life and salvation.

entries.farsi: حرمت اين اختيار و اين دخول / ‏شد نماز جمله‏ى خلقان قبول
entries.english: In honour of this choice and this appropriation the prayers of all the people have been accepted.”

sections.title_fa: تتمه‏ى اقرار ابليس به معاويه مكر خود را
sections.title_en: Conclusion of the confession made by Iblis to Mu’awiya of his deceit

entries.farsi: پس عزازيلش به گفت اى مير راد / مكر خود اندر ميان بايد نهاد
entries.english: Then `Azazil said to him, “O noble Amir, I must lay my deceit before.

entries.farsi: گر نمازت فوت مى‏شد آن زمان / مى‏زدى از درد دل آه و فغان
entries.english: If you had missed the prayers, you would then from heartache have uttered sighs and lamentations,

entries.farsi: آن تاسف و آن فغان و آن نياز / در گذشتى از دو صد ذكر و نماز
entries.english: And that regret and that lamentation and that yearning would have exceeded two hundred litanies and prayers.

entries.farsi: من ترا بيدار كردم از نهيب / ‏تا بسوزاند چنان آهى حجاب
entries.english: I awakened you in fear lest such a sigh might burn the veil,

entries.farsi: تا چنان آهى نباشد مر ترا / تا بدان راهى نباشد مر ترا
entries.english: In order that such a sigh should not be yours; in order that you should not have any way to it.

entries.farsi: من حسودم از حسد كردم چنين / ‏من عدويم كار من مكر است و كين
entries.english: I am envious: from envy I acted thus. I am the enemy: my work is deceit and malice.”

entries.farsi: گفت اكنون راست گفتى صادقى / از تو اين آيد تو اين را اليقى
entries.english: He said, “Now you have told the truth, you are veracious. This comes from you: to this you are adapted.

entries.farsi: عنكبوتى تو مگس دارى شكار / من نيم اى سگ مگس زحمت ميار
entries.english: You are a spider, you have flies as your prey; O cur, I am not a fly, do not worry.

entries.farsi: باز اسپيدم شكارم شه كند / عنكبوتى كى بگرد ما تند
entries.english: I am a white falcon: the King hunts me. How should a spider weave his web about me?

entries.farsi: رو مگس مى‏گير تا تانى هال / سوى دوغى زن مگسها را صال
entries.english: Go now; continue to catch flies as far as you can: invite the flies to some buttermilk;

entries.farsi: ور بخوانى تو به سوى انگبين / ‏هم دروغ و دوغ باشد آن يقين
entries.english: And if you call to honey, that too will certainly be lies and buttermilk.

entries.farsi: تو مرا بيدار كردى خواب بود / تو نمودى كشتى آن گرداب بود
entries.english: You awakened me, it was slumber : you showed a ship, that was a whirlpool.

entries.farsi: تو مرا در خير ز آن مى‏خواندى / ‏تا مرا از خير بهتر راندى
entries.english: You were calling me to good for the purpose that you might drive me away from the better good.

sections.title_fa: فوت شدن دزد به آواز دادن آن شخص صاحب خانه را كه نزديك آمده بود كه دزد را دريابد و بگيرد
sections.title_en: How a thief escaped because some one gave the alarm to the master of the house,
who had nearly overtaken and caught the thief.

entries.farsi: اين بدان ماند كه شخصى دزد ديد / در وثاق اندر پى او مى‏دويد
entries.english: This is like that, how a certain man saw a thief in the house and ran after him.

entries.farsi: تا دو سه ميدان دويد اندر پيش / ‏تا در افکند آن تعب اندر خويش
entries.english: He ran after him two or three fields, till the fatigue threw him into a sweat.

entries.farsi: اندر آن حمله كه نزديك آمدش / ‏تا بدو اندر جهد دريابدش
entries.english: At the moment when, rushing on, he had come so near to him that he might spring upon him and seize him,

entries.farsi: دزد ديگر بانگ كردش كه بيا / تا ببينى اين عالمات بال
entries.english: The second thief cried out to him, “Come, that you may see these signs of calamity.

entries.farsi: زود باش و باز گرد اى مرد كار / تا ببينى حال اينجا زار زار
entries.english: Be quick and turn back, O man of action, that you may see very pitiable the state of things here.”

entries.farsi: گفت باشد كان طرف دزدى بود / گر نگردم زود اين بر من رود
entries.english: He said, “Maybe a thief is yonder: if I do not return at once, this will befall me.

entries.farsi: در زن و فرزند من دستى زند / بستن اين دزد سودم كى كند
entries.english: He may lay hands upon my wife and child, how would it profit me to bind this thief?

sections.title_fa: ‏فضيلت حسرت خوردن آن مخلص بر فوت نماز جماعت
sections.title_en: The excellence of the remorse felt by one who was sincere for having missed the congregational prayers

entries.farsi: آن يكى مى‏رفت در مسجد درون / مردم از مسجد همى‏آمد برون
entries.english: A certain man was going into the mosque the people were coming out of the mosque.

entries.farsi: گفت پرسان كه جماعت را چه بود / كه ز مسجد مى‏برون آيند زود
entries.english: He began to ask, saying, “What ails the congregation that they are coming out of the mosque soon?”

entries.farsi: آن يكى گفتش كه پيغمبر نماز / با جماعت كرد و فارغ شد ز راز
entries.english: That person said to him, “The Prophet has prayed with the congregation and finished communion.

entries.farsi: تو كجا در مى‏روى اى مرد خام / ‏چون كه پيغمبر بداده ست السلام
entries.english: How art you going in, O foolish man, when the Prophet has given the blessing?”

entries.farsi: گفت آه و دود از آن اه شد برون / ‏آه او مى‏داد از دل بوى خون
entries.english: He cried, “Ah!” and smoke issued from that sigh: his sigh was giving forth the smell of blood from his heart.

entries.farsi: آن يكى از جمع گفت اين آه را / تو به من ده و آن نماز من ترا
entries.english: One of the congregation said, “Give me this sigh, and my prayers are yours.”

entries.farsi: گفت دادم آه و پذرفتم نماز / او ستد آن آه را با صد نياز
entries.english: He answered, “I give the sigh and accept the prayers.” He took that sigh with a hundred yearnings.

entries.farsi: شب به خواب اندر بگفتش هاتفى / كه خريدى آب حيوان و شفا
entries.english: At night, whilst asleep, a Voice said to him, “You have bought the Water of Life and salvation.

entries.farsi: حرمت اين اختيار و اين دخول / ‏شد نماز جمله‏ى خلقان قبول
entries.english: In honour of this choice and this appropriation the prayers of all the people have been accepted.”

sections.title_fa: تتمه‏ى اقرار ابليس به معاويه مكر خود را
sections.title_en: Conclusion of the confession made by Iblis to Mu’awiya of his deceit

entries.farsi: پس عزازيلش به گفت اى مير راد / مكر خود اندر ميان بايد نهاد
entries.english: Then `Azazil said to him, “O noble Amir, I must lay my deceit before.

entries.farsi: گر نمازت فوت مى‏شد آن زمان / مى‏زدى از درد دل آه و فغان
entries.english: If you had missed the prayers, you would then from heartache have uttered sighs and lamentations,

entries.farsi: آن تاسف و آن فغان و آن نياز / در گذشتى از دو صد ذكر و نماز
entries.english: And that regret and that lamentation and that yearning would have exceeded two hundred litanies and prayers.

entries.farsi: من ترا بيدار كردم از نهيب / ‏تا بسوزاند چنان آهى حجاب
entries.english: I awakened you in fear lest such a sigh might burn the veil,

entries.farsi: تا چنان آهى نباشد مر ترا / تا بدان راهى نباشد مر ترا
entries.english: In order that such a sigh should not be yours; in order that you should not have any way to it.

entries.farsi: من حسودم از حسد كردم چنين / ‏من عدويم كار من مكر است و كين
entries.english: I am envious: from envy I acted thus. I am the enemy: my work is deceit and malice.”

entries.farsi: گفت اكنون راست گفتى صادقى / از تو اين آيد تو اين را اليقى
entries.english: He said, “Now you have told the truth, you are veracious. This comes from you: to this you are adapted.

entries.farsi: عنكبوتى تو مگس دارى شكار / من نيم اى سگ مگس زحمت ميار
entries.english: You are a spider, you have flies as your prey; O cur, I am not a fly, do not worry.

entries.farsi: باز اسپيدم شكارم شه كند / عنكبوتى كى بگرد ما تند
entries.english: I am a white falcon: the King hunts me. How should a spider weave his web about me?

entries.farsi: رو مگس مى‏گير تا تانى هال / سوى دوغى زن مگسها را صال
entries.english: Go now; continue to catch flies as far as you can: invite the flies to some buttermilk;

entries.farsi: ور بخوانى تو به سوى انگبين / ‏هم دروغ و دوغ باشد آن يقين
entries.english: And if you call to honey, that too will certainly be lies and buttermilk.

entries.farsi: تو مرا بيدار كردى خواب بود / تو نمودى كشتى آن گرداب بود
entries.english: You awakened me, it was slumber : you showed a ship, that was a whirlpool.

entries.farsi: تو مرا در خير ز آن مى‏خواندى / ‏تا مرا از خير بهتر راندى
entries.english: You were calling me to good for the purpose that you might drive me away from the better good.

sections.title_fa: فوت شدن دزد به آواز دادن آن شخص صاحب خانه را كه نزديك آمده بود كه دزد را دريابد و بگيرد
sections.title_en: How a thief escaped because some one gave the alarm to the master of the house,
who had nearly overtaken and caught the thief.

entries.farsi: اين بدان ماند كه شخصى دزد ديد / در وثاق اندر پى او مى‏دويد
entries.english: This is like that, how a certain man saw a thief in the house and ran after him.

entries.farsi: تا دو سه ميدان دويد اندر پيش / ‏تا در افکند آن تعب اندر خويش
entries.english: He ran after him two or three fields, till the fatigue threw him into a sweat.

entries.farsi: اندر آن حمله كه نزديك آمدش / ‏تا بدو اندر جهد دريابدش
entries.english: At the moment when, rushing on, he had come so near to him that he might spring upon him and seize him,

entries.farsi: دزد ديگر بانگ كردش كه بيا / تا ببينى اين عالمات بال
entries.english: The second thief cried out to him, “Come, that you may see these signs of calamity.

entries.farsi: زود باش و باز گرد اى مرد كار / تا ببينى حال اينجا زار زار
entries.english: Be quick and turn back, O man of action, that you may see very pitiable the state of things here.”

entries.farsi: گفت باشد كان طرف دزدى بود / گر نگردم زود اين بر من رود
entries.english: He said, “Maybe a thief is yonder: if I do not return at once, this will befall me.

entries.farsi: در زن و فرزند من دستى زند / بستن اين دزد سودم كى كند
entries.english: He may lay hands upon my wife and child, how would it profit me to bind this thief?

entries.farsi: مسجد روز گل است و روز ابر / مسجد روز ضرورت وقت فقر
entries.english: It is a mosque for muddy and cloudy days, a mosque for days of sore distress in times of poverty,

entries.farsi: تا غريبى يابد آن جا خير و جا / تا فراوان گردد اين خدمت‏سرا
entries.english: That a stranger may get charity and room there, and that this house of service may be frequented,

entries.farsi: تا شعار دين شود بسيار و پر / ز انكه با ياران شود خوش كار مر
entries.english: So that the rites of the Religion may be multiplied and abound; because a bitter plight is sweetened with friends.

entries.farsi: ساعتى آن جايگه تشريف ده / ‏تزكيه‏ى ما كن ز ما تعريف ده
entries.english: Honour that place (by your presence) for a short while: declare us to be sincere, and give a good account of us

entries.farsi: مسجد و اصحاب مسجد را نواز / تو مهى ما شب دمى با ما بساز
entries.english: Show favour to the mosque and its founders. You art the moon, we are the night: comply with us for a moment,

entries.farsi: تا شود شب از جمالت همچو روز / اى جمالت آفتاب جان فروز
entries.english: In order that by your beauty night may be made like day, O you whose beauty is a night-illumining sun.”

entries.farsi: اى دريغا كان سخن از دل بدى / ‏تا مراد آن نفر حاصل شدى
entries.english: Alas! Would that those words had been from the heart, so that the desire of those folk might have been accomplished!

entries.farsi: لطف كايد بى‏دل و جان در زبان / ‏همچو سبزه‏ى تون بود اى دوستان
entries.english: Courtesy that comes to the tongue without heart and soul is like herbs on the ash-heap, O friends.

entries.farsi: هم ز دورش بنگر و اندر گذر / خوردن و بو را نشايد اى پسر
entries.english: Look at them from afar and pass on: they are not fit for eating or smelling, O son.

entries.farsi: سوى لطف بى‏وفايان هين مرو / كان پل ويران بود نيكو شنو
entries.english: Do not, indeed, go towards the courtesy of the faithless, for it is a ruined bridge: heed well.

entries.farsi: هر كجا لشكر شكسته مى‏شود / او دو سه سست مخنث مى‏بود
entries.english: If a fool set foot on it, the bridge will break, and will shatter that foot of his.

entries.farsi: گر قدم را جاهلى بر وى زند / بشكند پل و آن قدم را بشكند
entries.english: Wherever an army is routed, it is because of two or three effeminate weaklings.

entries.farsi: در صف آيد با سالح او مردوار / دل بر او بنهند كاينك يار غار
entries.english: He comes armed into the battle-line, like a man: they put their trust in him, saying, “Here’s the Comrade of the Cave.”

entries.farsi: اين دراز است و فراوان مى‏شود / و آن چه مقصود است پنهان مى‏شود
entries.english: He turns his face when he sees wounds: his going breaks your back.

entries.farsi: رو بگرداند چو بيند زخمها / رفتن او بشكند پشت ترا
entries.english: This is long and is expanding, and that which is aimed at is becoming hidden.

sections.title_fa: فريفتن منافقان پيغامبر را تا به مسجد ضرارش برند
sections.title_en: How the Hypocrites cajoled the Prophet—God bless and save him!— that they might take him to the Mosque of Opposition.

entries.farsi: بر رسول حق فسون‏ها خواندند / رخش دستان و حيل مى‏راندند
entries.english: They chanted spells over the Messenger of God: they were driving the steed of cunning and craft.

entries.farsi: آن رسول مهربان رحم كيش / ‏جز تبسم جز بلى ناورد پيش
entries.english: The kind and compassionate Messenger proffered nothing but smiles, nothing but “Yes.”

entries.farsi: شكرهاى آن جماعت ياد كرد / در اجابت قاصدان را شاد كرد
entries.english: He expressed thanks: he gladdened the envoys in assent.

entries.farsi: مى‏نمود آن مكر ايشان پيش او / يك به يك ز آن سان كه اندر شير مو
entries.english: Their deceit was apparent to him, point by point, in the same way as hairs in milk.

entries.farsi: موى را ناديده مى‏كرد آن لطيف / ‏شير را شاباش مى‏گفت آن ظريف
entries.english: That courteous one feigned not to see the hairs: that polite one said “Bravo!” to the milk.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران موى مكر و دمدمه / ‏چشم خوابانيد آن دم ز ان همه
entries.english: Myriad hairs of deceit and fraud, and at that time he closed his eyes to it all.

entries.farsi: راست مى‏فرمود آن بحر كرم / ‏بر شما من از شما مشفق‏ترم
entries.english: That ocean of bounty spoke truly, “I am kinder to you than you.

entries.farsi: من نشسته بر كنار آتشى / ‏با فروغ و شعله‏ى بس ناخوشى
entries.english: I am seated at the edge of a fire with an exceedingly unpleasant blaze and flame;

entries.farsi: همچو پروانه شما آن سو دوان / ‏هر دو دست من شده پروانه ران
entries.english: Ye are hastening towards it like moths; both my hands have become moth-flaps.”

entries.farsi: چون بر آن شد تا روان گردد رسول / ‏غيرت حق بانگ زد مشنو ز غول
entries.english: When the Prophet had resolved to set out, the jealousy of God cried, “do not listen to the ghoul’!

entries.farsi: قصد ايشان جز سيه رويى نبود / خير دين كى جست ترسا و جهود
entries.english: For these wicked men have used deceit and cunning: what they have put forward is entirely the reverse

entries.farsi: كاين خبيثان مكر و حيلت كرده‏اند / جمله مقلوب است آنچ آورده‏اند
entries.english: Their intent was nothing but black shame: when have Christians or Jews sought the welfare of the Religion?

entries.farsi: مسجدى بر جسر دوزخ ساختند / با خدا نرد دغاها باختند
entries.english: They have built a mosque upon Hell’s bridge: they have played the game of trickery with God.

entries.farsi: قصدشان تفريق اصحاب رسول / فضل حق را كى شناسد هر فضول
entries.english: Their aim is to cause disunion amongst the Companions of the Prophet: how should any vain fool understand the grace of God?

entries.farsi: تا جهودى را ز شام اينجا كشند / كه به وعظ او جهودان سر خوشند
entries.english: In order that they may fetch hither a Jew from Syria, with whose preaching the Jews are intoxicated.”

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه آرى ليك ما / بر سر راهيم و بر عزم غزا
entries.english: The Prophet said, “Yes, but we are intending to march and starting on a campaign.

entries.farsi: زين سفر چون باز گردم آن گهان / ‏سوى آن مسجد روان گردم روان
entries.english: As soon as I return from this expedition, I will then set out to that Mosque at once.”

entries.farsi: دفعشان كرد و به سوى غزو تاخت / ‏با دغايان از دغا نردى بباخت
entries.english: He put them off and hurried to the field of war: he played a game of trickery with the tricksters.

entries.farsi: چون بيامد از غزا باز آمدند / چنگ اندر وعده‏ى ماضى زدند
entries.english: When he came back from the campaign, they returned and sought that past promise.

entries.farsi: گفت حقش اى پيمبر فاش گو / عذر را ور جنگ باشد باش گو
entries.english: God said to him, “O Prophet, proclaim the treachery, and if war be, say, `Let it be!’”

entries.farsi: گفت اى قوم دغل خامش كنيد / تا نگويم رازهاتان تن زنيد
entries.english: He said, “ O false people, be silent! Hush, lest I tell your secret thoughts.”

entries.farsi: چون نشانى چند از اسرارشان / ‏در بيان آورد بد شد كارشان
entries.english: When he had declared a few indications of their inmost thoughts, they were in evil plight’.

entries.farsi: قاصدان زو باز گشتند آن زمان / ‏حاش هلل حاش هلل دم زنان
entries.english: Thereupon the envoys turned back from him crying, “God ward off! God avert!”

entries.farsi: هر منافق مصحفى زير بغل / سوى پيغمبر بياورد از دغل
entries.english: Every Hypocrite, by way of fraud, brought a Qur’an under his arm to the Prophet,

entries.farsi: بهر سوگندان كه ايمان جنتى است / ‏ز انكه سوگندان كژان را سنتى است
entries.english: In order to take oaths—for oaths are a shield; because oaths is a custom followed by the wicked.

entries.farsi: چون ندارد مرد كژ در دين وفا / هر زمانى بشكند سوگند را
entries.english: Since the wicked man does not keep faith in religion, he will break oath at any time.

entries.farsi: راستان را حاجت سوگند نيست / ‏ز انكه ايشان را دو چشم روشنى است
entries.english: The righteous have no need of oath, because they have two clear eyes.

entries.farsi: نقض ميثاق و عهود از احمقى است / ‏حفظ ايمان و وفا كار تقى است
entries.english: Breach of compacts and covenants is of stupidity; keeping of oaths and faithfulness is the practice of him that fears God.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه سوگند شما / راست گيرم يا كه سوگند خدا
entries.english: Said the Prophet, “Shall I take your oaths as true, or the oath of God?”

entries.farsi: باز سوگند دگر خوردند قوم / ‏مصحف اندر دست و بر لب مهر صوم
entries.english: Again those people, the Qur’an in their hands and the seal of fasting on their lips, swore another oath,

entries.farsi: كه به حق اين كالم پاك راست / ‏كان بناى مسجد از بهر خداست
entries.english: Saying, “By the truth of this holy and true Word that the building of the Mosque is for God’s sake.

entries.farsi: اندر آن جا هيچ مكر و حيله نيست / ‏اندر آن جا ذكر و صدق و يا ربى است
entries.english: In that place there is no contrivance of deceit: in that place there is commemoration and sincerity and calling unto the Lord.”

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه آواز خدا / مى‏رسد در گوش من همچون صدا
entries.english: The Prophet answered, “The voice of God is coming into my ear like an echo.

entries.farsi: مهر در گوش شما بنهاد حق / ‏تا به آواز خدا نارد سبق
entries.english: God hath put a seal upon your ears, so that they make not haste to the voice of God.

entries.farsi: نك صريح آواز حق مى‏آيدم / ‏همچو صاف از درد مى‏پالايدم
entries.english: Lo, the voice of God is coming to me distinctly: it is filtered clear for me, like the pure from the dregs “

entries.farsi: همچنان كه موسى از سوى درخت / ‏بانگ حق بشنيد كاى مسعود بخت
entries.english: Even as Moses from the direction of the Bush heard the voice of God saying, “O you of blessed fortune!”

entries.farsi: از درخت إِنِّي أَنَا اللهَُّ مى‏شنيد / با كالم انوار مى‏آمد پديد
entries.english: From the Bush he was hearing, “Lo, I am Allah,” and together with the words there appeared lights.

entries.farsi: چون ز نور وحى در مى‏ماندند / باز نو سوگندها مى‏خواندند
entries.english: Inasmuch as they were left in the lurch by the light of inspiration, they once more began to recite oaths anew.

entries.farsi: چون خدا سوگند را خواند سپر / كى نهد اسپر ز كف پيكارگر
entries.english: Since God calls an oath a shield, how should the quarrelling one lay down the shield from his hand?

entries.farsi: باز پيغمبر به تكذيب صريح / ‏قد كذبتم گفت با ايشان فصيح
entries.english: Again the Prophet, giving the lie direct, said to them in plain terms, “You have lied.”

sections.title_fa: انديشيدن يكى از صحابه به انكار كه رسول (ص) چرا ستارى نمى‏كند
sections.title_en: How one of the Companions—may God be well-pleased with them!—thought disapprovingly, “ Why does not the Prophet—God bless and save him!—throw a veil?”

entries.farsi: تا يكى يارى ز ياران رسول / ‏در دلش انكار آمد ز آن نكول
entries.english: So that one of the Prophet’s Companions conceived in his heart dislike of that dislike,

entries.farsi: كه چنين پيران با شيب و وقار / مى‏كندشان اين پيمبر شرمسار
entries.english: Thinking, “ Grey-haired and venerable old men like these— this Prophet is putting them to shame.

entries.farsi: كو كرم كو ستر پوشى كو حيا / صد هزاران عيب پوشند انبيا
entries.english: Where is generosity? Where cloaking? Where modesty? The prophets cover up hundreds of thousands of faults.”

entries.farsi: باز در دل زود استغفار كرد / تا نگردد ز اعتراض او روى زرد
entries.english: Quickly, again, in his heart he asked pardon of God, lest he should be disgraced for objecting.

entries.farsi: شومى يارى اصحاب نفاق / ‏كرد مومن را چو ايشان زشت و عاق
entries.english: The turpitude of befriending the Hypocrites made the true believer wicked and rebellious like them.

entries.farsi: باز مى‏زاريد كاى عالم سر / مر مرا مگذار بر كفران مصر
entries.english: Again he was crying in supplication, “O You who knows the inmost consciousness, do not leave me persisting in unbelief !

entries.farsi: دل به دستم نيست همچون ديد چشم / ‏ور نه دل را سوزمى اين دم به خشم
entries.english: My heart is not in my control, as eyesight; else I would at this moment burn my heart in anger.”

entries.farsi: اندر اين انديشه خوابش در ربود / مسجد ايشانش پر سرگين نمود
entries.english: In this thought, slumber seized him. To him, their mosque seemed full of dung:

entries.farsi: سنگهاش اندر حدث جاى تباه / ‏مى‏دميد از سنگها دود سياه
entries.english: A corrupted place, its stones in filth: from the stones floated up black smoke.

entries.farsi: دود در حلقش شد و حلقش بخست / ‏از نهيب دود تلخ از خواب جست
entries.english: The smoke went into his throat and made it smart: terrified by the bitter smoke, he sprang from sleep.

entries.farsi: در زمان در رو فتاد و مى‏گريست / ‏كاى خدا اينها نشان منكرى است
entries.english: Forthwith he fell on his face and wept, saying, “O God, these things are the sign of disbelief.

entries.farsi: خلم بهتر از چنين حلم اى خدا / كه كند از نور ايمانم جدا
entries.english: Wrath s better, O God, than such forbearance, which separates me from the light of Faith.”

entries.farsi: گر بكاوى كوشش اهل مجاز / تو به تو گنده بود همچون پياز
entries.english: If you scrutinise the labour of them that follow falsehood, it is stinking, coat upon coat, like an onion—

entries.farsi: هر يكى از يكديگر بى‏مغزتر / صادقان را يك ز ديگر نغزتر
entries.english: Every one weaker than another, in the case of the sincere, is more excellent than the other.

entries.farsi: صد كمر آن قوم بسته بر قبا / بهر هدم مسجد اهل قبا
entries.english: Those folk tied a hundred belts on their mantles in order to destroy the Mosque of the people of Quba

entries.farsi: همچو آن اصحاب فيل اندر حبش / ‏كعبه‏اى كردند حق آتش زدش
entries.english: Even as the Lords of the Elephant in Abyssinia made a Ka`ba, God set it afire;

entries.farsi: قصد كعبه ساختند از انتقام / ‏حالشان چون شد فرو خوان از كالم
entries.english: They made an attempt on the Ka`ba in revenge: read from the Word how they fared!

entries.farsi: مر سيه رويان دين را خود جهيز / نيست الا حيلت و مكر و ستيز
entries.english: The reprobates of the Religion have indeed no equipment but cunning and deceit and contentiousness.

entries.farsi: هر صحابى ديد ز آن مسجد عيان / ‏واقعه تا شد يقينشان سر آن
entries.english: Every Companion saw plainly some vision of that Mosque, so that the secret of it became to them certain knowledge.

entries.farsi: واقعات ار باز گويم يك به يك / ‏پس يقين گردد صفا بر اهل شك
entries.english: If I should relate the visions, one by one, then the purity would become certain to them that doubt;

entries.farsi: حكمت قرآن چو ضاله‏ى مومن است / ‏هر كسى در ضاله‏ى خود موقن است
entries.english: But I am afraid of revealing their mystery: they are the disdainful loved ones, and disdain becomes them.

entries.farsi: شرع بى‏تقليد مى‏پذرفته‏اند / بى‏محك آن نقد را بگرفته‏اند
entries.english: They have received the Law without mechanical imitation: have taken that coin without the touchstone.

entries.farsi: ليك مى‏ترسم ز كشف رازشان / ‏نازنينانند و زيبد نازشان
entries.english: The Wisdom of the Qur’an is like the true believer’s stray camel: every one has certain knowledge of his own stray.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى آن شخص كه اشتر ضاله‏ى خود مى‏جست و مى‏پرسيد
sections.title_en: Story of the person who was seeking after his stray camel and inquiring about it

entries.farsi: اشترى گم كردى و جستيش چست / چون بيابى چون ندانى كان تست
entries.english: You have lost a camel and sought it busily, how should you not know, when you find it, that it is yours?

entries.farsi: ضاله چه بود ناقه‏اى گم كرده‏اى / ‏از كفت بگريخته در پرده‏اى
entries.english: What is the stray? You have lost a she-camel: fled from your herd into a veil.

entries.farsi: آمده در بار كردن كاروان / ‏اشتر تو ز آن ميان گشته نهان
entries.english: The caravan drivers have begun to load; your camel is lost from the midst.

entries.farsi: مى‏دوى اين سو و آن سو خشك لب / ‏كاروان شد دور و نزديك است شب
entries.english: You are running to and fro with parched lips; the caravan is far away, and night is near.

entries.farsi: رخت مانده بر زمين در راه خوف / ‏تو پى اشتر دوان گشته به طوف
entries.english: Your baggage is left on the ground, on the road ofperil, you are running about in search of the camel,

entries.farsi: كاى مسلمانان كه ديده ست اشترى / ‏جسته بيرون بامداد از آخورى
entries.english: Crying, “O Moslems, who has seen a camel which this morning escaped from a stable?

entries.farsi: باز مى‏جويى نشان از هر كسى / ‏ريش‏خندت مى‏كند زين هر خسى
entries.english: Whoever will tell a clue to my camel, I will give so many dirhems as a reward.”

entries.farsi: هر كه بر گويد نشان از اشترم / ‏مژدگانى مى‏دهم چندين درم
entries.english: You are requesting clues from every one: every rascal is making a mock of you on this account,

entries.farsi: كاشترى ديديم مى‏رفت اين طرف / ‏اشتر سرخى به سوى آن علف
entries.english: Saying, “We saw a camel going in this direction, a reddish camel towards yonder pasturage.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى گويد بريده گوش بود / و آن دگر گويد جلش منقوش بود
entries.english: One says, “It was crop-eared,” and another says, “Its saddle-cloth was embroidered.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى گويد شتر يك چشم بود / و آن دگر گويد ز گر بى‏پشم بود
entries.english: One says, “The camel had one eye,” and another says, “It from mange had no hair.”

entries.farsi: از براى مژدگانى صد نشان / ‏از گزافه هر خسى كرده بيان
entries.english: For the sake of the reward every rascal, at random, sets forth a hundred clues.

sections.title_fa: متردد شدن در ميان مذهبهاى مخالف و بيرون شو و مخلص يافتن
sections.title_en: On being perplexed amidst discordant doctrines and finding escape and deliverance

entries.farsi: همچنان كه هر كسى در معرفت / ‏مى‏كند موصوف غيبى را صفت
entries.english: Even as in the matter of knowledge every one describes the Unseen Object of description.

entries.farsi: فلسفى از نوع ديگر كرده شرح / ‏باحثى مر گفت او را كرده جرح
entries.english: The philosopher gives an explanation of another kind; a scholastic theologian invalidates his statement;

entries.farsi: و آن دگر در هر دو طعنه مى‏زند / و آن دگر از زرق جانى مى‏كند
entries.english: And some one else jeers at both of them, while another hypocritically tires himself to death

entries.farsi: هر يك از ره اين نشانها ز آن دهند / تا گمان آيد كه ايشان ز آن ده‏اند
entries.english: Each one gives these indications of the Way, in order that it may be supposed that they belong to that Village.

entries.farsi: اين حقيقت دان نه حق‏اند اين همه / ‏نى بكلى گمرهانند اين رمه
entries.english: Know the truth to be this, all these are not in the right; nor are this herd entirely astray,

entries.farsi: ز انكه بى‏حق باطلى نايد پديد / قلب را ابله به بوى زر خريد
entries.english: Because nothing false is shown without the True: the fool bought spurious coin in the hope of gold.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى در جهان نقدى روان / ‏قلبها را خرج كردن كى توان
entries.english: If there were no current coin in the world, how would it be possible to issue false coins?

entries.farsi: تا نباشد راست كى باشد دروغ / ‏آن دروغ از راست مى‏گيرد فروغ
entries.english: Unless there be truth, how should there be falsehood? That falsehood receives brilliance from truth.

entries.farsi: بر اميد راست كژ را مى‏خرند / زهر در قندى رود آن گه خورند
