entries.english: They buy the wrong in hope of the right: poison goes into a piece of sugar, then they eat.

entries.farsi: گر نباشد گندم محبوب نوش / ‏چه برد گندم‏نماى جو فروش
entries.english: If there be no savoury wheat, what shall he get who sells barley, pretending that it is wheat?

entries.farsi: پس مگو كاين جمله دمها باطلند / باطالن بر بوى حق دام دلند
entries.english: Do not say, then, that all these utterances are false: the false are a snare to the heart on the ground of hope of truth.

entries.farsi: پس مگو جمله خيال است و ضالل / ‏بى‏حقيقت نيست در عالم خيال
entries.english: Do not say, then, that all is imagination and error: without truth imagination exists not in the world.

entries.farsi: حق شب قدر است در شبها نهان / ‏تا كند جان هر شبى را امتحان
entries.english: Truth is the Night of Power hidden amidst the nights in order that the soul may make trial of every night.

entries.farsi: نه همه شبها بود قدر اى جوان / ‏نه همه شبها بود خالى از آن
entries.english: Not all nights are Power, O youth, nor are all nights void of that.

entries.farsi: در ميان دلق پوشان يك فقير / امتحان كن و آن كه حق است آن بگير
entries.english: Amongst the wearers of the dervish-cloak there is one dervish: make trial, and accept him that is true.

entries.farsi: مومن كيس مميز كو كه تا / ‏باز داند هيزكان را از فتى
entries.english: Where is the sagacious and discerning believer, that he may distinguish effeminate wretches from men?

entries.farsi: پس بود كالا شناسى سخت سهل / ‏چون كه عيبى نيست چه نااهل و اهل
entries.english: If there be no faulty things in the world, all fools would be merchants.

entries.farsi: گر نه معيوبات باشد در جهان / ‏تاجران باشند جمله ابلهان
entries.english: Then it would be very easy to know goods: when there is no defect, what the incompetent and the competent?

entries.farsi: ور همه عيب است دانش سود نيست / ‏چون همه چوب است اينجا عود نيست
entries.english: And if everything is faulty, knowledge is of no advantage: since everything here is wood, aloes-wood is not.

entries.farsi: آن كه گويد جمله حقند احمقى است / ‏و انكه گويد جمله باطل او شقى است
entries.english: He that says, “All are true”—it is folly; and he that says, “All are false”—he is damned.

entries.farsi: تاجران انبيا كردند سود / تاجران رنگ و بو كور و كبود
entries.english: Those who trade with the prophets have gained; those who trade with colour and scent are blind and blue.

entries.farsi: مى‏نمايد مار اندر چشم مال / ‏هر دو چشم خويش را نيكو بمال
entries.english: The snake appears in the eye as riches: rub both your eyes well!

entries.farsi: منگر اندر غبطه‏ى اين بيع و سود / بنگر اندر خسر فرعون و ثمود
entries.english: Do not consider the happiness of this traffic and profit: consider the perdition of Pharaoh and Thamud.

sections.title_fa: امتحان هر چيزى تا ظاهر شود خير و شرى كه در وى است
sections.title_en: On making trial of everything, so that the good and evil which are in it may be brought to view.

entries.farsi: اندر اين گردون مكرر كن نظر / ز انكه حق فرمود ثم ارجع بصر
entries.english: The sky, which is beautiful and glorious— God said, Then turn your gaze again.

entries.farsi: يك نظر قانع مشو زين سقف نور / بارها بنگر ببين هل من فطور
entries.english: As regards this roof of light, be not content with one look: look times, see, Are there any flaws?

entries.farsi: چون كه گفتت كاندر اين سقف نكو / بارها بنگر چو مرد عيب جو
entries.english: Since He has told you to look often at this goodly roof, as a man seeking faults,

entries.farsi: پس زمين تيره را دانى كه چند / ديدن و تمييز بايد در پسند
entries.english: You may know, then, how much seeing and discerning the dark earth needs, to gain approval.

entries.farsi: تا بپالاييم صافان را ز درد / چند بايد عقل ما را رنج برد
entries.english: In order that we may strain the pure from the dregs, how much tribulation must our minds endure!

entries.farsi: امتحانهاى زمستان و خزان / ‏تاب تابستان بهار همچو جان
entries.english: The trials of winter and autumn, the heat of summer, spring like the spirit,

entries.farsi: بادها و ابرها و برقها / تا پديد آرد عوارض فرق‏ها
entries.english: Winds and clouds and lightning— that happenings may bring distinctions into view;

entries.farsi: تا برون آرد زمين خاك رنگ / ‏هر چه اندر جيب دارد لعل و سنگ
entries.english: That dust-coloured earth may bring forth all that it holds in its bosom, ruby or stone.

entries.farsi: هر چه دزديده ست اين خاك دژم / ‏از خزانه‏ى حق و درياى كرم
entries.english: Whatever this dark earth has stolen from the Treasury of God and the Sea of Bounty—

entries.farsi: شحنه‏ى تقدير گويد راست گو / آن چه بردى شرح واده مو به مو
entries.english: Providence, the governor, says, “Tell the truth ! Declare what you have carried off, hair by hair.”

entries.farsi: دزد يعنى خاك گويد هيچ هيچ / ‏شحنه او را در كشد در پيچ پيچ
entries.english: The thief, that is, the earth, says, “Nothing, nothing!” The Governor puts it to the torture.

entries.farsi: شحنه گاهش لطف گويد چون شكر / گه بر آويزد كند هر چه بتر
entries.english: Sometimes the Governor speaks to it with kindness as sugar; sometimes he hangs it up, and does his worst,

entries.farsi: تا ميان قهر و لطف آن خفيه‏ها / ظاهر آيد ز آتش خوف و رجا
entries.english: In order that, between force and favour, those concealed things may be brought to sight through the fire of fear and hope.

entries.farsi: آن بهاران لطف شحنه‏ى كبرياست / ‏و آن خزان تخويف و تهديد خداست
entries.english: Spring is the kindness of the Almighty Governor, and autumn is God’s intimidation and menace,

entries.farsi: و آن زمستان چار ميخ معنوى / ‏تا تو اى دزد خفى ظاهر شوى
entries.english: And winter is the allegorical crucifixion, to the end that you, O hidden thief, may be exposed.

entries.farsi: پس مجاهد را زمانى بسط دل / ‏يك زمانى قبض و درد و غش و غل
entries.english: Then, he that wages the warfare has at one time expansion of heart, at another time oppression and pain and torment,

entries.farsi: ز انكه اين آب و گلى كابدان ماست / ‏منكر و دزد و ضياى جان ماست
entries.english: Because this water and clay, which is our bodies, is the denier and thief of the light of souls

entries.farsi: حق تعالى گرم و سرد و رنج و درد / بر تن ما مى‏نهد اى شير مرد
entries.english: The High God lays upon our body, O man of fortitude, heat and cold and grief and pain,

entries.farsi: خوف و جوع و نقص اموال و بدن / ‏جمله بهر نقد جان ظاهر شدن
entries.english: Fear and hunger and impairment of wealth and body— all for the sake of the soul’s coin being brought into sight.

entries.farsi: اين وعيد و وعده‏ها انگيخته ست / ‏بهر اين نيك و بدى كاميخته ست
entries.english: These threats and promises He has sent forth on account of this good and evil which He has mingled.

entries.farsi: چون كه حق و باطلى آميختند / نقد و قلب اندر حرمدان ريختند
entries.english: Inasmuch as truth and falsehood have been mingled and the good and bad coin have been poured into the travelling-bag,

entries.farsi: پس محك مى‏بايدش بگزيده‏اى / ‏در حقايق امتحانها ديده‏اى
entries.english: Therefore they need a picked touchstone, one that has undergone tests in realities,

entries.farsi: تا شود فاروق اين تزويرها / تا بود دستور اين تدبيرها
entries.english: So that it may become a criterion for these impostures; so that it may be a standard for these acts of providence.

entries.farsi: شير ده اى مادر موسى و را / و اندر آب افكن مينديش از بال
entries.english: Give him milk, O mother of Moses, and cast him into the water: be not afraid of the trial.

entries.farsi: هر كه در روز أَ لَسْ تُ آن شير خورد / همچو موسى شير را تمييز كرد
entries.english: Whoever drank that milk on the Day of Alast distinguishes the milk, even as Moses.

entries.farsi: گر تو بر تمييز طفلت مولعى / اين زمان يا ام موسى ارضعى
entries.english: If you wish fondly for your child’s discrimination, suckle now, O mother of Moses,

entries.farsi: تا ببيند طعم شير مادرش / ‏تا فرو نايد بدايه‏ى بد سرش
entries.english: That he may know the taste of his mother’s milk, and that his head may not sink to a bad nurse.

sections.title_fa: ‏شرح فايده‏ى حكايت آن شخص شتر جوينده
sections.title_en: Explaining the moral of the story of the person seeking camel

entries.farsi: اشترى گم كرده‏اى اى معتمد / هر كسى ز اشتر نشانت مى‏دهد
entries.english: You have lost a camel, O trusty, and every one is giving you a clue to the camel.

entries.farsi: تو نمى‏دانى كه آن اشتر كجاست / ‏ليك دانى كاين نشانيها خطاست
entries.english: You know not where the camel is, but you know that these clues are wrong.

entries.farsi: و انكه اشتر گم نكرد او از مرى / ‏همچو آن گم كرده جويد اشترى
entries.english: And he that has not lost a camel—he in contention seeks a camel, just like him who has lost it,

entries.farsi: كه بلى من هم شتر گم كرده‏ام / ‏هر كه يابد اجرتش آورده‏ام
entries.english: Saying, “Yes; I too have lost a camel: I have brought a reward for any one who may find it.”

entries.farsi: تا در اشتر با تو انبازى كند / بهر طمع اشتر اين بازى كند
entries.english: That he may take a partner’s share with you in the camel: he plays this trick because of coveting the camel.

entries.farsi: هر چه را گويى خطا بود آن نشان / ‏او به تقليد تو مى‏گويد همان
entries.english: If you say to any one,” That clue was false,” he, in imitation of you, says the same.

entries.farsi: او نشان كژ بنشناسد ز راست / ‏ليك گفتت آن مقلد را عصاست
entries.english: He does not know wrong clues from right, but your words are a cue to that imitator.

entries.farsi: چون نشان راست گويند و شبيه / ‏پس يقين گردد ترا ال رَيْبَ فيه
entries.english: When they mention right and likely clues, then comes to you the certainty in which there is no doubt.

entries.farsi: آن شفاى جان رنجورت شود / رنگ روى و صحت و زورت شود
entries.english: That becomes balm to your sick soul; it becomes colour to your face and health and strength to you.

entries.farsi: چشم تو روشن شود پايت دوان / ‏جسم تو جان گردد و جانت روان
entries.english: Your eye becomes bright, your foot nimble; your body becomes soul, and your soul spirit.

entries.farsi: پس بگويى راست گفتى اى امين / ‏اين نشانيها بالغ آمد مبين
entries.english: Then you will say, “ O trusted, you have spoken the truth: these clues are a clear deliverance.

entries.farsi: فِيهِ آياتٌ ثقات بينات / ‏اين براتى باشد و قدر نجات
entries.english: There are signs, sure information, and evidence: this is a title-deed and an ordainment of salvation.”

entries.farsi: اين نشان چون داد گويى پيش رو / وقت آهنگ است پيش آهنگ شو
entries.english: When he has given this clue, you will say, “Go before! It is time for the enterprise: be the leader!

entries.farsi: پى روى تو كنم اى راست گو / بوى بردى ز اشترم بنما كه كو
entries.english: I will follow you, O truth-teller: you have got scent of my camel: show where.”

entries.farsi: پيش آن كس كه نه صاحب اشترى ست / ‏كاو در اين جست شتر بهر مرى ست
entries.english: to that person who is not the owner of a camel, and who is in this quest of the camel for contention’s sake

entries.farsi: زين نشان راست نفزودش يقين / ‏جز ز عكس ناقه جوى راستين
entries.english: His certainty is not increased by this right clue, save through reflection from the true camel-seeker.

entries.farsi: اندر اين اشتر نبودش حق ولى / ‏اشترى گم كرده است او هم بلى
entries.english: From his earnestness and ardour he gets a scent that these wild outcries of his are not babble.

entries.farsi: بوى برد از جد و گرميهاى او / كه گزافه نيست اين هيهاى او
entries.english: He had no just claim to this camel, but he too has lost a camel; yes.

entries.farsi: طمع ناقه‏ى غير رو پوشش شده / ‏آنچ ازو گم شد فراموشش شده
entries.english: Desire for another’s camel has become a veil to him; he has forgotten what he has lost.

entries.farsi: هر كجا او مى‏دود اين مى‏دود / از طمع هم درد صاحب مى‏شود
entries.english: Wherever he runs, this one runs: from greed, he becomes a partner in the owner’s pain.

entries.farsi: كاذبى يا صادقى چون شد روان / آن دروغش راستى شد ناگهان
entries.english: When a liar sets out with a truthful man, his falsehood turns to truth of a sudden.

entries.farsi: اندر آن صحرا كه آن اشتر شتافت / ‏اشتر خود نيز آن ديگر بيافت
entries.english: In the desert whither that camel had hastened, the other one also found his own camel.

entries.farsi: چون بديدش ياد آورد آن خويش / ‏بى‏طمع شد ز اشتر آن يار و خويش
entries.english: As soon as he saw it, he remembered his own, and ceased to covet the camel of that friend and kinsman.

entries.farsi: آن مقلد شد محقق چون بديد / اشتر خود را كه آن جا مى‏چريد
entries.english: That imitator became a true searcher when he saw his camel browsing there.

entries.farsi: او طلب كار شتر آن لحظه گشت / ‏مى‏نجستش تا نديد او را به دشت
entries.english: At that moment did he become a seeker of the camel: he was never seeking it till he saw it in the desert.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن تنها روى آغاز كرد / چشم سوى ناقه‏ى خود باز كرد
entries.english: After that, he began to go alone: he opened his eyes towards his own camel.

entries.farsi: گفت آن صادق مرا بگذاشتى / ‏تا به اكنون پاس من مى‏داشتى
entries.english: The sincere one said, “You have left me, till now you were paying regard to me.”

entries.farsi: گفت تا اكنون فسوسى بوده‏ام / ‏وز طمع در چاپلوسى بوده‏ام
entries.english: He replied, “Until now I have been an idle scoffer and, from greed, have been in flattering;

entries.farsi: اين زمان هم درد تو گشتم كه من / ‏در طلب از تو جدا گشتم به تن
entries.english: Now, when I have become parted from you physically in the search, I have become sympathetic with you.

entries.farsi: از تو مى‏دزديدمى وصف شتر / جان من ديد آن خود شد چشم پر
entries.english: I was stealing the camel’s description from you; my spirit saw its own camel, it had its eye filled.

entries.farsi: تا نيابيدم نبودم طالبش / ‏مس كنون مغلوب شد زر غالبش
entries.english: Till I found it, I was not seeking it; now the copper is overcome, the gold overpowers it.

entries.farsi: سيئاتم شد همه طاعات شكر / هزل شد فانى و جد اثبات شكر
entries.english: My evil deeds have become pious acts entirely—thanks! Jest is vanished and earnest is realised—thanks.

entries.farsi: سيئاتم چون وسيلت شد به حق / ‏پس مزن بر سيئاتم هيچ دق
entries.english: Since my evil deeds have become the means of attaining unto God, do not, then, throw any blame on my evil deeds.

entries.farsi: مر ترا صدق تو طالب كرده بود / مر مرا جد و طلب صدقى گشود
entries.english: Your sincerity made you a seeker; for me, toil and search opened a sincere feeling.

entries.farsi: صدق تو آورد در جستن ترا / جستنم آورد در صدقى مرا
entries.english: Your sincerity led you to seek; my seeking led me to a feeling of sincerity.

entries.farsi: آن نبد بيگار كسبى بود چست / ‏هر يكى دانه كه كشتم صد برست
entries.english: I was sowing the seed of fortune in the earth, I fancied it was labour without wages and hire.

entries.farsi: تخم دولت در زمين مى‏كاشتم / ‏سخره و بيگار مى‏پنداشتم
entries.english: It was not labour without hire; it was an excellent earning: every grain that I sowed, a hundred grew.

entries.farsi: دزد سوى خانه‏اى شد زير دست / ‏چون در آمد ديد كان خانه‏ى خود است
entries.english: The thief went underhand to a certain house: when he entered, he saw that it was his own house.”

entries.farsi: گرم باش اى سرد تا گرمى رسد / با درشتى ساز تا نرمى رسد
entries.english: Be hot, O cold one that heat may come: put up with roughness, that ease may come.

entries.farsi: آن دو اشتر نيست آن يك اشتر است / ‏تنگ آمد لفظ معنى بس پر است
entries.english: That is not two camels; it is a single camel. Verbal expression is confined; the meaning is very full.

entries.farsi: لفظ در معنى هميشه نارسان / ‏ز آن پيمبر گفت قد كل لسان
entries.english: The expression always fails to reach the meaning; hence the Prophet said,” His tongue falters.”

entries.farsi: نطق اصطرالب باشد در حساب / ‏چه قدر داند ز چرخ و آفتاب
entries.english: Speech is an astrolabe in reckoning: how much does it know of the sky and the sun?

entries.farsi: خاصه چرخى كاين فلك زو پره‏اى است / ‏آفتاب از آفتابش ذره‏اى است
entries.english: Especially, of that Sky whereof this heaven is a blade of straw; of whose Sun the sun is a mote?

sections.title_fa: ‏بيان آن كه در هر نفسى فتنه‏ى مسجد ضرار است
sections.title_en: Showing that there is in every soul the mischief of the

entries.farsi: چون پديد آمد كه آن مسجد نبود / خانه‏ى حيلت بد و دام جهود
entries.english: Mosque of Opposition

entries.farsi: پس نبى فرمود كان را بر كنيد / مطرحه‏ى خاشاك و خاكستر كنيد
entries.english: When it appeared that that was not a mosque, was a house of intrigue and a trap laid by the Jews,

entries.farsi: صاحب مسجد چو مسجد قلب بود / دانه‏ها بر دام ريزى نيست جود
entries.english: The Prophet then gave the command, “Raze it and make it a dumping-place for rubbish and ashes.”

entries.farsi: گوشت كاندر شست تو ماهى رباست / ‏آن چنان لقمه نه بخشش نه سخاست
entries.english: The founder of the Mosque was false, like the Mosque: it is not munificence if you sprinkle grain upon a snare.

entries.farsi: مسجد اهل قبا كان بد جماد / آن چه كفو او نبد راهش نداد
entries.english: The meat that catches the fish on the hook— such a morsel is neither bounty nor generosity.

entries.farsi: در جمادات اين چنين حيفى نرفت / ‏زد در آن ناكفو امير داد نفت
entries.english: The Mosque of the people of Quba, which was inanimate— he did not admit to it that which was not its equal.

entries.farsi: پس حقايق را كه اصل اصلهاست / ‏دان كه آن جا فرق‏ها و فصل‏هاست
entries.english: In the case of lifeless things such a wrong did not come to pass: the lord of justice set fire to that unequal.

entries.farsi: نه حياتش چون حيات او بود / نه مماتش چون ممات او بود
entries.english: Therefore in the case of the essences, which are the foundation of all fundamentals, know that there, there are differences and divisions.

entries.farsi: گور او هرگز چو گور او مدان / ‏خود چه گويم حال فرق آن جهان
entries.english: Neither is his life like his life, nor is his death like his death.

entries.farsi: بر محك زن كار خود اى مرد كار / تا نسازى مسجد اهل ضرار
entries.english: Never deem his grave like his grave. How indeed shall I describe the difference in that world?

entries.farsi: بس بر آن مسجد كنان تسخر زدى / ‏چون نظر كردى تو خود ز يشان بدى
entries.english: Put your work to the touchstone, O man of work, lest you build the Mosque of the Opposers.
Often have you mocked those Mosque-makers; when you consider, you yourself have been one of them.

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت هندو كه با يار خود جنگ مى‏كرد بر كارى و خبر نداشت كه او هم بدان مبتالست
sections.title_en: Story of the Indian who quarrelled with his friend over a certain action and was not aware that he too was afflicted with it

entries.farsi: چار هندو در يكى مسجد شدند / بهر طاعت راكع و ساجد شدند
entries.english: Four Indians went into a mosque: they bowed their heads and prostrated themselves for worship’s sake.

entries.farsi: موذن آمد از يكى لفظى بجست / ‏كاى موذن بانگ كردى وقت هست
entries.english: Each one performed the takbir upon a niyyat, and began to pray with lowliness and contrition.

entries.farsi: هر يكى بر نيتى تكبير كرد / در نماز آمد به مسكينى و درد
entries.english: The muezzin came, from one of them fell a remark— “O muezzin, have you given the call to prayers? Is it time?”

entries.farsi: گفت آن هندوى ديگر از نياز / هى سخن گفتى و باطل شد نماز
entries.english: The second Indian said on the spur of the moment, “Hey, you have spoken, and your prayer is null.”

entries.farsi: آن سوم گفت آن دوم را اى عمو / چه زنى طعنه بر او خود را بگو
entries.english: The third one said to the second, “O uncle, why do you rail at him? Tell yourself.”

entries.farsi: آن چهارم گفت حمد اللهَّ كه من / ‏در نيفتادم به چه چون آن سه تن
entries.english: Said the fourth, “Praise be to God that I have not fallen into the pit, like those three persons.”

entries.farsi: اى خنك جانى كه عيب خويش ديد / هر كه عيبى گفت آن بر خود خريد
entries.english: Hence the prayers of all the four were marred; and the faultfinders went astray more.

entries.farsi: پس نماز هر چهاران شد تباه / ‏عيب گويان بيشتر گم كرده راه
entries.english: Oh, happy the soul that saw its own fault, and if any one told a fault, wished eagerly that upon itself!—

entries.farsi: ز انكه نيم او ز عيبستان بده ست / ‏و آن دگر نيمش ز غيبستان بده ست
entries.english: Because half of him has always belonged to the realm faults, and the other half of him to the realm of the Unseen

entries.farsi: چون كه بر سر مر ترا ده ريش هست / ‏مرهمت بر خويش بايد كار بست
entries.english: Since you have ten sores on your head, you must apply the plaster to yourself.

entries.farsi: عيب كردن ريش را داروى اوست / ‏چون شكسته گشت جاى ارحمواست
entries.english: Finding fault with the sore is the remedy for him; when he has become broken, it is the occasion for, “Have pity.”

entries.farsi: گر همان عيبت نبود ايمن مباش / ‏بو كه آن عيب از تو گردد نيز فاش
entries.english: If you have not the same fault, be not secure; maybe, that fault will afterwards become notorious in you.

entries.farsi: ال تخافوا از خدا نشنيده‏اى / ‏پس چه خود را ايمن و خوش ديده‏اى
entries.english: You have not heard from God Do not fear: why, then, have you deemed yourself secure and happy?

entries.farsi: سالها ابليس نيكو نام زيست / ‏گشت رسوا بين كه او را نام چيست
entries.english: For years Iblis lived in good renown; he was disgraced: mark what is his name.

entries.farsi: در جهان معروف بد علياى او / گشت معروفى بعكس اى واى او
entries.english: His eminence was famed throughout the world; his fame turned to infamy—oh, alas for him!

entries.farsi: تا نه اى ايمن تو معروفى مجو / رو بشو از خوف پس بنماى رو
entries.english: Do not seek fame till you are secure: wash your face of fear, and then show your face.

entries.farsi: تا نرويد ريش تو اى خوب من / ‏بر دگر ساده ز نخ طعنه مزن
entries.english: Until your beard grows, my good man, do not jeer at another whose chin is smooth.

entries.farsi: اين نگر كه مبتال شد جان او / در چهى افتاد تا شد پند تو
entries.english: Consider this that his soul was tried, so that he is fallen in; and he became a warning to you.

entries.farsi: تو نيفتادى كه باشى پند او / زهر او نوشيد تو خور قند او
entries.english: You did not fall, so that you should be a warning to him. He drank the poison: eat his sugar!

sections.title_fa: قصد كردن غزان به كشتن يك مردى تا آن دگر بترسد
sections.title_en: How the Ghuzz set about killing one man in order that another might be terrorised

entries.farsi: آن غزان ترك خونريز آمدند / بهر يغما بر دهى ناگه زدند
entries.english: Those blood-shedding Ghuzz Turcomans came, and entered a village for plunder

entries.farsi: دو كس از اعيان آن ده يافتند / در هالك آن يكى بشتافتند
entries.english: They found two of the notables of that village, and made haste to put one to death.

entries.farsi: دست بستندش كه قربانش كنند / گفت اى شاهان و اركان بلند
entries.english: They tied his hands in order to sacrifice him. He said, “O princes and high pillars,

entries.farsi: در چه مرگم چرا مى‏افگنيد / از چه آخر تشنه‏ى خون منيد
entries.english: For what reason are you seeking to slay me? Why, pray, are you thirsting after my blood?

entries.farsi: چيست حكمت چه غرض در كشتنم / ‏چون چنين درويشم و عريان تنم
entries.english: What is the wisdom, what is the object, in killing me, when I am so poor and bare-bodied?”

entries.farsi: گفت تا هيبت بر اين يارت زند / تا بترسد او و زر پيدا كند
entries.english: He replied, “To strike awe into this friend of yours, so that he may be afraid and produce gold.”

entries.farsi: گفت آخر او ز من مسكين‏تر است / ‏گفت قاصد كرده است او را زر است
entries.english: He said, “Why, he is poorer than I.” “He has done it on purpose,” replied the other; “he has gold.”

entries.farsi: گفت چون وهم است ما هر دو يك‏ايم / ‏در مقام احتمال و در شك‏ايم
entries.english: He said, “ Since it is opinion, we are both the same: we are exposed to probability and doubt.

entries.farsi: خود و را بكشيد اول اى شهان / ‏تا بترسم من دهم زر را نشان
entries.english: Kill him first, O princes, in order that I may be afraid and point out the way to the gold.”

entries.farsi: پس كرمهاى الهى بين كه ما / آمديم آخر زمان در انتها
entries.english: See, then, the lovingkindnesses of God, in that we have come in the latter days, at the very end.

entries.farsi: آخرين قرنها پيش از قرون / ‏در حديث است آخرون السابقون
entries.english: The last epoch is in front of the epochs: in the Traditions of the Prophet is— “the last, the foremost.”

entries.farsi: تا هالك قوم نوح و قوم هود / عارض رحمت به جان ما نمود
entries.english: In order that the destruction of the people of Noah and the people of Hud might display to our souls the proclaimer of mercy,

entries.farsi: كشت ايشان را كه ما ترسيم از او / ور خود اين بر عكس كردى واى تو
entries.english: He slew them, that we might fear Him; and if indeed He had done contrariwise, alas for you !

sections.title_fa: ‏بيان حال خود پرستان و ناشكران در نعمت وجود انبيا و اوليا عليهم السلام
sections.title_en: Explaining the state of those who are self-conceited and unthankful for the blessing of the existence of the prophets and saints—peace be unto them!

entries.farsi: هر ك از ايشان گفت از عيب و گ / ‏وز دل چون سنگ وز جان سياه
entries.english: Whosoever of them has spoken of fault and sin, and of a heart like stone, and of a black soul?

entries.farsi: و ز سبك دارى فرمان‏هاى او / و ز فراغت از غم فرداى او
entries.english: And of holding light His commands, and of being free from care for His To-morrow;

entries.farsi: و ز هوس و ز عشق اين دنياى دون / ‏چون زنان مر نفس را بودن زبون
entries.english: And of being, like women, enslaved to the fleshly soul by passion and by love of this vile world;

entries.farsi: و آن فرار از نكته‏هاى ناصحان / ‏و آن رميدن از لقاى صالحان
entries.english: And of fleeing from the pungent sayings of sincere counsellors, and of shrinking from the countenance of the righteous;

entries.farsi: با دل و با اهل دل بيگانگى / ‏با شهان تزوير و روبه‏شانگى
entries.english: Estrangement from the spirit and spiritual folk, fraud and fox-like behaviour towards the kings;

entries.farsi: سير چشمان را گدا پنداشتن / ‏از حسدشان خفيه دشمن داشتن
entries.english: Thinking the fully satisfied to be beggars, secretly regarding them with enmity from envy

entries.farsi: گر پذيرد چيز تو گويى گداست / ‏ور نه گويى زرق و مكر است و دغاست
entries.english: If he accepts anything, you say he is a beggar; and if not, you say it is hypocrisy and deceit and guile.

entries.farsi: گر در آميزد تو گويى طامع است / ‏ور نه گويى در تكبر مولع است
entries.english: If he mixes, you say he is covetous; and if not, you say he is excessively given to pride;

entries.farsi: نه مرا پرواى سر خاريدن است / ‏نه مرا پرواى دين ورزيدن است
entries.english: Or you hypocritically excuse yourself, saying, “I am held back in maintaining my wife and children.

entries.farsi: يا منافق‏وار عذر آرى كه من / ‏مانده‏ام در نفقه‏ى فرزند و زن
entries.english: Neither have I leisure to scratch my head, nor have I leisure to cultivate religion.

entries.farsi: اى فالن ما را به همت ياد دار / تا شويم از اوليا پايان كار
entries.english: O so-and-so, remember me in your benedictions, that in the end I may become one of the saints.”

entries.farsi: اين سخن نه هم ز درد و سوز گفت / ‏خوابناكى هرزه گفت و باز خفت
entries.english: These words he does not even speak from passion and ardour; a drowsy man muttered some idle talk and went to sleep again.

entries.farsi: هيچ چاره نيست از قوت عيال / ‏از بن دندان كنم كسب حالل
entries.english: “I cannot help feeding my family: I strain every nerve to earn a lawful livelihood.”

entries.farsi: چه حالل اى گشته از اهل ضالل / ‏غير خون تو نمى‏بينم حالل
entries.english: How lawful, O you that have become one of the lost? I deem nothing lawful but your blood.

entries.farsi: از خدا چاره‏ستش و از لوت نه / ‏چاره‏ش است از دين و از طاغوت نه
entries.english: He can do without God, but not without food; he can do without the Religion, but not without the idols.

entries.farsi: اى كه صبرت نيست از دنياى دون / ‏صبر چون دارى ز نعم الماهدون
entries.english: O you that can not refrain your self from this vile world, how can you refrain yourself from Him who spread the earth as a carpet?

entries.farsi: اى كه صبرت نيست از ناز و نعيم / ‏صبر چون دارى از اللهَّ كريم
entries.english: O you that can not refrain yourself from delight and luxury, how can you refrain yourself from the Bountiful God?

entries.farsi: اى كه صبرت نيست از پاك و پليد / صبر چون دارى از آن كاين آفريد
entries.english: O you that can not refrain yourself from aught pure or foul, how can you refrain yourself from Him who created this?

entries.farsi: كو خليلى كه برون آمد ز غار / گفت هذا رب هان كو كردگار
entries.english: Where is the Friend, who came forth from the cave, and said, “This is my Lord. Take heed! Where is the Maker?”

entries.farsi: من نخواهم در دو عالم بنگريست / ‏تا نبينم اين دو مجلس آن كيست
entries.english: “I will not look at the two worlds until I see to whom these two assembly-places belong.

entries.farsi: بى‏تماشاى صفتهاى خدا / گر خورم نان در گلو ماند مرا
entries.english: If I eat bread without the view of God’s attributes, it will stick in my throat.”

entries.farsi: چون گوارد لقمه بى‏ديدار او / بى‏تماشاى گل و گلزار او
entries.english: How should a morsel digest without the sight of Him, without the view of His roses and rose-garden?

entries.farsi: جز بر اميد خدا زين آب خور / كى خورد يك لحظه الا گاو و خر
entries.english: Save in hope of God, who but an ox or ass would for one moment drink from this pond?

entries.farsi: آن كه كالانعام بد بل هم اضل / ‏گر چه پر مكر است آن گنده بغل
entries.english: He that was like the cattle, nay, more lost?— though that stinker is full of cunning

entries.farsi: مكر او سر زير و او سر زير شد / روزگارى برد و روزش دير شد
entries.english: His cunning went headlong, and he went headlong: he passed a little while, and his day set.

entries.farsi: فكرگاهش كند شد عقلش خرف / ‏عمر شد چيزى ندارد چون الف
entries.english: His brain became dull, his mind doting: his life is gone— and like alif he hath nothing.

entries.farsi: آن چه مى‏گويد در اين انديشه‏ام / ‏آن هم از دستان آن نفس است هم
entries.english: His saying, “I am thinking about it”— that too is only of the deceit of the fleshly soul;

entries.farsi: و انچه مى‏گويد غفور است و رحيم / ‏نيست آن جز حيله‏ى نفس لئيم
entries.english: And his saying, “He is forgiving and merciful” — that is nothing but a trick of the villainous flesh.

entries.farsi: اى ز غم مرده كه دست از نان تهى است / ‏چون غفور است و رحيم اين ترس چيست
entries.english: O you that art dead with anxiety because your hands are empty of bread, what is this fear, since He is forgiving and merciful?

sections.title_fa: شكايت گفتن پير مردى به طبيب از رنجوريها و جواب گفتن طبيب او را
sections.title_en: How an old man complained of his ailments to a doctor, and how the doctor answered him.

entries.farsi: گفت پيرى مر طبيبى را كه من / ‏در زحيرم از دماغ خويشتن
entries.english: An old man said to a doctor, “I am in torment because of my brain.”

entries.farsi: گفت از پيرى است آن ضعف دماغ / ‏گفت بر چشمم ز ظلمت هست داغ
entries.english: The doctor replied, “That weakness of brain is from age.” Said the old man, “There are spots of darkness on my eyes”

entries.farsi: گفت از پيرى است اى شيخ قديم / ‏گفت پشتم درد مى‏آيد عظيم
entries.english: “It is from age, O ancient Shaykh,” said the doctor. “Awful pain comes in my back,” said he.

entries.farsi: گفت از پيرى است اى شيخ نزار / گفت هر چه مى‏خورم نبود گوار
entries.english: “It is from age, O emaciated Shaykh “said the doctor. “Whatever I eat,” said he, “is not digested.”

entries.farsi: گفت ضعف معده هم از پيرى است / ‏گفت وقت دم مرا دم گيرى است
entries.english: The doctor replied, “Weakness of stomach also is of age.” Said he, “When I breathe, respiration is hard for me.”

entries.farsi: گفت آرى انقطاع دم بود / چون رسد پيرى دو صد علت شود
entries.english: “Yes,” he said, “it is asthma; when old age arrives, two hundred diseases come on.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى احمق بر اين بر دوختى / ‏از طبيبى تو همين آموختى
entries.english: “O fool,” he exclaimed, “you have stuck at this: this is all that you have learned of medicine.

entries.farsi: اى مدمغ عقلت اين دانش نداد / كه خدا هر رنج را درمان نهاد
entries.english: O crack-brained man, your intellect has not given you this knowledge, that God bath appointed a remedy for every pain.

entries.farsi: تو خر احمق ز اندك مايگى / ‏بر زمين ماندى ز كوته‏پايگى
entries.english: You, stupid ass, from poorness of ability have remained on the ground for want of a sufficient foothold.”

entries.farsi: پس طبيبش گفت اى عمر تو شصت / ‏اين غضب وين خشم هم از پيرى است
entries.english: Then the doctor said to him, “O sexagenarian, this anger and this choler are also from old age.

entries.farsi: چون همه اوصاف و اجزا شد نحيف / ‏خويشتن‏دارى و صبرت شد ضعيف
entries.english: Since all the functions and parts are atrophied, your self-control and patience have become weak.”

entries.farsi: بر نتابد دو سخن زو هى كند / تاب يك جرعه ندارد قى كند
entries.english: He cannot endure two words, he cries out thereat; he cannot retain one draught, he vomits —

entries.farsi: از برون پير است و در باطن صبى / ‏خود چه چيز است آن ولى و آن نبى
entries.english: Except, to be sure, the Pir that is drunken with God, and in whose inward being there is “a goodly life.”

entries.farsi: جز مگر پيرى كه از حق است مست / ‏در درون او حيات طيبه است
entries.english: Outwardly he is old, but within he is young. What thing, verily, is he? He is the saint and the prophet.

entries.farsi: گر نه پيدايند پيش نيك و بد / چيست با ايشان خسان را اين حسد
entries.english: If they are not manifest to the good and the evil, what is this envy which the worthless bear against them?

entries.farsi: ور نمى‏دانندشان علم اليقين / ‏چيست اين بغض و حيل سازى و كين
entries.english: And if they do not know them with certain knowledge, what is this hatred and hatching of plots and enmity?

entries.farsi: ور نمى‏دانند بعث و رستخيز / چون زنندى خويش بر شمشير تيز
entries.english: And, if they know of the Resurrection and rising from the dead, how should they dash themselves against a sharp sword?

entries.farsi: بر تو مى‏خندد مبين او را چنان / ‏صد قيامت در درون استش نهان
entries.english: He smiles upon you, do not deem him to be such, in his inward consciousness are hidden a hundred Resurrections.

entries.farsi: دوزخ و جنت همه اجزاى اوست / ‏هر چه انديشى تو او بالاى اوست
entries.english: Hell and Paradise are entirely parts of him: he is beyond any thought that you may conceive.

entries.farsi: هر چه انديشى پذيراى فناست / ‏آن كه در انديشه نايد آن خداست
entries.english: All that you may think of is liable to pass away; he that comes not into thought is God.

entries.farsi: بر در اين خانه گستاخى ز چيست / ‏گر همى‏دانند كاندر خانه كيست
entries.english: Wherefore presumption at the door of this house, if they know who is within the house?

entries.farsi: ابلهان تعظيم مسجد مى‏كنند / در جفاى اهل دل جد مى‏كنند
entries.english: Fools venerate the mosque and endeavour to destroy them that have the heart.

entries.farsi: آن مجاز است اين حقيقت اى خران / ‏نيست مسجد جز درون سروران
entries.english: That is phenomenal; this is real, O asses! The mosque is naught but the hearts of the captains.

entries.farsi: مسجدى كان اندرون اولياست / ‏سجده‏گاه جمله است آن جا خداست
entries.english: The mosque that is the inward of the saints is the place of worship for all: God is there.

entries.farsi: تا دل مرد خدا نامد به درد / هيچ قومى را خدا رسوا نكرد
entries.english: Until the heart of the man of God was grieved, never did God put any generation to shame.

entries.farsi: قصد جنگ انبيا مى‏داشتند / جسم ديدند آدمى پنداشتند
entries.english: They were going to make war on the prophets: they saw the body; they supposed he was a man.

entries.farsi: در تو هست اخالق آن پيشينيان / ‏چون نمى‏ترسى كه تو باشى همان
entries.english: In you are the moral natures of those peoples of yore: how are not you afraid lest you be the same?

entries.farsi: آن نشانيها همه چون در تو هست / ‏چون تو زيشانى كجا خواهى برست
entries.english: As for as all those marks are in you, and you are of them, how will you be saved?

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى جوحى و آن كودك كه پيش جنازه‏ى پدر خويش نوحه مى‏كرد
sections.title_en: The story of Juhi and the child who cried lamentably beside his father’s bier

entries.farsi: كودكى در پيش تابوت پدر / زار مى‏ناليد و بر مى‏كوفت سر
entries.english: A child was crying bitterly and beating his head beside his father’s coffin,

entries.farsi: كاى پدر آخر كجايت مى‏برند / تا ترا در زير خاكى بسپرند
entries.english: Saying, “Why, father, where are they taking you to press you tight under some earth?

entries.farsi: مى‏برندت خانه‏ى تنگ و زحير / نى در او قالى و نه در وى حصير
entries.english: They are taking you to a narrow and noisome house: there is no carpet in it, nor any mat;

entries.farsi: نى چراغى در شب و نه روز نان / ‏نى در او بوى طعام و نه نشان
entries.english: No lamp at night and no bread by day; neither smell nor sign of food is there.

entries.farsi: نى درش معمور و نى در بام راه / ‏نى يكى همسايه كاو باشد پناه
entries.english: No door in good repair, no way to the roof; not one neighbour to be refuge.

entries.farsi: چشم تو كه بوسه گاه خلق بود / چون رود در خانه‏ى كور و كبود
entries.english: Your body, which was a place for the people’s kisses— how should it go into a blind and murky house?—

entries.farsi: خانه‏ى بى‏زينهار و جاى تنگ / ‏كه در او نه روى مى‏ماند نه رنگ
entries.english: A pitiless house and narrow room, where neither face will be lasting nor colour.”

entries.farsi: زين نسق اوصاف خانه مى‏شمرد / وز دو ديده اشك خونين مى‏فشرد
entries.english: In this manner was he enumerating the qualities of the house, whilst he wrung tears of blood from his two eyes.

entries.farsi: گفت جوحى را پدر اى ارجمند / و اهلل اين را خانه‏ى ما مى‏برند
entries.english: Juhi said to his father, “O worthy, by God they are taking this to our house.”

entries.farsi: گفت جوحى را پدر ابله مشو / گفت اى بابا نشانيها شنو
entries.english: The father said to Juhi, “Don’t be a fool!” “O papa,” said he, “hear the marks.

entries.farsi: اين نشانيها كه گفت او يك به يك / ‏خانه‏ى ما راست بى‏ترديد و شك
entries.english: These marks which he mentioned one by one— our house has them, without uncertainty or doubt

entries.farsi: نى حصير و نه چراغ و نه طعام / ‏نه درش معمور و نه صحن و نه بام
entries.english: Neither mat nor lamp nor food; neither its door is in good repair, nor its court nor its roof.”

entries.farsi: زين نمط دارند بر خود صد نشان / ‏ليك كى بينند آن را طاغيان
entries.english: In this wise the disobedient have a hundred marks upon themselves, but how should they see them?

entries.farsi: خانه‏ى آن دل كه ماند بى‏ضيا / از شعاع آفتاب كبريا
entries.english: The house, namely, the heart that remains unlighted by the beams of the sun of Majesty,

entries.farsi: تنگ و تاريك است چون جان جهود / بى‏نوا از ذوق سلطان ودود
entries.english: Is narrow and dark as the souls of Jews, destitute of savour of the loving King

entries.farsi: نى در آن دل تافت نور آفتاب / ‏نى گشاد عرصه و نه فتح باب
entries.english: Neither has the radiance of the Sun shone into that heart, nor is there spaciousness or opening of the door.

entries.farsi: گور خوشتر از چنين دل مر ترا / آخر از گور دل خود برتر آ
entries.english: The tomb is better for you than a heart like this. Come now; arise from the tomb which is your heart!

entries.farsi: زنده‏اى و زنده زاد اى شوخ و شنگ / ‏دم نمى‏گيرد ترا زين گور تنگ
entries.english: You art living and born of the living. O gay and winsome one, art not you choked by this narrow tomb?

entries.farsi: يوسف وقتى و خورشيد سما / زين چه و زندان بر آ و رو نما
entries.english: You art the Joseph of the time and the sun of heaven: arise from this pit and prison, and show your face!

entries.farsi: يونست در بطن ماهى پخته شد / مخلصش را نيست از تسبيح بد
entries.english: Your Jonah has been cooked in the fish’s belly: for his deliverance there is no means but glorification of God.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى او مسيح بطن نون / ‏حبس و زندانش بدى تا يبعثون
entries.english: If he had not glorified, the fish’s belly would have been his jail and prison until they shall be raised.

entries.farsi: او به تسبيح از تن ماهى بجست / ‏ُچيست تسبيح آيت روز أَ لَسْ ت
entries.english: Through glorification he escaped from the body of the fish. What is glorification? The sign of the Day of Alast

entries.farsi: گر فراموشت شد آن تسبيح جان / ‏بشنو اين تسبيحهاى ماهيان
entries.english: If you have forgotten that glorification by your spirit, listen to the glorifications of those Fishes.

entries.farsi: هر كه ديد اللهَّ را اللهى است / ‏هر كه ديد آن بحر را آن ماهى است
entries.english: Whoever has seen God is of God: whoever has seen that Sea is that Fish.

entries.farsi: اين جهان درياست و تن ماهى و روح / ‏يونس محجوب از نور صبوح
entries.english: This world is a sea, and the body a fish, and the spirit is the Jonah debarred from the light of the dawn.

entries.farsi: گر مسبح باشد از ماهى رهيد / ور نه در وى هضم گشت و ناپديد
entries.english: If it be a glorifier, it is delivered from the fish; otherwise, it becomes digested therein and vanishes.

entries.farsi: ماهيان جان در اين دريا پرند / تو نمى‏بينى كه كورى اى نژند
entries.english: The spiritual Fishes abound in this sea; you do not see them, they are flying around you. Those Fishes are darting at you: open your eye, that you may see them clearly.

entries.farsi: بر تو خود را مى‏زنند آن ماهيان / ‏چشم بگشا تا ببينى‏شان عيان
entries.english: If you art not seeing the Fishes plain— after all, your ear has heard their glorification.

entries.farsi: صبر كردن جان تسبيحات تست / ‏صبر كن كان است تسبيح درست
entries.english: To practice patience is the soul of your glorifications: have patience, for that is the true glorification.

entries.farsi: ماهيان را گر نمى‏بينى پديد / گوش تو تسبيحشان آخر شنيد
entries.english: No glorification has such a degree; have patience: patience is the key to relief.

entries.farsi: هيچ تسبيحى ندارد آن درج / ‏صبر كن الصبر مفتاح الفرج
entries.english: Patience is like the bridge Sirat, Paradise on the other side: with every fair there is an ugly pedagogue.

entries.farsi: صبر چون پول صراط آن سو بهشت / ‏هست با هر خوب يك الالى زشت
entries.english: So long as you flee from the chaperone, there is no meeting , because there is no parting of the handsome boy from the bodyguard

entries.farsi: تا ز الال مى‏گريزى وصل نيست / ‏ز انكه الال را ز شاهد فصل نيست
entries.english: What should you know of the savour of patience, O you of brittle heart— especially, of patience for the sake of that Beauty of Chigil?

entries.farsi: تو چه دانى ذوق صبر اى شيشه دل / ‏خاصه صبر از بهر آن نقش چگل
entries.english: A man’s delight is in campaigns and in the glory and pomp the gay fellow delights in his penis.

entries.farsi: مرد را ذوق غزا و كر و فر / مر مخنث را بود ذوق‏از ذكر
entries.english: His religion and zikr are nothing but his penis: his thought has borne him down to the lowest depth.

entries.farsi: جز ذكر نه دين او و ذكر او / سوى اسفل برد او را فكر او
entries.english: Though he rise to the sky, be not afraid of him, for in love of lowness he has studied.

entries.farsi: گر بر آيد بر فلك از وى مترس / ‏كاو بعشق سفل آموزيد درس
entries.english: He gallops his horse towards lowness, albeit he rings the bell aloft.

entries.farsi: از علمهاى گدايان ترس چيست / ‏كان علمها لقمه‏ى نان را رهى است
entries.english: What is there to fear from the flags of beggars?— for those flags are a means for a mouthful of bread.

sections.title_fa: ترسيدن كودك از آن شخص صاحب جثه و گفتن آن شخص كه اى كودك مترس كه من نامردم
sections.title_en: A child being terrified by a full bodied man, and the man telling him, “Do not be afraid as I am impotent”

entries.farsi: كنگ زفتى كودكى را يافت فرد / زرد شد كودك ز بيم قصد مرد
entries.english: A large man found a child alone, the child turned yellow in fear of the man’s attention.

entries.farsi: گفت ايمن باش اى زيباى من / ‏كه تو خواهى بود بر بالاى من
entries.english: The big man said, “Feel safe my beauty, because you would be on top of me.”

entries.farsi: من اگر هولم مخنث دان مرا / همچو اشتر بر نشين مى‏ران مرا
entries.english: Although I am big, think of me as impotent, mount me like a camel and ride me.”

entries.farsi: صورت مردان و معنى اين چنين / ‏از برون آدم درون ديو لعين
entries.english: The appearance of men and the reality like this— Adam without, the accursed Devil within—

entries.farsi: آن دهل را مانى اى زفت چو عاد / كه بر او آن شاخ را مى‏كوفت باد
entries.english: O you that are big as the people of Ad, you resemble the drum against which a branch was beaten by the wind.

entries.farsi: روبهى اشكار خود را باد داد / بهر طبلى همچو خيك پر ز باد
entries.english: A fox abandoned his prey for the sake of a drum like a wind- filled leathern bag,

entries.farsi: چون نديد اندر دهل او فربهى / ‏گفت خوكى به ازين خيك تهى
entries.english: When he found no fatness in the drum, he said, “A hog is better than this empty bag.”

entries.farsi: روبهان ترسند ز آواز دهل / ‏عاقلش چندان زند كه ال تقل
entries.english: Foxes are afraid of the noise of the drum; the wise man beats it ever so much, saying, “Speak not!”

sections.title_fa: ‏قصه‏ى تير اندازى و ترسيدن او از سوارى كه در بيشه مى‏رفت
sections.title_en: The story of an archer and his fear of a horseman who was riding in a forest

entries.farsi: يك سوارى با سالح و بس مهيب / ‏مى‏شد اندر بيشه بر اسبى نجيب
entries.english: A horseman, armed and very terrible, was riding in the forest on a high-bred horse.

entries.farsi: تير اندازى به حكم او را بديد / پس ز خوف او كمان را در كشيد
entries.english: An expert archer espied him, and then from fear of him drew his bow,

entries.farsi: تا زند تيرى سوارش بانگ زد / من ضعيفم گر چه زفت استم جسد
entries.english: To shoot an arrow; the horseman shouted to him, “I am a weakling, though my body is big.

entries.farsi: هان و هان منگر تو در زفتى من / ‏كه كمم در وقت جنگ از پير زن
entries.english: Take heed! Take heed! Do not regard my bigness, for in the hour of battle I am less than an old woman.”

entries.farsi: گفت رو كه نيك گفتى ور نه نيش / ‏بر تو مى‏انداختم از ترس خويش
entries.english: “Pass on,” said he; “you have spoken well, else by reason of my fear I should have shot a barb at you.”

entries.farsi: بس كسان را كالت پيكار كشت / ‏بى‏رجوليت چنان تيغى به مشت
entries.english: Many are they whom implements of war have slain, such a sword in their hands, without the manhood.

entries.farsi: گر بپوشى تو سالح رستمان / ‏رفت جانت چون نباشى مرد آن
entries.english: If you don the armour of Rustam, your soul goes when you are not the man for it.

entries.farsi: جان سپر كن تيغ بگذار اى پسر / هر كه بى‏سر بود از اين شه برد سر
entries.english: Make your soul a shield and drop the sword, O son: whoever is headless saves his head from this King.

entries.farsi: آن سالحت حيله و مكر تو است / ‏هم ز تو زاييد و هم جان تو خست
entries.english: Those weapons of yours are your contriving and plotting; they have sprung from you and at the same time have wounded your soul.

entries.farsi: چون نكردى هيچ سودى زين حيل / ‏ترك حيلت كن كه پيش آيد دول
entries.english: Since you have gained nothing by this contriving, abandon contrivance, that happy fortunes may meet.

entries.farsi: چون كه يك لحظه نخوردى بر ز فن / ‏ترك فن گو مى‏طلب رب المنن
entries.english: Since you have not for one moment enjoyed fruit from the arts, bid farewell to the arts, and seek always the Lord of bounties.

entries.farsi: چون مبارك نيست بر تو اين علوم / ‏خويشتن گولى كن و بگذر ز شوم
entries.english: Since these sciences bring you no blessing, make yourself a dunce and leave ill-luck behind.

entries.farsi: چون ماليك گو كه ال عِلْمَ لنا / يا الهى غير ما علمتنا
entries.english: Like the angels, say, “We have no knowledge, O God, except what You have taught us.”

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى اعرابى و ريگ در جوال كردن و مالمت كردن آن فيلسوف او را
sections.title_en: Story of the desert Arab and his putting sand in the sack and the philosopher’s rebuking him

entries.farsi: يك عرابى بار كرده اشترى / ‏دو جوال زفت از دانه پرى
entries.english: A certain Arab of the desert loaded a camel with two big sacks-- one full of grain.

entries.farsi: از وطن پرسيد و آوردش به گفت / ‏و اندر آن پرسش بسى درها بسفت
entries.english: He was seated on the top of both sacks. A glib philosopher questioned him.

entries.farsi: او نشسته بر سر هر دو جوال / ‏يك حديث انداز كرد او را سؤال
entries.english: He asked him about his native land and led him to talk and said many fine things in the course of enquiry.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفتش كه اين هر دو جوال / ‏چيست آگنده بگو مصدوق حال
entries.english: Afterwards he said to him, “What are those two sacks filled with? Tell the truth of the matter.”

entries.farsi: گفت اندر يك جوالم گندم است / ‏در دگر ريگى نه قوت مردم است
entries.english: He replied, “In one sack I have wheat; in the other is some sand —not food for men.”

entries.farsi: گفت تو چون بار كردى اين رمال / ‏گفت تا تنها نماند آن جوال
entries.english: “Why,” he asked, “ did you load this sand?” “In order that the other sack might not remain alone,” he replied.

entries.farsi: گفت نيم گندم آن تنگ را / در دگر ريز از پى فرهنگ را
entries.english: “For wisdom’s sake,” said he, “pour half the wheat of that pannier into the other,

entries.farsi: تا سبك گردد جوال و هم شتر / گفت شاباش اى حكيم اهل و حر
entries.english: So that the sacks may be lightened, and the camel too.” He cried, “Bravo! O clever and noble sage!

entries.farsi: اين چنين فكر دقيق و راى خوب / ‏تو چنين عريان پياده در لغوب
entries.english: Such subtle thought and excellent judgment! And you so naked, on foot and in fatigue!”

entries.farsi: اين چنين عقل و كفايت كه تراست / ‏تو وزيرى يا شهى بر گوى راست
entries.english: The good man took pity on the philosopher and resolved to mount him on the camel.

entries.farsi: باز گفتش اى حكيم خوش سخن / ‏شمه‏اى از حال خود هم شرح كن
entries.english: He said to him again, “ O fair-spoken sage, explain a little about your own circumstances as well.

entries.farsi: رحمتش آمد بر حكيم و عزم كرد / كش بر اشتر بر نشاند نيك مرد
entries.english: Such intelligence and talent as you have, are you a vizier or a king? Tell the truth.”

entries.farsi: گفت اين هر دو نيم از عامه‏ام / ‏بنگر اندر حال و اندر جامه‏ام
entries.english: He answered, “ I am not these two: I am of the common folk. Look at my appearance and dress.”

entries.farsi: گفت اشتر چند دارى چند گاو / گفت نه اين و نه آن ما را مكاو
entries.english: He asked, “How many camels have you? How many oxen?” “I have neither these nor those,” he replied: “do not dig at me”

entries.farsi: گفت رختت چيست بارى در دكان / ‏گفت ما را كو دكان و كو مكان
entries.english: He said, “At any rate, what goods have you in your shop? “He answered, “Where have I a shop, and where a dwelling-place?”

entries.farsi: گفت پس از نقد پرسم نقد چند / كه تويى تنها رو و محبوب پند
entries.english: “Then,” said he, “I will ask about money. How much money ?— for you are a solitary wanderer and one whose counsel is prized.

entries.farsi: كيمياى مس عالم با تو است / ‏عقل و دانش را گهر تو بر تو است
entries.english: With you is the elixir which changes the copper of the world gold: your understanding and knowledge are inlaid with pearls.”

entries.farsi: گفت و اهلل نيست يا وجه العرب / ‏در همه ملكم وجوه قوت شب
entries.english: “By God,” he replied,” O chief of the Arabs, in my whole property there is not the means of food for the night.

entries.farsi: پا برهنه تن برهنه مى‏دوم / ‏هر كه نانى مى‏دهد آن جا روم
entries.english: I run about with bare feet and naked body. If any one will give me a loaf of bread—there I go.

entries.farsi: مر مرا زين حكمت و فضل و هنر / نيست حاصل جز خيال و درد سر
entries.english: From this wisdom and learning and excellence have got nothing but imagination and headache.”

entries.farsi: پس عرب گفتش كه شو دور از برم / ‏تا نبارد شومى تو بر سرم
entries.english: Then the Arab said to him, “Go far away, so that your ill-luck may not rain upon me.

entries.farsi: دور بر آن حكمت شومت ز من / ‏نطق تو شرم است بر اهل زمن
entries.english: Take far away from me that unlucky wisdom of yours: your speech is unlucky for the people of the time.

entries.farsi: يا تو آن سو رو من اين سو مى‏دوم / ‏ور ترا ره پيش من واپس روم
entries.english: Either go you in that direction, and I will run in this direction; or if your way be forwards, I will go back.

entries.farsi: يك جوالم گندم و ديگر ز ريگ / ‏به بود زين حيله‏هاى مرده‏ريگ
entries.english: One sack of wheat and the other of sand is better for me than these vain contriving.

entries.farsi: احمقى‏ام بس مبارك احمقى است / ‏كه دلم با برگ و جانم متقى است
entries.english: My foolishness is a very blessed foolishness, for my heart is well-furnished and my soul is devout.”

entries.farsi: گر تو خواهى كت شقاوت كم شود / جهد كن تا از تو حكمت كم شود
entries.english: If you desire that misery should vanish, endeavour that wisdom may vanish from you

entries.farsi: حكمتى كز طبع زايد وز خيال / ‏حكمتى بى‏فيض نور ذو الجلال
entries.english: The wisdom which is born of nature and imagination, the wisdom which lacks the overflowing grace of the Light of the Glorious

entries.farsi: حكمت دنيا فزايد ظن و شك / ‏حكمت دينى برد فوق فلك
entries.english: The wisdom of this world brings increase of supposition and doubt; the wisdom of the Religion soars above the sky.

entries.farsi: زوبعان زيرك آخر زمان / ‏بر فزوده خويش بر پيشينيان
entries.english: The ingenious rascals of latter time have raised themselves over the ancients;

entries.farsi: حيله آموزان جگرها سوخته / حيله آموزان جگرها سوخته
entries.english: The learners of cunning have burnt their hearts and have learned feints and tricks;

entries.farsi: صبر و ايثار و سخاى نفس و جود / باد داده كان بود اكسير سود
entries.english: They have thrown to the winds patience and altruism and self-sacrifice and generosity - which are the elixir of profit.

entries.farsi: فكر آن باشد كه بگشايد رهى / ‏راه آن باشد كه پيش آيد شهى
entries.english: The thought is that which opens a way: the way is that on which a king advances.

entries.farsi: شاه آن باشد كه از خود شه بود / نه به مخزنها و لشكر شه شود
entries.english: The king is he that is king in himself, and is not made king by treasuries and armies;

entries.farsi: تا بماند شاهى او سرمدى / ‏همچو عز ملك دين احمدى
entries.english: So that his kingship remains unto everlasting, like the glory of the empire of the Mohammedan Religion

sections.title_fa: كرامات ابراهيم ادهم بر لب دريا
sections.title_en: The miracles of Ibrahim son of Adham—may God sanctify his holy spirit!— on the sea-shore.

entries.farsi: هم ز ابراهيم ادهم آمده ست / ‏كاو ز راهى بر لب دريا نشست
entries.english: Thus, it is related of Ibrahim son of Adham that after a journey he sat down by the edge of the sea.

entries.farsi: دلق خود مى‏دوخت آن سلطان جان / ‏يك اميرى آمد آن جا ناگهان
entries.english: He was stitching his Sufi mantle, an Amir, walking on the shore, suddenly came to that spot.

entries.farsi: آن امير از بندگان شيخ بود / شيخ را بشناخت سجده كرد زود
entries.english: That Amir had been one of the Shaykh’s servants; he recognized the Shaykh and at once bowed low.

entries.farsi: خيره شد در شيخ و اندر دلق او / شكل ديگر گشته خلق و خلق او
entries.english: He was astounded at the Shaykh and at his dervish garb— his nature and outward guise had become transformed

entries.farsi: كاو رها كرد آن چنان ملك شگرف / ‏بر گزيد آن فقر بس باريك حرف
entries.english: That he gave up such a grand kingdom, and chose that very pettifogging poverty;

entries.farsi: ترك كرد او ملك هفت اقليم را / مى‏زند بر دلق سوزن چون گدا
entries.english: He lets the sovereignty of the Seven Climes be lost, and plies the needle on his dervish-cloak, like a beggar.

entries.farsi: شخ واقف گشت از انديشه‏اش / ‏شيخ چون شير است و دلها بيشه‏اش
entries.english: The Shaykh became aware of his thought: a Shaykh is as the lion, and hearts are his jungle.

entries.farsi: چون رجا و خوف در دلها روان / ‏نيست مخفى بر وى اسرار جهان
entries.english: He is entering, like hope and fear, into hearts: not hid from him are the secrets of the world.

entries.farsi: دل نگه داريد اى بى‏حاصالن / ‏در حضور حضرت صاحب دالن
entries.english: Keep watch over your hearts, O fruitless ones, in the presence of the majesty of the men of heart.

entries.farsi: پيش اهل تن ادب بر ظاهر است / ‏كه خدا ز ايشان نهان را ساتر است
entries.english: Before the men of body, respect is outwardly, for God is veiling the occult from them.

entries.farsi: پيش اهل دل ادب بر باطن است / ‏ز انكه دلشان بر سراير فاطن است
entries.english: Before the men of heart, respect is inwardly, because their hearts have insight into the secret thoughts.

entries.farsi: تو بعكسى پيش كوران بهر جاه / ‏با حضور آيى نشينى پايگاه
entries.english: You are contrary: for the sake of position you come with reverence before them that are blind, and sit in the vestibule;

entries.farsi: پيش بينايان كنى ترك ادب / ‏نار شهوت را از آن گشتى حطب
entries.english: Before the seers you behave disrespectfully: hence you have become fuel for the fire of lust.

entries.farsi: چون ندارى فطنت و نور هدى / بهر كوران روى را مى‏زن جال
entries.english: Since you have not perception and the light of guidance, continue to polish your face for the sake of the blind!

entries.farsi: شيخ سوزن زود در دريا فگند / خواست سوزن را به آواز بلند
entries.english: Before the seers, daub your face with dirt! Act haughtily notwithstanding such a stinking state !

entries.farsi: پيش بينايان حدث در روى مال / ‏ناز مى‏كن با چنين گنديده حال
entries.english: The Shaykh quickly threw his needle into the sea, and with a loud voice called for the needle.

entries.farsi: سر بر آوردند از درياى حق / ‏كه بگير اى شيخ سوزنهاى حق
entries.english: Myriads of Divine fishes— in the lips of each fish a needle of gold

entries.farsi: صد هزاران ماهى اللهيى / ‏سوزن زر در لب هر ماهيى
entries.english: Lifted their heads from God’s sea, saying, “Take, O Shaykh, God’s needles”

entries.farsi: رو بدو كرد و بگفتش اى امير / ملك دل به يا چنان ملك حقير
entries.english: He turned his face towards him and said to him, “O Amir is the kingdom of the heart better, or such a despicable kingdom?”

entries.farsi: اين نشان ظاهر است اين هيچ نيست / ‏تا بباطن در روى بينى تو بيست
entries.english: This is the outward sign, this is nothing: wait till you enter the inward see!

entries.farsi: سوى شهر از باغ شاخى آورند / باغ و بستان را كجا آن جا برند
entries.english: From the garden they bring to town a branch: how should they carry thither the garden and orchard?

entries.farsi: خاصه باغى كاين فلك يك برگ اوست / ‏بلكه اين مغز است وين عالم چو پوست
entries.english: Especially, a Garden whereof this heaven is one leaf; nay, that is the kernel and this other is as the husk

entries.farsi: بر نمى‏دارى سوى آن باغ گام / ‏بوى افزون جوى و كن دفع زكام
entries.english: You are not stepping on towards that Garden, seek more scent, and get rid of phlegm,

entries.farsi: تا كه آن بو جاذب جانت شود / تا كه آن بو نور چشمانت شود
entries.english: In order that that scent may draw your soul; in order that that scent may become the light of your eyes.

entries.farsi: گفت يوسف ابن يعقوب نبى / ‏بهر بو ألقوا على وجه أبي
entries.english: For the scent’s sake Joseph, son of Jacob the prophet, said: “Cast upon my father’s face.”

entries.farsi: بهر اين بو گفت احمد در عظات / دايما قرة عيني فى الصالة
entries.english: For this scent’s sake Ahmad constantly said in exhortations: “In the ritual prayer is the delight of mine eye.”

entries.farsi: پنج حس با همدگر پيوسته‏اند / ز انكه اين هر پنج از اصلى رسته‏اند
entries.english: The five senses are linked with one another, because all these five have grown from one root.

entries.farsi: قوت يك قوت باقى شود / ما بقى را هر يكى ساقى شود
entries.english: The strength of one becomes the strength of the rest: each one becomes a cup-bearer to the rest.

entries.farsi: ديدن ديده فزايد عشق را / عشق در ديده فزايد صدق را
entries.english: Seeing with the eye increases speech; speech increases penetration in the eye.

entries.farsi: صدق بيدارى هر حس مى‏شود / حسها را ذوق مونس مى‏شود
entries.english: Penetration becomes the awakening every sense, perception becomes familiar to the senses.

sections.title_fa: ‏آغاز منور شدن عارف به نور غيب بين
sections.title_en: The beginning of the gnostic’s illumination by the Light which sees the invisible world

entries.farsi: چون يكى حس در روش بگشاد بند / ما بقى حسها همه مبدل شوند
entries.english: When one sense in progress has loosed bonds, all the rest of the senses become changed.

entries.farsi: چون يكى حس غير محسوسات ديد / گشت غيبى بر همه حسها پديد
entries.english: When one sense has perceived things that are not objects of sense-perception, that which is of the invisible world becomes apparent to all the senses.

entries.farsi: چون ز جو جست از گله يك گوسفند / پس پياپى جمله ز آن سو بر جهند
entries.english: When one sheep of the flock has jumped over a stream, then they all jump across on each other’s heels.

entries.farsi: گوسفندان حواست را بران / ‏در چرا از أَخْ رَجَ الْمَرْعى‏ چران
entries.english: Drive the sheep, your senses, to pasture: let them browse on— He who hath brought forth the herbage,

entries.farsi: تا در آن جا سنبل و نسرين چرند / تا به گلزار حقايق ره برند
entries.english: That there they may browse on hyacinth and wild-rose; that they may make their way to the verdant meadows of the Realities;

entries.farsi: هر حست پيغمبر حسها شود / تا يكايك سوى آن جنت رود
entries.english: Every sense of yours may become an apostle to the senses, and lead all senses into that Paradise;

entries.farsi: حسها با حس تو گويند راز / بى‏زبان و بى‏حقيقت بى‏مجاز
entries.english: Senses will tell their secret to your senses, without tongue and without the proper or the metaphorical meaning;

entries.farsi: كاين حقيقت قابل تاويلهاست / ‏وين توهم مايه‏ى تخييلهاست
entries.english: For this proper meaning admits of interpretations, and this guess-work is the source of imaginings;

entries.farsi: آن حقيقت را كه باشد از عيان / ‏هيچ تاويلى نگنجد در ميان
entries.english: That truth which is immediate and intuitive, there is no room for any interpretation.

entries.farsi: چون كه هر حس بنده‏ى حس تو شد / مر فلك‏ها را نباشد از تو بد
entries.english: When senses have become subject to your sense, the heavenly spheres cannot avoid you.

entries.farsi: چون كه دعويى رود در ملك پوست / ‏مغز آن كى بود قشر آن اوست
entries.english: When a dispute takes place as to the ownership of the husk, the husk belongs to him who possesses the kernel.

entries.farsi: پس فلك قشر است و نور روح مغز / اين پديد است آن خفى زين رو ملغز
entries.english: When there happens to be a quarrel about a load of straw, observe who is the owner of the grain.

entries.farsi: چون تنازع در فتد در تنگ كاه / ‏دانه آن كيست آن را كن نگاه
entries.english: The heavenly sphere, then, is the husk, and the light of the spirit is the kernel. This is visible, that is concealed; do not stumble on this account.

entries.farsi: جسم ظاهر روح مخفى آمده ست / ‏جسم همچون آستين جان همچو دست
entries.english: The body is manifest; the spirit is concealed: the body is as the sleeve, the spirit as the hand.

entries.farsi: باز عقل از روح مخفى‏تر بود / حس سوى روح زوتر ره برد
entries.english: Again, the intellect is more concealed than the spirit: perception makes its way to the spirit sooner.

entries.farsi: جنبشى بينى بدانى زنده است / ‏اين ندانى كه ز عقل آگنده است
entries.english: You see a movement, you know that he is alive; this you do not know, that he is full of intellect,

entries.farsi: تا كه جنبشهاى موزون سر كند / جنبش مس را به دانش زر كند
entries.english: Until regulated movements appear, and he by means of knowledge turns the motion of copper into gold

entries.farsi: روح وحى از عقل پنهان‏تر بود / ز انكه او غيب است او ز ان سر بود
entries.english: From manual actions being conformable you may perceive that there is intellect.

entries.farsi: ز آن مناسب آمدن افعال دست / ‏فهم آيد مر ترا كه عقل هست
entries.english: The spirit of Divine inspiration is more concealed than the intellect, because it is the Unseen: it belongs to that side.

entries.farsi: عقل احمد از كسى پنهان نشد / روح وحيش مدرك هر جان نشد
entries.english: The intellect of Ahmad was not hidden from any one; his spirit of inspiration was not apprehended by every soul.

entries.farsi: روح وحيى را مناسبهاست نيز / در نيابد عقل كان آمد عزيز
entries.english: The spirit of prophecy also has actions conformable, the intellect does not apprehend, for that is exalted.

entries.farsi: گه جنون بيند گهى حيران شود / ز انكه موقوف است تا او آن شود
entries.english: Sometimes he regards as madness, sometime, he is bewildered, since it depends on his becoming that;

entries.farsi: چون مناسبهاى افعال خضر / عقل موسى بود در ديدش كدر
entries.english: As the intellect of Moses was troubled by seeing the reasonable actions of Khidr

entries.farsi: نامناسب مى‏نمود افعال او / پيش موسى چون نبودش حال او
entries.english: His actions seemed unreasonable to Moses, since he had not his state.

entries.farsi: عقل موسى چون شود در غيب بند / عقل موشى خود كى است اى ارجمند
entries.english: Inasmuch as the intellect of Moses becomes tied up in the mysterious, who is the intellect of a mouse, O excellent?

entries.farsi: علم تقليدى بود بهر فروخت / ‏چون بيابد مشترى خوش بر فروخت
entries.english: Conventional knowledge is for sale: when it finds a purchaser, it glows with delight.

entries.farsi: مشترى علم تحقيقى حق است / ‏دايما بازار او با رونق است
entries.english: The purchaser of real knowledge is God: its market is always splendid.

entries.farsi: لب ببسته مست در بيع و شرى / ‏مشترى بى‏حد كه اللهَّ اشترى
entries.english: He has closed his lips enraptured in trading: the purchasers are without end, for God hath purchased

entries.farsi: درس آدم را فرشته مشترى / ‏محرم درسش نه ديو است و پرى
entries.english: The angels purchase Adam’s teaching; the devils and Jinn are not privileged to receive it.

entries.farsi: آدم أنبئهم بأسما درس گو / شرح كن اسرار حق را مو به مو
entries.english: Adam, inform them of the Names, teach; explain the mysteries of God, hair by hair.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كس را كه كوته بين بود / در تلون غرق و بى‏تمكين بود
entries.english: Such a person as is short-sighted, plunged in variability and without steadfastness,

entries.farsi: موش گفتم ز انكه در خاك است جاش / ‏خاك باشد موش را جاى معاش
entries.english: I called a “mouse,” because his place is in the earth: earth is the place of living for the mouse.

entries.farsi: راهها داند ولى در زير خاك / ‏هر طرف او خاك را كرده ست چاك
entries.english: He knows ways, but underground: he has pierced the earth in every direction.

entries.farsi: نفس موشى نيست الا لقمه رند / قدر حاجت موش را عقلى دهند
entries.english: The mouse-soul is nothing but a nibbler: to the mouse is given a mind proportionate to its need,

entries.farsi: ز انكه بى‏حاجت خداوند عزيز / مى‏نبخشد هيچ كس را هيچ چيز
entries.english: Because without need the Almighty God does not give anything to any one

entries.farsi: گر نبودى حاجت عالم زمين / ‏نافريدى هيچ رب العالمين
entries.english: If the earth had not been needed by the world, the Lord of all beings would not have created any;

entries.farsi: وين زمين مضطرب محتاج كوه / گر نبودى نافريدى پر شكوه
entries.english: And if this quaking earth had not needed mountains, He would not have created them sublime;

entries.farsi: ور نبودى حاجت افلاک هم / ‏هفت گردون نافريدى از عدم
entries.english: And if there had not been need of the heavenly spheres also, He would not have created from non-existence the Seven Skies.

entries.farsi: آفتاب و ماه و اين استارگان / ‏جز به حاجت كى پديد آمد عيان
entries.english: The sun and moon and these stars— how did they come plain into view except through need?

entries.farsi: پس كمند هستها حاجت بود / قدر حاجت مرد را آلت دهد
entries.english: Need, then, is the noose for things that exist: Man has instruments in proportion to his need.

entries.farsi: پس بيفزا حاجت اى محتاج زود / تا بجوشد در كرم درياى جود
entries.english: Therefore quickly augment your need, O needy one, in order that the Sea of Bounty may surge up in loving-kindness.

entries.farsi: اين گدايان بر ره و هر مبتال / حاجت خود مى‏نمايد خلق را
entries.english: These beggars on the road, and every sufferer is displaying his need to the people

entries.farsi: كورى و شلى و بيمارى و درد / تا از اين حاجت بجنبد رحم مرد
entries.english: Blindness and palsy and sickness and pain— that men’s pity may be aroused by this need.

entries.farsi: هيچ گويد نان دهيد اى مردمان / ‏كه مرا مال است و انبار است و خوان
entries.english: Does he ever say, “Give bread, O people, for I have riches and granaries and trays?”

entries.farsi: چشم ننهاده‏ست حق در كور موش / ‏ز انكه حاجت نيست چشمش بهر نوش
entries.english: God has not put eyes in the mole, because it does not need eyes for food.

entries.farsi: مى‏تواند زيست بى‏چشم و بصر / فارغ است از چشم او در خاك تر
entries.english: It is able to live without eyes and sight: in the dank earth it is independent of eyes.

entries.farsi: جز به دزدى او برون نايد ز خاك / ‏تا كند خالق از آن دزديش پاك
entries.english: It never comes out from the earth but for theft, to the end that the Creator may purge it of that thievishness.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن پر يابد و مرغى شود / چون ماليك جانب گردون رود
entries.english: After that, it will get wings and become a bird, flying and glorifying the Creator.

entries.farsi: هر زمان در گلشن شكر خدا / او بر آرد همچو بلبل صد نوا
entries.english: Every moment, in the rose-garden of thanksgiving to God, it will produce a hundred notes, like the nightingale,

entries.farsi: كاى رهاننده مرا از وصف زشت / ‏اى كننده دوزخى را تو بهشت
entries.english: Singing, “O You who delivers me from evil qualities! O You that make a hell Paradise!

entries.farsi: در يكى پيهى نهى تو روشنى / ‏استخوانى را دهى سمع اى غنى
entries.english: You put light in a piece of fat; You, O Self-sufficing One, give hearing to a bone.”

entries.farsi: چه تعلق آن معانى را به جسم / ‏چه تعلق فهم اشيا را به اسم
entries.english: How are hose concepts connected with the body? What connection has the apprehension of things with names?

entries.farsi: او روان است و تو گويى واقف است / ‏او دوان است و تو گويى عاكف است
entries.english: The word is like the nest, and the meaning is the bird: the body is the river-bed, and the spirit is the rolling water.

entries.farsi: لفظ چون وكرست و معنى طاير است / ‏جسم جوى و روح آب ساير است
entries.english: It is moving, and you say it is standing: it is running, and you say it is keeping still.

entries.farsi: گر نبينى سير آب از خاكها / چيست بر وى نو به نو خاشاكها
entries.english: If you see not the movement of the water through the clods of earth: what are the sticks and straws anew on it?

entries.farsi: هست خاشاك تو صورتهاى فكر / نو به نو در مى‏رسد اشكال بكر
entries.english: Your sticks and straws are the forms of thought: virgin forms are always coming on anew.

entries.farsi: روى آب جوى فكر اندر روش / ‏نيست بى‏خاشاك محبوب و وحش
entries.english: The surface of the water of the stream of thought, as it rolls, is not without sticks and straws, pleasing and unsightly.

entries.farsi: قشرها بر روى اين آب روان / ‏از ثمار باغ غيبى شد دوان
entries.english: The husks on the surface of this rolling water have sped along from the fruits of the Invisible Garden.

entries.farsi: قشرها را مغز اندر باغ جو / ز انكه آب از باغ مى‏آيد به جو
entries.english: Seek the kernels of the husks in the Garden, because the water comes from the Garden into the river-bed.

entries.farsi: گر نبينى رفتن آب حيات / ‏بنگر اندر جوى و اين سير نبات
entries.english: If you see not the flow of the Water of Life, look at this movement of weeds in the stream.

entries.farsi: آب چون انبه‏تر آيد در گذر / زو كند قشر صور زوتر گذر
entries.english: When the water begins to pass by in fuller volume, the husks, the ideas, pass along it more quickly.

entries.farsi: چون به غايت تيز شد اين جو روان / ‏غم نپايد در ضمير عارفان
entries.english: When this stream has become extremely rapid in its flow, no care lingers in the minds of the gnostics.

entries.farsi: چون به غايت ممتلى بود و شتاب / ‏پس نگنجيد اندر او الا كه آب
entries.english: Since it is exceedingly full and swift, on that account there is no room in it for anything but the water.

sections.title_fa: طعنه زدن بيگانه اى در شيخ و جواب گفتن مريد شيخ او را
sections.title_en: How a stranger reviled the Shaykh and how the Shaykh’s disciple answered him.

entries.farsi: آن يكى يك شيخ را تهمت نهاد / كاو بد است و نيست بر راه رشاد
entries.english: A certain man brought charges against a Shaykh, saying, “He is wicked and not on the path of righteousness;

entries.farsi: شارب خمر است و سالوس و خبيث / ‏مر مريدان را كجا باشد مغيث
entries.english: He is a wine-drinker and a hypocrite and a scoundrel: how should he be one to succour his disciples?”

entries.farsi: آن يكى گفتش ادب را هوش دار / خرد نبود اين چنين ظن بر كبار
entries.english: One said to him, “Observe respect: it is no light matter to think so ill of the great.

entries.farsi: دور از او و دور از آن اوصاف او / كه ز سيلى تيره گردد صاف او
entries.english: Far is it from him and far from those qualities of his that his clear (spirit) should be darkened by a flood.

entries.farsi: اين چنين بهتان منه بر اهل حق / ‏اين خيال تست بر گردان ورق
entries.english: Do not put such slander on the people of God! This is fancy on your part. Turn over leaf.

entries.farsi: اين نباشد ور بود اى مرغ خاك / ‏بحر قلزم را ز مردارى چه باك
entries.english: This is not; and if it should be, O land-fowl, what harm to the Red Sea from a carcass?

entries.farsi: نيست دون القلتين و حوض خرد / كى تواند قطره‏ايش از كار برد
entries.english: He is not less than the two jugfuls or the small tank, so that a single drop should be able to disqualify him.

entries.farsi: آتش ابراهيم را نبود زيان / ‏هر كه نمرودى است گو مى‏ترس از آن
entries.english: The fire is no damage to Abraham, let any one who is a Nimrod beware of it!”

entries.farsi: نفس نمرود است و عقل و جان خليل / ‏روح در عين است و نفس اندر دليل
entries.english: The fleshly soul is Nimrod, and the intellect and spirit are the Friend of God: the spirit is concerned with reality itself, and the fleshly soul with the proofs.

entries.farsi: اين دليل راه رهرو را بود / كاو به هر دم در بيابان گم شود
entries.english: These indications of the way are for the traveller who at every moment becomes lost in the desert.

entries.farsi: واصالن را نيست جز چشم و چراغ / ‏از دليل و راهشان باشد فراغ
entries.english: For them that have attained there is nothing except the eye and the lamp: they have no concern with indications or with a road.

entries.farsi: گر دليلى گفت آن مرد وصال / ‏گفت بهر فهم اصحاب جدال
entries.english: If the man that is united has mentioned some indication, he has mentioned in order that the dialecticians may understand.

entries.farsi: بهر طفل نو پدر تى‏تى كند / گر چه عقلش هندسه‏ى گيتى كند
entries.english: For a new-born child the father makes babbling sounds, though his intellect may make a survey of the world.

entries.farsi: كم نگردد فضل استاد از علو / گر الف چيزى ندارد گويد او
entries.english: The dignity of the master’s learning is not diminished if he say that alif has nothing.

entries.farsi: از پى تعليم آن بسته دهن / ‏از زبان خود برون بايد شدن
entries.english: For the sake of teaching that tongue-tied, one must go outside of one’s own language

entries.farsi: در زبان او ببايد آمدن / ‏تا بياموزد ز تو او علم و فن
entries.english: You must come into his language, in order that he may learn knowledge and science from you.

entries.farsi: پس همه خلقان چو طفالن وى‏اند / الزم است اين پير را در وقت پند
entries.english: All the people, then, are as his children: this is necessary for the Pir when he gives instruction.

entries.farsi: كفر را حد است و اندازه بدان / ‏شيخ و نور شيخ را نبود كران
entries.english: Infidelity has a fixed limit and range—know; the Shaykh and the light of the Shaykh have no bound.

entries.farsi: كفر و ايمان نيست آن جايى كه اوست / ‏ز انكه او مغز است و اين دو رنگ و پوست
entries.english: Before the infinite all that is finite is naught: everything except the Face of God is passing away.

entries.farsi: پيش بى‏حد هر چه محدود است الست / ‏كل شى‏ء غير وجه اللهَّ فناست
entries.english: Infidelity and faith do not exist in the place where he is, because he is the kernel, while these two are colour and husk.

entries.farsi: اين فناها پرده‏ى آن وجه گشت / ‏چون چراغ خفيه اندر زير طشت
entries.english: These fleeting things have become a veil over that Face, like a lantern concealed beneath a bowl.

entries.farsi: پس سر اين تن حجاب آن سر است / ‏پيش آن سر اين سر تن كافر است
entries.english: So then, this bodily head is a screen to that head before that head this bodily head is an infidel.

entries.farsi: كيست كافر غافل از ايمان شيخ / ‏چيست مرده بى‏خبر از جان شيخ
entries.english: Who is the infidel? One forgetful of the faith of the Shaykh. What is the dead? One ignorant of the life of the Shaykh

entries.farsi: جان نباشد جز خبر در آزمون / ‏هر كه را افزون خبر جانش فزون
entries.english: Life is naught but knowledge in trial: the more knowledge one has, the more life one has.

entries.farsi: جان ما از جان حيوان بيشتر / از چه ز آن رو كه فزون دارد خبر
entries.english: Our spirit is more than the spirit of animals. How? In respect that it has more knowledge.

entries.farsi: پس فزون از جان ما جان ملك / ‏كاو منزه شد ز حس مشترك
entries.english: Hence the spirit of the angels is more than our spirit, for it is exempt from the common sense;

entries.farsi: ز آن سبب آدم بود مسجودشان / ‏جان او افزون‏تر است از بودشان
entries.english: And the spirit of mystical adepts is more than the angels. Cease from bewilderment!

entries.farsi: و ز ملك جان خداوندان دل / ‏باشد افزون تو تحير را بهل
entries.english: For that reason Adam is their object of worship: his spirit is greater than their being.

entries.farsi: ور نه بهتر را سجود دون‏ترى / ‏امر كردن هيچ نبود در خورى
entries.english: Else: it would not be at all a suitable thing to command the superior to worship an inferior.

entries.farsi: كى پسندد عدل و لطف كردگار / كه گلى سجده كند در پيش خار
entries.english: How can the justice and kindness of the Maker approve that a rose should fall down in worship before a thorn?

entries.farsi: جان چو افزون شد گذشت از انتها / شد مطيعش جان جمله‏ى چيزها
entries.english: Since the spirit has become superior and has passed beyond the utmost limit, the soul of all things has become obedient to it

entries.farsi: مرغ و ماهى و پرى و آدمى / ‏ز انكه او بيش است و ايشان در كمى
entries.english: Birds, fish, jinn and men—because it exceeds, and they are deficient

entries.farsi: ماهيان سوزنگر دلقش شوند / سوزنان را رشته‏ها تابع بوند
entries.english: The fish make needles for his mantle: threads follow needles.

sections.title_fa: بقيه‏ى قصه‏ى ابراهيم ادهم بر لب آن دريا
sections.title_en: The rest of the story of Ibrahim son of Adham—may God sanctify his spirit!— on the sea-shore.

entries.farsi: چون نفاذ امر شيخ آن مير ديد / ز آمد ماهى شدش و جدى پديد
entries.english: When that Amir saw the Shaykh’s command take effect in the coming of the fish, he fell into an ecstasy.

entries.farsi: گفت اه ماهى ز پيران آگه است / ‏شه تنى را كاو لعين درگه است
entries.english: He said, “Ah, the fish know the Pirs. Fie on a person who is an outcast of the Court!

entries.farsi: ماهيان از پير آگه ما بعيد / ما شقى زين دولت و ايشان سعيد
entries.english: The fish have knowledge of the Pir, and we afar! We damned to lack this fortune, and they blest!”

entries.farsi: سجده كرد و رفت گريان و خراب / ‏گشت ديوانه ز عشق فتح باب
entries.english: He bowed low, and departed weeping and desolate: he became mad for love of the opening of the door.

entries.farsi: پس تو اى ناشسته رو در چيستى / ‏در نزاع و در حسد با كيستى
entries.english: Then, O you with unwashed face, what are you about? Whom are you combating and envying?

entries.farsi: با دم شيرى تو بازى مى‏كنى / ‏بر ماليك ترك تازى مى‏كنى
entries.english: You are playing with a lion’s tail: you are making a foray against the angels.

entries.farsi: بد چه مى‏گويى تو خير محض را / هين ترفع كم شمر آن خفض را
entries.english: Why are you speaking evil of pure good? Beware, deem not that lowness to be eminence!

entries.farsi: بد چه باشد مس محتاج مهان / ‏شيخ كه بود كيمياى بى‏كران
entries.english: What is evil? The needy, despicable copper. Who is the Shaykh? The infinite elixir

entries.farsi: مس اگر از كيميا قابل نبد / كيميا از مس هرگز مس نشد
entries.english: If the copper was incapable by the elixir, the elixir was never turned into copper by the copper.
