entries.farsi: بد چه باشد سركشى آتش عمل / ‏شيخ كه بود عين درياى ازل
entries.english: What is evil? A rebel who works like fire. Who is the Shaykh? The very Sea of Eternity.

entries.farsi: دايم آتش را بترسانند از آب / ‏آب كى ترسيد هرگز ز التهاب
entries.english: Fire is always terrified by water. When was water ever afraid of being set aflame?

entries.farsi: در رخ مه عيب بينى مى‏كنى / ‏در بهشتى خارچينى مى‏كنى
entries.english: You are observing defects on the face of the moon: you are picking thorns in a Paradise.

entries.farsi: گر بهشت اندر روى تو خار جو / هيچ خار آن جا نيابى غير تو
entries.english: Picker of thorns, if you go into Paradise, you will find there no thorn but yourself.

entries.farsi: مى‏بپوشى آفتابى در گلى / ‏رخنه مى‏جويى ز بدر كاملى
entries.english: You are covering a sun with a sod: you are seeking flaws in a perfect full-moon.

entries.farsi: آفتابى كه بتابد در جهان / ‏بهر خفاشى كجا گردد نهان
entries.english: A sun which shines throughout the world— how shall it be hidden for the sake of a bat?

entries.farsi: عيبها از رد پيران عيب شد / غيبها از رشك ايشان غيب شد
entries.english: Sins are made sinful by the disapproval of Pirs; mysteries are made mysterious by the jealousy of Pirs.

entries.farsi: بارى از دورى ز خدمت يار باش / ‏در ندامت چابك و بر كار باش
entries.english: If you are far, at any rate be joined through respect: be alert and active in penitence,

entries.farsi: تا از آن راهت نسيمى مى‏رسد / آب رحمت را چه بندى از حسد
entries.english: That a breeze may be coming to you from that way. Why do you shut off the water of mercy by enviousness?

entries.farsi: گر چه دورى دور مى‏جنبان تو دم / ‏حيث ما كنتم فولوا وجهكم
entries.english: Though you are far aloof, at distance wag your tail: wherever you are, turn your faces.

entries.farsi: جاى را هموار نكند بهر باش / ‏داند او كه نيست آن جاى معاش
entries.english: When an ass falls in mire through a rapid pace, he moves incessantly for the purpose of rising.

entries.farsi: چون خرى در گل فتد از گام تيز / دم‏به‏دم جنبد براى عزم خيز
entries.english: He does not make the place smooth to stay in: he knows that it is not the place where he should live.

entries.farsi: حس تو از حس خر كمتر بده ست / ‏كه دل تو زين وحلها بر نجست
entries.english: Your sense has been less than the sense of the ass, for your heart has not recoiled from these clods of mud.

entries.farsi: در وحل تاويل رخصت مى‏كنى / ‏چون نمى‏خواهى كز آن دل بر كنى
entries.english: You interpret as an indulgence in the mud, since you are not willing to tear your heart from it.

entries.farsi: كاين روا باشد مرا من مضطرم / ‏حق نگيرد عاجزى را از كرم
entries.english: “This is allowable for me: I am under compulsion. God in His kindness will not chastise a helpless one.”

entries.farsi: خود گرفته ستت تو چون كفتار كور / اين گرفتن را نبينى از غرور
entries.english: Indeed He has chastised you, like the blind hyena from self-deception you do not see this chastisement.

entries.farsi: مى‏گوند اين جايگه كفتار نيست / ‏از برون جوييد كاندر غار نيست
entries.english: They are saying, “The hyena is not in this place; look for him outside, for he is not in the cave.”

entries.farsi: اين همى‏گويند و بندش مى‏نهند / او همى‏گويد ز من بى‏آگهند
entries.english: This they say and put bonds on him, he is saying, “They do not know of me.

entries.farsi: گر ز من آگاه بودى اين عدو / كى ندا كردى كه آن كفتار كو
entries.english: If this enemy had known of me, how should he have exclaimed, ‘Where is this hyena’?”

sections.title_fa: دعوى‏كردن آن شخص كه خداى تعالى مرا نمى‏گيرد به گناه و جواب گفتن شعيب عليه السلام مر او را
sections.title_en: The statement of a certain individual that God most High would not punish him for sin, and Shu’ayb’s answer to him.

entries.farsi: آن يكى مى‏گفت در عهد شعيب / ‏كه خدا از من بسى ديده ست عيب
entries.english: In the time of Shu’ayb a certain man was saying, “God has seen many a fault from me.

entries.farsi: چند ديد از من گناه و جرمها / و ز كرم يزدان نمى‏گيرد مرا
entries.english: How many sins and trespasses has He seen me commit! And, God in His kindness does not punish me.”

entries.farsi: حق تعالى گفت در گوش شعيب / ‏در جواب او فصيح از راه غيب
entries.english: In answer to him God most High by the mysterious way spoke clearly into the ear of Shu’ayb,

entries.farsi: كه بگفتى چند كردم من گناه / ‏و ز كرم نگرفت در جرمم اله
entries.english: Saying, “You have said, how many sins have I committed? And God in His kindness has not punished me for my trespasses.’

entries.farsi: عكس مى‏گويى و مقلوب اى سفيه / ‏اى رها كرده ره و بگرفته تيه
entries.english: You are saying the opposite and reverse, O fool, O you that have abandoned the road and taken to the wilderness!

entries.farsi: چند چندت گيرم و تو بى‏خبر / در سالسل مانده‏اى پا تا به سر
entries.english: How often, how often do I chastise you and you unaware! You are lying in chains from head to foot.

entries.farsi: بر دلت زنگار بر زنگارها / جمع شد تا كور شد ز اسرارها
entries.english: Your rust, coat on coat, O black pot, has marred the visage of your heart.

entries.farsi: زنگ تو بر تويت اى ديگ سياه / ‏كرد سيماى درونت را تباه
entries.english: Layers of rust have collected upon your heart, so that it has become blind to mysteries.”

entries.farsi: گر زند آن دود بر ديگ نوى / ‏آن اثر بنمايد ار باشد جوى
entries.english: If that smoke should beat upon a new pot, the traces of it would show, though it were a barley-corn,

entries.farsi: ز انكه هر چيزى به ضد پيدا شود / بر سپيدى آن سيه رسوا شود
entries.english: Because everything is made manifest by contrary: upon a white object the black becomes conspicuous;

entries.farsi: چون سيه شد ديگ پس تاثير دود / بعد از اين بروى كه بيند زود زود
entries.english: When the pot has been blackened, then after this who will at once perceive the effect of the smoke upon it?

entries.farsi: مرد آهنگر كه او زنگى بود / دود را با روش هم رنگى بود
entries.english: The ironsmith who is an Ethiopian— the smoke is of the same colour as his face;

entries.farsi: مرد رومى كاو كند آهنگرى / ‏رويش ابلق گردد از دود آورى
entries.english: The Greek who does the work of an ironsmith— his face, from gathering smoke, becomes spotted.

entries.farsi: پس بداند زود تاثير گناه / ‏تا بنالد زود گويد اى اله
entries.english: Therefore he will quickly recognise the effect of sin, so that he will soon lament say, “O God!”

entries.farsi: چون كند اصرار و بد پيشه كند / خاك اندر چشم انديشه كند
entries.english: When he persists and makes a practice of evil, and puts dust in the eye of meditation,

entries.farsi: توبه ننديشد دگر شيرين شود / بر دلش آن جرم تا بى‏دين شود
entries.english: He thinks of penitence no more: that sin becomes so sweet to his heart that he comes to be without the Faith.

entries.farsi: آن پشيمانى و يا رب رفت از او / شست بر آيينه زنگ پنج تو
entries.english: That repenting and “O Lord!” is gone from him: five layers of rust have settled on the mirror.

entries.farsi: آهنش را زنگها خوردن گرفت / ‏گوهرش را زنگ كم كردن گرفت
entries.english: The coats of rust have begun to eat his iron: the rust has begun to lessen its sheen.

entries.farsi: چون نويسى كاغذ اسپيد بر / آن نبشته خوانده آيد در نظر
entries.english: When you write upon white paper, that writing may be read at sight.

entries.farsi: چون نويسى بر سر بنوشته خط / فهم نايد خواندنش گردد غلط
entries.english: When you write script over that which has been written, it is not understood: the reading of it will be erroneous;

entries.farsi: كان سياهى بر سياهى اوفتاد / هر دو خط شد كور و معنيى نداد
entries.english: For that blackness has fallen upon blackness; both scripts have become obscure and have given no meaning.

entries.farsi: ور سوم باره نويسى بر سرش / ‏پس سيه كردى چو جان كافرش
entries.english: And if you write a third time on the top of it, then you make it black as the infidel’s soul.

entries.farsi: پس چه چاره جز پناه چاره‏گر / نااميدى مس و اكسيرش نظر
entries.english: What help is there, then, but refuge with the Helper? Despair is copper, and the elixir for it is regard.

entries.farsi: نااميديها به پيش او نهيد / تا ز درد بى‏دوا بيرون جهيد
entries.english: Lay your despairs before Him that you may escape from irremediable pain.

entries.farsi: چون شعيب اين نكته‏ها با او بگفت / ‏ز آن دم جان در دل او گل شكفت
entries.english: When Shu’ayb had told him these deep sayings, at that breath of the spirit roses blossomed in his heart.

entries.farsi: جان او بشنيد وحى آسمان / ‏گفت اگر بگرفت ما را كو نشان
entries.english: His soul hearkened to the inspiration from Heaven; he said,, “ If He has punished me, where is the sign? “

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب دفع من مى‏گويد او / آن گرفتن را نشان مى‏جويد او
entries.english: He cried, “O Lord, he rebuts me, he seeks the sign of that punishment.”

entries.farsi: گفت ستارم نگويم رازهاش / ‏جز يكى رمز از براى ابتالش
entries.english: He said, “ I am the Coverer: I will not tell his secrets, but one indication for the sake of trying him.

entries.farsi: يك نشان آن كه مى‏گيرم و را / آن كه طاعت دارد از صوم و دعا
entries.english: One sign of my punishing him is this, that he has pious acts of fasting and prayer

entries.farsi: و ز نماز و از زكات و غير آن / ‏ليك يك ذره ندارد ذوق جان
entries.english: And ritual prayer and almsgiving et cetera, but he has not one atom of spiritual savour.

entries.farsi: مى‏كند طاعات و افعال سنى / ‏ليك يك ذره ندارد چاشنى
entries.english: He performs high acts and deeds of devotion, but he has not one atom of relish.

entries.farsi: طاعتش نغز است و معنى نغز نى / ‏جوزها بسيار و در وى مغز نى
entries.english: His devotions are good, but the spirit is not good: the walnuts are many, but there is no kernel therein.”

entries.farsi: ذوق بايد تا دهد طاعات بر / مغز بايد تا دهد دانه شجر
entries.english: Spiritual savour is required, in order that devotions yield fruit: a kernel is required, in order that the berry produce a tree.

entries.farsi: دانه‏ى بى‏مغز كى گردد نهال / ‏صورت بى‏جان نباشد جز خيال
entries.english: How shall a berry without kernel become a sapling? The soulless form is naught but vain imaginings.

sections.title_fa: ‏بقيه‏ى قصه‏ى طعنه زدن آن مرد بيگانه در شيخ
sections.title_en: Remainder of the story of the stranger’s reviling the Shaykh.

entries.farsi: آن خبيث از شيخ مى‏الييد ژاژ / كژنگر باشد هميشه عقل كاژ
entries.english: That malign wretch was gabbling silly nonsense about the Shaykh: the squinting man is always of distorted understanding.

entries.farsi: كه منش ديدم ميان مجلسى / ‏او ز تقوى عارى است و مفلسى
entries.english: “I saw him amidst a company: he is one denuded and destitute of piety.

entries.farsi: ور كه باور نيستت خيز امشبان / ‏تا ببينى فسق شيخت را عيان
entries.english: And if you do not believe it, get up to-night, that you may see plainly your Shaykh’s depravity.”

entries.farsi: شب ببردش بر سر يك روزنى / ‏گفت بنگر فسق و عشرت كردنى
entries.english: At night he took him to a window and said, “Behold a debauch and merry-making!

entries.farsi: بنگر آن سالوس روز و فسق شب / ‏روز همچون مصطفى شب بو لهب
entries.english: Behold such hypocrisy by day and profligacy by night!— in the daytime like Mustafa, at night Bit Lahab.

entries.farsi: روز عبد اهلل او را گشته نام / ‏شب نعوذ باهلل و در دست جام
entries.english: By day his name has become `Abdullah; by night— God save us! And, the wine-cup in his hand!”

entries.farsi: ديد شيشه در كف آن پير پر / گفت شيخا مر ترا هم هست غر
entries.english: He saw a full glass in the Shaykh’s hand. “O Shaykh,” said he “is there a tumour even in you?

entries.farsi: تو نمى‏گفتى كه در جام شراب / ‏ديو مى‏ميزد شتابان ناشتاب
entries.english: The Devil’s urine will not fit inside the wine cup.

entries.farsi: گفت جامم را چنان پر كرده‏اند / كاندر او اندر نگنجد يك سپند
entries.english: He replied, “They have made my cup so full that there is not room in it for a single rue-seed.

entries.farsi: بنگر اينجا هيچ گنجد ذره‏اى / ‏اين سخن را كژ شنيده غره‏اى
entries.english: Look, is there any room here for a single mote? A deluded man has wrongly apprehended this matter.”

entries.farsi: جام ظاهر خمر ظاهر نيست اين / ‏دور دار اين را ز شيخ غيب بين
entries.english: This is not the apparent cup the apparent wine: deem this far from the Shaykh who sees the Unseen.

entries.farsi: جام مى هستى شيخ است اى فليو / كاندر او اندر نگنجد بول ديو
entries.english: The wine-cup, O fool, is the being of the Shaykh, In which the bowel of a demon does not fit.

entries.farsi: پر و مالامال از نور حق است / ‏جام تن بشكست نور مطلق است
entries.english: He is full and brimming with the Light of God: he has shattered the bodily cup, he is the Absolute Light.

entries.farsi: نور خورشيد ار بيفتد بر حدث / ‏او همان نور است نپذيرد خبث
entries.english: If sunlight falls upon filth, it is the same light: it suffers no defilement.

entries.farsi: شيخ گفت اين خود نه جام است و نه مى / ‏هين به زير آن منكرا بنگر به وى
entries.english: The Shaykh said, “Indeed this is not a cup, nor wine. Hey, unbeliever, come down and look at it!”

entries.farsi: آمد و ديد انگبين خاص بود / كور شد آن دشمن كور و كبود
entries.english: He came, and saw it was fine honey. That miserable enemy became blind.

entries.farsi: گفت پير آن دم مريد خويش را / رو براى من بجو مى اى كيا
entries.english: Thereupon the Pir said to his disciple, “Go, seek wine for me, O noble sir;

entries.farsi: كه مرا رنجى است مضطر گشته‏ام / ‏من ز رنج از مخمصه بگذشته‏ام
entries.english: For I have a pain; I am reduced to necessity: because of the pain, I have passed beyond starvation.

entries.farsi: در ضرورت هست هر مردار پاك / ‏بر سر منكر ز لعنت باد خاك
entries.english: In sore need any carcass is clean— may curses fall like dust on the head of him that denies it!”

entries.farsi: گرد خمخانه بر آمد آن مريد / بهر شيخ از هر خمى او مى‏چشيد
entries.english: The disciple went round the wine-cellar, tasting of every jar on the Shaykh’s behalf.

entries.farsi: در همه خمخانه‏ها او مى نديد / گشته بد پر از عسل خم نبيد
entries.english: In all the wine-cellars he found no wine: the jars of wine had become full of honey.

entries.farsi: گفت اى رندان چه حال است اين چه كار / هيچ خمى در نمى‏بينم عقار
entries.english: He said, “O drunkardss, what state of things is this? What is the matter? I find no wine in any jar.”

entries.farsi: جمله رندان نزد آن شيخ آمدند / چشم گريان دست بر سر مى‏زدند
entries.english: All the drunkards came to that Shaykh, weeping and beating their heads with their hands.

entries.farsi: در خرابات آمدى شيخ اجل / ‏جمله مى‏ها از قدومت شد عسل
entries.english: “You came into the tavern, O most exalted Shaykh, and in consequence of your coming all the wines have turned to honey.

entries.farsi: كرده اى مبدل تو مى را از حدث / ‏جان ما را هم بدل كن از خبث
entries.english: You have changed the wine from filth: change our souls also from defilement!”

entries.farsi: گر شود عالم پر از خون مال مال / ‏كى خورد بنده‏ى خدا الا حالل
entries.english: If the world be filled to the brim with blood, how should the servant of God drink aught but what is hallowed?

sections.title_fa: گفتن عايشه مصطفى را عليه السلام كه تو بى‏مصال به هر جا نماز مى‏كنى چون است
sections.title_en: How A’isha—may God be well-pleased with her!—said to Mustafa, on whom be peace, “You perform the prayer anywhere, without a prayer-carpet.”

entries.farsi: عايشه روزى به پيغمبر بگفت / ‏يا رسول اهلل تو پيدا و نهفت
entries.english: One day `A’isha said to the Prophet, “O Messenger of Allah, openly and secretly

entries.farsi: مستحاضه و طفل و آلوده‏ى پليد / كرد مستعمل به هر جا كه رسيد
entries.english: You perform a prayer in whatever place you may find, unclean and low are running about in the house;

entries.farsi: هر كجا يابى نمازى مى‏كنى / ‏مى‏دود در خانه ناپاك و دنى
entries.english: Although you know that any dirty child pollutes every place he enters.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه از بهر مهان / ‏حق نجس را پاك گرداند بدان
entries.english: The Prophet said, “ Know that God makes impure pure for the great.

entries.farsi: سجده‏گاهم را از آن رو لطف حق / ‏پاك گردانيد تا هفتم طبق
entries.english: On that account the grace of God has made my place of worship to be pure up to the seventh tier.”

entries.farsi: هان و هان ترك حسد كن با شهان / ‏ور نه ابليسى شوى اندر جهان
entries.english: Beware and beware! Cease from envying the kings, else you will become a devil in the world.

entries.farsi: كاو اگر زهرى خورد شهدى شود / تو اگر شهدى خورى زهرى بود
entries.english: For if he drinks poison, it turns to honey; if you eat honey, it is poison;

entries.farsi: كاو بدل گشت و بدل شد كار او / لطف گشت و نور شد هر نار او
entries.english: For he has been changed, and his action has been changed: he has become the Grace, and every fire in him has been turned into Light.

entries.farsi: قوت حق بود مر بابيل را / ور نه مرغى چون كشد مر پيل را
entries.english: The ababil (swifts) had the power of God; else, how should a bird kill an elephant?

entries.farsi: لشكرى را مرغكى چندى شكست / ‏تا بدانى كان صالبت از حق است
entries.english: A number of little birds broke an army— so that you may know that that strength is from God.

entries.farsi: گر تو را وسواس آيد زين قبيل / ‏رو بخوان تو سوره‏ى اصحاب فيل
entries.english: If temptation of this kind comes to you go, read the Sura concerning the Possessors of the Elephant.

entries.farsi: ور كنى با او مرى و همسرى / ‏كافرم دان گر تو ز ايشان سر برى
entries.english: And if you contend and engage in rivalry with him, deem me an infidel if you save your head from them.

sections.title_fa: كشيدن موش مهار شتر را و متعجب شدن موش در خود
sections.title_en: How the mouse pulled the camel’s nose-ring and became self-conceited.

entries.farsi: موشكى در كف مهار اشترى / ‏در ربود و شد روان او از مرى
entries.english: A little mouse caught in his forelegs a camel’s leading-rope and from emulation went off.

entries.farsi: اشتر از چستى كه با او شد روان / ‏موش غره شد كه هستم پهلوان
entries.english: By reason of the readiness with which the camel set out along with him, the mouse was duped into thinking himself a hero.

entries.farsi: بر شتر زد پرتو انديشه‏اش / ‏گفت بنمايم ترا تو باش خوش
entries.english: The ray of his thought struck the camel. He said, “I will show you! Enjoy yourself!”

entries.farsi: تا بيامد بر لب جوى بزرگ / ‏كاندر او گشتى زبون پيل سترگ
entries.english: Till he came to the bank of a great river, at which any lion or wolf would have lost heart.

entries.farsi: موش آن جا ايستاد و خشك گشت / ‏گفت اشتر اى رفيق كوه و دشت
entries.english: There the mouse stopped and became paralysed. The camel said, “O my companion over hill and plain,

entries.farsi: اين توقف چيست حيرانى چرا / پا بنه مردانه اندر جو در آ
entries.english: What is this standing still? Why art you dismayed? Step like a man ! Go into the river!

entries.farsi: تو قالووزى و پيش آهنگ من / ‏در ميان ره مباش و تن مزن
entries.english: You art my guide and leader: don’t halt midway and be dumbfounded!”

entries.farsi: گفت اين آب شگرف است و عميق / ‏من همى‏ترسم ز غرقاب اى رفيق
entries.english: He said, “This is a huge and deep river: I am afraid of being drowned, O comrade.”

entries.farsi: گفت اشتر تا ببينم حد آب / ‏پا در او بنهاد آن اشتر شتاب
entries.english: Said the camel, “Let me see the limit of the water,” and he quickly set foot in it.

entries.farsi: گفت تا زانوست آب اى كور موش / ‏از چه حيران گشتى و رفتى ز هوش
entries.english: “The water,” he said,” is up to the knee. O blind mouse, wherefore did you become dismayed and lose your wits?”

entries.farsi: گفت مور تست و ما را اژدهاست / ‏كه ز زانو تا به زانو فرق‏هاست
entries.english: He replied, “It is an ant to you, but to me it is a dragon, for there are differences between one knee and another.

entries.farsi: گر ترا تا زانو است اى پر هنر / مر مرا صد گز گذشت از فرق سر
entries.english: f it is up to your knee, O excellent one, it is a hundred ells higher than the crown of my head.”

entries.farsi: گفت گستاخى مكن بار دگر / تا نسوزد جسم و جانت زين شرر
entries.english: He said, “Another time, do not behave boldly, lest your body and soul be consumed by these sparks.

entries.farsi: تو مرى با مثل خود موشان بكن / ‏با شتر مر موش را نبود سخن
entries.english: Contend with mice like yourself: a mouse has nothing to say to a camel.”

entries.farsi: گفت توبه كردم از بهر خدا / بگذران زين آب مهلك مر مرا
entries.english: He said, “I repent. For God’s sake, get me across this deadly water!”

entries.farsi: رحم آمد مر شتر را گفت هين / ‏برجه و بر كودبان من نشين
entries.english: The camel took pity. “Listen,” said he, “jump up and sit on my hump.

entries.farsi: اين گذشتن شد مسلم مر مرا / بگذرانم صد هزاران چون ترا
entries.english: This passage has been vouchsafed to me: I would take across hundreds of thousands like you.”

entries.farsi: چون پيمبر نيستى پس رو به راه / ‏تا رسى از چاه روزى سوى جاه
entries.english: Since you are not a prophet, go on the road, that one day you may come from the pit to place and power.

entries.farsi: تو رعيت باش چون سلطان نه‏اى / ‏خود مران چون مرد كشتيبان نه‏اى
entries.english: Be a vassal since you are not a lord: do not steer yourself, since you are not the boatman.

entries.farsi: چون نه‏اى كامل دكان تنها مگير / دست‏خوش مى‏باش تا گردى خمير
entries.english: Since you are not perfect, do not take a shop alone. Be pliant to the hand, in order that you may become leavened

entries.farsi: أَنْصِ تُوا را گوش كن خاموش باش / ‏چون زبان حق نگشتى گوش باش
entries.english: Give ear to, “Keep silence,” be mute; since you have not become the tongue of God, be an ear.

entries.farsi: ور بگويى شكل استفسار گو / با شهنشاهان تو مسكين‏وار گو
entries.english: And if you speak, speak in the form of a request for explanation: speak to the emperors as a lowly beggar.

entries.farsi: ابتداى كبر و كين از شهوت است / ‏راسخى شهوتت از عادت است
entries.english: The beginning of pride and hatred is in lust, and the rootedness of your lust is from habit.

entries.farsi: چون ز عادت گشت محكم خوى بد / خشم آيد بر كسى كت واكشد
entries.english: When an evil disposition becomes confirmed by habit, you are enraged with any one who restrains you.

entries.farsi: چون كه تو گل خوار گشتى هر كه او / واكشد از گل ترا باشد عدو
entries.english: After you have become an eater of clay, any one who restrains you from clay is your enemy.

entries.farsi: بت پرستان چون كه خو با بت كنند / مانعان راه بت را دشمنند
entries.english: Since idolaters are accustomed to the idol, they are foes to them that stop the way to the idol.

entries.farsi: چون كه كرد ابليس خو با سرورى / ‏ديد آدم را حقير او از خرى
entries.english: Since Iblis had become accustomed to being leader, he looked on Adam with disbelief,

entries.farsi: كه به از من سرورى ديگر بود / تا كه او مسجود چون من كس شود
entries.english: Saying, “Is there another leader superior to me, so that he should be worshipped by one like me?”

entries.farsi: سرورى زهر است جز آن روح را / كاو بود ترياق النى ز ابتدا
entries.english: Leadership is poison, except to the spirit that from the beginning has abundance of the antidote.

entries.farsi: كوه اگر پر مار شد باكى مدار / كاو بود در اندرون ترياق‏زار
entries.english: If the mountain is full of snakes, have no fear, for it is a mine of antidote within.

entries.farsi: سرورى چون شد دماغت را نديم / ‏هر كه بشكستت شود خصم قديم
entries.english: When leadership has become a bosom-friend to your brain, any one who breaks you becomes an ancient adversary.

entries.farsi: چون خالف خوى تو گويد كسى / ‏كينه‏ها خيزد ترا با او بسى
entries.english: When any one contradicts your disposition, many feelings of hatred against him arise in you.

entries.farsi: كه مرا از خوى من بر مى‏كند / خويش را بر من چو سرور مى‏كند
entries.english: “He is tearing me from my disposition; he is making me a pupil and follower.”

entries.farsi: چون نباشد خوى بد سركش در او / كى فروزد آن خالف آتش در او
entries.english: Unless the evil disposition has become strongly implanted, how should the fire-temple blaze up through being opposed?

entries.farsi: با مخالف او مدارايى كند / در دل او خويش را جايى كند
entries.english: He may show some feigned courtesy to the opponent, he may make a place for himself in his heart,

entries.farsi: ز انكه خوى بد بگشته ست استوار / مور شهوت شد ز عادت همچو مار
entries.english: Because the evil disposition has waxed strong: the ant of lust has through habit become as a snake.

entries.farsi: مار شهوت را بكش در ابتدا / ور نه اينك گشت مارت اژدها
entries.english: Kill the snake of lust at the beginning; else, look you, your snake is become a dragon.

entries.farsi: ليك هر كس مور بيند مار خويش / ‏تو ز صاحب دل كن استفسار خويش
entries.english: But every one deems his own snake an ant: do you seek the explanation of yourself from him that is lord of the heart.

entries.farsi: تا نشد زر مس نداند من مسم / ‏تا نشد شه دل نداند مفلسم
entries.english: Until copper becomes gold, it does not know itself to be copper: until the heart becomes a king, it does not know itself to be an insolvent.

entries.farsi: خدمت اكسير كن مس‏وار تو / جور مى‏كش اى دل از دل دار تو
entries.english: Do service to the elixir, like copper: endure oppression, O heart, from him that holds the heart in fee.

entries.farsi: كيست دل دار اهل دل نيكو بدان / ‏كه چو روز و شب جهانند از جهان
entries.english: Who is it that holds the heart in fee? Know well, it is the lords of the heart who, like day and night, are recoiling from the world.

entries.farsi: عيب كم گو بنده‏ى اهلل را / متهم كم كن به دزدى شاه را
entries.english: Do not find fault with the Servant of God: do not suspect the King of being a thief.

sections.title_fa: كرامات آن درويش كه در كشتى متهمش كردند
sections.title_en: The miracles of the dervish who was suspected of theft in a ship

entries.farsi: بود درويشى درون كشتيى / ‏ساخته از رخت مردى پشتيى
entries.english: A dervish was in a ship: he had made a bolster from the goods of saintly fortitude.

entries.farsi: ياوه شد هميان زر او خفته بود / جمله را جستند و او را هم نمود
entries.english: A purse of gold was lost. He was asleep. They searched all and brought him also to view,

entries.farsi: كاين فقير خفته را جوييم هم / ‏كرد بيدارش ز غم صاحب درم
entries.english: Saying, “Let us search this sleeping mendicant as well.” the owner of the money, by grief, awakened him.

entries.farsi: كه در اين كشتى حرمدان گمشدست / ‏جمله را جستيم نتوانى تو رست
entries.english: “A bag of valuables,” said he “has been lost in this ship. We have searched the whole company: you cannot escape.

entries.farsi: دلق بيرون كن برهنه شو ز دلق / ‏تا ز تو فارغ شود اوهام خلق
entries.english: Put off your dervish-cloak; strip yourself of it, in order that the people’s suspicions may be cleared away from you.”

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب مر غالمت را خسان / ‏متهم كردند فرمان در رسان
entries.english: He cried, “ O Lord, these vile wretches have made an accusation against your slave: bring your command to pass!”

entries.farsi: چون به درد آمد دل درويش از آن / ‏سر برون كردند هر سو در زمان
entries.english: When the heart of the dervish was pained by that, at once there put forth their heads on every side

entries.farsi: صد هزاران ماهى از درياى ژرف / ‏در دهان هر يكى درى شگرف
entries.english: From the deep sea myriads of fishes, and in the mouth of each a superb pearl:

entries.farsi: صد هزاران ماهى از درياى پر / در دهان هر يكى در و چه در
entries.english: Myriads of fishes out of the full sea, each with a pearl in its mouth—and what pearls!

entries.farsi: هر يكى درى خراج ملكتى / ‏كز اله است اين ندارد شركتى
entries.english: Every pearl the revenue of a kingdom. “These,” they said, “are from God, they have no association.”

entries.farsi: در چند انداخت در كشتى و جست / ‏مر هوا را ساخت كرسى و نشست
entries.english: He dropped a quantity of pearls on the ship and sprang: he made the air his high-seat and sat,

entries.farsi: خوش مربع چون شهان بر تخت خويش / ‏او فراز اوج و كشتى‏اش به پيش
entries.english: At ease, cross-legged, as kings upon their thrones— he above the zenith, and the ship before him

entries.farsi: تا كه را باشد خسارت زين فراق / ‏من خوشم جفت حق و با خلق طاق
entries.english: He said, “Go! The ship for you, God for me, so that a beggarly thief may not be with you!

entries.farsi: گفت رو كشتى شما را حق مرا / تا نباشد با شما دزد گدا
entries.english: Let us see who will be the loser by this separation! I am pleased, paired with God and singled from creatures.

entries.farsi: نه مرا او تهمت دزدى نهد / نه مهارم را به غمازى دهد
entries.english: He does not accuse me of theft, He does not hand me over to an informer’.”

entries.farsi: بانگ كردند اهل كشتى كاى همام / ‏از چه دادندت چنين عالى مقام
entries.english: The people in the ship cried out, “O noble chief, wherefore has such a high estate been given to you?”

entries.farsi: گفت از تهمت نهادن بر فقير / و ز حق آزارى پى چيزى حقير
entries.english: He answered, “For throwing suspicion on dervishes and offending God on account of a despicable thing

entries.farsi: حاش هلل بل ز تعظيم شهان / ‏كه نبودم در فقيران بد گمان
entries.english: God forbid! Nay, for showing reverence to kings, inasmuch as I did not conceive ill thoughts against dervishes

entries.farsi: آن فقيران لطيف خوش نفس / ‏كز پى تعظيمشان آمد عبس
entries.english: Those gracious dervishes of sweet breath, for whose magnification Abasa was revealed”

entries.farsi: آن فقيرى بهر پيچا پيچ نيست / ‏بل پى آن كه بجز حق هيچ نيست
entries.english: That dervishhood is not for the sake of entanglement; no, because nothing exists but God

entries.farsi: متهم چون دارم آنها را كه حق / ‏كرد امين مخزن هفتم طبق
entries.english: How should I hold in suspicion those whom God has entrusted with the treasury of the Seventh Heaven?

entries.farsi: متهم نفس است نه عقل شريف / ‏متهم حس است نه نور لطيف
entries.english: The fleshly soul is suspect, not the sublime Reason: the senses are suspect, not the subtle Light.

entries.farsi: نفس سوفسطايى آمد مى‏زنش / ‏كش زدن سازد نه حجت گفتنش
entries.english: The fleshly soul is a sophist: beat it constantly, for beating does it good, not arguing with it.

entries.farsi: معجزه بيند فروزد آن زمان / ‏بعد از آن گويد خيالى بود آن
entries.english: It sees a miracle, and at the moment it glows; afterwards it says, “It was an imagining;

entries.farsi: ور حقيقت بودى آن ديد عجب / ‏چون مقيم چشم نامد روز و شب
entries.english: For if that wondrous sight had been real, and then it would have been abiding, day and night, in the eye.”

entries.farsi: آن مقيم چشم پاكان مى‏بود / نه قرين چشم حيوان مى‏شود
entries.english: It is abiding in the eyes of the pure, it does not haunt the eyes of animals;

entries.farsi: كان عجب زين حس دارد عار و ننگ / ‏كى بود طاوس اندر چاه تنگ
entries.english: For the miracle is ashamed and scornful of these senses: how should a peacock be in a narrow pit?

entries.farsi: تا نگويى مر مرا بسيار گو / من ز صد يك گويم و آن همچو مو
entries.english: Take heed not to call me garrulous: I say one in a hundred, and that like a hair.

sections.title_fa: تشنيع صوفيان بر آن صوفى كه پيش شيخ بسيار مى‏گويد
sections.title_en: How some Sufis abused a certain Sufi, saying that he talked too much in the presence of the Shaykh.

entries.farsi: شيخ را گفتند داد جان ما / تو از اين صوفى بجو اى پيشوا
entries.english: Some Sufis abused a certain Sufi, and came to the Shaykh of the convent,

entries.farsi: صوفيان بر صوفيى شنعت زدند / پيش شيخ خانقاهى آمدند
entries.english: And said to the Shaykh, “Demand justice for our souls from this Sufi, O Guide!”

entries.farsi: گفت آخر چه گله ست اى صوفيان / ‏گفت اين صوفى سه خو دارد گران
entries.english: He said, “Why, what is the complaint, O Sufis?” He replied, “This Sufi has three annoying habits:

entries.farsi: در سخن بسيار گو همچون جرس / در خورش افزون خورد از بيست كس
entries.english: In speech he is garrulous as a bell; in eating he eats more than twenty persons;

entries.farsi: ور بخسبد هست چون اصحاب كهف / ‏صوفيان كردند پيش شيخ زحف
entries.english: And if he sleeps, he is like the Men of the Cave.” did the Sufis march to war before the Shaykh.

entries.farsi: شيخ رو آورد سوى آن فقير / كه ز هر حالى كه هست اوساط گير
entries.english: The Shaykh turned his face towards that dervish, saying, “In every case that exists, take the middle.

entries.farsi: در خبر خير األمور أوساطها / نافع آمد ز اعتدال أخالطه
entries.english: In Tradition that the best things are the mean; the (four) humours are beneficial through being in balance.

entries.farsi: گر يكى خلطى فزون شد از عرض / ‏در تن مردم پديد آيد مرض
entries.english: If by accident one humour becomes excessive, disease appears in the human body.

entries.farsi: كه در اين كشتى حرمدان گمشدست / ‏جمله را جستيم نتوانى تو رست
entries.english: “A bag of valuables,” said he “has been lost in this ship. We have searched the whole company: you cannot escape.

entries.farsi: دلق بيرون كن برهنه شو ز دلق / ‏تا ز تو فارغ شود اوهام خلق
entries.english: Put off your dervish-cloak; strip yourself of it, in order that the people’s suspicions may be cleared away from you.”
